```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\rc18763\\.copilot\\session-state\\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\\plan.md\n+# Stock recommendation system plan\n+\n+## Problem\n+Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. The first version should behave like a research and decision-support product, not a \"magic best stock\" generator.\n+\n+## Current state\n+- Workspace `C:\\copilot` is empty; there is no existing application code to extend.\n+- No repository structure, app framework, data pipeline, or model stack is present yet.\n+- This plan assumes a greenfield implementation.\n+\n+## Proposed approach\n+Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:\n+\n+1. Product definition\n+   - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.\n+   - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.\n+\n+2. Data foundation\n+   - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data, and optional news or macro inputs.\n+   - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.\n+\n+3. Research feature layer\n+   - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.\n+   - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.\n+\n+4. Modeling and recommendation logic\n+   - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.\n+   - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.\n+   - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.\n+\n+5. Evaluation and controls\n+   - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.\n+   - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.\n+   - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.\n+\n+6. Explainability and delivery\n+   - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.\n+   - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.\n+\n+## Draft todo list\n+- `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.\n+- `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.\n+- `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.\n+- `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.\n+- `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.\n+- `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.\n+- `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.\n+- `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.\n+- `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard for viewing rankings and diagnostics.\n+- `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.\n+\n+## Notes and considerations\n+- \"Like BlackRock\" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.\n+- The safest and most realistic MVP is a research dashboard that recommends ranked candidates with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.\n+- Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.\n+- A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.\n+- Key open question: what kind of first product do you want to ship?\n*** End Patch\n"
```

</details>

When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.\n@@\n 4. Modeling and recommendation logic\n    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.\n    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.\n-   - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.\n+   - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.\n+   - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.\n@@\n 6. Explainability and delivery\n    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.\n    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.\n    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.\n+   - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.\n+\n+7. UI template recommendation\n+   - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.\n+   - Recommended starting point: a clean admin template style such as Next.js with shadcn/ui or Ant Design Pro.\n+   - Layout suggestion:\n+     - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.\n+     - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.\n+     - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.\n+   - Stock detail page should include:\n+     - Recommendation summary card.\n+     - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.\n+     - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.\n+     - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.\n@@\n - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.\n+- `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.\n - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.\n - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.\n-- `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard for viewing rankings and diagnostics.\n+- `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.\n - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.\n@@\n - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.\n+- Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.\n - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.\n - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.\n - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.\n-- Remaining scope question for implementation: should the daily run publish recommendations after market close, before market open, or both?\n+- Remaining scope question for implementation: what holding horizon should the entry, target, and stop-loss logic optimize for?\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 49m 10s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md

<details>
<summary>82 lines</summary>

```
1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - Workspace `C:\copilot` is empty; there is no existing application code to extend.
8. - No repository structure, app framework, data pipeline, or model stack is present yet.
9. - This plan assumes a greenfield implementation.
10. 
11. ## Proposed approach
12. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
13. 
14. 1. Product definition
15.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
16.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
17.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
18. 
19. 2. Data foundation
20.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
21.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
22.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
23. 
24. 3. Research feature layer
25.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
26.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
27. 
28. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
29.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
30.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
31.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
32.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
33.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
34. 
35. 5. Evaluation and controls
36.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
37.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
38.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
39. 
40. 6. Explainability and delivery
41.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
42.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
43.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
44.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
45. 
46. 7. UI template recommendation
47.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
48.    - Recommended starting point: a clean admin template style such as Next.js with shadcn/ui or Ant Design Pro.
49.    - Layout suggestion:
50.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.
51.      - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.
52.      - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.
53.    - Stock detail page should include:
54.      - Recommendation summary card.
55.      - Horizon selector so the same stock can show swing, position, and long-term variants when available.
56.      - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.
57.      - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.
58.      - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.
59. 
60. ## Draft todo list
61. - `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.
62. - `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.
63. - `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.
64. - `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.
65. - `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.
66. - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.
67. - `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.
68. - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.
69. - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.
70. - `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.
71. - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.
72. 
73. ## Notes and considerations
74. - "Like BlackRock" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.
75. - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.
76. - Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.
77. - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.
78. - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.
79. - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.
80. - Revised scope assumption: the platform should be designed for multiple horizons, but daily-data MVP coverage should focus on swing, position, and long-term profiles first.
81. - Intraday recommendations require separate high-frequency data ingestion, tighter execution assumptions, and different backtesting logic, so they should be treated as a later phase rather than bundled into the first daily-data release.
82.
```

</details>

}

The product is designed to feel institutional and explainable: each daily recommendation should show **why** it was made and expose a proposed **entry**, **target**, and **stop-loss** instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.\n+\n+## Chosen product scope\n+\n+- **User:** analyst or serious retail investor\n+- **Market:** liquid NSE equities, initially a Nifty 500-derived universe\n+- **Delivery:** web dashboard\n+- **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution\n+- **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan\n+- **MVP horizons:** swing, position, and long-term\n+- **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists\n+\n+## Chosen stack\n+\n+| Area | Choice | Why |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |\n+| UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |\n+| API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |\n+| Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |\n+| Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |\n+| Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |\n+\n+## Initial repository layout\n+\n+```text\n+apps/\n+  web/          Next.js analyst dashboard\n+services/\n+  api/          FastAPI recommendation API\n+data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding\n+```\n+\n+## MVP user experience\n+\n+1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch.\n+2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.\n+3. The detail view explains:\n+   - key factors behind the recommendation\n+   - recommended entry price\n+   - target price\n+   - stop-loss\n+   - risk-reward profile\n+   - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\.gitignore\n+node_modules\n+.next\n+dist\n+coverage\n+__pycache__\n+.pytest_cache\n+.mypy_cache\n+.venv\n+venv\n+*.pyc\n+*.pyo\n+.env\n+.env.local\n+.DS_Store\n*** End Patch\n"

grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n+  }\n+}\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\components\\dashboard.tsx\n+\"use client\";\n+\n+import { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n+import type {\n+ formatPrice(value: number) {\n+  return new Intl.NumberFormat(\"en-IN\", {\n+    style: \"currency\",\n+    currency: \"INR\",\n+    maximumFractionDigits: 0\n+  }).format(value);\n+}\n+\n+function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan[\"conviction\"]) {\n+  return conviction.toLowerCase();\n+}\n+\n+export function Dashboard({ data }: DashboardProps) {\n+  const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(\"swing\");\n+  const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>(data.stocks[0]?.symbol ?? \"\");\n+\n+  const selectedStock = useMemo<StockAnalysis | undefined>(\n+    () => data.stocks.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? data.stocks[0],\n+    [data.stocks, selectedSymbol]\n+  );\n+\n+  const selectedPlan = selectedStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];\n+\n+  return (\n+    <main className=\"shell\">\n+      <div className=\"topbar\">\n+        <div className=\"headline\">\n+          <span className=\"eyebrow\">Chosen UI foundation: Next.js dashboard</span>\n+          <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>\n+          <p>\n+            This foundation is built for daily post-market analysis. Click any stock to inspect\n+            the rationale, trade plan, and horizon-specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels.\n+          </p>\n+        </div>\n+\n+        <section className=\"card run-card\">\n+          <div className=\"metric-label\">Latest batch</div>\n+          <div className=\"metric-value\">{new Date(data.generatedAt).toLocaleString(\"en-IN\")}</div>\n+          <p className=\"footnote\">\n+            {data.exchange} · {data.universe} · published for the next trading session\n+          </p>\n+        </section>\n+      </div>\n+\n+      <section className=\"meta-grid\">\n+        <article className=\"card\">\n+          <div className=\"metric-label\">Coverage universe</div>\n+          <div className=\"metric-value\">{data.universe}</div>\n+        </article>\n+        <article className=\"card\">\n+          <div className=\"metric-label\">Market</div>\n+          <div className=\"metric-value\">{data.market}</div>\n+        </article>\n+        <article className=\"card\">\n+          <div className=\"metric-label\">Tracked stocks</div>\n+          <div className=\"metric-value\">{data.stocks.length}</div>\n+        </article>\n+        <article className=\"card\">\n+          <div className=\"metric-label\">Active horizon</div>\n+          <div className=\"metric-value\">\n+            {data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)?.label}\n+          </div>\n+        </article>\n+      </section>\n+\n+      <div className=\"workspace\">\n+        <section className=\"card\">\n+          <div className=\"section-title\">\n+            <div>\n+              <h2>Today&apos;s ranked ideas</h2>\n+              <div className=\"section-subtitle\">\n+                Each row is a research recommendation with a transparent trade plan.\n+              </div>\n+            </div>\n+\n+            <div className=\"pills\">\n+              {data.profiles.map((profile) => (\n+                <button\n+                  key={profile.id}\n+                  className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? \" active\" : \"\"}`}\n+                  onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}\n+                  type=\"button\"\n+                >\n+                  {profile.label} · {profile.window}\n+                </button>\n+              ))}\n+            </div>\n+          </div>\n+\n+          <div className=\"table-wrap\">\n+            <table className=\"stock-table\">\n+              <thead>\n+                <tr>\n+                  <th>Stock</th>\n+                  <th>Sector</th>\n+                  <th>Conviction</th>\n+                  <th>Entry</th>\n+                  <th>Target</th>\n+                  <th>Stop-loss</th>\n+                  <th>Expected return</th>\n+                  <th>R:R</th>\n+                </tr>\n+              </thead>\n+              <tbody>\n+                {data.stocks.map((stock) => {\n+                  const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];\n+\n+                  return (\n+                    <tr\n+                      key={stock.symbol}\n+                      className={`stock-row${stock.symbol === selectedStock?.symbol ? \" active\" : \"\"}`}\n+                      onClick={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}\n+                    >\n+                      <td>\n+                        <div className=\"symbol\">{stock.symbol}</div>\n+                        <div className=\"company\">{stock.companyName}</div>\n+                      </td>\n+                      <td>{stock.sector}</td>\n+                      <td>\n+                        <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>\n+                          {plan.conviction}\n+                        </span>\n+                      </td>\n+                      <td>{formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)}</td>\n+                      <td>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</td>\n+                      <td>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</td>\n+                      <td>{plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%</td>\n+                      <td>{plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</td>\n+                    </tr>\n+                  );\n+                })}\n+              </tbody>\n+            </table>\n+          </div>\n+        </section>\n+\n+        {selectedStock && selectedPlan ? (\n+          <aside className=\"detail-grid\">\n+            <section className=\"card\">\n+              <div className=\"detail-header\">\n+                <div>\n+                  <h2>\n+                    {selectedStock.companyName} ({selectedStock.symbol})\n+                  </h2>\n+                  <div className=\"detail-subhead\">\n+                    {selectedStock.sector} · {selectedStock.marketCapBucket} ·{\" \"}\n+                    {selectedStock.liquidityTier}\n+                  </div>\n+                </div>\n+\n+                <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(selectedPlan.conviction)}`}>\n+                  {selectedPlan.conviction} conviction\n+                </span>\n+              </div>\n+\n+              <p Literal[\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]\n+\n+\n+class Signal(BaseModel):\n+    name: str\n+    value: str\n+\n+\n+class RecommendationPlan(BaseModel):\n+    conviction: Conviction\n+    entryPrice: float\n+    targetPrice: float\n+    stopLoss: float\n+    expectedReturnPct: float\n+    riskReward: float\n+    summary: str\n+    drivers: list[str]\n+    technicalSignals: list[Signal]\n+    fundamentalSignals: list[Signal]\n+    newsContext: list[str]\n+\n+\n+class StockAnalysis(BaseModel):\n+    symbol: str\n+    companyName: str\n+    sector: str\n+    marketCapBucket: str\n+    liquidityTier: str\n+    profiles: dict[HorizonId, RecommendationPlan]\n+\n+\n+class HorizonProfile(BaseModel):\n+    id: HorizonId\n+    label: str\n+    window: str\n+\n+\n+class RecommendationDataset(BaseModel):\n+    market: str\n+    exchange: str\n+    universe: str\n+    generatedAt: str\n+    profiles: list[HorizonProfile]\n+    stocks: list[StockAnalysis]\n+\n+\n+class RecommendationSummary(BaseModel):\n+    symbol: str\n+    companyName: str\n+    sector: str\n+    horizon: HorizonId\n+    conviction: Conviction\n+    entryPrice: float\n+    targetPrice: float\n+    stopLoss: float\n+    expectedReturnPct: float\n+    riskReward: float\n+    summary: str\n+\n+\n+class StockDetailResponse(BaseModel):\n+    generatedAt: str\n+    market: str\n+    exchange: str\n+    universe: str\n+    selectedHorizon: HorizonId\n+    stock: StockAnalysis\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\services\\api\\app\\main.py\n+import json\n+from pathlib import Path\n+\n+from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException\n+\n+from .schemas import (\n+    HorizonId,\n+    RecommendationDataset,\n+    RecommendationSummary,\n+    StockAnalysis,\n+    StockDetailResponse,\n+)\n+\n+DATA_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / \"data\" / \"sample-recommendations.json\"\n+\n+app = FastAPI(\n+    title=\"Recommendation API\",\n+    version=\"0.1.0\",\n+    description=\"Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.\",\n+)\n+\n+\n+def load_dataset() -> RecommendationDataset:\n+    payload = json.loads(DATA_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n+    return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)\n+\n+\n+def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:\n+    plan = stock.profiles[horizon]\n+\n+    return RecommendationSummary(\n+        symbol=stock.symbol,\n+        companyName=stock.companyName,\n+        sector=stock.sector,\n+        horizon=horizon,\n+        conviction=plan.conviction,\n+        entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,\n+        targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,\n+        stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,\n+        expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,\n+        riskReward=plan.riskReward,\n+        summary=plan.summary,\n+    )\n+\n+\n+@app.get(\"/healthz\")\n+def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:\n+    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n+\n+\n+@app.get(\"/api/v1/recommendations\")\n+def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = \"swing\") -> dict[str, object]:\n+    dataset = load_dataset()\n+    rows = [build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump() for stock in dataset.stocks]\n+\n+    return {\n+        \"generatedAt\": dataset.generatedAt,\n+        \"market\": dataset.market,\n+        \"exchange\": dataset.exchange,\n+        \"universe\": dataset.universe,\n+        \"selectedHorizon\": horizon,\n+        \"recommendations\": rows,\n+    }\n+\n+\n+@app.get(\"/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}\")\n+def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = \"swing\") -> StockDetailResponse:\n+    dataset = load_dataset()\n+    stock = next((row for row in dataset.stocks if row.symbol == symbol.upper()), None)\n+\n+    if stock is None:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}\")\n+\n+    return StockDetailResponse(\n+        generatedAt=dataset.generatedAt,\n+        market=dataset.market,\n+        exchange=dataset.exchange,\n+        universe=dataset.universe,\n+        selectedHorizon=horizon,\n+        stock=stock,\n+    )\n*** End Patch\n"

39.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,

<sub>⏱️ 61m 59s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\README.md.**

C:\copilot\README.md

<details>
<summary>47 lines</summary>

````
1. # Indian equities recommendation platform
2. 
3. This repository starts a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. The product is designed to feel institutional and explainable: each daily recommendation should show **why** it was made and expose a proposed **entry**, **target**, and **stop-loss** instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.
4. 
5. ## Chosen product scope
6. 
7. - **User:** analyst or serious retail investor
8. - **Market:** liquid NSE equities, initially a Nifty 500-derived universe
9. - **Delivery:** web dashboard
10. - **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution
11. - **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan
12. - **MVP horizons:** swing, position, and long-term
13. - **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists
14. 
15. ## Chosen stack
16. 
17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Initial repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## MVP user experience
37. 
38. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch.
39. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
40. 3. The detail view explains:
41.    - key factors behind the recommendation
42.    - recommended entry price
43.    - target price
44.    - stop-loss
45.    - risk-reward profile
46.    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes
47.
````

</details>

{
  "description": "Finish stack todo and query next",
  "query": "UPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'choose-stack';\n\nSELECT t.id, t.title, t.description\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\nAND NOT EXISTS (\n  SELECT 1\n  FROM todo_deps td\n  JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n  WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n)\nORDER BY t.id;"
}
```

   | design-data-model | Design data model | Define source adapters, schemas, identifier normalization, training snapshots, and feature contracts for market and company data. |
   | design-horizon-profiles | Design horizon strategy profiles | Define swing, position, and long-term recommendation profiles, including holding windows, evaluation rules, UI presentation, and the later-phase path for intraday support. |

---

<sub>⏱️ 63m 10s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**I chose `Next.js + TypeScript` for the UI, paired with `FastAPI` for the recommendation API.** It’s the better fit here than a prebuilt admin template because you want a stock click to open a custom analysis view with **entry, target, stop-loss, rationale, and horizon switching**.

I scaffolded that foundation in `C:\copilot`:
- `apps\web` — dashboard shell with a ranked stock table and click-through detail panel
- `services\api` — FastAPI app with `healthz`, recommendation list, and stock detail endpoints
- `data\sample-recommendations.json` — shared mock data for the UI and API
- `README.md` — chosen scope and architecture for the Indian equities MVP

---

<sub>⏱️ 65m 2s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Run the dashboard from `C:\copilot` with `npm install` and `npm run dev:web`.** Then open `http://localhost:3000`.

```powershell
Set-Location C:\copilot
npm install
npm run dev:web
```

**Run the API separately** in another terminal with Python 3.11+, then open `http://localhost:8000/docs`.

```powershell
Set-Location C:\copilot\services\api
python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
pip install -e .
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

**Important:** the current UI already reads `data\sample-recommendations.json` directly, so the dashboard works **even without** starting the API. The API is there for the next step, when the web app starts fetching live recommendation data instead of local mock data.

17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Initial repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## MVP user experience
37. 
38. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch.
39. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
40. 3. The detail view explains:
41.    - key factors behind the recommendation
42.    - recommended entry price
43.    - target price
44.    - stop-loss
45.    - risk-reward profile
46.    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes
47.
````

</details>

The detail view explains:\n    - key factors behind the recommendation\n    - recommended entry price\n    - target price\n    - stop-loss\n    - risk-reward profile\n    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes\n+\n+## Windows quick start\n+\n+- Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.\n+- On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.\n+- The dashboard then starts at `http://localhost:3000`.\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 67m 37s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\run-app.bat.**

C:\copilot\run-app.bat

<details>
<summary>33 lines</summary>

```
1. @echo off
2. setlocal
3. 
4. set "ROOT_DIR=%~dp0"
5. cd /d "%ROOT_DIR%"
6. 
7. where npm >nul 2>nul
8. if errorlevel 1 (
9.   echo Node.js and npm are required to run this app.
10.   echo Install Node.js from https://nodejs.org/ and try again.
11.   pause
12.   exit /b 1
13. )
14. 
15. if not exist "%ROOT_DIR%node_modules" (
16.   echo Installing dependencies...
17.   call npm install
18.   if errorlevel 1 (
19.     echo Failed to install dependencies.
20.     pause
21.     exit /b 1
22.   )
23. )
24. 
25. echo Starting the dashboard at http://localhost:3000
26. echo Keep this window open while the app is running.
27. echo.
28. echo Press Ctrl+C to stop the app.
29. echo.
30. 
31. call npm run dev:web
32. 
33.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 68m 31s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Created `C:\copilot\run-app.bat`.** Double-click it to start the dashboard.

It will:
- install npm dependencies automatically on first run,
- start the app at `http://localhost:3000`,
- keep the window open while the app is running.

**Note:** this launcher starts the **web app only**, which is enough for the current version because the UI reads local sample data and does not require the FastAPI service yet.

<sub>⏱️ 73m 0s</sub>

### ✅ `sql`

**Query ready todos**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.description\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\nAND NOT EXISTS (\n  SELECT 1\n  FROM todo_deps td\n  JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n  WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n)\nORDER BY t.id;"
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>5 lines</summary>

```
2 row(s) returned:

| id | title | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| design-data-model | Design data model | Define source adapters, schemas, identifier normalization, training snapshots, and feature contracts for market and company data. |
| design-horizon-profiles | Design horizon strategy profiles | Define swing, position, and long-term recommendation profiles, including holding windows, evaluation rules, UI presentation, and the later-phase path for intraday support. |
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 73m 0s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\apps\web\components\dashboard.tsx.**

C:\copilot\apps\web\components\dashboard.tsx

<details>
<summary>242 lines</summary>

```
1. "use client";
2. 
3. import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
4. import type {
5.   HorizonId,
6.   RecommendationDataset,
7.   RecommendationPlan,
8.   StockAnalysis
9. } from "@/lib/types";
10. 
11. type DashboardProps = {
12.   data: RecommendationDataset;
13. };
14. 
15. function formatPrice(value: number) {
16.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
17.     style: "currency",
18.     currency: "INR",
19.     maximumFractionDigits: 0
20.   }).format(value);
21. }
22. 
23. function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {
24.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
25. }
26. 
27. export function Dashboard({ data }: DashboardProps) {
28.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>("swing");
29.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>(data.stocks[0]?.symbol ?? "");
30. 
31.   const selectedStock = useMemo<StockAnalysis | undefined>(
32.     () => data.stocks.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? data.stocks[0],
33.     [data.stocks, selectedSymbol]
34.   );
35. 
36.   const selectedPlan = selectedStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
37. 
38.   return (
39.     <main className="shell">
40.       <div className="topbar">
41.         <div className="headline">
42.           <span className="eyebrow">Chosen UI foundation: Next.js dashboard</span>
43.           <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
44.           <p>
45.             This foundation is built for daily post-market analysis. Click any stock to inspect
46.             the rationale, trade plan, and horizon-specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels.
47.           </p>
48.         </div>
49. 
50.         <section className="card run-card">
51.           <div className="metric-label">Latest batch</div>
52.           <div className="metric-value">{new Date(data.generatedAt).toLocaleString("en-IN")}</div>
53.           <p className="footnote">
54.             {data.exchange} · {data.universe} · published for the next trading session
55.           </p>
56.         </section>
57.       </div>
58. 
59.       <section className="meta-grid">
60.         <article className="card">
61.           <div className="metric-label">Coverage universe</div>
62.           <div className="metric-value">{data.universe}</div>
63.         </article>
64.         <article className="card">
65.           <div className="metric-label">Market</div>
66.           <div className="metric-value">{data.market}</div>
67.         </article>
68.         <article className="card">
69.           <div className="metric-label">Tracked stocks</div>
70.           <div className="metric-value">{data.stocks.length}</div>
71.         </article>
72.         <article className="card">
73.           <div className="metric-label">Active horizon</div>
74.           <div className="metric-value">
75.             {data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)?.label}
76.           </div>
77.         </article>
78.       </section>
79. 
80.       <div className="workspace">
81.         <section className="card">
82.           <div className="section-title">
83.             <div>
84.               <h2>Today&apos;s ranked ideas</h2>
85.               <div className="section-subtitle">
86.                 Each row is a research recommendation with a transparent trade plan.
87.               </div>
88.             </div>
89. 
90.             <div className="pills">
91.               {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
92.                 <button
93.                   key={profile.id}
94.                   className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
95.                   onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
96.                   type="button"
97.                 >
98.                   {profile.label} · {profile.window}
99.                 </button>
100.               ))}
101.             </div>
102.           </div>
103. 
104.           <div className="table-wrap">
105.             <table className="stock-table">
106.               <thead>
107.                 <tr>
108.                   <th>Stock</th>
109.                   <th>Sector</th>
110.                   <th>Conviction</th>
111.                   <th>Entry</th>
112.                   <th>Target</th>
113.                   <th>Stop-loss</th>
114.                   <th>Expected return</th>
115.                   <th>R:R</th>
116.                 </tr>
117.               </thead>
118.               <tbody>
119.                 {data.stocks.map((stock) => {
120.                   const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
121. 
122.                   return (
123.                     <tr
124.                       key={stock.symbol}
125.                       className={`stock-row${stock.symbol === selectedStock?.symbol ? " active" : ""}`}
126.                       onClick={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}
127.                     >
128.                       <td>
129.                         <div className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</div>
130.                         <div className="company">{stock.companyName}</div>
131.                       </td>
132.                       <td>{stock.sector}</td>
133.                       <td>
134.                         <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>
135.                           {plan.conviction}
136.                         </span>
137.                       </td>
138.                       <td>{formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)}</td>
139.                       <td>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</td>
140.                       <td>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</td>
141.                       <td>{plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%</td>
142.                       <td>{plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</td>
143.                     </tr>
144.                   );
145.                 })}
146.               </tbody>
147.             </table>
148.           </div>
149.         </section>
150. 
151.         {selectedStock && selectedPlan ? (
152.           <aside className="detail-grid">
153.             <section className="card">
154.               <div className="detail-header">
155.                 <div>
156.                   <h2>
157.                     {selectedStock.companyName} ({selectedStock.symbol})
158.                   </h2>
159.                   <div className="detail-subhead">
160.                     {selectedStock.sector} · {selectedStock.marketCapBucket} ·{" "}
161.                     {selectedStock.liquidityTier}
162.                   </div>
163.                 </div>
164. 
165.                 <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(selectedPlan.conviction)}`}>
166.                   {selectedPlan.conviction} conviction
167.                 </span>
168.               </div>
169. 
170.               <p className="summary">{selectedPlan.summary}</p>
171.             </section>
172. 
173.             <section className="card">
174.               <h3>Trade plan</h3>
175.               <div className="stat-grid">
176.                 <article className="stat-card">
177.                   <div className="metric-label">Entry price</div>
178.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.entryPrice)}</strong>
179.                 </article>
180.                 <article className="stat-card">
181.                   <div className="metric-label">Target price</div>
182.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.targetPrice)}</strong>
183.                 </article>
184.                 <article className="stat-card">
185.                   <div className="metric-label">Stop-loss</div>
186.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.stopLoss)}</strong>
187.                 </article>
188.                 <article className="stat-card">
189.                   <div className="metric-label">Risk-reward</div>
190.                   <strong>{selectedPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</strong>
191.                 </article>
192.               </div>
193.             </section>
194. 
195.             <section className="card">
196.               <h3>Why this stock ranks well</h3>
197.               <ul className="list">
198.                 {selectedPlan.drivers.map((driver) => (
199.                   <li key={driver}>{driver}</li>
200.                 ))}
201.               </ul>
202.             </section>
203. 
204.             <section className="card">
205.               <h3>Technical analysis</h3>
206.               <div className="signal-grid">
207.                 {selectedPlan.technicalSignals.map((signal) => (
208.                   <div key={signal.name} className="signal-row">
209.                     <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
210.                     <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
211.                   </div>
212.                 ))}
213.               </div>
214.             </section>
215. 
216.             <section className="card">
217.               <h3>Fundamental analysis</h3>
218.               <div className="signal-grid">
219.                 {selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals.map((signal) => (
220.                   <div key={signal.name} className="signal-row">
221.                     <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
222.                     <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
223.                   </div>
224.                 ))}
225.               </div>
226.             </section>
227. 
228.             <section className="card">
229.               <h3>Recent context</h3>
230.               <ul className="list">
231.                 {selectedPlan.newsContext.map((item) => (
232.                   <li key={item}>{item}</li>
233.                 ))}
234.               </ul>
235.             </section>
236.           </aside>
237.         ) : null}
238.       </div>
239.     </main>
240.   );
241. }
242.
```

</details>

1. export type HorizonId = "swing" | "position" | "long_term";
2. 
3. export type Signal = {
4.   name: string;
5.   value: string;
6. };
7. 
8. export type RecommendationPlan = {
9.   conviction: "High" | "Medium" | "Low";
10.   entryPrice: number;
11.   targetPrice: number;
12.   stopLoss: number;
13.   expectedReturnPct: number;
14.   riskReward: number;
15.   summary: string;
16.   drivers: string[];
17.   technicalSignals: Signal[];
18.   fundamentalSignals: Signal[];
19.   newsContext: string[];
20. };
21. 
22. export type StockAnalysis = {
23.   symbol: string;
24.   companyName: string;
25.   sector: string;
26.   marketCapBucket: string;
27.   liquidityTier: string;
28.   profiles: Record<HorizonId, RecommendationPlan>;
29. };
30. 
31. export type HorizonProfile = {
32.   id: HorizonId;
33.   label: string;
34.   window: string;
35. };
36. 
37. export type RecommendationDataset = {
38.   market: string;
39.   exchange: string;
40.   universe: string;
41.   generatedAt: string;
42.   profiles: HorizonProfile[];
43.   stocks: StockAnalysis[];
44. };
45.
```

### 💬 Copilot

I’m using the data-model and horizon-profile work items for this change, because daily success tracking depends on both the historical schema and the evaluation rules for each horizon.

 market: string;\n   exchange: string;\n   universe: string;\n-  generatedAt: string;\n   profiles: HorizonProfile[];\n-  stocks: StockAnalysis[];\n+  currentBatch: RecommendationBatch;\n+  history: HistoricalBatch[];\n };\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\analytics.ts\n+import type {\n+  DailyPerformance,\n+  HistoricalBatch,\n+  HistoricalRecommendationPlan,\n+  HorizonId,\n+  OutcomeResult,\n+  RecommendationDataset,\n+  StockPerformanceHistoryEntry\n+} from \"@/lib/types\";\n+\n+const CLOSED_RESULTS: OutcomeResult[] = [\"target_hit\", \"stop_loss_hit\"];\n+\n+function roundMetric(value: number) {\n+  return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;\n+}\n+\n+function isClosedResult(result: OutcomeResult) {\n+  return CLOSED_RESULTS.includes(result);\n+}\n+\n+function isSuccessfulResult(result: OutcomeResult) {\n+  return result === \"target_hit\";\n+}\n+\n+function planAverageReturn(plans: HistoricalRecommendationPlan[]) {\n+  const closedPlans = plans.filter((plan) => isClosedResult(plan.outcome.result));\n+\n+  if (!closedPlans.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const average =\n+    closedPlans.reduce((total, plan) => total + plan.outcome.returnPct, 0) / closedPlans.length;\n+\n+  return roundMetric(average);\n+}\n+\n+export function buildDailyPerformance(\n+  history: RecommendationDataset[\"history\"],\n+  horizon: HorizonId\n+): DailyPerformance[] {\n+  return [...history]\n+    .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))\n+    .map((batch) => {\n+      const plans = batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => recommendation.profiles[horizon]);\n+      const successful = plans.filter((plan) => isSuccessfulResult(plan.outcome.result)).length;\n+      const failed = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === \"stop_loss_hit\").length;\n+      const open = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === \"open\").length;\n+      const closed = successful + failed;\n+\n+      return {\n+        batchDate: batch.batchDate,\n+        publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,\n+        total: plans.length,\n+        closed,\n+        open,\n+        successful,\n+        failed,\n+        successRate: closed ? roundMetric((successful / closed) * 100) : null,\n+        averageReturnPct: planAverageReturn(plans)\n+      };\n+    });\n+}\n+\n+export function buildPerformanceSummary(rows: DailyPerformance[]) {\n+  const completedRows = rows.filter((row) => row.successRate !== null);\n+  const latestCompleted = completedRows[0] ?? null;\n+\n+  if (!completedRows.length) {\n+    return {\n+      latestCompleted,\n+      averageSuccessRate: null,\n+      averageClosedReturnPct: null\n+    };\n+  }\n+\n+  const averageSuccessRate =\n+    completedRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.successRate ?? 0), 0) / completedRows.length;\n+\n+  const returnRows = completedRows.filter((row) => row.averageReturnPct !== null);\n+  const averageClosedReturnPct = returnRows.length\n+    ? returnRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.averageReturnPct ?? 0), 0) / returnRows.length\n+    : null;\n+\n+  return {\n+    latestCompleted,\n+    averageSuccessRate: roundMetric(averageSuccessRate),\n+    averageClosedReturnPct:\n+      averageClosedReturnPct === null ? null : roundMetric(averageClosedReturnPct)\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+export function buildStockPerformanceHistory(\n+  history: HistoricalBatch[],\n+  symbol: string,\n+  horizon: HorizonId\n+): StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[] {\n+  return [...history]\n+    .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))\n+    .flatMap((batch) =>\n+      batch.recommendations\n+        .filter((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol === symbol)\n+        .map((recommendation) => ({\n+          batchDate: batch.batchDate,\n+          publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,\n+          symbol: recommendation.symbol,\n+          companyName: recommendation.companyName,\n+          sector: recommendation.sector,\n+          horizon,\n+          conviction: recommendation.profiles[horizon].conviction,\n+          entryPrice: summary: recommendation.profiles[horizon].summary,\n+          outcome: recommendation.profiles[horizon].outcome\n+        }))\n+    );\n+}\n+\n+export function outcomeLabel(result: OutcomeResult) {\n+  switch (result) {\n+    case \"target_hit\":\n+      return \"Target hit\";\n+    case \"stop_loss_hit\":\n+      return \"Stop-loss hit\";\n+    default:\n+      return \"Open\";\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+export function outcomeClass(result: OutcomeResult) {\n+  switch (result) {\n+    case \"target_hit\":\n+      return \"success\";\n+    case \"stop_loss_hit\":\n+      return StockPerformanceHistoryEntry,\n+)\n+\n+CLOSED_RESULTS: set[OutcomeResult] = {\"target_hit\", \"stop_loss_hit\"}\n+\n+\n+def _round_metric(value: float | None) -> float | None:\n+    if value is None:\n+        return None\n+\n+    return round(value, 2)\n+\n+\n+def _is_closed_result(result: OutcomeResult) -> bool:\n+    return result in CLOSED_RESULTS\n+\n+\n+def build_daily_performance(dataset: RecommendationDataset, horizon: HorizonId) -> list[DailyPerformance]:\n+    rows: list[DailyPerformance] = []\n+\n+    for batch in sorted(dataset.history, key=lambda item: item.batchDate, reverse=True):\n+        plans = [recommendation.profiles[horizon] for recommendation in batch.recommendations]\n+        successful = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == \"target_hit\")\n+        failed = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == \"stop_loss_hit\")\n+        open_count = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == \"open\")\n+        closed = successful + failed\n+        closed_returns = [\n+            plan.outcome.returnPct for plan in plans if _is_closed_result(plan.outcome.result)\n+        ]\n+\n+        rows.append(\n+            DailyPerformance(\n+                batchDate=batch.batchDate,\n+                publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,\n+                total=len(plans),\n+                closed=closed,\n+                open=open_count,\n+                successful=successful,\n+                failed=failed,\n+                successRate=_round_metric((successful / closed) * 100) if closed else None,\n+                averageReturnPct=(\n+                    _round_metric(sum(closed_returns) / len(closed_returns))\n+                    if closed_returns\n+                    else None\n+                ),\n+            )\n+        )\n+\n+    return rows\n+\n+\n+def build_stock_history(\n+    dataset: RecommendationDataset, symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId\n+) -> item.batchDate, reverse=True):\n+        for recommendation in batch.recommendations:\n+            if recommendation.symbol != requested_symbol:\n+                continue\n+\n+            plan = recommendation.profiles[horizon]\n+            rows.append(\n+                StockPerformanceHistoryEntry(\n+                    batchDate=batch.batchDate,\n+                    publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,\n+                    symbol=recommendation.symbol,\n+                    companyName=recommendation.companyName,\n+                    sector=recommendation.sector,\n+                    horizon=horizon,\n+                    conviction=plan.conviction,\n+                    entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,\n+                    targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,\n+                    stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,\n+                    summary=plan.summary,\n+                    outcome=plan.outcome,\n+                )\n+            )\n+\n+    return rows\n*** Add File: for Indian equities recommendation batches.\",\n+)\n+\n+\n+def load_dataset() -> RecommendationDataset:\n+    payload = json.loads(DATA_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n+    return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)\n+\n+\n+def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:\n+    plan = stock.profiles[horizon]\n+\n+    return RecommendationSummary(\n+        symbol=stock.symbol,\n+        companyName=stock.companyName,\n+        sector=stock.sector,\n+        horizon=horizon,\n+        conviction=plan.conviction,\n+ list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = \"swing\") -> dict[str, object]:\n+    dataset = load_dataset()\n+    rows = [\n+        build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()\n+        for stock in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations\n+    ]\n+    performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)\n+\n+    return {\n+        \"batchDate\": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,\n+ ### 💬 Copilot

It appears the environment has an issue with PowerShell setup. Let me check what shell tools are available in the environment documentation:

Based on the error messages, it seems there's a configuration issue with the PowerShell environment on this Windows system. The powershell tool is attempting to verify PowerShell 6+ (pwsh) is available, but it's not installed on this system.

Unfortunately, I'm unable to proceed with running the npm install and build commands due to the shell environment configuration issue. The system requires either:

1. **PowerShell 7+ (pwsh)** to be installed from https://aka.ms/powershell, OR
2. The development environment to be configured with an alternative shell

**What you need to do:**
1. Install PowerShell 7+ or ensure it's available in the PATH
2. Alternatively, you can run these commands directly from your terminal:
   ```cmd
   cd C:\copilot
   npm install
   npm run build:web
   ```

**Expected commands to reproduce the build failure:**
- `npm install` - Install dependencies for the monorepo
- `npm run build:web` - Run the web build (which runs `npm run build --workspace web`)

Once you have the shell environment configured or run these commands manually, please share the build output and I can help diagnose the module not found errors.

<details>
<summary>141 lines</summary>

```
1. import type {
2.   DailyPerformance,
3.   HistoricalBatch,
4.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
5.   HorizonId,
6.   OutcomeResult,
7.   RecommendationDataset,
8.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
9. } from "@/lib/types";
10. 
11. const CLOSED_RESULTS: OutcomeResult[] = ["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit"];
12. 
13. function roundMetric(value: number) {
14.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
15. }
16. 
17. function isClosedResult(result: OutcomeResult) {
18.   return CLOSED_RESULTS.includes(result);
19. }
20. 
21. function isSuccessfulResult(result: OutcomeResult) {
22.   return result === "target_hit";
23. }
24. 
25. function planAverageReturn(plans: HistoricalRecommendationPlan[]) {
26.   const closedPlans = plans.filter((plan) => isClosedResult(plan.outcome.result));
27. 
28.   if (!closedPlans.length) {
29.     return null;
30.   }
31. 
32.   const average =
33.     closedPlans.reduce((total, plan) => total + plan.outcome.returnPct, 0) / closedPlans.length;
34. 
35.   return roundMetric(average);
36. }
37. 
38. export function buildDailyPerformance(
39.   history: RecommendationDataset["history"],
40.   horizon: HorizonId
41. ): DailyPerformance[] {
42.   return [...history]
43.     .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))
44.     .map((batch) => {
45.       const plans = batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => recommendation.profiles[horizon]);
46.       const successful = plans.filter((plan) => isSuccessfulResult(plan.outcome.result)).length;
47.       const failed = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === "stop_loss_hit").length;
48.       const open = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === "open").length;
49.       const closed = successful + failed;
50. 
51.       return {
52.         batchDate: batch.batchDate,
53.         publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,
54.         total: plans.length,
55.         closed,
56.         open,
57.         successful,
58.         failed,
59.         successRate: closed ? roundMetric((successful / closed) * 100) : null,
60.         averageReturnPct: planAverageReturn(plans)
61.       };
62.     });
63. }
64. 
65. export function buildPerformanceSummary(rows: DailyPerformance[]) {
66.   const completedRows = rows.filter((row) => row.successRate !== null);
67.   const latestCompleted = completedRows[0] ?? null;
68. 
69.   if (!completedRows.length) {
70.     return {
71.       latestCompleted,
72.       averageSuccessRate: null,
73.       averageClosedReturnPct: null
74.     };
75.   }
76. 
77.   const averageSuccessRate =
78.     completedRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.successRate ?? 0), 0) / completedRows.length;
79. 
80.   const returnRows = completedRows.filter((row) => row.averageReturnPct !== null);
81.   const averageClosedReturnPct = returnRows.length
82.     ? returnRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.averageReturnPct ?? 0), 0) / returnRows.length
83.     : null;
84. 
85.   return {
86.     latestCompleted,
87.     averageSuccessRate: roundMetric(averageSuccessRate),
88.     averageClosedReturnPct:
89.       averageClosedReturnPct === null ? null : roundMetric(averageClosedReturnPct)
90.   };
91. }
92. 
93. export function buildStockPerformanceHistory(
94.   history: HistoricalBatch[],
95.   symbol: string,
96.   horizon: HorizonId
97. ): StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[] {
98.   return [...history]
99.     .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))
100.     .flatMap((batch) =>
101.       batch.recommendations
102.         .filter((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol === symbol)
103.         .map((recommendation) => ({
104.           batchDate: batch.batchDate,
105.           publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,
106.           symbol: recommendation.symbol,
107.           companyName: recommendation.companyName,
108.           sector: recommendation.sector,
109.           horizon,
110.           conviction: recommendation.profiles[horizon].conviction,
111.           entryPrice: recommendation.profiles[horizon].entryPrice,
112.           targetPrice: recommendation.profiles[horizon].targetPrice,
113.           stopLoss: recommendation.profiles[horizon].stopLoss,
114.           summary: recommendation.profiles[horizon].summary,
115.           outcome: recommendation.profiles[horizon].outcome
116.         }))
117.     );
118. }
119. 
120. export function outcomeLabel(result: OutcomeResult) {
121.   switch (result) {
122.     case "target_hit":
123.       return "Target hit";
124.     case "stop_loss_hit":
125.       return "Stop-loss hit";
126.     default:
127.       return "Open";
128.   }
129. }
130. 
131. export function outcomeClass(result: OutcomeResult) {
132.   switch (result) {
133.     case "target_hit":
134.       return "success";
135.     case "stop_loss_hit":
136.       return "danger";
137.     default:
138.       return "neutral";
139.   }
140. }
141.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>64 lines</summary>

```
1. @echo off
2. setlocal EnableExtensions EnableDelayedExpansion
3. 
4. set "ROOT_DIR=%~dp0"
5. set "APP_URL=http://localhost:3000"
6. set "SERVER_TITLE=Stock Recommendation App"
7. cd /d "%ROOT_DIR%"
8. 
9. where npm >nul 2>nul
10. if errorlevel 1 (
11.   echo Node.js and npm are required to run this app.

13.   pause
14.   exit /b 1
15. )
16.

18.   echo Installing dependencies...
19.   call npm install
20.   if errorlevel 1 (
21.     echo Failed to install dependencies.
22.     pause
23.     exit /b 1
24.   )
25. )
26. 
27. call :is_port_listening
28. if not errorlevel 1 (
29.   echo The app is already running. Opening browser...
30.   start "" "%APP_URL%"
31.   exit /b 0
32. )
33. 
34. echo Starting the dashboard server in a new window...
35. start "%SERVER_TITLE%" cmd /k "cd /d ""%ROOT_DIR%"" && npm run dev:web"
36. 
37. echo Waiting for the app to become available...
38. set /a ATTEMPTS=0
39. 
40. :wait_for_server
41. call :is_port_listening
42. if not errorlevel 1 goto open_browser
43. 
44. set /a ATTEMPTS+=1
45. if !ATTEMPTS! GEQ 30 (
46.   echo The app did not start in time.
47.   echo Check the "%SERVER_TITLE%" window for errors.
48.   pause
49.   exit /b 1
50. )
51. 
52. timeout /t 2 /nobreak >nul
53. goto wait_for_server
54. 
55. :open_browser
56. echo Opening browser...
57. start "" "%APP_URL%"
58. exit /b 0
59. 
60. :is_port_listening
61. netstat -ano | findstr /r /c:":3000" | findstr /c:"LISTENING" >nul
62. exit /b %errorlevel%
63. 
64.
```

</details>

44.    - stop-loss
45.    - risk-reward profile
46.    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes
47. 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, shows whether each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.
48. 
49. ## Windows quick start
50. 
51. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
52. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
53. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
54. 
55. ## Analytics test command
56. 
57. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
58.
```

<sub>⏱️ 92m 17s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**`run-app.bat` now does the full one-click flow.** Double-clicking it will install dependencies if needed, start the dashboard server in a new window, wait for `http://localhost:3000` to come up, and open your browser automatically.

It also handles the case where the app is already running: it skips starting a second server and just opens the browser.

{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.description\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\nAND NOT EXISTS (\n  SELECT 1\n  FROM todo_deps td\n  JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n  WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n)\nORDER BY t.id;"
}
```
