<details>
<summary>378 lines</summary>

```
1. "use client";
2. 
3. import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
4. import {
5.   buildDailyPerformance,
6.   buildPerformanceSummary,
7.   buildStockPerformanceHistory,
8.   outcomeClass,
9.   outcomeLabel
10. } from "@/lib/analytics";
11. import type {
12.   DailyPerformance,
13.   HorizonId,
14.   RecommendationDataset,
15.   RecommendationPlan,
16.   StockAnalysis,
17.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
18. } from "@/lib/types";
19. 
20. type DashboardProps = {
21.   data: RecommendationDataset;
22. };
23. 
24. function formatPrice(value: number) {
25.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
26.     style: "currency",
27.     currency: "INR",
28.     maximumFractionDigits: 0
29.   }).format(value);
30. }
31. 
32. function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {
33.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
34. }
35. 
36. function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
37.   if (value === null) {
38.     return "Pending";
39.   }
40. 
41.   return `${value.toFixed(2)}%`;
42. }
43. 
44. export function Dashboard({ data }: DashboardProps) {
45.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>("swing");
46.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>(
47.     data.currentBatch.recommendations[0]?.symbol ?? ""
48.   );
49. 
50.   const selectedStock = useMemo<StockAnalysis | undefined>(
51.     () =>
52.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ??
53.       data.currentBatch.recommendations[0],
54.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations, selectedSymbol]
55.   );
56. 
57.   const selectedPlan = selectedStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
58.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo<DailyPerformance[]>(
59.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
60.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
61.   );
62.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
63.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
64.     [dailyPerformance]
65.   );
66.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
67.     () =>
68.       selectedStock
69.         ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, selectedStock.symbol, activeHorizon)
70.         : [],
71.     [activeHorizon, data.history, selectedStock]
72.   );
73. 
74.   return (
75.     <main className="shell">
76.       <div className="topbar">
77.         <div className="headline">
78.           <span className="eyebrow">Chosen UI foundation: Next.js dashboard</span>
79.           <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
80.           <p>
81.             This foundation is built for daily post-market analysis. Click any stock to inspect
82.             the rationale, trade plan, and horizon-specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels.
83.           </p>
84.         </div>
85. 
86.         <section className="card run-card">
87.           <div className="metric-label">Latest batch</div>
88.           <div className="metric-value">
89.             {new Date(data.currentBatch.generatedAt).toLocaleString("en-IN")}
90.           </div>
91.           <p className="footnote">
92.             {data.exchange} · {data.universe} · batch date {data.currentBatch.batchDate}
93.           </p>
94.         </section>
95.       </div>
96. 
97.       <section className="meta-grid">
98.         <article className="card">
99.           <div className="metric-label">Coverage universe</div>
100.           <div className="metric-value">{data.universe}</div>
101.         </article>
102.         <article className="card">
103.           <div className="metric-label">Market</div>
104.           <div className="metric-value">{data.market}</div>
105.         </article>
106.         <article className="card">
107.           <div className="metric-label">Tracked stocks</div>
108.           <div className="metric-value">{data.currentBatch.recommendations.length}</div>
109.         </article>
110.         <article className="card">
111.           <div className="metric-label">Active horizon</div>
112.           <div className="metric-value">
113.             {data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)?.label}
114.           </div>
115.         </article>
116.         <article className="card">
117.           <div className="metric-label">Latest completed success rate</div>
118.           <div className="metric-value">
119.             {formatPercent(performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? null)}
120.           </div>
121.         </article>
122.         <article className="card">
123.           <div className="metric-label">Average daily success rate</div>
124.           <div className="metric-value">{formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate)}</div>
125.         </article>
126.       </section>
127. 
128.       <div className="workspace">
129.         <div className="stack">
130.           <section className="card">
131.             <div className="section-title">
132.               <div>
133.                 <h2>Today&apos;s ranked ideas</h2>
134.                 <div className="section-subtitle">
135.                   Each row is a research recommendation with a transparent trade plan.
136.                 </div>
137.               </div>
138. 
139.               <div className="pills">
140.                 {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
141.                   <button
142.                     key={profile.id}
143.                     className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
144.                     onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
145.                     type="button"
146.                   >
147.                     {profile.label} · {profile.window}
148.                   </button>
149.                 ))}
150.               </div>
151.             </div>
152. 
153.             <div className="table-wrap">
154.               <table className="stock-table">
155.                 <thead>
156.                   <tr>
157.                     <th>Stock</th>
158.                     <th>Sector</th>
159.                     <th>Conviction</th>
160.                     <th>Entry</th>
161.                     <th>Target</th>
162.                     <th>Stop-loss</th>
163.                     <th>Expected return</th>
164.                     <th>R:R</th>
165.                   </tr>
166.                 </thead>
167.                 <tbody>
168.                   {data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => {
169.                     const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
170. 
171.                     return (
172.                       <tr
173.                         key={stock.symbol}
174.                         className={`stock-row${stock.symbol === selectedStock?.symbol ? " active" : ""}`}
175.                         onClick={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}
176.                       >
177.                         <td>
178.                           <div className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</div>
179.                           <div className="company">{stock.companyName}</div>
180.                         </td>
181.                         <td>{stock.sector}</td>
182.                         <td>
183.                           <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>
184.                             {plan.conviction}
185.                           </span>
186.                         </td>
187.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)}</td>
188.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</td>
189.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</td>
190.                         <td>{plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%</td>
191.                         <td>{plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</td>
192.                       </tr>
193.                     );
194.                   })}
195.                 </tbody>
196.               </table>
197.             </div>
198.           </section>
199. 
200.           <section className="card">
201.             <div className="section-title">
202.               <div>
203.                 <h2>Daily recommendation scorecard</h2>
204.                 <div className="section-subtitle">
205.                   Previous day recommendations are tested against target-hit and stop-loss outcomes.
206.                 </div>
207.               </div>
208.             </div>
209. 
210.             <div className="table-wrap">
211.               <table className="stock-table compact-table">
212.                 <thead>
213.                   <tr>
214.                     <th>Batch date</th>
215.                     <th>Published</th>
216.                     <th>Success rate</th>
217.                     <th>Successes</th>
218.                     <th>Failures</th>
219.                     <th>Open</th>
220.                     <th>Avg return</th>
221.                   </tr>
222.                 </thead>
223.                 <tbody>
224.                   {dailyPerformance.map((row) => (
225.                     <tr key={row.batchDate}>
226.                       <td>{row.batchDate}</td>
227.                       <td>{new Date(row.publishedAt).toLocaleDateString("en-IN")}</td>
228.                       <td>{formatPercent(row.successRate)}</td>
229.                       <td>{row.successful}</td>
230.                       <td>{row.failed}</td>
231.                       <td>{row.open}</td>
232.                       <td>{formatPercent(row.averageReturnPct)}</td>
233.                     </tr>
234.                   ))}
235.                 </tbody>
236.               </table>
237.             </div>
238.           </section>
239.         </div>
240. 
241.         {selectedStock && selectedPlan ? (
242.           <aside className="detail-grid">
243.             <section className="card">
244.               <div className="detail-header">
245.                 <div>
246.                   <h2>
247.                     {selectedStock.companyName} ({selectedStock.symbol})
248.                   </h2>
249.                   <div className="detail-subhead">
250.                     {selectedStock.sector} · {selectedStock.marketCapBucket} ·{" "}
251.                     {selectedStock.liquidityTier}
252.                   </div>
253.                 </div>
254. 
255.                 <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(selectedPlan.conviction)}`}>
256.                   {selectedPlan.conviction} conviction
257.                 </span>
258.               </div>
259. 
260.               <p className="summary">{selectedPlan.summary}</p>
261.             </section>
262. 
263.             <section className="card">
264.               <h3>Trade plan</h3>
265.               <div className="stat-grid">
266.                 <article className="stat-card">
267.                   <div className="metric-label">Entry price</div>
268.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.entryPrice)}</strong>
269.                 </article>
270.                 <article className="stat-card">
271.                   <div className="metric-label">Target price</div>
272.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.targetPrice)}</strong>
273.                 </article>
274.                 <article className="stat-card">
275.                   <div className="metric-label">Stop-loss</div>
276.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.stopLoss)}</strong>
277.                 </article>
278.                 <article className="stat-card">
279.                   <div className="metric-label">Risk-reward</div>
280.                   <strong>{selectedPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</strong>
281.                 </article>
282.               </div>
283.             </section>
284. 
285.             <section className="card">
286.               <h3>Why this stock ranks well</h3>
287.               <ul className="list">
288.                 {selectedPlan.drivers.map((driver) => (
289.                   <li key={driver}>{driver}</li>
290.                 ))}
291.               </ul>
292.             </section>
293. 
294.             <section className="card">
295.               <h3>Technical analysis</h3>
296.               <div className="signal-grid">
297.                 {selectedPlan.technicalSignals.map((signal) => (
298.                   <div key={signal.name} className="signal-row">
299.                     <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
300.                     <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
301.                   </div>
302.                 ))}
303.               </div>
304.             </section>
305. 
306.             <section className="card">
307.               <h3>Fundamental analysis</h3>
308.               <div className="signal-grid">
309.                 {selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals.map((signal) => (
310.                   <div key={signal.name} className="signal-row">
311.                     <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
312.                     <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
313.                   </div>
314.                 ))}
315.               </div>
316.             </section>
317. 
318.             <section className="card">
319.               <h3>Recent context</h3>
320.               <ul className="list">
321.                 {selectedPlan.newsContext.map((item) => (
322.                   <li key={item}>{item}</li>
323.                 ))}
324.               </ul>
325.             </section>
326. 
327.             <section className="card">
328.               <h3>Previous recommendation outcomes</h3>
329.               <div className="section-subtitle">
330.                 Earlier daily calls for this stock in the selected horizon.
331.               </div>
332. 
333.               <div className="table-wrap">
334.                 <table className="stock-table compact-table">
335.                   <thead>
336.                     <tr>
337.                       <th>Batch date</th>
338.                       <th>Outcome</th>
339.                       <th>Return</th>
340.                       <th>Days held</th>
341.                       <th>Entry</th>
342.                       <th>Target</th>
343.                       <th>Stop-loss</th>
344.                     </tr>
345.                   </thead>
346.                   <tbody>
347.                     {stockHistory.map((row) => (
348.                       <tr key={`${row.symbol}-${row.batchDate}-${row.horizon}`}>
349.                         <td>{row.batchDate}</td>
350.                         <td>
351.                           <span className={`result-chip ${outcomeClass(row.outcome.result)}`}>
352.                             {outcomeLabel(row.outcome.result)}
353.                           </span>
354.                         </td>
355.                         <td>{`${row.outcome.returnPct.toFixed(2)}%`}</td>
356.                         <td>{row.outcome.holdingDays}</td>
357.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.entryPrice)}</td>
358.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.targetPrice)}</td>
359.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.stopLoss)}</td>
360.                       </tr>
361.                     ))}
362.                   </tbody>
363.                 </table>
364.               </div>
365. 
366.               {stockHistory[0] ? (
367.                 <p className="footnote">
368.                   Latest reviewed note: {stockHistory[0].outcome.notes}
369.                 </p>
370.               ) : null}
371.             </section>
372.           </aside>
373.         ) : null}
374.       </div>
375.     </main>
376.   );
377. }
378.
```

</details>

1. export type HorizonId = "swing" | "position" | "long_term";

3. 
4. export type Signal = {
5.   name: string;
6.   value: string;
7. };
8. 
9. export type RecommendationPlan = {
10.   conviction: "High" | "Medium" | "Low";
11.   entryPrice: number;
12.   targetPrice: number;
13.   stopLoss: number;
14.   expectedReturnPct: number;
15.   riskReward: number;
16.   summary: string;
17.   drivers: string[];
18.   technicalSignals: Signal[];
19.   fundamentalSignals: Signal[];
20.   newsContext: string[];
21. };
22. 
23. export type StockAnalysis = {
24.   symbol: string;
25.   companyName: string;
26.   sector: string;
27.   marketCapBucket: string;
28.   liquidityTier: string;
29.   profiles: Record<HorizonId, RecommendationPlan>;
30. };
31. 
32. export type RecommendationOutcome = {
33.   result: OutcomeResult;
34.   evaluatedOn: string;
35.   holdingDays: number;
36.   returnPct: number;
37.   notes: string;
38. };
39. 
40. export type HistoricalRecommendationPlan = {
41.   conviction: "High" | "Medium" | "Low";
42.   entryPrice: number;
43.   targetPrice: number;
44.   stopLoss: number;
45.   summary: string;
46.   outcome: RecommendationOutcome;
47. };
48. 
49. export type HistoricalStockRecommendation = {
50.   symbol: string;
51.   companyName: string;
52.   sector: string;
53.   profiles: Record<HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>;
54. };
55. 
56. export type HorizonProfile = {
57.   id: HorizonId;
58.   label: string;
59.   window: string;
60. };
61. 
62. export type RecommendationBatch = {
63.   batchDate: string;
64.   generatedAt: string;
65.   recommendations: StockAnalysis[];
66. };
67. 
68. export type HistoricalBatch = {
69.   batchDate: string;
70.   publishedAt: string;
71.   recommendations: HistoricalStockRecommendation[];
72. };
73. 
74. export type DailyPerformance = {
75.   batchDate: string;
76.   publishedAt: string;
77.   total: number;
78.   closed: number;
79.   open: number;
80.   successful: number;
81.   failed: number;
82.   successRate: number | null;
83.   averageReturnPct: number | null;
84. };
85. 
86. export type StockPerformanceHistoryEntry = {
87.   batchDate: string;
88.   publishedAt: string;
89.   symbol: string;
90.   companyName: string;
91.   sector: string;
92.   horizon: HorizonId;

95.   targetPrice: number;

97.   summary: string;
98.   outcome: RecommendationOutcome;
99. };
100.

103.   exchange: string;
104.   universe: string;
105.   profiles: HorizonProfile[];
106.   currentBatch: RecommendationBatch;
107.   history: HistoricalBatch[];

```
1. from typing import Literal
2. 
3. from pydantic import BaseModel
4. 
5. HorizonId = Literal["swing", "position", "long_term"]
6. Conviction = Literal["High", "Medium", "Low"]
7. OutcomeResult = Literal["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit", "open"]
8. 
9. 
10. class Signal(BaseModel):
11.     name: str
12.     value: str
13. 
14. 
15. class RecommendationOutcome(BaseModel):
16.     result: OutcomeResult
17.     evaluatedOn: str
18.     holdingDays: int
19.     returnPct: float
20.     notes: str
21. 
22. 
23. class RecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
24.     conviction: Conviction
25.     entryPrice: float
26.     targetPrice: float
27.     stopLoss: float
28.     expectedReturnPct: float
29.     riskReward: float
30.     summary: str
31.     drivers: list[str]
32.     technicalSignals: list[Signal]
33.     fundamentalSignals: list[Signal]
34.     newsContext: list[str]
35. 
36. 
37. class HistoricalRecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
38.     conviction: Conviction
39.     entryPrice: float
40.     targetPrice: float
41.     stopLoss: float
42.     summary: str
43.     outcome: RecommendationOutcome
44. 
45. 
46. class StockAnalysis(BaseModel):
47.     symbol: str
48.     companyName: str
49.     sector: str
50.     marketCapBucket: str
51.     liquidityTier: str
52.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, RecommendationPlan]
53. 
54. 
55. class HistoricalStockRecommendation(BaseModel):
56.     symbol: str
57.     companyName: str
58.     sector: str
59.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan]
60. 
61. 
62. class HorizonProfile(BaseModel):
63.     id: HorizonId
64.     label: str
65.     window: str
66. 
67. 
68. class RecommendationBatch(BaseModel):
69.     batchDate: str
70.     generatedAt: str
71.     recommendations: list[StockAnalysis]
72. 
73. 
74. class HistoricalBatch(BaseModel):
75.     batchDate: str
76.     publishedAt: str
77.     recommendations: list[HistoricalStockRecommendation]
78. 
79. 
80. class RecommendationDataset(BaseModel):
81.     market: str
82.     exchange: str
83.     universe: str
84.     profiles: list[HorizonProfile]
85.     currentBatch: RecommendationBatch
86.     history: list[HistoricalBatch]
87. 
88. 
89. class RecommendationSummary(BaseModel):
90.     symbol: str
91.     companyName: str
92.     sector: str
93.     horizon: HorizonId
94.     conviction: Conviction
95.     entryPrice: float
96.     targetPrice: float
97.     stopLoss: float
98.     expectedReturnPct: float
99.     riskReward: float
100.     summary: str
101. 
102. 
103. class DailyPerformance(BaseModel):
104.     batchDate: str
105.     publishedAt: str
106.     total: int
107.     closed: int
108.     open: int
109.     successful: int
110.     failed: int
111.     successRate: float | None
112.     averageReturnPct: float | None
113. 
114. 
115. class StockPerformanceHistoryEntry(BaseModel):
116.     batchDate: str
117.     publishedAt: str
118.     symbol: str
119.     companyName: str
120.     sector: str
121.     horizon: HorizonId
122.     conviction: Conviction
123.     entryPrice: float
124.     targetPrice: float
125.     stopLoss: float
126.     summary: str
127.     outcome: RecommendationOutcome
128. 
129. 
130. class StockDetailResponse(BaseModel):
131.     generatedAt: str
132.     batchDate: str
133.     market: str
134.     exchange: str
135.     universe: str
136.     selectedHorizon: HorizonId
137.     stock: StockAnalysis
138.     history: list[StockPerformanceHistoryEntry]
139.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>109 lines</summary>

```
1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. 
4. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
5. 
6. from .analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
7. from .schemas import (
8.     HorizonId,
9.     RecommendationDataset,
10.     RecommendationSummary,
11.     StockAnalysis,
12.     StockDetailResponse,
13. )
14. 
15. DATA_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data" / "sample-recommendations.json"
16. 
17. app = FastAPI(
18.     title="Recommendation API",
19.     version="0.1.0",
20.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
21. )
22. 
23. 
24. def load_dataset() -> RecommendationDataset:
25.     payload = json.loads(DATA_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
26.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
27. 
28. 
29. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
30.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
31. 
32.     return RecommendationSummary(
33.         symbol=stock.symbol,
34.         companyName=stock.companyName,
35.         sector=stock.sector,
36.         horizon=horizon,
37.         conviction=plan.conviction,
38.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
39.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
40.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
41.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
42.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
43.         summary=plan.summary,
44.     )
45. 
46. 
47. @app.get("/healthz")
48. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
49.     return {"status": "ok"}
50. 
51. 
52. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
53. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
54.     dataset = load_dataset()
55.     rows = [
56.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
57.         for stock in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
58.     ]
59.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
60. 
61.     return {
62.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
63.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
64.         "market": dataset.market,
65.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
66.         "universe": dataset.universe,
67.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
68.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
69.         "recommendations": rows,
70.     }
71. 
72. 
73. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
74. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
75.     dataset = load_dataset()
76.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
77. 
78.     return {
79.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
80.         "performance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
81.     }
82. 
83. 
84. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
85. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
86.     dataset = load_dataset()
87.     stock = next(
88.         (
89.             row
90.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
91.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
92.         ),
93.         None,
94.     )
95. 
96.     if stock is None:
97.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
98. 
99.     return StockDetailResponse(
100.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
101.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
102.         market=dataset.market,
103.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
104.         universe=dataset.universe,
105.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
106.         stock=stock,
107.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
108.     )
109.
```

</details>

```
1. import unittest
2. 
3. from app.analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
4. from app.main import load_dataset
5. 
6. 
7. class RecommendationAnalyticsTests(unittest.TestCase):
8.     @classmethod
9.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
10.         cls.dataset = load_dataset()
11. 
12.     def test_swing_daily_performance_counts_successes_and_failures(self) -> None:
13.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "swing")
14.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
15. 
16.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 2)
17.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
18.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 3)
19.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 0)
20.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 66.67, places=2)
21. 
22.     def test_position_success_rate_ignores_open_trades(self) -> None:
23.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "position")
24.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
25. 
26.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 1)
27.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
28.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 1)
29.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 2)
30.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 50.0, places=2)
31. 
32.     def test_long_term_daily_performance_is_pending_when_nothing_is_closed(self) -> None:
33.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "long_term")
34.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
35. 
36.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 3)
37.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 0)
38.         self.assertIsNone(latest.successRate)
39.         self.assertIsNone(latest.averageReturnPct)
40. 
41.     def test_stock_history_filters_by_symbol_in_latest_first_order(self) -> None:
42.         history = build_stock_history(self.dataset, "RELIANCE", "swing")
43. 
44.         self.assertEqual(
45.             [entry.batchDate for entry in history],
46.             ["2026-04-14", "2026-04-11", "2026-04-10"],
47.         )
48.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.symbol == "RELIANCE" for entry in history))
49. 
50. 
51. if __name__ == "__main__":
52.     unittest.main()
53.
```

</details>

<details>

```
1. import { readFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2. import path from "node:path";
3. import type {
4.   HistoricalBatch,
5.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
6.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
7.   RecommendationDataset,
8.   RecommendationPlan,
9.   StockAnalysis
10. } from "@/lib/types";
11. 
12. const DATASET_PATH_CANDIDATES = [
13.   path.join(process.cwd(), "data", "sample-recommendations.json"),
14.   path.join(process.cwd(), "..", "..", "data", "sample-recommendations.json")
15. ];
16. 
17. type YahooChartResponse = {
18.   chart?: {
19.     result?: Array<{
20.       meta?: {
21.         regularMarketPrice?: number;
22.         previousClose?: number;
23.       };
24.     }>;
25.   };
26. };
27. 
28. function roundPrice(value: number) {
29.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
30. }
31. 
32. function scaleNumericText(value: string, factor: number) {
33.   return value.replace(/\d+(\.\d+)?/g, (match) => {
34.     const numericValue = Number.parseFloat(match);
35. 
36.     if (!Number.isFinite(numericValue)) {
37.       return match;
38.     }
39. 
40.     return `${roundPrice(numericValue * factor)}`;
41.   });
42. }
43. 
44. function scaleCurrentPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan, factor: number): RecommendationPlan {
45.   return {
46.     ...plan,
47.     entryPrice: roundPrice(plan.entryPrice * factor),
48.     targetPrice: roundPrice(plan.targetPrice * factor),
49.     stopLoss: roundPrice(plan.stopLoss * factor),
50.     technicalSignals: plan.technicalSignals.map((signal) => ({
51.       ...signal,
52.       value: scaleNumericText(signal.value, factor)
53.     }))
54.   };
55. }
56. 
57. function scaleHistoricalPlan(
58.   plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
59.   factor: number
60. ): HistoricalRecommendationPlan {
61.   return {
62.     ...plan,
63.     entryPrice: roundPrice(plan.entryPrice * factor),
64.     targetPrice: roundPrice(plan.targetPrice * factor),
65.     stopLoss: roundPrice(plan.stopLoss * factor)
66.   };
67. }
68. 
69. function scaleCurrentRecommendation(stock: StockAnalysis, livePrice: number): StockAnalysis {
70.   if (!stock.currentMarketPrice) {
71.     return stock;
72.   }
73. 
74.   const factor = livePrice / stock.currentMarketPrice;
75. 
76.   if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
77.     return stock;
78.   }
79. 
80.   return {
81.     ...stock,
82.     currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(livePrice),
83.     profiles: {
84.       swing: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.swing, factor),
85.       position: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.position, factor),
86.       long_term: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.long_term, factor)
87.     }
88.   };
89. }
90. 
91. function scaleHistoricalRecommendation(
92.   recommendation: HistoricalStockRecommendation,
93.   factor: number
94. ): HistoricalStockRecommendation {
95.   if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
96.     return recommendation;
97.   }
98. 
99.   return {
100.     ...recommendation,
101.     profiles: {
102.       swing: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.swing, factor),
103.       position: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.position, factor),
104.       long_term: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.long_term, factor)
105.     }
106.   };
107. }
108. 
109. function buildPriceFactors(dataset: RecommendationDataset, livePrices: Record<string, number>) {
110.   return Object.fromEntries(
111.     dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.flatMap((recommendation) => {
112.       const livePrice = livePrices[recommendation.symbol];
113. 
114.       if (!livePrice || !recommendation.currentMarketPrice) {
115.         return [];
116.       }
117. 
118.       const factor = livePrice / recommendation.currentMarketPrice;
119. 
120.       if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
121.         return [];
122.       }
123. 
124.       return [[recommendation.symbol, factor]];
125.     })
126.   ) as Record<string, number>;
127. }
128. 
129. function applyLivePriceAdjustments(
130.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
131.   livePrices: Record<string, number>
132. ): RecommendationDataset {
133.   const factors = buildPriceFactors(dataset, livePrices);
134. 
135.   if (!Object.keys(factors).length) {
136.     return dataset;
137.   }
138. 
139.   const recommendations = dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
140.     const livePrice = livePrices[recommendation.symbol];
141. 
142.     return livePrice ? scaleCurrentRecommendation(recommendation, livePrice) : recommendation;
143.   });
144. 
145.   const history: HistoricalBatch[] = dataset.history.map((batch) => ({
146.     ...batch,
147.     recommendations: batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
148.       const factor = factors[recommendation.symbol];
149. 
150.       return factor
151.         ? scaleHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, factor)
152.         : recommendation;
153.     })
154.   }));
155. 
156.   return {
157.     ...dataset,
158.     currentBatch: {
159.       ...dataset.currentBatch,
160.       recommendations
161.     },
162.     history
163.   };
164. }
165. 
166. async function readDatasetFile() {
167.   for (const candidatePath of DATASET_PATH_CANDIDATES) {
168.     try {
169.       return await readFile(candidatePath, "utf-8");
170.     } catch {
171.       continue;
172.     }
173.   }
174. 
175.   throw new Error("Unable to locate sample-recommendations.json");
176. }
177. 
178. async function fetchLivePrice(symbol: string) {
179.   try {
180.     const response = await fetch(
181.       `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${symbol}.NS?interval=1d&range=1d`,
182.       {
183.         cache: "no-store"
184.       }
185.     );
186. 
187.     if (!response.ok) {
188.       return null;
189.     }
190. 
191.     const payload = (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;
192.     const price =
193.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.regularMarketPrice ??
194.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.previousClose;
195. 
196.     return typeof price === "number" && Number.isFinite(price) ? roundPrice(price) : null;
197.   } catch {
198.     return null;
199.   }
200. }
201. 
202. async function fetchLivePrices(symbols: string[]) {
203.   const prices = await Promise.all(
204.     symbols.map(async (symbol) => [symbol, await fetchLivePrice(symbol)] as const)
205.   );
206. 
207.   return Object.fromEntries(
208.     prices.filter((entry): entry is readonly [string, number] => entry[1] !== null)
209.   );
210. }
211. 
212. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
213.   const payload = await readDatasetFile();
214.   const dataset = JSON.parse(payload) as RecommendationDataset;
215.   const livePrices = await fetchLivePrices(
216.     dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol)
217.   );
218. 
219.   return applyLivePriceAdjustments(dataset, livePrices);
220. }
221.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 110m 46s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Fixed the price source in the web app.** The UI was showing the mock JSON value for `RELIANCE` (`2998`) instead of a live market reference. It now fetches live NSE-style quote data at page load and rescales the displayed recommendation price levels to match.

**What changed**
- `apps\web\app\page.tsx` now loads data through a live-price-aware loader
- `apps\web\lib\recommendation-data.ts` was added to:
  - read the sample dataset,
  - fetch live prices for current symbols,
  - update **current market price**,
  - rescale current and historical displayed price levels so they stay on the same price scale

**What to do**
1. Close the old app window.
2. Double-click `C:\copilot\run-app.bat` again.
3. Refresh the browser.

If it still shows the old value after restart, the machine likely couldn’t reach the live quote endpoint and fell back to the sample dataset.

114.       if (!livePrice || !recommendation.currentMarketPrice) {
115.         return [];
116.       }
117. 
118.       const factor = livePrice / recommendation.currentMarketPrice;
119. 
120.       if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
121.         return [];
122.       }
123. 
124.       return [[recommendation.symbol, factor]];
125.     })
126.   ) as Record<string, number>;
127. }
128. 
129. function applyLivePriceAdjustments(
130.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
131.   livePrices: Record<string, number>
132. ): RecommendationDataset {
133.   const factors = buildPriceFactors(dataset, livePrices);
134. 
135.   if (!Object.keys(factors).length) {
136.     return dataset;
137.   }
138. 
139.   const recommendations = dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
140.     const livePrice = livePrices[recommendation.symbol];
141. 
142.     return livePrice ? scaleCurrentRecommendation(recommendation, livePrice) : recommendation;
143.   });
144. 
145.   const history: HistoricalBatch[] = dataset.history.map((batch) => ({
146.     ...batch,
147.     recommendations: batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
148.       const factor = factors[recommendation.symbol];
149. 
150.       return factor
151.         ? scaleHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, factor)
152.         : recommendation;
153.     })
154.   }));
155. 
156.   return {
157.     ...dataset,
158.     currentBatch: {
159.       ...dataset.currentBatch,
160.       recommendations
161.     },
162.     history
163.   };
164. }
165. 
166. async function readDatasetFile() {
167.   for (const candidatePath of DATASET_PATH_CANDIDATES) {
168.     try {
169.       return await readFile(candidatePath, "utf-8");
170.     } catch {
171.       continue;
172.     }
173.   }
174. 
175.   throw new Error("Unable to locate sample-recommendations.json");
176. }
177. 
178. async function fetchLivePrice(symbol: string) {
179.   try {
180.     const response = await fetch(
181.       `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${symbol}.NS?interval=1d&range=1d`,
182.       {
183.         cache: "no-store"
184.       }
185.     );
186. 
187.     if (!response.ok) {
188.       return null;
189.     }
190. 
191.     const payload = (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;
192.     const price =
193.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.regularMarketPrice ??
194.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.previousClose;
195. 
196.     return typeof price === "number" && Number.isFinite(price) ? roundPrice(price) : null;
197.   } catch {
198.     return null;
199.   }
200. }
201. 
202. async function fetchLivePrices(symbols: string[]) {
203.   const prices = await Promise.all(
204.     symbols.map(async (symbol) => [symbol, await fetchLivePrice(symbol)] as const)
205.   );
206. 
207.   return Object.fromEntries(
208.     prices.filter((entry): entry is readonly [string, number] => entry[1] !== null)
209.   );
210. }
211. 
212. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
213.   const payload = await readDatasetFile();
214.   const dataset = JSON.parse(payload) as RecommendationDataset;
215.   const livePrices = await fetchLivePrices(
216.     dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol)
217.   );
218. 
219.   return applyLivePriceAdjustments(dataset, livePrices);
220. }
221.
```

</details>

16. 
17. app = FastAPI(
18.     title="Recommendation API",
19.     version="0.1.0",
20.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
21. )
22. 
23. 
24. def load_dataset() -> RecommendationDataset:
25.     payload = json.loads(DATA_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
26.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)

28. 
29. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
30.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
31. 
32.     return RecommendationSummary(
33.         symbol=stock.symbol,
34.         companyName=stock.companyName,
35.         sector=stock.sector,
36.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
37.         horizon=horizon,
38.         conviction=plan.conviction,
39.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
40.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
41.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
42.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
43.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
44.         summary=plan.summary,
45.     )
46. 
47.

56.     rows = [
57.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
58.         for stock in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
59.     ]
60.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
61. 
62.     return {
63.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
64.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
65.         "market": dataset.market,
66.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
67.         "universe": dataset.universe,
68.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
69.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
70.         "recommendations": rows,
71.     }
72. 
73. 
74. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
75. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:

77.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
78. 
79.     return {

82.     }
83. 
84. 
85. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
86. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
87.     dataset = load_dataset()
88.     stock = next(
89.         (
90.             row
91.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
92.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
93.         ),
94.         None,
95.     )
96. 
97.     if stock is None:
98.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
99. 
100.     return StockDetailResponse(
101.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
102.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
103.         market=dataset.market,
104.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
105.         universe=dataset.universe,
106.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
107.         stock=stock,
108.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
109.     )
110.

<sub>⏱️ 112m 38s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Because the app is still built on a mock dataset, not a real ingestion pipeline.** Right now:

1. **The base data always comes from** `data\sample-recommendations.json`.
   - Web: `apps\web\lib\recommendation-data.ts`
   - API: `services\api\app\main.py`

2. **The “live” part is only a best-effort price overlay** for the web page.
   - It tries to fetch a quote from Yahoo.
   - If that fetch fails, returns no price, or gets blocked, the code **falls back to the sample JSON unchanged**.

3. **Recommendations, history, success rates, entry/target/stop-loss are all still mock data.**
   - Only the displayed current price was partially wired for live refresh.
   - The actual recommendation engine is not connected to a live market data provider yet.

4. **The API is still 100% mock-backed.**
   - It does not use the live-price loader at all.

So the honest answer is: **real-time data is not fully implemented yet**. What exists now is a demo UI with a temporary live-price patch on top.

To make it truly real-time, the pending `build-ingestion` work needs to be done:
- connect to a reliable market data source,
- fetch/store latest NSE prices and fundamentals,
- generate recommendations from that live data,
- serve both web and API from the same live store instead of `sample-recommendations.json`.

---

<sub>⏱️ 116m 7s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m moving this from a demo into a real data flow: live quotes, recent history, computed signals, and recommendations generated from those signals instead of the mock JSON. I’m mapping the current surfaces first so I can replace the mock loader cleanly rather than stacking another patch on top.

```
1. import { readFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2. import path from "node:path";
3. import type {
4.   HistoricalBatch,
5.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
6.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
7.   RecommendationDataset,
8.   RecommendationPlan,
9.   StockAnalysis
10. } from "@/lib/types";
11. 
12. const DATASET_PATH_CANDIDATES = [
13.   path.join(process.cwd(), "data", "sample-recommendations.json"),
14.   path.join(process.cwd(), "..", "..", "data", "sample-recommendations.json")
15. ];
16. 
17. type YahooChartResponse = {
18.   chart?: {
19.     result?: Array<{
20.       meta?: {
21.         regularMarketPrice?: number;
22.         previousClose?: number;
23.       };
24.     }>;
25.   };
26. };
27. 
28. function roundPrice(value: number) {
29.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
30. }
31. 
32. function scaleNumericText(value: string, factor: number) {
33.   return value.replace(/\d+(\.\d+)?/g, (match) => {
34.     const numericValue = Number.parseFloat(match);
35. 
36.     if (!Number.isFinite(numericValue)) {
37.       return match;
38.     }
39. 
40.     return `${roundPrice(numericValue * factor)}`;
41.   });
42. }
43. 
44. function scaleCurrentPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan, factor: number): RecommendationPlan {
45.   return {
46.     ...plan,
47.     entryPrice: roundPrice(plan.entryPrice * factor),
48.     targetPrice: roundPrice(plan.targetPrice * factor),
49.     stopLoss: roundPrice(plan.stopLoss * factor),
50.     technicalSignals: plan.technicalSignals.map((signal) => ({
51.       ...signal,
52.       value: scaleNumericText(signal.value, factor)
53.     }))
54.   };
55. }
56. 
57. function scaleHistoricalPlan(
58.   plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
59.   factor: number
60. ): HistoricalRecommendationPlan {
61.   return {
62.     ...plan,
63.     entryPrice: roundPrice(plan.entryPrice * factor),
64.     targetPrice: roundPrice(plan.targetPrice * factor),
65.     stopLoss: roundPrice(plan.stopLoss * factor)
66.   };
67. }
68. 
69. function scaleCurrentRecommendation(stock: StockAnalysis, livePrice: number): StockAnalysis {
70.   if (!stock.currentMarketPrice) {
71.     return stock;
72.   }
73. 
74.   const factor = livePrice / stock.currentMarketPrice;
75. 
76.   if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
77.     return stock;
78.   }
79. 
80.   return {
81.     ...stock,
82.     currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(livePrice),
83.     profiles: {
84.       swing: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.swing, factor),
85.       position: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.position, factor),
86.       long_term: scaleCurrentPlan(stock.profiles.long_term, factor)
87.     }
88.   };
89. }
90. 
91. function scaleHistoricalRecommendation(
92.   recommendation: HistoricalStockRecommendation,
93.   factor: number
94. ): HistoricalStockRecommendation {
95.   if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
96.     return recommendation;
97.   }
98. 
99.   return {
100.     ...recommendation,
101.     profiles: {
102.       swing: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.swing, factor),
103.       position: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.position, factor),
104.       long_term: scaleHistoricalPlan(recommendation.profiles.long_term, factor)
105.     }
106.   };
107. }
108. 
109. function buildPriceFactors(dataset: RecommendationDataset, livePrices: Record<string, number>) {
110.   return Object.fromEntries(
111.     dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.flatMap((recommendation) => {
112.       const livePrice = livePrices[recommendation.symbol];
113. 
114.       if (!livePrice || !recommendation.currentMarketPrice) {
115.         return [];
116.       }
117. 
118.       const factor = livePrice / recommendation.currentMarketPrice;
119. 
120.       if (!Number.isFinite(factor) || factor <= 0) {
121.         return [];
122.       }
123. 
124.       return [[recommendation.symbol, factor]];
125.     })
126.   ) as Record<string, number>;
127. }
128. 
129. function applyLivePriceAdjustments(
130.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
131.   livePrices: Record<string, number>
132. ): RecommendationDataset {
133.   const factors = buildPriceFactors(dataset, livePrices);
134. 
135.   if (!Object.keys(factors).length) {
136.     return dataset;
137.   }
138. 
139.   const recommendations = dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
140.     const livePrice = livePrices[recommendation.symbol];
141. 
142.     return livePrice ? scaleCurrentRecommendation(recommendation, livePrice) : recommendation;
143.   });
144. 
145.   const history: HistoricalBatch[] = dataset.history.map((batch) => ({
146.     ...batch,
147.     recommendations: batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => {
148.       const factor = factors[recommendation.symbol];
149. 
150.       return factor
151.         ? scaleHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, factor)
152.         : recommendation;
153.     })
154.   }));
155. 
156.   return {
157.     ...dataset,
158.     currentBatch: {
159.       ...dataset.currentBatch,
160.       recommendations
161.     },
162.     history
163.   };
164. }
165. 
166. async function readDatasetFile() {
167.   for (const candidatePath of DATASET_PATH_CANDIDATES) {
168.     try {
169.       return await readFile(candidatePath, "utf-8");
170.     } catch {
171.       continue;
172.     }
173.   }
174. 
175.   throw new Error("Unable to locate sample-recommendations.json");
176. }
177. 
178. async function fetchLivePrice(symbol: string) {
179.   try {
180.     const response = await fetch(
181.       `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${symbol}.NS?interval=1d&range=1d`,
182.       {
183.         cache: "no-store"
184.       }
185.     );
186. 
187.     if (!response.ok) {
188.       return null;
189.     }
190. 
191.     const payload = (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;
192.     const price =
193.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.regularMarketPrice ??
194.       payload.chart?.result?.[0]?.meta?.previousClose;
195. 
196.     return typeof price === "number" && Number.isFinite(price) ? roundPrice(price) : null;
197.   } catch {
198.     return null;
199.   }
200. }
201. 
202. async function fetchLivePrices(symbols: string[]) {
203.   const prices = await Promise.all(
204.     symbols.map(async (symbol) => [symbol, await fetchLivePrice(symbol)] as const)
205.   );
206. 
207.   return Object.fromEntries(
208.     prices.filter((entry): entry is readonly [string, number] => entry[1] !== null)
209.   );
210. }
211. 
212. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
213.   const payload = await readDatasetFile();
214.   const dataset = JSON.parse(payload) as RecommendationDataset;
215.   const livePrices = await fetchLivePrices(
216.     dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol)
217.   );
218. 
219.   return applyLivePriceAdjustments(dataset, livePrices);
220. }
221.
```

</details>

100.     return StockDetailResponse(
101.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
102.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
103.         market=dataset.market,
104.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
105.         universe=dataset.universe,
106.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
107.         stock=stock,
108.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
109.     )
110.
```

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\services\api\app\analytics.py.**

C:\copilot\services\api\app\analytics.py

<details>
<summary>88 lines</summary>

```
1. from .schemas import (
2.     DailyPerformance,
3.     HistoricalBatch,
4.     HorizonId,
5.     OutcomeResult,
6.     RecommendationDataset,
7.     StockPerformanceHistoryEntry,
8. )
9. 
10. CLOSED_RESULTS: set[OutcomeResult] = {"target_hit", "stop_loss_hit"}
11. 
12. 
13. def _round_metric(value: float | None) -> float | None:
14.     if value is None:
15.         return None
16. 
17.     return round(value, 2)
18. 
19. 
20. def _is_closed_result(result: OutcomeResult) -> bool:
21.     return result in CLOSED_RESULTS
22. 
23. 
24. def build_daily_performance(dataset: RecommendationDataset, horizon: HorizonId) -> list[DailyPerformance]:
25.     rows: list[DailyPerformance] = []
26. 
27.     for batch in sorted(dataset.history, key=lambda item: item.batchDate, reverse=True):
28.         plans = [recommendation.profiles[horizon] for recommendation in batch.recommendations]
29.         successful = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "target_hit")
30.         failed = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "stop_loss_hit")
31.         open_count = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "open")
32.         closed = successful + failed
33.         closed_returns = [
34.             plan.outcome.returnPct for plan in plans if _is_closed_result(plan.outcome.result)
35.         ]
36. 
37.         rows.append(
38.             DailyPerformance(
39.                 batchDate=batch.batchDate,
40.                 publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,
41.                 total=len(plans),
42.                 closed=closed,
43.                 open=open_count,
44.                 successful=successful,
45.                 failed=failed,
46.                 successRate=_round_metric((successful / closed) * 100) if closed else None,
47.                 averageReturnPct=(
48.                     _round_metric(sum(closed_returns) / len(closed_returns))
49.                     if closed_returns
50.                     else None
51.                 ),
52.             )
53.         )
54. 
55.     return rows
56. 
57. 
58. def build_stock_history(
59.     dataset: RecommendationDataset, symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId
60. ) -> list[StockPerformanceHistoryEntry]:
61.     rows: list[StockPerformanceHistoryEntry] = []
62.     requested_symbol = symbol.upper()
63. 
64.     for batch in sorted(dataset.history, key=lambda item: item.batchDate, reverse=True):
65.         for recommendation in batch.recommendations:
66.             if recommendation.symbol != requested_symbol:
67.                 continue
68. 
69.             plan = recommendation.profiles[horizon]
70.             rows.append(
71.                 StockPerformanceHistoryEntry(
72.                     batchDate=batch.batchDate,
73.                     publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,
74.                     symbol=recommendation.symbol,
75.                     companyName=recommendation.companyName,
76.                     sector=recommendation.sector,
77.                     horizon=horizon,
78.                     conviction=plan.conviction,
79.                     entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
80.                     targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
81.                     stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
82.                     summary=plan.summary,
83.                     outcome=plan.outcome,
84.                 )
85.             )
86. 
87.     return rows
88.
```

</details>

1. import unittest
2. 
3. from app.analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
4. from app.main import load_dataset
5. 
6. 
7. class RecommendationAnalyticsTests(unittest.TestCase):
8.     @classmethod
9.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
10.         cls.dataset = load_dataset()
11. 
12.     def test_swing_daily_performance_counts_successes_and_failures(self) -> None:
13.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "swing")
14.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
15. 
16.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 2)
17.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
18.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 3)
19.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 0)
20.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 66.67, places=2)
21. 
22.     def test_position_success_rate_ignores_open_trades(self) -> None:
23.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "position")
24.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
25. 
26.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 1)
27.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
28.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 1)
29.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 2)
30.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 50.0, places=2)
31. 
32.     def test_long_term_daily_performance_is_pending_when_nothing_is_closed(self) -> None:
33.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "long_term")
34.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
35. 
36.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 3)
37.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 0)
38.         self.assertIsNone(latest.successRate)
39.         self.assertIsNone(latest.averageReturnPct)
40. 
41.     def test_stock_history_filters_by_symbol_in_latest_first_order(self) -> None:
42.         history = build_stock_history(self.dataset, "RELIANCE", "swing")
43. 
44.         self.assertEqual(
45.             [entry.batchDate for entry in history],
46.             ["2026-04-14", "2026-04-11", "2026-04-10"],
47.         )
48.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.symbol == "RELIANCE" for entry in history))
49. 
50. 
51. if __name__ == "__main__":
52.     unittest.main()
53.
```

1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - Workspace `C:\copilot` is empty; there is no existing application code to extend.
8. - No repository structure, app framework, data pipeline, or model stack is present yet.
9. - This plan assumes a greenfield implementation.
10. 
11. ## Proposed approach
12. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
13. 
14. 1. Product definition
15.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
16.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
17.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
18.    - Chosen MVP scope:
19.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
20.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
21.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
22.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
23.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
24.      - Supported horizons in the MVP: swing, position, and long-term; intraday deferred to a later high-frequency phase.
25. 
26. 2. Data foundation
27.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
28.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
29.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
30. 
31. 3. Research feature layer
32.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
33.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
34. 
35. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
36.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
37.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
38.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
39.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
40.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
41. 
42. 5. Evaluation and controls
43.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
44.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
45.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
46. 
47. 6. Explainability and delivery
48.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
49.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
50.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
51.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
52. 
53. 7. UI template recommendation
54.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
55.    - Chosen starting point: Next.js with TypeScript and shadcn/ui-style dashboard patterns for a clean analyst-oriented UI.
56.    - Layout suggestion:
57.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.
58.      - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.
59.      - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.
60.    - Stock detail page should include:
61.      - Recommendation summary card.
62.      - Horizon selector so the same stock can show swing, position, and long-term variants when available.
63.      - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.
64.      - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.
65.      - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.
66. 
67. ## Draft todo list
68. - `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.
69. - `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.
70. - `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.
71. - `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.
72. - `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.
73. - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.
74. - `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.
75. - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.
76. - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.
77. - `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.
78. - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.
79. 
80. ## Notes and considerations
81. - "Like BlackRock" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.
82. - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.
83. - Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.
84. - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.
85. - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.
86. - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.
87. - Revised scope assumption: the platform should be designed for multiple horizons, but daily-data MVP coverage should focus on swing, position, and long-term profiles first.
88. - Intraday recommendations require separate high-frequency data ingestion, tighter execution assumptions, and different backtesting logic, so they should be treated as a later phase rather than bundled into the first daily-data release.
89. - Chosen foundation stack:
90.   - Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript with a clean dashboard UI.
91.   - API and analytics services: FastAPI + Pydantic in Python.
92.   - Operational storage: PostgreSQL for users, recommendation batches, and audit history.
93.   - Research storage: Parquet or DuckDB for snapshots, factor data, and backtesting workloads.
94.   - Scheduling: market-calendar-aware daily batch jobs, starting with end-of-day runs.
95.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 135m 27s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve settled the architecture: the app will analyze a live NSE watchlist from Yahoo chart data, score it per horizon, generate recommendations and trade plans, backtest prior days from the same historical series, and cache the latest successful snapshot locally so the UI doesn’t collapse if a quote request fails.

---

<sub>⏱️ 135m 53s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>

}\n+\n+  return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;\n+}\n+\n+function compactNumber(value: number | null) {\n+  if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {\n+    return \"n/a\";\n+  }\n+\n+  return new Intl.NumberFormat(\"en-IN\", {\n+    notation: \"compact\",\n+    maximumFractionDigits: 1\n+  }).format(value);\n+}\n+\n+function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {\n+  if (values.length < period) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return average(values.slice(-period));\n+}\n+\n+function annualizedVolatility(closes: number[], period: number) {\n+  if (closes.length < period + 1) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));\n+  const returns = window.slice(1).map((close, index) => (close - window[index]) / window[index]);\n+  const mean = average(returns);\n+\n+  if (mean === null) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const variance =\n+    returns.reduce((total, value) => total + (value - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;\n+\n+  return Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100;\n+}\n+\n+function relativeStrengthIndex(closes: number[], period: number) {\n+  if (closes.length <= period) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  let gains = 0;\n+  let losses = 0;\n+  const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));\n+\n+  for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {\n+    const change = window[index] - window[index - 1];\n+\n+    if (change > 0) {\n+      gains += change;\n+    } else {\n+      losses += Math.abs(change);\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  if (losses === 0) {\n+    return 100;\n+  }\n+\n+  const averageGain = gains / period;\n+  const averageLoss = losses / period;\n+  const relativeStrength = averageGain / averageLoss;\n+\n+  return 100 - 100 / (1 + relativeStrength);\n+}\n+\n+function averageTrueRange(bars: PriceBar[], period: number) {\n+  if (bars.length <= period) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const window = bars.slice(-(period + 1));\n+  const trueRanges: number[] = [];\n+\n+  for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {\n+    const current = window[index];\n+    const previous = window[index - 1];\n+    const trueRange = Math.max(\n+      current.high - current.low,\n+      Math.abs(current.high - previous.close),\n+      Math.abs(current.low - previous.close)\n+    );\n+    trueRanges.push(trueRange);\n+  }\n+\n+  return average(trueRanges);\n+}\n+\n+function rangePosition(current: number, low: number, high: number) {\n+  if (high <= low) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return ((current - low) / (high - low)) * 100;\n+}\n+\n+function tradingDate(timestamp: number) {\n+  return new Date(timestamp * 1000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);\n+}\n+\n+function publishedTimestamp(date: string) {\n+  return fallbackCompanyName: string) {\n+  return meta.longName ?? meta.shortName ?? fallbackCompanyName;\n+}\n+\n+function convictionFromScore(score: number) {\n+  if (score >= 74) {\n+    return \"High\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (score >= 60) {\n+    return \"Medium\";\n+  }\n+\n+  return \"Low\";\n+}\n+\n+function weightedScore(parts: Array<[number, number]>) {\n+  const total = parts.reduce((sum,
