resolveDataDirectoryFromSample(samplePath)\n+    : DATA_DIRECTORY_CANDIDATES[0];\n+\n+  await mkdir(directory, { recursive: true });\n+\n+  return path.join(directory, GENERATED_FILE_NAME);\n+}\n+\n+async function readSampleDataset() {\n+  const samplePath = await findFirstReadableFile(sampleFileCandidates());\n+\n+  if (!samplePath) {\n+    throw new Error(\"Unable to locate sample-recommendations.json\");\n+  }\n+\n+  return readJsonFile<RecommendationDataset>(samplePath);\n+}\n+\n+async function readGeneratedDataset() {\n+  try {\n+    return await readJsonFile<RecommendationDataset>(await generatedFilePath());\n+  } catch {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+async function writeGeneratedDataset(dataset: RecommendationDataset) {\n+  await writeFile(await generatedFilePath(), JSON.stringify(dataset, null, 2), \"utf-8\");\n+}\n+\n+async function fetchChartPayload(yahooSymbol: string) {\n+  const encodedSymbol = encodeURIComponent(yahooSymbol);\n+  const urls = [\n+    `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`,\n+    `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`\n+  ];\n+\n+  for (const url of urls) {\n+    try {\n+      const response = await fetch(url, {\n+        cache: \"no-store\",\n+        headers: {\n+          Accept: \"application/json\",\n+          \"User-Agent\": \"Mozilla/5.0\"\n+        }\n+      });\n+\n+      if (!response.ok) {\n+        continue;\n+      }\n+\n+      return (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;\n+    } catch {\n+      continue;\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  return null;\n+}\n+\n+function barsFromPayload(payload: YahooChartResponse) {\n+  const result = payload.chart?.result?.[0];\n+  const quote = result?.indicators?.quote?.[0];\n+  const timestamps = result?.timestamp ?? [];\n+  const opens = quote?.open ?? [];\n+  const highs = quote?.high ?? [];\n+  const lows = quote?.low ?? [];\n+  const closes = quote?.close ?? [];\n+  const volumes = quote?.volume ?? [];\n+  const bars: PriceBar[] = [];\n+\n+  for (let index = 0; index < timestamps.length; index += 1) {\n+    const open = opens[index];\n+    const high = highs[index];\n+    const low = lows[index];\n+    const close = closes[index];\n+    const volume = volumes[index];\n+\n+    if (\n+      open === null ||\n+      high === null ||\n+      low === null ||\n+      close === null ||\n+      volume === null ||\n+      open === undefined ||\n+      high === undefined ||\n+      low === undefined ||\n+      close === undefined ||\n+      volume === undefined\n+    ) {\n+      continue;\n+    }\n+\n+    bars.push({\n+      date: tradingDate(timestamps[index]),\n+      timestamp: timestamps[index],\n+      open,\n+      high,\n+      low,\n+      close,\n+      volume\n+    });\n+  }\n+\n+  return {\n+    bars,\n+    meta: result?.meta ?? {}\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+async function fetchSeries(entry: UniverseEntry) {\n+  const payload = await fetchChartPayload(entry.yahooSymbol);\n+\n+  if (!payload) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const parsed = barsFromPayload(payload);\n+\n+  if (!parsed.bars.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const companyName = companyNameFromMeta(parsed.meta, entry.fallbackCompanyName);\n+\n+  return {\n+    ...entry,\n+    companyName,\n+    bars: parsed.bars,\n+    indexByDate: new Map(parsed.bars.map((bar, index) => [bar.date, index])),\n+    meta: parsed.meta\n+  } satisfies MarketSeries;\n+}\n+\n+function benchmarkReturn(benchmark: MarketSeries, date: string, lookback: number) {\n+  const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(date);\n+\n+  if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex < lookback) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return pctChange(\n+    benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,\n+    benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - lookback].close\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function snapshotMetrics(series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries, index: number): SnapshotMetrics | null {\n+  if (index < 200) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const bars = series.bars.slice(0, index + 1);\n+  const current = bars.at(-1);\n+\n+  if (!current) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const previous = bars.at(-2) ?? null;\n+  const closes = bars.map((bar) => bar.close);\n+  const highs = bars.map((bar) => bar.high);\n+  const lows = bars.map((bar) => bar.low);\n+  const volumes = bars.map((bar) => bar.volume);\n+  const sma20 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 20);\n+  const sma50 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 50);\n+  const sma200 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 200);\n+  const atr14 = averageTrueRange(bars, 14);\n+  const avgVolume20 = average(volumes.slice(-20));\n+  const fiftyTwoWeekHigh = Math.max(...highs.slice(-252));\n+  const fiftyTwoWeekLow = Math.min(...lows.slice(-252));\n+\n+  return {\n+    date: current.date,\n+    companyName: series.companyName,\n+    currentPrice: current.close,\n+    previousClose: previous?.close ?? null,\n+    sessionChangePct: previous ? pctChange(current.close, previous.close) : null,\n+    sma20,\n+    sma50,\n+    sma200,\n+    rsi14: relativeStrengthIndex(closes, 14),\n+    atr14,\n+    atrPct: atr14 ? pctChange(current.close + atr14, current.close) : null,\n+    return20: closes.length > 20 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 21]) : null,\n+    return60: closes.length > 60 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 61]) : null,\n+    return120: closes.length > 120 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 121]) : null,\n+    benchmarkReturn20: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 20),\n+    benchmarkReturn60: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 60),\n+    benchmarkReturn120: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 120),\n+    relativeStrength20: null,\n+    relativeStrength60: null,\n+    relativeStrength120: null,\n+    volatility20: annualizedVolatility(closes, 20),\n+    avgVolume20,\n+    volumeRatio: avgVolume20 ? current.volume / avgVolume20 : null,\n+    rangePosition: rangePosition(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),\n+    fiftyTwoWeekHigh,\n+    fiftyTwoWeekLow,\n+    distanceFromHighPct: pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),\n+    distanceFromLowPct: fiftyTwoWeekLow ? pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow) : null,\n+    benchmarkSessionChangePct: (() => {\n+      const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(current.date);\n+\n+      if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex === 0) {\n+        return null;\n+      }\n+\n+      return pctChange(\n+        benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,\n+        benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - 1].close\n+      );\n+    })(),\n+    currentVolume: current.volume\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function enrichRelativeStrength(metrics: SnapshotMetrics): SnapshotMetrics {\n+  return {\n+    ...metrics,\n+    relativeStrength20:\n+      metrics.return20 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn20 !== null\n+        ? metrics.return20 - metrics.benchmarkReturn20\n+        : null,\n+    relativeStrength60:\n+      metrics.return60 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn60 !== null\n+        ? metrics.return60 - metrics.benchmarkReturn60\n+        : null,\n+    relativeStrength120:\n+      metrics.return120 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn120 !== null\n+        ? metrics.return120 - metrics.benchmarkReturn120\n+        : null\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function normalizeRsi(rsi: number | null) {\n+  if (rsi === null) {\n+    return 0.5;\n+  }\n+\n+  return 1 - clamp(Math.abs(rsi - 56) / 24, 0, 1);\n+}\n+\n+function liquidityScore(liquidityTier: string) {\n+  return liquidityTier === \"Tier 1\" ? 1 : 0.7;\n+}\n+\n+function marketCapScore(marketCapBucket: string) {\n+  return marketCapBucket === \"Large Cap\" ? 1 : 0.75;\n+}\n+\n+function scoreForHorizon(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: UniverseEntry, horizon: HorizonId) {\n+  switch (horizon) {\n+    case \"swing\":\n+      return weightedScore([\n+        [0.24, scaleRange(metrics.return20, -8, 16)],\n+        [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength20, -6, 10)],\n+        [0.12, metrics.sma20 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma20 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.11, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.1, metrics.sma20 && metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma20 > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.1, normalizeRsi(metrics.rsi14)],\n+        [0.08, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.75, 1.8)],\n+        [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 50)]\n+      ]);\n+    case \"position\":\n+      return weightedScore([\n+        [0.23, scaleRange(metrics.return60, -12, 22)],\n+        [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength60, -8, 14)],\n+        [0.13, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.13, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.12, metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma200 && metrics.sma50 > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 30, 95)],\n+        [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 48)],\n+        [0.05, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.8, 1.6)]\n+      ]);\n+    case \"long_term\":\n+      return weightedScore([\n+        [0.25, scaleRange(metrics.return120, -15, 30)],\n+        [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength120, -10, 18)],\n+        [0.16, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],\n+        [0.12, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 28, 95)],\n+        [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.distanceFromHighPct, -35, 0)],\n+        [0.08, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 16, 45)],\n+        [0.07, marketCapScore(entry.marketCapBucket)],\n+        [0.05, liquidityScore(entry.liquidityTier)]\n+      ]);\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+function projectedPct(score: number, [minPct, maxPct]: [number, number]) {\n+  const normalizedScore = scaleRange(score, 45, 85);\n+  return minPct + (maxPct - minPct) * normalizedScore;\n+}\n+\n+function buildTradePlan(\n+  metrics: SnapshotMetrics,\n+  entry: UniverseEntry,\n+  horizon: HorizonId\n+): PlanTemplate {\n+  const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];\n+  const score = scoreForHorizon(metrics, entry, horizon);\n+  const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);\n+  const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);\n+  const targetPct = Math.max(\n+    projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),\n+    atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple\n+  );\n+  const stopPct = Math.max(\n+    projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),\n+    atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple\n+  );\n+  const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));\n+  const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));\n+  const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;\n+  const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));\n+  const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);\n+  const strengthLabel =\n+    horizon === \"swing\"\n+      ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength20)\n+      : horizon === \"position\"\n+        ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength60)\n+        : percentText(metrics.relativeStrength120);\n+  const returnLabel =\n+    horizon === \"swing\"\n+      ? percentText(metrics.return20)\n+      : horizon === \"position\"\n+        ? percentText(metrics.return60)\n+        : percentText(metrics.return120);\n+  const summary =\n+    horizon === \"swing\"\n+      ? `${metrics.companyName} is holding a constructive short-term trend with ${returnLabel} recent momentum and ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus Nifty 50.`\n+      : horizon === \"position\"\n+        ? `${metrics.companyName} ranks well on the medium-term trend stack, combining ${returnLabel} price follow-through with ${strengthLabel} benchmark outperformance.`\n+        : `${metrics.companyName} remains a longer-duration accumulation candidate with ${returnLabel} multi-month momentum and ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus the index.`;\n+\n+  const drivers = [\n+    `${HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label} score ${signalScoreText(score)} driven by ${returnLabel} price momentum.`,\n+    `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${strengthLabel}.`,\n+    `RSI (14) is ${metrics.rsi14 ? roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : \"n/a\"} with volume at ${ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average.`,\n+    `The stock sits ${percentText(metrics.distanceFromHighPct)} from the 52-week high and ${metrics.rangePosition ? roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1) : \"n/a\"}% through its 52-week range.`\n+  ];\n+\n+  const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [\n+    { name: \"Model score\", value: signalScoreText(score) },\n+    {\n+      name: horizon === \"swing\" ? \"20D momentum\" : horizon === \"position\" ? \"60D momentum\" : \"120D momentum\",\n+      value: returnLabel\n+    },\n+    {\n+      name: horizon === \"swing\" ? \"20D relative strength\" : horizon === \"position\" ? \"60D relative strength\" : \"120D relative strength\",\n+      value: strengthLabel\n+    },\n+    {\n+      name: \"Price vs 50DMA\",\n+      value: metrics.sma50 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma50)) : \"n/a\"\n+    }\n+  ];\n+\n+  const structuralSignals: Signal[] = [\n+    { name: \"Market cap bucket\", value: entry.marketCapBucket },\n+    { name: \"Liquidity tier\", value: entry.liquidityTier },\n+    { name: \"20D volatility\", value: percentText(metrics.volatility20) },\n+    { name: \"52W range position\", value: metrics.rangePosition ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\" }\n+  ];\n+\n+  const marketContext = [\n+    `Latest session change: ${percentText(metrics.sessionChangePct)} while Nifty 50 moved ${percentText(metrics.benchmarkSessionChangePct)}.`,\n+    `Current price ${priceText(metrics.currentPrice)} versus 52-week range ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekLow)} to ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekHigh)}.`,\n+    `Current volume ${compactNumber(metrics.currentVolume)} versus 20-day average ${compactNumber(metrics.avgVolume20)}.`\n+  ];\n+\n+  return {\n+    score,\n+    conviction,\n+    entryPrice,\n+    targetPrice,\n+    stopLoss,\n+    expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(expectedReturnPct),\n+    riskReward,\n+    summary,\n+    drivers,\n+    technicalSignals,\n+    fundamentalSignals: structuralSignals,\n+    newsContext: marketContext\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function applyRanking(recommendations: StockAnalysis[]) {\n+  const ranked = recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({\n+    ...recommendation,\n+    profiles: {\n+      swing: { ...recommendation.profiles.swing },\n+      position: { ...recommendation.profiles.position },\n+      long_term: { ...recommendation.profiles.long_term }\n+    }\n+  }));\n+\n+  for (const horizon of Object.keys(HORIZON_SETTINGS) as HorizonId[]) {\n+    const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];\n+    const ordered = [...ranked].sort(\n+      (left, right) =>\n+        (right.profiles[horizon].score ?? right.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct) -\n+        (left.profiles[horizon].score ?? left.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct)\n+    );\n+    const thresholdCount = ordered.filter(\n+      (stock, index) =>\n+        index < settings.recommendationCount &&\n+        (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore\n+    ).length;\n+    const forcedCount = Math.min(settings.minimumRecommendationCount, ordered.length);\n+\n+    ordered.forEach((stock, index) => {\n+      stock.profiles[horizon].rank = index + 1;\n+      stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended =\n+        (index < settings.recommendationCount &&\n+          (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore) ||\n+        (thresholdCount < forcedCount && index < forcedCount);\n+    });\n+  }\n+\n+  return ranked;\n+}\n+\n+function buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(\n+  date: string,\n+  liveSeries: MarketSeries[],\n+  benchmark: MarketSeries\n+) {\n+  const recommendations = liveSeries\n+    .map((series) => {\n+      const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);\n+\n+      if (index === undefined) {\n+        return null;\n+      }\n+\n+      const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);\n+\n+      if (!metrics) {\n+        return null;\n+      }\n+\n+      const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);\n+\n+      return {\n+        symbol: series.symbol,\n+        companyName: series.companyName,\n+        sector: series.sector,\n+        marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,\n+        liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,\n+        currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),\n+        profiles: {\n+          swing: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, \"swing\"),\n+          position: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, \"position\"),\n+          long_term: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, \"long_term\")\n+        }\n+      } satisfies StockAnalysis;\n+    })\n+    .filter((recommendation): recommendation is StockAnalysis => recommendation !== null);\n+\n+  return applyRanking(recommendations);\n+}\n+\n+function resolveOutcomeResult(\n+  openPrice: number,\n+  targetPrice: number,\n+  stopLoss: number,\n+  targetHit: boolean,\n+  stopHit: boolean\n+): OutcomeResult {\n+  if (targetHit && stopHit) {\n+    return Math.abs(openPrice - stopLoss) <= Math.abs(targetPrice - openPrice)\n+      ? \"stop_loss_hit\"\n+      : \"target_hit\";\n+  }\n+\n+  return targetHit ? \"target_hit\" : \"stop_loss_hit\";\n+}\n+\n+function evaluateOutcome(\n+  series: MarketSeries,\n+  batchDate: string,\n+  horizon: HorizonId,\n+  entryPrice: number,\n+  targetPrice: number,\n+  stopLoss: number\n+): RecommendationOutcome {\n+  const batchIndex = series.indexByDate.get(batchDate);\n+\n+  if (batchIndex === undefined) {\n+    return {\n+      result: \"open\",\n+      evaluatedOn: batchDate,\n+      holdingDays: 0,\n+      returnPct: 0,\n+      notes: \"The recommendation date could not be mapped to live price history.\"\n+    };\n+  }\n+\n+  const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];\n+  const lastIndex = Math.min(series.bars.length - 1, batchIndex + settings.maxHoldDays);\n+\n+  for (let index = batchIndex + 1; index <= lastIndex; index += 1) {\n+    const bar = series.bars[index];\n+    const targetHit = bar.high >= targetPrice;\n+    const stopHit = bar.low <= stopLoss;\n+\n+    if (!targetHit && !stopHit) {\n+      continue;\n+    }\n+\n+    const result = resolveOutcomeResult(bar.open, targetPrice, stopLoss, targetHit, stopHit);\n+    const exitPrice = result === \"target_hit\" ? targetPrice : stopLoss;\n+\n+    return {\n+      result,\n+      evaluatedOn: bar.date,\n+      holdingDays: index - batchIndex,\n+      returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(exitPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0),\n+      notes:\n+        result === \"target_hit\"\n+          ? `Target was reached on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`\n+          : `Stop-loss was triggered on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`\n+    };\n+  }\n+\n+  const finalBar = series.bars[lastIndex];\n+\n+  return {\n+    result: \"open\",\n+    evaluatedOn: finalBar.date,\n+    holdingDays: lastIndex - batchIndex,\n+    returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(finalBar.close, entryPrice) ?? 0),\n+    notes: `The trade remains open after ${lastIndex - batchIndex} trading day(s); latest close is ${priceText(finalBar.close)}.`\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function toHistoricalRecommendation(\n+  recommendation: StockAnalysis,\n+  batchDate: string,\n+  seriesBySymbol: Map<string, MarketSeries>\n+): HistoricalStockRecommendation {\n+  const series = seriesBySymbol.get(recommendation.symbol);\n+\n+  if (!series) {\n+    throw new Error(`Missing series for ${recommendation.symbol}`);\n+  }\n+\n+  const buildHistoricalPlan = (horizon: HorizonId): HistoricalRecommendationPlan => {\n+    const livePlan = recommendation.profiles[horizon];\n+\n+    return {\n+      score: livePlan.score,\n+      rank: livePlan.rank,\n+      isRecommended: livePlan.isRecommended,\n+      conviction: livePlan.conviction,\n+      entryPrice: livePlan.entryPrice,\n+      targetPrice: livePlan.targetPrice,\n+      stopLoss: livePlan.stopLoss,\n+      summary: livePlan.summary,\n+      outcome: evaluateOutcome(\n+        series,\n+        batchDate,\n+        horizon,\n+        livePlan.entryPrice,\n+        livePlan.targetPrice,\n+        livePlan.stopLoss\n+      )\n+    };\n+  };\n+\n+  return {\n+    symbol: recommendation.symbol,\n+    companyName: recommendation.companyName,\n+    sector: recommendation.sector,\n+    profiles: {\n+      swing: buildHistoricalPlan(\"swing\"),\n+      position: buildHistoricalPlan(\"position\"),\n+      long_term: buildHistoricalPlan(\"long_term\")\n+    }\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function historicalBatchDates(benchmark: MarketSeries) {\n+  return benchmark.bars\n+    .slice(-(HISTORY_BATCH_COUNT + 1), -1)\n+    .map((bar) => bar.date)\n+    .filter((date) => date !== benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date);\n+}\n+\n+function buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries: MarketSeries[], benchmark: MarketSeries) {\n+  const seriesBySymbol = new Map(liveSeries.map((series) => [series.symbol, series]));\n+\n+  return historicalBatchDates(benchmark).map((date) => {\n+    const recommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(date, liveSeries, benchmark).map(\n+      (recommendation) => toHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, date, seriesBySymbol)\n+    );\n+\n+    return {\n+      batchDate: date,\n+      publishedAt: publishedTimestamp(date),\n+      recommendations\n+    } satisfies HistoricalBatch;\n+  });\n+}\n+\n+function liveDataSourceInfo(detail: string, analyzedSymbols: number): DataSourceInfo {\n+  return {\n+    mode: \"live\",\n+    provider: \"Yahoo Finance chart data\",\n+    asOf: new Date().toISOString(),\n+    detail,\n+    analyzedSymbols\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function fallbackDataSourceInfo(\n+  mode: \"cached\" | \"sample\",\n+  detail: string,\n+  analyzedSymbols: number\n+): DataSourceInfo {\n+  return {\n+    mode,\n+    provider: mode === \"cached\" ? \"Local generated snapshot\" : \"Sample fallback dataset\",\n+    asOf: new Date().toISOString(),\n+    detail,\n+    analyzedSymbols\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+async function buildLiveDataset() {\n+  const [benchmark, ...liveSeries] = (\n+    await Promise.all([fetchSeries(BENCHMARK), ...MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry))])\n+  ).filter((series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null);\n+\n+  if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const latestDate = benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date;\n+\n+  if (!latestDate) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(latestDate, liveSeries, benchmark);\n+\n+  if (!currentRecommendations.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const dataset: RecommendationDataset = {\n+    market: \"Indian Equities\",\n+    exchange: \"NSE\",\n+    universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,\n+    dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(\n+      `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,\n+      liveSeries.length\n+    ),\n+    profiles: buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark)\n+  };\n+\n+  await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);\n+\n+  return dataset;\n+}\n+\n+function annotateFallbackDataset(\n+  dataset: dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.length)\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {\n+  const liveDataset = await buildLiveDataset();\n+\n+ readSampleDataset(),\n+    \"sample\",\n+    \"Live market requests failed and no cached snapshot was available, so the demo dataset is being shown.\"\n+  );\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

306. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
307.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
308.     return 0.5;
309.   }
310. 
311.   if (max === min) {
312.     return 0.5;
313.   }
314. 
315.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
316. }
317. 
318. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
319.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
320. }
321. 
322. function percentText(value: number | null) {
323.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
324.     return "n/a";
325.   }
326. 
327.   const prefix = value > 0 ? "+" : "";
328.   return `${prefix}${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
329. }
330. 
331. function ratioText(value: number | null) {
332.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
333.     return "n/a";
334.   }
335. 
336.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}x`;
337. }
338. 
339. function priceText(value: number | null) {
340.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
341.     return "n/a";
342.   }
343. 
344.   return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;
345. }
346. 
347. function compactNumber(value: number | null) {
348.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
349.     return "n/a";
350.   }
351. 
352.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
353.     notation: "compact",
354.     maximumFractionDigits: 1
355.   }).format(value);
356. }
357. 
358. function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {
359.   if (values.length < period) {
360.     return null;
361.   }
362. 
363.   return average(values.slice(-period));
364. }
365. 
366. function annualizedVolatility(closes: number[], period: number) {
367.   if (closes.length < period + 1) {
368.     return null;
369.   }
370. 
371.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
372.   const returns = window.slice(1).map((close, index) => (close - window[index]) / window[index]);
373.   const mean = average(returns);
374. 
375.   if (mean === null) {
376.     return null;
377.   }
378. 
379.   const variance =
380.     returns.reduce((total, value) => total + (value - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;
381. 
382.   return Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100;
383. }
384. 
385. function relativeStrengthIndex(closes: number[], period: number) {
386.   if (closes.length <= period) {
387.     return null;
388.   }
389. 
390.   let gains = 0;
391.   let losses = 0;
392.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
393. 
394.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
395.     const change = window[index] - window[index - 1];
396. 
397.     if (change > 0) {
398.       gains += change;
399.     } else {
400.       losses += Math.abs(change);
401.     }
402.   }
403. 
404.   if (losses === 0) {
405.     return 100;
406.   }
407. 
408.   const averageGain = gains / period;
409.   const averageLoss = losses / period;
410.   const relativeStrength = averageGain / averageLoss;
411. 
412.   return 100 - 100 / (1 + relativeStrength);
413. }
414. 
415. function averageTrueRange(bars: PriceBar[], period: number) {
416.   if (bars.length <= period) {
417.     return null;
418.   }
419. 
420.   const window = bars.slice(-(period + 1));
421.   const trueRanges: number[] = [];
422. 
423.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
424.     const current = window[index];
425.     const previous = window[index - 1];
426.     const trueRange = Math.max(
427.       current.high - current.low,
428.       Math.abs(current.high - previous.close),
429.       Math.abs(current.low - previous.close)
430.     );
431.     trueRanges.push(trueRange);
432.   }
433. 
434.   return average(trueRanges);
435. }
436. 
437. function rangePosition(current: number, low: number, high: number) {
438.   if (high <= low) {
439.     return null;
440.   }
441. 
442.   return ((current - low) / (high - low)) * 100;
443. }
444.

446.   return new Date(timestamp * 1000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
447. }
448.

451. }

473. function weightedScore(parts: Array<[number, number]>) {
474.   const total = parts.reduce((sum, [weight, score]) => sum + weight * score, 0);

476. }

537. async function fetchChartPayload(yahooSymbol: string) {
538.   const encodedSymbol = encodeURIComponent(yahooSymbol);
539.   const urls = [
540.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`,
541.     `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`
542.   ];
543. 
544.   for (const url of urls) {
545.     try {
546.       const response = await fetch(url, {
547.         cache: "no-store",
548.         headers: {
549.           Accept: "application/json",
550.           "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
551.         }
552.       });
553. 
554.       if (!response.ok) {
555.         continue;
556.       }
557. 
558.       return (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;
559.     } catch {
560.       continue;
561.     }
562.   }
563. 
564.   return null;
565. }
566. 
567. function barsFromPayload(payload: YahooChartResponse) {
568.   const result = payload.chart?.result?.[0];
569.   const quote = result?.indicators?.quote?.[0];
570.   const timestamps = result?.timestamp ?? [];
571.   const opens = quote?.open ?? [];
572.   const highs = quote?.high ?? [];
573.   const lows = quote?.low ?? [];
574.   const closes = quote?.close ?? [];
575.   const volumes = quote?.volume ?? [];
576.   const bars: PriceBar[] = [];
577. 
578.   for (let index = 0; index < timestamps.length; index += 1) {
579.     const open = opens[index];
580.     const high = highs[index];
581.     const low = lows[index];
582.     const close = closes[index];
583.     const volume = volumes[index];
584. 
585.     if (
586.       open === null ||
587.       high === null ||
588.       low === null ||
589.       close === null ||
590.       volume === null ||
591.       open === undefined ||
592.       high === undefined ||
593.       low === undefined ||
594.       close === undefined ||
595.       volume === undefined
596.     ) {
597.       continue;
598.     }
599. 
600.     bars.push({
601.       date: tradingDate(timestamps[index]),
602.       timestamp: timestamps[index],
603.       open,
604.       high,
605.       low,
606.       close,
607.       volume
608.     });
609.   }
610. 
611.   return {
612.     bars,
613.     meta: result?.meta ?? {}
614.   };
615. }
616.

640. 
641. function benchmarkReturn(benchmark: MarketSeries, date: string, lookback: number) {
642.   const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(date);
643. 
644.   if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex < lookback) {
645.     return null;
646.   }
647. 
648.   return pctChange(
649.     benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,
650.     benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - lookback].close
651.   );
652. }
653. 
654. function snapshotMetrics(series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries, index: number): SnapshotMetrics | null {
655.   if (index < 200) {
656.     return null;
657.   }
658. 
659.   const bars = series.bars.slice(0, index + 1);
660.   const current = bars.at(-1);
661. 
662.   if (!current) {
663.     return null;
664.   }
665. 
666.   const previous = bars.at(-2) ?? null;
667.   const closes = bars.map((bar) => bar.close);
668.   const highs = bars.map((bar) => bar.high);
669.   const lows = bars.map((bar) => bar.low);
670.   const volumes = bars.map((bar) => bar.volume);
671.   const sma20 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 20);
672.   const sma50 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 50);
673.   const sma200 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 200);
674.   const atr14 = averageTrueRange(bars, 14);
675.   const avgVolume20 = average(volumes.slice(-20));
676.   const fiftyTwoWeekHigh = Math.max(...highs.slice(-252));
677.   const fiftyTwoWeekLow = Math.min(...lows.slice(-252));
678. 
679.   return {
680.     date: current.date,
681.     companyName: series.companyName,
682.     currentPrice: current.close,
683.     previousClose: previous?.close ?? null,
684.     sessionChangePct: previous ? pctChange(current.close, previous.close) : null,
685.     sma20,
686.     sma50,
687.     sma200,
688.     rsi14: relativeStrengthIndex(closes, 14),
689.     atr14,
690.     atrPct: atr14 ? pctChange(current.close + atr14, current.close) : null,
691.     return20: closes.length > 20 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 21]) : null,
692.     return60: closes.length > 60 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 61]) : null,
693.     return120: closes.length > 120 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 121]) : null,
694.     benchmarkReturn20: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 20),
695.     benchmarkReturn60: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 60),
696.     benchmarkReturn120: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 120),
697.     relativeStrength20: null,
698.     relativeStrength60: null,
699.     relativeStrength120: null,
700.     volatility20: annualizedVolatility(closes, 20),
701.     avgVolume20,
702.     volumeRatio: avgVolume20 ? current.volume / avgVolume20 : null,
703.     rangePosition: rangePosition(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),
704.     fiftyTwoWeekHigh,
705.     fiftyTwoWeekLow,
706.     distanceFromHighPct: pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),
707.     distanceFromLowPct: fiftyTwoWeekLow ? pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow) : null,
708.     benchmarkSessionChangePct: (() => {
709.       const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(current.date);
710. 
711.       if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex === 0) {
712.         return null;
713.       }
714. 
715.       return pctChange(
716.         benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,
717.         benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - 1].close
718.       );
719.     })(),
720.     currentVolume: current.volume
721.   };
722. }
723. 
724. function enrichRelativeStrength(metrics: SnapshotMetrics): SnapshotMetrics {
725.   return {
726.     ...metrics,
727.     relativeStrength20:
728.       metrics.return20 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn20 !== null
729.         ? metrics.return20 - metrics.benchmarkReturn20
730.         : null,
731.     relativeStrength60:
732.       metrics.return60 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn60 !== null
733.         ? metrics.return60 - metrics.benchmarkReturn60
734.         : null,
735.     relativeStrength120:
736.       metrics.return120 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn120 !== null
737.         ? metrics.return120 - metrics.benchmarkReturn120
738.         : null
739.   };
740. }
741. 
742. function normalizeRsi(rsi: number | null) {
743.   if (rsi === null) {
744.     return 0.5;
745.   }
746. 
747.   return 1 - clamp(Math.abs(rsi - 56) / 24, 0, 1);
748. }
749. 
750. function liquidityScore(liquidityTier: string) {
751.   return liquidityTier === "Tier 1" ? 1 : 0.7;
752. }
753. 
754. function marketCapScore(marketCapBucket: string) {
755.   return marketCapBucket === "Large Cap" ? 1 : 0.75;
756. }
757. 
758. function scoreForHorizon(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: UniverseEntry, horizon: HorizonId) {
759.   switch (horizon) {
760.     case "swing":
761.       return weightedScore([
762.         [0.24, scaleRange(metrics.return20, -8, 16)],
763.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength20, -6, 10)],
764.         [0.12, metrics.sma20 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma20 ? 1 : 0],
765.         [0.11, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
766.         [0.1, metrics.sma20 && metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma20 > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
767.         [0.1, normalizeRsi(metrics.rsi14)],
768.         [0.08, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.75, 1.8)],
769.         [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 50)]
770.       ]);
771.     case "position":
772.       return weightedScore([
773.         [0.23, scaleRange(metrics.return60, -12, 22)],
774.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength60, -8, 14)],
775.         [0.13, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
776.         [0.13, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
777.         [0.12, metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma200 && metrics.sma50 > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
778.         [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 30, 95)],
779.         [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 48)],
780.         [0.05, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.8, 1.6)]
781.       ]);
782.     case "long_term":
783.       return weightedScore([
784.         [0.25, scaleRange(metrics.return120, -15, 30)],
785.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength120, -10, 18)],
786.         [0.16, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
787.         [0.12, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 28, 95)],
788.         [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.distanceFromHighPct, -35, 0)],
789.         [0.08, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 16, 45)],
790.         [0.07, marketCapScore(entry.marketCapBucket)],
791.         [0.05, liquidityScore(entry.liquidityTier)]
792.       ]);
793.   }
794. }
795. 
796. function projectedPct(score: number, [minPct, maxPct]: [number, number]) {
797.   const normalizedScore = scaleRange(score, 45, 85);
798.   return minPct + (maxPct - minPct) * normalizedScore;
799. }
800. 
801. function buildTradePlan(
802.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
803.   entry: UniverseEntry,
804.   horizon: HorizonId
805. ): PlanTemplate {
806.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
807.   const score = scoreForHorizon(metrics, entry, horizon);
808.   const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);
809.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);
810.   const targetPct = Math.max(
811.     projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),
812.     atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple
813.   );
814.   const stopPct = Math.max(
815.     projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),
816.     atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple
817.   );
818.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
819.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));
820.   const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;
821.   const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));
822.   const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);
823.   const strengthLabel =
824.     horizon === "swing"
825.       ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength20)
826.       : horizon === "position"
827.         ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength60)
828.         : percentText(metrics.relativeStrength120);
829.   const returnLabel =
830.     horizon === "swing"
831.       ? percentText(metrics.return20)
832.       : horizon === "position"
833.         ? percentText(metrics.return60)
834.         : percentText(metrics.return120);
835.   const summary =
836.     horizon === "swing"
837.       ? `${metrics.companyName} is holding a constructive short-term trend with ${returnLabel} recent momentum and ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus Nifty 50.`
838.       : horizon === "position"
839.         ? `${metrics.companyName} ranks well on the medium-term trend stack, combining ${returnLabel} price follow-through with ${strengthLabel} benchmark outperformance.`
840.         : `${metrics.companyName} remains a longer-duration accumulation candidate with ${returnLabel} multi-month momentum and ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus the index.`;
841. 
842.   const drivers = [
843.     `${HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label} score ${signalScoreText(score)} driven by ${returnLabel} price momentum.`,
844.     `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${strengthLabel}.`,
845.     `RSI (14) is ${metrics.rsi14 ? roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : "n/a"} with volume at ${ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average.`,
846.     `The stock sits ${percentText(metrics.distanceFromHighPct)} from the 52-week high and ${metrics.rangePosition ? roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1) : "n/a"}% through its 52-week range.`
847.   ];
848. 
849.   const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [
850.     { name: "Model score", value: signalScoreText(score) },
851.     {
852.       name: horizon === "swing" ? "20D momentum" : horizon === "position" ? "60D momentum" : "120D momentum",
853.       value: returnLabel
854.     },
855.     {
856.       name: horizon === "swing" ? "20D relative strength" : horizon === "position" ? "60D relative strength" : "120D relative strength",
857.       value: strengthLabel
858.     },
859.     {
860.       name: "Price vs 50DMA",
861.       value: metrics.sma50 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma50)) : "n/a"
862.     }
863.   ];
864. 
865.   const structuralSignals: Signal[] = [
866.     { name: "Market cap bucket", value: entry.marketCapBucket },
867.     { name: "Liquidity tier", value: entry.liquidityTier },
868.     { name: "20D volatility", value: percentText(metrics.volatility20) },
869.     { name: "52W range position", value: metrics.rangePosition ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a" }
870.   ];
871. 
872.   const marketContext = [
873.     `Latest session change: ${percentText(metrics.sessionChangePct)} while Nifty 50 moved ${percentText(metrics.benchmarkSessionChangePct)}.`,
874.     `Current price ${priceText(metrics.currentPrice)} versus 52-week range ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekLow)} to ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekHigh)}.`,
875.     `Current volume ${compactNumber(metrics.currentVolume)} versus 20-day average ${compactNumber(metrics.avgVolume20)}.`
876.   ];
877. 
878.   return {
879.     score,
880.     conviction,
881.     entryPrice,
882.     targetPrice,
883.     stopLoss,
884.     expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(expectedReturnPct),
885.     riskReward,
886.     summary,
887.     drivers,
888.     technicalSignals,
889.     fundamentalSignals: structuralSignals,
890.     newsContext: marketContext
891.   };
892. }
893. 
894. function applyRanking(recommendations: StockAnalysis[]) {
895.   const ranked = recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
896.     ...recommendation,
897.     profiles: {
898.       swing: { ...recommendation.profiles.swing },
899.       position: { ...recommendation.profiles.position },
900.       long_term: { ...recommendation.profiles.long_term }
901.     }
902.   }));
903. 
904.   for (const horizon of Object.keys(HORIZON_SETTINGS) as HorizonId[]) {
905.     const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
906.     const ordered = [...ranked].sort(
907.       (left, right) =>
908.         (right.profiles[horizon].score ?? right.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct) -
909.         (left.profiles[horizon].score ?? left.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct)
910.     );
911.     const thresholdCount = ordered.filter(
912.       (stock, index) =>
913.         index < settings.recommendationCount &&
914.         (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore
915.     ).length;
916.     const forcedCount = Math.min(settings.minimumRecommendationCount, ordered.length);
917. 
918.     ordered.forEach((stock, index) => {
919.       stock.profiles[horizon].rank = index + 1;
920.       stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended =
921.         (index < settings.recommendationCount &&
922.           (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore) ||
923.         (thresholdCount < forcedCount && index < forcedCount);
924.     });
925.   }
926. 
927.   return ranked;
928. }
929. 
930. function buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(
931.   date: string,
932.   liveSeries: MarketSeries[],
933.   benchmark: MarketSeries
934. ) {
935.   const recommendations = liveSeries
936.     .map((series) => {
937.       const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
938. 
939.       if (index === undefined) {
940.         return null;
941.       }
942. 
943.       const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
944. 
945.       if (!metrics) {
946.         return null;
947.       }
948. 
949.       const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
950. 
951.       return {
952.         symbol: series.symbol,
953.         companyName: series.companyName,
954.         sector: series.sector,
955.         marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
956.         liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
957.         currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
958.         profiles: {
959.           swing: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "swing"),
960.           position: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "position"),
961.           long_term: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "long_term")
962.         }
963.       } satisfies StockAnalysis;
964.     })
965.     .filter((recommendation): recommendation is StockAnalysis => recommendation !== null);
966. 
967.   return applyRanking(recommendations);
968. }
969. 
970. function resolveOutcomeResult(
971.   openPrice: number,
972.   targetPrice: number,
973.   stopLoss: number,
974.   targetHit: boolean,
975.   stopHit: boolean
976. ): OutcomeResult {
977.   if (targetHit && stopHit) {
978.     return Math.abs(openPrice - stopLoss) <= Math.abs(targetPrice - openPrice)
979.       ? "stop_loss_hit"
980.       : "target_hit";
```

</details>

```
980.       : "target_hit";
981.   }
982. 
983.   return targetHit ? "target_hit" : "stop_loss_hit";
984. }
985. 
986. function evaluateOutcome(
987.   series: MarketSeries,
988.   batchDate: string,
989.   horizon: HorizonId,
990.   entryPrice: number,
991.   targetPrice: number,
992.   stopLoss: number
993. ): RecommendationOutcome {
994.   const batchIndex = series.indexByDate.get(batchDate);
995. 
996.   if (batchIndex === undefined) {
997.     return {
998.       result: "open",
999.       evaluatedOn: batchDate,
1000.       holdingDays: 0,
1001.       returnPct: 0,
1002.       notes: "The recommendation date could not be mapped to live price history."
1003.     };
1004.   }
1005. 
1006.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1007.   const lastIndex = Math.min(series.bars.length - 1, batchIndex + settings.maxHoldDays);
1008. 
1009.   for (let index = batchIndex + 1; index <= lastIndex; index += 1) {
1010.     const bar = series.bars[index];
1011.     const targetHit = bar.high >= targetPrice;
1012.     const stopHit = bar.low <= stopLoss;
1013. 
1014.     if (!targetHit && !stopHit) {
1015.       continue;
1016.     }
1017. 
1018.     const result = resolveOutcomeResult(bar.open, targetPrice, stopLoss, targetHit, stopHit);
1019.     const exitPrice = result === "target_hit" ? targetPrice : stopLoss;
1020. 
1021.     return {
1022.       result,
1023.       evaluatedOn: bar.date,
1024.       holdingDays: index - batchIndex,
1025.       returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(exitPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0),
1026.       notes:
1027.         result === "target_hit"
1028.           ? `Target was reached on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`
1029.           : `Stop-loss was triggered on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`
1030.     };
1031.   }
1032. 
1033.   const finalBar = series.bars[lastIndex];
1034. 
1035.   return {
1036.     result: "open",
1037.     evaluatedOn: finalBar.date,
1038.     holdingDays: lastIndex - batchIndex,
1039.     returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(finalBar.close, entryPrice) ?? 0),
1040.     notes: `The trade remains open after ${lastIndex - batchIndex} trading day(s); latest close is ${priceText(finalBar.close)}.`
1041.   };
1042. }
1043. 
1044. function toHistoricalRecommendation(
1045.   recommendation: StockAnalysis,
1046.   batchDate: string,
1047.   seriesBySymbol: Map<string, MarketSeries>
1048. ): HistoricalStockRecommendation {
1049.   const series = seriesBySymbol.get(recommendation.symbol);
1050. 
1051.   if (!series) {
1052.     throw new Error(`Missing series for ${recommendation.symbol}`);
1053.   }
1054. 
1055.   const buildHistoricalPlan = (horizon: HorizonId): HistoricalRecommendationPlan => {
1056.     const livePlan = recommendation.profiles[horizon];
1057. 
1058.     return {
1059.       score: livePlan.score,
1060.       rank: livePlan.rank,
1061.       isRecommended: livePlan.isRecommended,
1062.       conviction: livePlan.conviction,
1063.       entryPrice: livePlan.entryPrice,
1064.       targetPrice: livePlan.targetPrice,
1065.       stopLoss: livePlan.stopLoss,
1066.       summary: livePlan.summary,
1067.       outcome: evaluateOutcome(
1068.         series,
1069.         batchDate,
1070.         horizon,
1071.         livePlan.entryPrice,
1072.         livePlan.targetPrice,
1073.         livePlan.stopLoss
1074.       )
1075.     };
1076.   };
1077. 
1078.   return {
1079.     symbol: recommendation.symbol,
1080.     companyName: recommendation.companyName,
1081.     sector: recommendation.sector,
1082.     profiles: {
1083.       swing: buildHistoricalPlan("swing"),
1084.       position: buildHistoricalPlan("position"),
1085.       long_term: buildHistoricalPlan("long_term")
1086.     }
1087.   };
1088. }
1089. 
1090. function historicalBatchDates(benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1091.   return benchmark.bars
1092.     .slice(-(HISTORY_BATCH_COUNT + 1), -1)
1093.     .map((bar) => bar.date)
1094.     .filter((date) => date !== benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date);
1095. }
1096. 
1097. function buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries: MarketSeries[], benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1098.   const seriesBySymbol = new Map(liveSeries.map((series) => [series.symbol, series]));
1099. 
1100.   return historicalBatchDates(benchmark).map((date) => {
1101.     const recommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(date, liveSeries, benchmark).map(
1102.       (recommendation) => toHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, date, seriesBySymbol)
1103.     );
1104. 
1105.     return {
1106.       batchDate: date,
1107.       publishedAt: publishedTimestamp(date),
1108.       recommendations
1109.     } satisfies HistoricalBatch;
1110.   });
1111. }
1112. 
1113. function liveDataSourceInfo(detail: string, analyzedSymbols: number): DataSourceInfo {
1114.   return {
1115.     mode: "live",
1116.     provider: "Yahoo Finance chart data",
1117.     asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
1118.     detail,
1119.     analyzedSymbols
1120.   };
1121. }
1122. 
1123. function fallbackDataSourceInfo(
1124.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1125.   detail: string,
1126.   analyzedSymbols: number
1127. ): DataSourceInfo {
1128.   return {
1129.     mode,
1130.     provider: mode === "cached" ? "Local generated snapshot" : "Sample fallback dataset",
1131.     asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
1132.     detail,
1133.     analyzedSymbols
1134.   };
1135. }
1136. 
1137. async function buildLiveDataset() {
1138.   const [benchmark, ...liveSeries] = (
1139.     await Promise.all([fetchSeries(BENCHMARK), ...MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry))])
1140.   ).filter((series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null);
1141. 
1142.   if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
1143.     return null;
1144.   }
1145. 
1146.   const latestDate = benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date;
1147. 
1148.   if (!latestDate) {
1149.     return null;
1150.   }
1151. 
1152.   const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(latestDate, liveSeries, benchmark);
1153. 
1154.   if (!currentRecommendations.length) {
1155.     return null;
1156.   }
1157. 
1158.   const dataset: RecommendationDataset = {
1159.     market: "Indian Equities",
1160.     exchange: "NSE",
1161.     universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,
1162.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
1163.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,
1164.       liveSeries.length
1165.     ),
1166.     profiles: PROFILE_LIST,
1167.     currentBatch: {
1168.       batchDate: latestDate,
1169.       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1170.       recommendations: currentRecommendations
1171.     },
1172.     history: buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark)
1173.   };
1174. 
1175.   await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);
1176. 
1177.   return dataset;
1178. }
1179. 
1180. function annotateFallbackDataset(
1181.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
1182.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1183.   detail: string
1184. ): RecommendationDataset {
1185.   return {
1186.     ...dataset,
1187.     dataSource: fallbackDataSourceInfo(mode, detail, dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.length)
1188.   };
1189. }
1190. 
1191. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
1192.   const liveDataset = await buildLiveDataset();
1193. 
1194.   if (liveDataset) {
1195.     return liveDataset;
1196.   }
1197. 
1198.   const cachedDataset = await readGeneratedDataset();
1199. 
1200.   if (cachedDataset) {
1201.     return annotateFallbackDataset(
1202.       cachedDataset,
1203.       "cached",
1204.       `Using the last successful live snapshot from ${cachedDataset.currentBatch.generatedAt}.`
1205.     );
1206.   }
1207. 
1208.   return annotateFallbackDataset(
1209.     await readSampleDataset(),
1210.     "sample",
1211.     "Live market requests failed and no cached snapshot was available, so the demo dataset is being shown."
1212.   );
1213. }
1214.

<details>
<summary>58 lines</summary>

````
1. # Indian equities recommendation platform
2. 
3. This repository starts a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. The product is designed to feel institutional and explainable: each daily recommendation should show **why** it was made and expose a proposed **entry**, **target**, and **stop-loss** instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.
4. 
5. ## Chosen product scope
6. 
7. - **User:** analyst or serious retail investor
8. - **Market:** liquid NSE equities, initially a Nifty 500-derived universe
9. - **Delivery:** web dashboard
10. - **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution
11. - **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan
12. - **MVP horizons:** swing, position, and long-term
13. - **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists
14. 
15. ## Chosen stack
16. 
17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Initial repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## MVP user experience
37. 
38. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch.
39. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
40. 3. The detail view explains:
41.    - key factors behind the recommendation
42.    - recommended entry price
43.    - target price
44.    - stop-loss
45.    - risk-reward profile
46.    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes
47. 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, shows whether each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.
48. 
49. ## Windows quick start
50. 
51. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
52. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
53. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
54. 
55. ## Analytics test command
56. 
57. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
58.
````

</details>

1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - Workspace `C:\copilot` is empty; there is no existing application code to extend.
8. - No repository structure, app framework, data pipeline, or model stack is present yet.
9. - This plan assumes a greenfield implementation.
10. 
11. ## Proposed approach
12. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
13. 
14. 1. Product definition
15.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
16.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
17.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
18.    - Chosen MVP scope:
19.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
20.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
21.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
22.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
23.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
24.      - Supported horizons in the MVP: swing, position, and long-term; intraday deferred to a later high-frequency phase.
25. 
26. 2. Data foundation
27.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
28.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
29.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
30. 
31. 3. Research feature layer
32.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
33.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
34. 
35. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
36.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
37.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
38.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
39.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
40.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
41. 
42. 5. Evaluation and controls
43.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
44.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
45.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
46. 
47. 6. Explainability and delivery
48.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
49.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
50.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
51.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
52. 
53. 7. UI template recommendation
54.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
55.    - Chosen starting point: Next.js with TypeScript and shadcn/ui-style dashboard patterns for a clean analyst-oriented UI.
56.    - Layout suggestion:
57.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.
58.      - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.
59.      - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.
60.    - Stock detail page should include:
61.      - Recommendation summary card.
62.      - Horizon selector so the same stock can show swing, position, and long-term variants when available.
63.      - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.
64.      - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.
65.      - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.
66. 
67. ## Draft todo list
68. - `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.
69. - `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.
70. - `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.
71. - `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.
72. - `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.
73. - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.
74. - `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.
75. - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.
76. - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.
77. - `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.
78. - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.
79. 
80. ## Notes and considerations
81. - "Like BlackRock" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.
82. - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.
83. - Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.
84. - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.
85. - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.
86. - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.
87. - Revised scope assumption: the platform should be designed for multiple horizons, but daily-data MVP coverage should focus on swing, position, and long-term profiles first.
88. - Intraday recommendations require separate high-frequency data ingestion, tighter execution assumptions, and different backtesting logic, so they should be treated as a later phase rather than bundled into the first daily-data release.
89. - Chosen foundation stack:
90.   - Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript with a clean dashboard UI.
91.   - API and analytics services: FastAPI + Pydantic in Python.
92.   - Operational storage: PostgreSQL for users, recommendation batches, and audit history.
93.   - Research storage: Parquet or DuckDB for snapshots, factor data, and backtesting workloads.
94.   - Scheduling: market-calendar-aware daily batch jobs, starting with end-of-day runs.
95.
```

</details>

The product is designed to feel institutional and explainable: each daily recommendation should show **why** it was made and expose a proposed **entry**, **target**, and **stop-loss** instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.\n+This repository now includes a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. 1. "use client";
2. 
3. import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
4. import {
5.   buildDailyPerformance,
6.   buildPerformanceSummary,
7.   buildStockPerformanceHistory,
8.   outcomeClass,
9.   outcomeLabel
10. } from "@/lib/analytics";
11. import type {
12.   DailyPerformance,
13.   HorizonId,
14.   RecommendationDataset,
15.   RecommendationPlan,
16.   StockAnalysis,
17.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
18. } from "@/lib/types";
19. 
20. type DashboardProps = {
21.   data: RecommendationDataset;
22. };
23. 
24. function formatPrice(value: number) {
25.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
26.     style: "currency",
27.     currency: "INR",
28.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
29.     maximumFractionDigits: 2
30.   }).format(value);
31. }
32. 
33. function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {
34.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
35. }
36. 
37. function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
38.   if (value === null) {
39.     return "Pending";
40.   }
41. 
42.   return `${value.toFixed(2)}%`;
43. }
44. 
45. function planScore(plan: RecommendationPlan) {
46.   return plan.score ?? plan.expectedReturnPct;
47. }
48. 
49. function isRecommendedPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan | { isRecommended?: boolean }) {
50.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
51. }
52. 
53. export function Dashboard({ data }: DashboardProps) {
54.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>("swing");
55.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>("");
56. 
57.   const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {
58.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
59.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
60.     );
61.     const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));
62. 
63.     return recommended.length ? recommended : sorted.slice(0, 5);
64.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);
65. 
66.   useEffect(() => {
67.     if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {
68.       setSelectedSymbol("");
69.       return;
70.     }
71. 
72.     if (!visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {
73.       setSelectedSymbol(visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);
74.     }
75.   }, [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);
76. 
77.   const selectedStock = useMemo<StockAnalysis | undefined>(
78.     () => visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? visibleRecommendations[0],
79.     [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]
80.   );
81. 
82.   const selectedPlan = selectedStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
83.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo<DailyPerformance[]>(
84.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
85.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
86.   );
87.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
88.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
89.     [dailyPerformance]
90.   );
91.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
92.     () =>
93.       selectedStock
94.         ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, selectedStock.symbol, activeHorizon)
95.         : [],
96.     [activeHorizon, data.history, selectedStock]
97.   );
98. 
99.   return (
100.     <main className="shell">
101.       <div className="topbar">
102.         <div className="headline">
103.           <span className="eyebrow">Chosen UI foundation: Next.js dashboard</span>
104.           <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
105.           <p>
106.             This dashboard now analyzes a live NSE watchlist, scores each stock across multiple
107.             horizons, and turns that analysis into ranked recommendations with entry, target, and
108.             stop-loss levels.
109.           </p>
110.         </div>
111. 
112.         <section className="card run-card">
113.           <div className="metric-label">Latest batch</div>
114.           <div className="metric-value">
115.             {new Date(data.currentBatch.generatedAt).toLocaleString("en-IN")}
116.           </div>
117.           <p className="footnote">
118.             {data.exchange} · {data.universe} · batch date {data.currentBatch.batchDate}
119.           </p>
120.           {data.dataSource ? (
121.             <p className="footnote">
122.               Source: {data.dataSource.mode.toUpperCase()} · {data.dataSource.detail}
123.             </p>
124.           ) : null}
125.         </section>
126.       </div>
127. 
128.       <section className="meta-grid">
129.         <article className="card">
130.           <div className="metric-label">Coverage universe</div>
131.           <div className="metric-value">{data.universe}</div>
132.         </article>
133.         <article className="card">
134.           <div className="metric-label">Market</div>
135.           <div className="metric-value">{data.market}</div>
136.         </article>
137.         <article className="card">
138.           <div className="metric-label">Analyzed stocks</div>
139.           <div className="metric-value">{data.currentBatch.recommendations.length}</div>
140.         </article>
141.         <article className="card">
142.           <div className="metric-label">Active horizon</div>
143.           <div className="metric-value">
144.             {data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)?.label}
145.           </div>
146.         </article>
147.         <article className="card">
148.           <div className="metric-label">Latest completed success rate</div>
149.           <div className="metric-value">
150.             {formatPercent(performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? null)}
151.           </div>
152.         </article>
153.         <article className="card">
154.           <div className="metric-label">Average daily success rate</div>
155.           <div className="metric-value">{formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate)}</div>
156.         </article>
157.       </section>
158. 
159.       <div className="workspace">
160.         <div className="stack">
161.           <section className="card">
162.             <div className="section-title">
163.               <div>
164.                 <h2>Today&apos;s ranked ideas</h2>
165.                 <div className="section-subtitle">
166.                   Ranked from live price history, trend strength, relative performance, and
167.                   volatility.
168.                 </div>
169.               </div>
170. 
171.               <div className="pills">
172.                 {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
173.                   <button
174.                     key={profile.id}
175.                     className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
176.                     onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
177.                     type="button"
178.                   >
179.                     {profile.label} · {profile.window}
180.                   </button>
181.                 ))}
182.               </div>
183.             </div>
184. 
185.             <div className="table-wrap">
186.               <table className="stock-table">
187.                 <thead>
188.                   <tr>
189.                     <th>Symbol</th>
190.                     <th>Company</th>
191.                     <th>Current market price</th>
192.                     <th>Sector</th>
193.                     <th>Conviction</th>
194.                     <th>Entry</th>
195.                     <th>Target</th>
196.                     <th>Stop-loss</th>
197.                     <th>Expected return</th>
198.                     <th>R:R</th>
199.                   </tr>
200.                 </thead>
201.                 <tbody>
202.                   {visibleRecommendations.map((stock) => {
203.                     const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
204. 
205.                     return (
206.                       <tr
207.                         key={stock.symbol}
208.                         className={`stock-row${stock.symbol === selectedStock?.symbol ? " active" : ""}`}
209.                         onClick={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}
210.                       >

212.                           <div className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</div>
213.                         </td>
214.                         <td>{stock.companyName}</td>
215.                         <td>{formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)}</td>
216.                         <td>{stock.sector}</td>
217.                         <td>
218.                           <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>
219.                             {plan.conviction}
220.                           </span>

1. export type HorizonId = "swing" | "position" | "long_term";

1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. 
4. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
5. 
6. from .analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
7. from .schemas import (
8.     HorizonId,
9.     RecommendationDataset,
10.     RecommendationSummary,
11.     StockAnalysis,
12.     StockDetailResponse,
13. )
14. 
15. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
16. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
17. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
18. 
19. app = FastAPI(
20.     title="Recommendation API",
21.     version="0.1.0",
22.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
23. )
24. 
25. 
26. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
27.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
28.     payload = json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
29.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
30. 
31. 
32. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
33.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
34. 
35.     return RecommendationSummary(
36.         symbol=stock.symbol,
37.         companyName=stock.companyName,
38.         sector=stock.sector,
39.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
40.         horizon=horizon,
41.         score=plan.score,
42.         rank=plan.rank,
43.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
44.         conviction=plan.conviction,
45.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
46.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
47.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
48.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
49.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
50.         summary=plan.summary,
51.     )
52. 
53. 
54. @app.get("/healthz")
55. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
56.     return {"status": "ok"}
57. 
58. 
59. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
60. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
61.     dataset = load_dataset()
62.     rows = [
63.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
64.         for stock in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
65.     ]
66.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
67. 
68.     return {
69.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
70.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
71.         "market": dataset.market,
72.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
73.         "universe": dataset.universe,
74.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
75.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
76.         "recommendations": rows,
77.     }
78. 
79. 
80. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
81. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
82.     dataset = load_dataset()
83.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
84. 
85.     return {
86.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
87.         "performance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
88.     }
89. 
90. 
91. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
92. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
93.     dataset = load_dataset()
94.     stock = next(
95.         (
96.             row
97.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
98.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
99.         ),
100.         None,
101.     )
102. 
103.     if stock is None:
104.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
105. 
106.     return StockDetailResponse(
107.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
108.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
109.         market=dataset.market,
110.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
111.         universe=dataset.universe,
112.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
113.         stock=stock,
114.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
115.     )
116.
```

</details>
