```
798. 
799. function enrichRelativeStrength(metrics: SnapshotMetrics): SnapshotMetrics {
800.   return {
801.     ...metrics,
802.     relativeStrength20:
803.       metrics.return20 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn20 !== null
804.         ? metrics.return20 - metrics.benchmarkReturn20
805.         : null,
806.     relativeStrength60:
807.       metrics.return60 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn60 !== null
808.         ? metrics.return60 - metrics.benchmarkReturn60
809.         : null,
810.     relativeStrength120:
811.       metrics.return120 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn120 !== null
812.         ? metrics.return120 - metrics.benchmarkReturn120
813.         : null
814.   };
815. }
816. 
817. function normalizeRsi(rsi: number | null) {
818.   if (rsi === null) {
819.     return 0.5;
820.   }
821. 
822.   return 1 - clamp(Math.abs(rsi - 56) / 24, 0, 1);
823. }
824. 
825. function liquidityScore(liquidityTier: string) {
826.   return liquidityTier === "Tier 1" ? 1 : 0.7;
827. }
828. 
829. function marketCapScore(marketCapBucket: string) {
830.   return marketCapBucket === "Large Cap" ? 1 : 0.75;
831. }
832. 
833. function scoreForHorizon(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: UniverseEntry, horizon: HorizonId) {
834.   switch (horizon) {
835.     case "swing":
836.       return weightedScore([
837.         [0.24, scaleRange(metrics.return20, -8, 16)],
838.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength20, -6, 10)],
839.         [0.12, metrics.sma20 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma20 ? 1 : 0],
840.         [0.11, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
841.         [0.1, metrics.sma20 && metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma20 > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
842.         [0.1, normalizeRsi(metrics.rsi14)],
843.         [0.08, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.75, 1.8)],
844.         [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 50)]
845.       ]);
846.     case "position":
847.       return weightedScore([
848.         [0.23, scaleRange(metrics.return60, -12, 22)],
849.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength60, -8, 14)],
850.         [0.13, metrics.sma50 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma50 ? 1 : 0],
851.         [0.13, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
852.         [0.12, metrics.sma50 && metrics.sma200 && metrics.sma50 > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
853.         [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 30, 95)],
854.         [0.07, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 18, 48)],
855.         [0.05, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.8, 1.6)]
856.       ]);
857.     case "long_term":
858.       return weightedScore([
859.         [0.25, scaleRange(metrics.return120, -15, 30)],
860.         [0.18, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength120, -10, 18)],
861.         [0.16, metrics.sma200 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma200 ? 1 : 0],
862.         [0.12, scaleRange(metrics.rangePosition, 28, 95)],
863.         [0.09, scaleRange(metrics.distanceFromHighPct, -35, 0)],
864.         [0.08, inverseScale(metrics.volatility20, 16, 45)],
865.         [0.07, marketCapScore(entry.marketCapBucket)],
866.         [0.05, liquidityScore(entry.liquidityTier)]
867.       ]);
868.   }
869. }
870. 
871. function blendedModelScore(entry: MarketSeries, metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
872.   const technicalScore = scoreForHorizon(metrics, entry, horizon);
873.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
874.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
875.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
876.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
877. 
878.   switch (horizon) {
879.     case "swing":
880.       return roundMetric(
881.         technicalScore * 0.72 +
882.           fundamentalScore * 0.08 +
883.           sentimentScore * 0.1 +
884.           earningsScore * 0.05 +
885.           analystScore * 0.05
886.       );
887.     case "position":
888.       return roundMetric(
889.         technicalScore * 0.6 +
890.           fundamentalScore * 0.18 +
891.           sentimentScore * 0.1 +
892.           earningsScore * 0.06 +
893.           analystScore * 0.06
894.       );
895.     case "long_term":
896.       return roundMetric(
897.         technicalScore * 0.52 +
898.           fundamentalScore * 0.24 +
899.           sentimentScore * 0.08 +
900.           earningsScore * 0.08 +
901.           analystScore * 0.08
902.       );
903.   }
904. }
905. 
906. function projectedPct(score: number, [minPct, maxPct]: [number, number]) {
907.   const normalizedScore = scaleRange(score, 45, 85);
908.   return minPct + (maxPct - minPct) * normalizedScore;
909. }
910. 
911. function buildTradePlan(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: MarketSeries, horizon: HorizonId): PlanTemplate {
912.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
913.   const score = blendedModelScore(entry, metrics, horizon);
914.   const technicalScore = scoreForHorizon(metrics, entry, horizon);
915.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
916.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
917.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
918.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
919.   const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);
920.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);
921.   const targetPct = Math.max(
922.     projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),
923.     atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple
924.   );
925.   const stopPct = Math.max(
926.     projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),
927.     atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple
928.   );
929.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
930.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));
931.   const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;
932.   const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));
933.   const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);
934.   const strengthLabel =
935.     horizon === "swing"
936.       ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength20)
937.       : horizon === "position"
938.         ? percentText(metrics.relativeStrength60)
939.         : percentText(metrics.relativeStrength120);
940.   const returnLabel =
941.     horizon === "swing"
942.       ? percentText(metrics.return20)
943.       : horizon === "position"
944.         ? percentText(metrics.return60)
945.         : percentText(metrics.return120);
946.   const summary =
947.     horizon === "swing"
948.       ? `${metrics.companyName} is holding a constructive short-term trend with ${returnLabel} recent momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus Nifty 50, and a ${entry.sentiment?.overall?.toLowerCase() ?? "neutral"} news tone.`
949.       : horizon === "position"
950.         ? `${metrics.companyName} ranks well on the medium-term trend stack, combining ${returnLabel} price follow-through, ${strengthLabel} benchmark outperformance, and improving fundamental support.`
951.         : `${metrics.companyName} remains a longer-duration accumulation candidate with ${returnLabel} multi-month momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus the index, and supportive fundamental quality markers.`;
952. 
953.   const drivers = [
954.     `${HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label} score ${signalScoreText(score)} blends technical ${signalScoreText(technicalScore)}, fundamental ${signalScoreText(fundamentalScore)}, and sentiment ${signalScoreText(sentimentScore)} inputs.`,
955.     `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${strengthLabel}, while RSI (14) is ${metrics.rsi14 !== null ? roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : "n/a"}.`,
956.     `Volume is running at ${ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average with 20D volatility at ${percentText(metrics.volatility20)}.`,
957.     entry.fundamentals
958.       ? `Fundamentals show ${entry.fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel ?? "mixed"} sales growth (${entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}) and ${entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel ?? "mixed"} ROE (${entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}).`
959.       : `Fundamental data is unavailable, so the model leans more heavily on price structure and liquidity.`
960.   ];
961. 
962.   const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [
963.     { name: "Model score", value: signalScoreText(score) },
964.     { name: "Technical score", value: signalScoreText(technicalScore) },
965.     {
966.       name: horizon === "swing" ? "20D momentum" : horizon === "position" ? "60D momentum" : "120D momentum",
967.       value: returnLabel
968.     },
969.     {
970.       name: horizon === "swing" ? "20D relative strength" : horizon === "position" ? "60D relative strength" : "120D relative strength",
971.       value: strengthLabel
972.     },
973.     {
974.       name: "Price vs 50DMA",
975.       value: metrics.sma50 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma50)) : "n/a"
976.     },
977.     {
978.       name: "Price vs 200DMA",
979.       value: metrics.sma200 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma200)) : "n/a"
980.     }
981.   ];
982. 
983.   const fundamentalSignals: Signal[] = entry.fundamentals
984.     ? [
985.         { name: "Fundamental score", value: signalScoreText(fundamentalScore) },
986.         { name: "Market cap", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.marketCapCrore) },
987.         { name: "Revenue", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.revenueCrore) },
988.         { name: "Profit", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.profitCrore) },
989.         {
990.           name: "5Y sales growth",
991.           value:
992.             entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct !== null
993.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct).toFixed(2)}%`
994.               : "n/a"
995.         },
996.         {
997.           name: "ROE",
998.           value:
999.             entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct !== null
1000.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct).toFixed(2)}%`
1001.               : "n/a"
1002.         },
1003.         {
1004.           name: "Promoter holding",
1005.           value:
1006.             entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct !== null
1007.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct).toFixed(2)}%`
1008.               : "n/a"
1009.         }
1010.       ]
1011.     : [
1012.         { name: "Fundamental score", value: signalScoreText(fundamentalScore) },
1013.         { name: "Market cap bucket", value: entry.marketCapBucket },
1014.         { name: "Liquidity tier", value: entry.liquidityTier },
1015.         { name: "20D volatility", value: percentText(metrics.volatility20) },
1016.         {
1017.           name: "52W range position",
1018.           value:
1019.             metrics.rangePosition !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1020.         }
1021.       ];
1022. 
1023.   const sentimentSignals: Signal[] = entry.sentiment
1024.     ? [
1025.         { name: "Sentiment score", value: signalScoreText(sentimentScore) },
1026.         { name: "Overall tone", value: entry.sentiment.overall },
1027.         {
1028.           name: "Headline balance",
1029.           value: `${entry.sentiment.positiveCount} positive / ${entry.sentiment.negativeCount} negative`
1030.         },
1031.         { name: "Neutral headlines", value: `${entry.sentiment.neutralCount}` }
1032.       ]
1033.     : [{ name: "Sentiment score", value: signalScoreText(sentimentScore) }];
1034. 
1035.   const earningsSignals: Signal[] = [
1036.     { name: "Earnings headline score", value: signalScoreText(earningsScore) },
1037.     {
1038.       name: "Earnings mentions",
1039.       value: `${entry.sentiment?.earningsMentionCount ?? 0} recent headlines`
1040.     }
1041.   ];
1042. 
1043.   const analystSignals: Signal[] = [
1044.     { name: "Analyst tone score", value: signalScoreText(analystScore) },
1045.     {
1046.       name: "Analyst mentions",
1047.       value: `${entry.sentiment?.analystMentionCount ?? 0} recent headlines`
1048.     }
1049.   ];
1050. 
1051.   const marketContext = [
1052.     `Latest session change: ${percentText(metrics.sessionChangePct)} while Nifty 50 moved ${percentText(metrics.benchmarkSessionChangePct)}.`,
1053.     `Current price ${priceText(metrics.currentPrice)} versus 52-week range ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekLow)} to ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekHigh)}.`,
1054.     `Current volume ${compactNumber(metrics.currentVolume)} versus 20-day average ${compactNumber(metrics.avgVolume20)}.`,
1055.     ...(entry.sentiment?.headlines.slice(0, 3).map((headline) => `${headline.source}: ${headline.title}`) ?? [])
1056.   ];
1057. 
1058.   return {
1059.     score,
1060.     conviction,
1061.     entryPrice,
1062.     targetPrice,
1063.     stopLoss,
1064.     expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(expectedReturnPct),
1065.     riskReward,
1066.     summary,
1067.     drivers,
1068.     technicalSignals,
1069.     fundamentalSignals,
1070.     sentimentSignals,
1071.     earningsSignals,
1072.     analystSignals,
1073.     newsContext: marketContext
1074.   };
1075. }
1076. 
1077. function applyRanking(recommendations: StockAnalysis[]) {
1078.   const ranked = recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
1079.     ...recommendation,
1080.     profiles: {
1081.       swing: { ...recommendation.profiles.swing },
1082.       position: { ...recommendation.profiles.position },
1083.       long_term: { ...recommendation.profiles.long_term }
1084.     }
1085.   }));
1086. 
1087.   for (const horizon of Object.keys(HORIZON_SETTINGS) as HorizonId[]) {
1088.     const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1089.     const ordered = [...ranked].sort(
1090.       (left, right) =>
1091.         (right.profiles[horizon].score ?? right.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct) -
1092.         (left.profiles[horizon].score ?? left.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct)
1093.     );
1094.     const thresholdCount = ordered.filter(
1095.       (stock, index) =>
1096.         index < settings.recommendationCount &&
1097.         (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore
1098.     ).length;
1099.     const forcedCount = Math.min(settings.minimumRecommendationCount, ordered.length);
1100. 
1101.     ordered.forEach((stock, index) => {
1102.       stock.profiles[horizon].rank = index + 1;
1103.       stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended =
1104.         (index < settings.recommendationCount &&
1105.           (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore) ||
1106.         (thresholdCount < forcedCount && index < forcedCount);
1107.     });
1108.   }
1109. 
1110.   return ranked;
1111. }
1112. 
1113. function buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(
1114.   date: string,
1115.   liveSeries: MarketSeries[],
1116.   benchmark: MarketSeries
1117. ) {
1118.   const recommendations = liveSeries
1119.     .map((series) => {
1120.       const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
1121. 
1122.       if (index === undefined) {
1123.         return null;
1124.       }
1125. 
1126.       const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
1127. 
1128.       if (!metrics) {
1129.         return null;
1130.       }
1131. 
1132.       const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
1133. 
1134.       return {
1135.         symbol: series.symbol,
1136.         companyName: series.companyName,
1137.         sector: series.sector,
1138.         industry: series.industry,
1139.         marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
1140.         liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
1141.         currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
1142.         fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
1143.         sentiment: series.sentiment,
1144.         profiles: {
1145.           swing: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "swing"),
1146.           position: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "position"),
1147.           long_term: buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, "long_term")
1148.         }
1149.       } satisfies StockAnalysis;
1150.     })
1151.     .filter((recommendation): recommendation is StockAnalysis => recommendation !== null);
1152. 
1153.   return applyRanking(recommendations);
1154. }
1155. 
1156. function resolveOutcomeResult(
1157.   openPrice: number,
1158.   targetPrice: number,
1159.   stopLoss: number,
1160.   targetHit: boolean,
1161.   stopHit: boolean
1162. ): OutcomeResult {
1163.   if (targetHit && stopHit) {
1164.     return Math.abs(openPrice - stopLoss) <= Math.abs(targetPrice - openPrice)
1165.       ? "stop_loss_hit"
1166.       : "target_hit";
1167.   }
1168. 
1169.   return targetHit ? "target_hit" : "stop_loss_hit";
1170. }
1171. 
1172. function evaluateOutcome(
1173.   series: MarketSeries,
1174.   batchDate: string,
1175.   horizon: HorizonId,
1176.   entryPrice: number,
1177.   targetPrice: number,
1178.   stopLoss: number
1179. ): RecommendationOutcome {
1180.   const batchIndex = series.indexByDate.get(batchDate);
1181. 
1182.   if (batchIndex === undefined) {
1183.     return {
1184.       result: "open",
1185.       evaluatedOn: batchDate,
1186.       holdingDays: 0,
1187.       returnPct: 0,
1188.       notes: "The recommendation date could not be mapped to live price history."
1189.     };
1190.   }
1191. 
1192.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1193.   const lastIndex = Math.min(series.bars.length - 1, batchIndex + settings.maxHoldDays);
1194. 
1195.   for (let index = batchIndex + 1; index <= lastIndex; index += 1) {
1196.     const bar = series.bars[index];
1197.     const targetHit = bar.high >= targetPrice;
1198.     const stopHit = bar.low <= stopLoss;
1199. 
1200.     if (!targetHit && !stopHit) {
1201.       continue;
1202.     }
1203. 
1204.     const result = resolveOutcomeResult(bar.open, targetPrice, stopLoss, targetHit, stopHit);
1205.     const exitPrice = result === "target_hit" ? targetPrice : stopLoss;
1206. 
1207.     return {
1208.       result,
1209.       evaluatedOn: bar.date,
1210.       holdingDays: index - batchIndex,
1211.       returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(exitPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0),
1212.       notes:
1213.         result === "target_hit"
1214.           ? `Target was reached on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`
1215.           : `Stop-loss was triggered on ${bar.date} after ${index - batchIndex} trading day(s).`
1216.     };
1217.   }
1218. 
1219.   const finalBar = series.bars[lastIndex];
1220. 
1221.   return {
1222.     result: "open",
1223.     evaluatedOn: finalBar.date,
1224.     holdingDays: lastIndex - batchIndex,
1225.     returnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(finalBar.close, entryPrice) ?? 0),
1226.     notes: `The trade remains open after ${lastIndex - batchIndex} trading day(s); latest close is ${priceText(finalBar.close)}.`
1227.   };
1228. }
1229. 
1230. function toHistoricalRecommendation(
1231.   recommendation: StockAnalysis,
1232.   batchDate: string,
1233.   seriesBySymbol: Map<string, MarketSeries>
1234. ): HistoricalStockRecommendation {
1235.   const series = seriesBySymbol.get(recommendation.symbol);
1236. 
1237.   if (!series) {
1238.     throw new Error(`Missing series for ${recommendation.symbol}`);
1239.   }
1240. 
1241.   const buildHistoricalPlan = (horizon: HorizonId): HistoricalRecommendationPlan => {
1242.     const livePlan = recommendation.profiles[horizon];
1243. 
1244.     return {
1245.       score: livePlan.score,
1246.       rank: livePlan.rank,
1247.       isRecommended: livePlan.isRecommended,
1248.       conviction: livePlan.conviction,
1249.       entryPrice: livePlan.entryPrice,
1250.       targetPrice: livePlan.targetPrice,
1251.       stopLoss: livePlan.stopLoss,
1252.       summary: livePlan.summary,
1253.       outcome: evaluateOutcome(
1254.         series,
1255.         batchDate,
1256.         horizon,
1257.         livePlan.entryPrice,
1258.         livePlan.targetPrice,
1259.         livePlan.stopLoss
1260.       )
1261.     };
1262.   };
1263. 
1264.   return {
1265.     symbol: recommendation.symbol,
1266.     companyName: recommendation.companyName,
1267.     sector: recommendation.sector,
1268.     profiles: {
1269.       swing: buildHistoricalPlan("swing"),
1270.       position: buildHistoricalPlan("position"),
1271.       long_term: buildHistoricalPlan("long_term")
1272.     }
1273.   };
1274. }
1275. 
1276. function historicalBatchDates(benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1277.   return benchmark.bars
1278.     .slice(-(HISTORY_BATCH_COUNT + 1), -1)
1279.     .map((bar) => bar.date)
1280.     .filter((date) => date !== benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date);
```

```
1283. function buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries: MarketSeries[], benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1284.   const seriesBySymbol = new Map(liveSeries.map((series) => [series.symbol, series]));
1285. 
1286.   return historicalBatchDates(benchmark).map((date) => {
1287.     const recommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(date, liveSeries, benchmark).map(
1288.       (recommendation) => toHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, date, seriesBySymbol)
1289.     );
1290. 
1291.     return {
1292.       batchDate: date,
1293.       publishedAt: publishedTimestamp(date),
1294.       recommendations
1295.     } satisfies HistoricalBatch;
1296.   });
1297. }
1298. 
1299. function liveDataSourceInfo(detail: string, analyzedSymbols: number): DataSourceInfo {
1300.   return {
1301.     mode: "live",
1302.     provider: "Yahoo Finance chart data",
1303.     asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
1304.     detail,
1305.     analyzedSymbols
1306.   };
1307. }
1308. 
1309. function latestCommonTradingDate(series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1310.   for (let index = series.bars.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
1311.     const date = series.bars[index].date;
1312. 
1313.     if (benchmark.indexByDate.has(date)) {
1314.       return date;
1315.     }
1316.   }
1317. 
1318.   return null;
1319. }
1320. 
1321. function buildSearchStockForDate(date: string, series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1322.   const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
1323. 
1324.   if (index === undefined) {
1325.     return null;
1326.   }
1327. 
1328.   const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
1329. 
1330.   if (!metrics) {
1331.     return null;
1332.   }
1333. 
1334.   const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
1335.   const buildSearchPlan = (horizon: HorizonId) => {
1336.     const plan = buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, horizon);
1337. 
1338.     return {
1339.       ...plan,
1340.       rank: 1,
1341.       isRecommended: (plan.score ?? 0) >= HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].minimumScore
1342.     };
1343.   };
1344. 
1345.   return {
1346.     symbol: series.symbol,
1347.     companyName: series.companyName,
1348.     sector: series.sector,
1349.     industry: series.industry,
1350.     marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
1351.     liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
1352.     currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
1353.     fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
1354.     sentiment: series.sentiment,
1355.     profiles: {
1356.       swing: buildSearchPlan("swing"),
1357.       position: buildSearchPlan("position"),
1358.       long_term: buildSearchPlan("long_term")
1359.     }
1360.   } satisfies StockAnalysis;
1361. }
1362. 
1363. function searchVerdict(stock: StockAnalysis) {
1364.   const recommendedHorizons = (Object.keys(HORIZON_SETTINGS) as HorizonId[]).filter(
1365.     (horizon) => stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
1366.   );
1367. 
1368.   if (!recommendedHorizons.length) {
1369.     return {
1370.       recommendedHorizons,
1371.       shouldConsider: false,
1372.       verdict:
1373.         "Do not prioritize this stock right now. Its current technical, fundamental, and sentiment mix is below the recommendation threshold across all supported horizons."
1374.     };
1375.   }
1376. 
1377.   const labels = recommendedHorizons.map((horizon) => HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label.toLowerCase());
1378. 
1379.   return {
1380.     recommendedHorizons,
1381.     shouldConsider: true,
1382.     verdict: `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(", ")} setups. The current score clears the model threshold for those horizons.`
1383.   };
1384. }
1385. 
1386. function fallbackDataSourceInfo(
1387.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1388.   detail: string,
1389.   analyzedSymbols: number
1390. ): DataSourceInfo {
1391.   return {
1392.     mode,
1393.     provider: mode === "cached" ? "Local generated snapshot" : "Sample fallback dataset",
1394.     asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
1395.     detail,
1396.     analyzedSymbols
1397.   };
1398. }
1399. 
1400. async function buildLiveDataset() {
1401.   const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
1402.   const liveSeries = (await Promise.all(MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry)))).filter(
1403.     (series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null
1404.   );
1405. 
1406.   if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
1407.     return null;
1408.   }
1409. 
1410.   const latestDate = benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date;
1411. 
1412.   if (!latestDate) {
1413.     return null;
1414.   }
1415. 
1416.   const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(latestDate, liveSeries, benchmark);
1417. 
1418.   if (currentRecommendations.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
1419.     return null;
1420.   }
1421. 
1422.   const dataset: RecommendationDataset = {
1423.     market: "Indian Equities",
1424.     exchange: "NSE",
1425.     universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,
1426.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
1427.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,
1428.       liveSeries.length
1429.     ),
1430.     profiles: PROFILE_LIST,
1431.     currentBatch: {
1432.       batchDate: latestDate,
1433.       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1434.       recommendations: currentRecommendations
1435.     },
1436.     history: buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark)
1437.   };
1438. 
1439.   await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);
1440. 
1441.   return dataset;
1442. }
1443. 
1444. function annotateFallbackDataset(
1445.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
1446.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1447.   detail: string
1448. ): RecommendationDataset {
1449.   return {
1450.     ...dataset,
1451.     dataSource: fallbackDataSourceInfo(mode, detail, dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.length)
1452.   };
1453. }
1454. 
1455. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
1456.   const liveDataset = await buildLiveDataset();
1457. 
1458.   if (liveDataset) {
1459.     return liveDataset;
1460.   }
1461. 
1462.   const cachedDataset = await readGeneratedDataset();
1463. 
1464.   if (cachedDataset) {
1465.     return annotateFallbackDataset(
1466.       cachedDataset,
1467.       "cached",
1468.       `Using the last successful live snapshot from ${cachedDataset.currentBatch.generatedAt}.`
1469.     );
1470.   }
1471. 
1472.   return annotateFallbackDataset(
1473.     await readSampleDataset(),
1474.     "sample",
1475.     "Live market requests failed and no cached snapshot was available, so the demo dataset is being shown."
1476.   );
1477. }
```

```
1. import { Dashboard } from "@/components/dashboard";

3. import { analyzeSearchSymbol, loadRecommendationData } from "@/lib/recommendation-data";

5. export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
6. 
7. type PageProps = {
8.   searchParams?: Promise<{
9.     symbol?: string;
10.   }>;
11. };

13. export default async function Page({ searchParams }: PageProps) {

18.     query ? analyzeSearchSymbol(query) : Promise.resolve<SearchAnalysisResult | null>(null)
19.   ]);
20.
```

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\rc18763\\.copilot\\session-state\\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\\plan.md\n@@\n ## Current state\n - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\\copilot`.\n - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.\n-- Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.\n-- Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.\n+- Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.\n+- The dashboard now includes:\n+  - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,\n+  - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,\n+  - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS.\n+- Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.\n@@\n 3. Research feature layer\n    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.\n    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.\n+   - Current implementation note: the live model now combines technical factors with lightweight fundamentals and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone heuristics.\n@@\n 6. Explainability and delivery\n    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.\n    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.\n    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.\n    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.\n+   - Current implementation note: the dashboard includes a searchable stock analyzer and a recommendation-history menu with achieved/not-achieved outcome inspection.\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\README.md\n@@\n ## Live recommendation flow\n \n - The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for a liquid NSE watchlist plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.\n-- Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, and volatility.\n+- Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, volatility, lightweight fundamentals, and headline-based sentiment / earnings / analyst signals.\n - The app also backtests prior daily batches from the same historical price series so the dashboard can maintain horizon-specific success rates.\n - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.\n - If live requests fail later, the app falls back to that cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.\n@@\n 3. The detail view explains:\n    - key factors behind the recommendation\n    - recommended entry price\n    - target price\n    - stop-loss\n    - risk-reward profile\n    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes\n 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, shows whether each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.\n+5. The search panel can analyze an NSE stock on demand and say whether it should be **considered or avoided** for each supported horizon.\n*** End Patch\n"
```

```
615.                     {stockHistory.map((row) => (
616.                       <tr key={`${row.symbol}-${row.batchDate}-${row.horizon}`}>
617.                         <td>{row.batchDate}</td>
618.                         <td>
619.                           <span className={`result-chip ${outcomeClass(row.outcome.result)}`}>
620.                             {outcomeLabel(row.outcome.result)}
621.                           </span>
622.                         </td>
623.                         <td>{`${row.outcome.returnPct.toFixed(2)}%`}</td>
624.                         <td>{row.outcome.holdingDays}</td>
625.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.entryPrice)}</td>
626.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.targetPrice)}</td>
627.                         <td>{formatPrice(row.stopLoss)}</td>
628.                       </tr>
629.                     ))}
630.                   </tbody>
631.                 </table>
632.               </div>
633. 
634.               {stockHistory[0] ? (
635.                 <p className="footnote">
636.                   Latest reviewed note: {stockHistory[0].outcome.notes}
637.                 </p>
638.               ) : null}
639.             </section>
640.           </aside>
641.         ) : null}
642.       </div>
643.     </main>
644.   );
645. }
646.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 190m 25s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Done.** The app now has **headline-based sentiment analysis** in the live scoring model, a **stock search analyzer** that tells you whether an NSE stock should be considered for **swing / position / long-term** setups, and a **previous recommendation menu** that shows past calls as **achieved / not achieved / open**.

The main work is in:
- `apps\web\lib\company-research.ts` — fundamentals + news / earnings / analyst-tone research
- `apps\web\lib\recommendation-data.ts` — blended scoring and searched-stock analysis
- `apps\web\components\dashboard.tsx` — search UI, history menu, and new analysis cards

I also updated the README and plan so the repo now reflects these features. The remaining unfinished area is **ingestion hardening and broader market coverage**, not the sentiment/search/history functionality.

### ✅ `sql`

**Query ready todos**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.status\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\n  AND NOT EXISTS (\n    SELECT 1\n    FROM todo_deps td\n    JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n    WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n  )\nORDER BY t.created_at;"
}
```

</details>

```
1 row(s) returned:

| id | title | status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| build-backtesting | Build backtesting and evaluation | pending |
```

<sub>⏱️ 233m 9s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md.**

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>104 lines</summary>

```
1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
13.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS.
14. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
15. 
16. ## Proposed approach
17. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
18. 
19. 1. Product definition
20.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
21.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
22.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
23.    - Chosen MVP scope:
24.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
25.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
26.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
27.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
28.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
29.      - Supported horizons in the MVP: swing, position, and long-term; intraday deferred to a later high-frequency phase.
30. 
31. 2. Data foundation
32.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
33.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
34.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
35.    - Current implementation note: the MVP now ingests live Yahoo Finance chart data plus a cached generated snapshot fallback.
36. 
37. 3. Research feature layer
38.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
39.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
40.    - Current implementation note: the live model now combines technical factors with lightweight fundamentals and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone heuristics.
41. 
42. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
43.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
44.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
45.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
46.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
47.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
48.    - Current implementation note: the first live model uses momentum, moving-average trend, relative strength versus Nifty 50, RSI, volume, and volatility to produce ranked recommendations.
49. 
50. 5. Evaluation and controls
51.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
52.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
53.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
54. 
55. 6. Explainability and delivery
56.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
57.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
58.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
59.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
60.    - Current implementation note: the dashboard includes a searchable stock analyzer and a recommendation-history menu with achieved/not-achieved outcome inspection.
61. 
62. 7. UI template recommendation
63.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
64.    - Chosen starting point: Next.js with TypeScript and shadcn/ui-style dashboard patterns for a clean analyst-oriented UI.
65.    - Layout suggestion:
66.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.
67.      - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.
68.      - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.
69.    - Stock detail page should include:
70.      - Recommendation summary card.
71.      - Horizon selector so the same stock can show swing, position, and long-term variants when available.
72.      - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.
73.      - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.
74.      - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.
75. 
76. ## Draft todo list
77. - `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.
78. - `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.
79. - `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.
80. - `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.
81. - `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.
82. - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.
83. - `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.
84. - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.
85. - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.
86. - `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.
87. - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.
88. 
89. ## Notes and considerations
90. - "Like BlackRock" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.
91. - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.
92. - Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.
93. - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.
94. - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.
95. - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.
96. - Revised scope assumption: the platform should be designed for multiple horizons, but daily-data MVP coverage should focus on swing, position, and long-term profiles first.
97. - Intraday recommendations require separate high-frequency data ingestion, tighter execution assumptions, and different backtesting logic, so they should be treated as a later phase rather than bundled into the first daily-data release.
98. - Chosen foundation stack:
99.   - Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript with a clean dashboard UI.
100.   - API and analytics services: FastAPI + Pydantic in Python.
101.   - Operational storage: PostgreSQL for users, recommendation batches, and audit history.
102.   - Research storage: Parquet or DuckDB for snapshots, factor data, and backtesting workloads.
103.   - Scheduling: market-calendar-aware daily batch jobs, starting with end-of-day runs.
104.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 233m 9s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

```
27. function formatPrice(value: number) {

30.     currency: "INR",
31.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
32.     maximumFractionDigits: 2

36. function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {

40. function formatPercent(value: number | null) {

42.     return "Pending";

44.

46. }

49.   return plan.score ?? plan.expectedReturnPct;
50. }

52. function isRecommendedPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan | { isRecommended?: boolean }) {
53.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
54. }
55. 
56. function historyDecisionLabel(result: "target_hit" | "stop_loss_hit" | "open") {
57.   switch (result) {

59.       return "Achieved";
60.     case "stop_loss_hit":
61.       return "Not achieved";
62.     default:

75.     return null;

86.           </div>
87.         ))}
88.       </div>
89.     </section>
90.   );
91. }

93. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
94.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>("swing");

97.     data.history[0]?.batchDate ?? ""
98.   );
99. 
100.   const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {

102.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
103.     );
104.     const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));
105. 
106.     return recommended.length ? recommended : sorted.slice(0, 5);
107.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);
108. 
109.   useEffect(() => {
110.     if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {
111.       setSelectedSymbol("");
112.       return;
113.     }
114. 
115.     if (!visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {
116.       setSelectedSymbol(visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);

118.   }, [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);
119. 
120.   useEffect(() => {

125.

130.

142.       selectedStock
143.         ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, selectedStock.symbol, activeHorizon)

146.   );
147.   const selectedHistoryBatch =

153.

155.       .filter((recommendation) => isRecommendedPlan(recommendation.profiles[activeHorizon]))

158.           (left.profiles[activeHorizon].rank ?? 999) - (right.profiles[activeHorizon].rank ?? 999)

161.

167.         plan: searchedStock.profiles[horizon]
168.       }))
169.     : [];

171.   return (
172.     <main className="shell">

174.         <div className="headline">
```

```
58.   "bearish",

77.   "slumps",

81. ];

84.   "earnings",
85.   "results",
86.   "quarter",
87.   "q1",
88.   "q2",
89.   "q3",
90.   "q4",
91.   "revenue",
92.   "profit",
93.   "margin"
94. ];
95.

97.   "analyst",

101.   "sell",
102.   "target",
103.   "rating",

112. function decodeHtmlEntities(value: string) {
113.   return value
114.     .replace(/&amp;/g, "&")
115.     .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
116.     .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
117.     .replace(/&lt;/g, "<")
118.     .replace(/&gt;/g, ">");
119. }
120. 
121. function normalizeSearchSymbol(query: string) {
122.   return query.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
123. }
124. 
125. function parseIndianNumber(value: string | null) {
126.   if (!value) {
127.     return null;
128.   }
129. 
130.   const normalized = value.replace(/,/g, "").trim();
131.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(normalized);
132. 
133.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
134. }
135. 
136. function parsePercent(value: string | null) {
137.   if (!value) {
138.     return null;
139.   }
140. 
141.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(value.replace(/,/g, "").trim());
142. 
143.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
144. }
145. 
146. function textContent(regex: RegExp, text: string) {
147.   const match = regex.exec(text);
148.   return match?.[1] ?? null;
149. }
150. 
151. function titleTone(title: string) {
152.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
153.   const positiveHits = POSITIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
154.   const negativeHits = NEGATIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
155. 
156.   if (positiveHits > negativeHits) {
157.     return "positive" as const;
158.   }
159. 
160.   if (negativeHits > positiveHits) {
161.     return "negative" as const;
162.   }
163. 
164.   return "neutral" as const;
165. }
166. 
167. function headlineCategory(title: string) {
168.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
169. 
170.   if (ANALYST_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
171.     return "analyst" as const;
172.   }
173. 
174.   if (EARNINGS_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
175.     return "earnings" as const;
176.   }
177. 
178.   return "news" as const;
179. }
180. 
181. function sentimentScore(headlines: NewsHeadline[]) {
182.   if (!headlines.length) {
183.     return 50;
184.   }
185. 
186.   const toneTotal = headlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
187.     if (headline.tone === "positive") {
188.       return total + 1;
189.     }
190. 
191.     if (headline.tone === "negative") {
192.       return total - 1;
193.     }
194. 
195.     return total;
196.   }, 0);
197. 
198.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + (toneTotal / headlines.length) * 35));
199. }
200. 
201. function overallSentiment(score: number) {
202.   if (score >= 58) {
203.     return "Positive" as const;
204.   }
205. 
206.   if (score <= 42) {
207.     return "Negative" as const;
208.   }
209. 
210.   return "Neutral" as const;
211. }

213. async function fetchText(url: string) {
214.   const response = await fetch(url, {
215.     cache: "no-store",
216.     headers: {
217.       Accept: "text/html,application/xml,application/json",
218.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
219.     }
220.   });
221. 
222.   if (!response.ok) {
223.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
224.   }
225. 
226.   return response.text();
227. }
228. 
229. async function fetchJson<T>(url: string) {
230.   const response = await fetch(url, {
231.     cache: "no-store",
232.     headers: {
233.       Accept: "application/json",
234.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
235.     }
236.   });
237. 
238.   if (!response.ok) {
239.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
240.   }
241. 
242.   return (await response.json()) as T;
243. }
```

```
245. export async function lookupNseSymbol(query: string): Promise<CompanyLookup | null> {
246.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
247. 
248.   if (!normalized) {
249.     return null;
250.   }
251. 
252.   const payload = await fetchJson<YahooSearchResponse>(
253.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}&quotesCount=6&newsCount=0&enableFuzzyQuery=true`
254.   );
255.   const quote = payload.quotes?.find(
256.     (item) => item.exchange === "NSI" && item.quoteType === "EQUITY" && item.symbol
257.   );
258. 
259.   if (!quote?.symbol) {
260.     return null;
261.   }
262. 
263.   return {
264.     symbol: normalizeSearchSymbol(quote.symbol),
265.     yahooSymbol: quote.symbol,
266.     companyName: quote.longname ?? quote.shortname ?? normalized,
267.     sector: quote.sectorDisp ?? quote.sector ?? "Unknown",
268.     industry: quote.industryDisp ?? quote.industry ?? undefined
269.   };
270. }
271. 
272. async function fetchScreenerFundamentals(symbol: string) {
273.   try {
274.     const html = await fetchText(`https://www.screener.in/company/${encodeURIComponent(symbol)}/consolidated/`);
275.     const description = decodeHtmlEntities(
276.       textContent(/<meta name="description" content="([^"]+)"/i, html) ?? ""
277.     );
278. 
279.     if (!description) {
280.       return null;
281.     }
282. 
283.     const marketCapCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Mkt Cap:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Crore/i, description));
284.     const marketCapChange1YPct = parsePercent(
285.       textContent(/Mkt Cap:[^(]+\((?:up|down)\s*([\d.]+)% in 1 year\)/i, description)
286.     );
287.     const revenueCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Revenue:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
288.     const profitCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Profit:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
289.     const salesGrowthLabel = textContent(/company has delivered a ([a-z]+) sales growth/i, description);
290.     const salesGrowth5YPct = parsePercent(textContent(/sales growth of ([\d.]+)% over past five years/i, description));
291.     const returnOnEquityLabel = textContent(/company has a ([a-z]+) return on equity/i, description);
292.     const returnOnEquityPct = parsePercent(textContent(/return on equity of ([\d.]+)%/i, description));
293.     const promoterHoldingPct = parsePercent(textContent(/Promoter Holding:\s*([\d.]+)%/i, description));
294.     const netMarginPct =
295.       revenueCrore && profitCrore ? Number(((profitCrore / revenueCrore) * 100).toFixed(2)) : null;
296. 
297.     return {
298.       source: "Screener.in",
299.       summary: description,
300.       marketCapCrore,
301.       marketCapChange1YPct,
302.       revenueCrore,
303.       profitCrore,
304.       netMarginPct,
305.       salesGrowth5YPct,
306.       salesGrowthLabel,
307.       returnOnEquityPct,
308.       returnOnEquityLabel,
309.       promoterHoldingPct
310.     } satisfies FundamentalSnapshot;
311.   } catch {
312.     return null;
313.   }
314. }
315. 
316. async function fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(companyName: string, symbol: string) {
317.   try {
318.     const query = `"${companyName}" OR ${symbol} NSE stock when:30d`;
319.     const xml = await fetchText(
320.       `https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN:en`
321.     );
322.     const headlines: NewsHeadline[] = [];
323. 
324.     for (const match of xml.matchAll(/<item>([\s\S]*?)<\/item>/g)) {
325.       const item = match[1];
326.       const rawTitle = textContent(/<title>([\s\S]*?)<\/title>/i, item);
327. 
328.       if (!rawTitle) {
329.         continue;
330.       }
331. 
332.       const title = decodeHtmlEntities(rawTitle).replace(/\s+-\s+[^-]+$/, "").trim();
333.       const source = decodeHtmlEntities(textContent(/<source[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/source>/i, item) ?? "Google News");
334.       const publishedAt = textContent(/<pubDate>([\s\S]*?)<\/pubDate>/i, item) ?? "";
335.       const tone = titleTone(title);
336.       const category = headlineCategory(title);
337. 
338.       headlines.push({
339.         title,
340.         source,
341.         publishedAt,
342.         tone,
343.         category
344.       });
345. 
346.       if (headlines.length >= 6) {
347.         break;
348.       }
349.     }
350. 
351.     if (!headlines.length) {
352.       return null;
353.     }
354. 
355.     const score = Number(sentimentScore(headlines).toFixed(2));
356. 
357.     return {
358.       query,
359.       overall: overallSentiment(score),
360.       score,
361.       positiveCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "positive").length,
362.       neutralCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "neutral").length,
363.       negativeCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "negative").length,
364.       earningsMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings").length,
365.       analystMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "analyst").length,
366.       headlines
367.     } satisfies SentimentSnapshot;
368.   } catch {
369.     return null;
370.   }
371. }
372. 
373. export async function fetchCompanyResearch(lookup: CompanyLookup): Promise<CompanyResearch> {
374.   const [fundamentals, sentiment] = await Promise.all([
375.     fetchScreenerFundamentals(lookup.symbol),
376.     fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(lookup.companyName, lookup.symbol)
377.   ]);
378. 
379.   return {
380.     companyName: lookup.companyName,
381.     sector: lookup.sector,
382.     industry: lookup.industry,
383.     fundamentals,
384.     sentiment
385.   };
386. }
387. 
388. export function scoreFundamentals(fundamentals: FundamentalSnapshot | null) {
389.   if (!fundamentals) {
390.     return 50;
391.   }
392. 
393.   const growthScore =
394.     fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct === null
395.       ? 50
396.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 35 + fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct * 3));
397.   const roeScore =
398.     fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct === null
399.       ? 50
400.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct * 3));
401.   const marginScore =
402.     fundamentals.netMarginPct === null
403.       ? 50
404.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 30 + fundamentals.netMarginPct * 2.5));
405.   const promoterScore =
406.     fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct === null
407.       ? 50
408.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct));
409.   const labelBonus =
410.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "strong" || fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "high" ? 7 : 0) +
411.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "high" || fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "good" ? 6 : 0) -
412.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "poor" ? 8 : 0) -
413.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "low" ? 8 : 0);
414. 
415.   return Number(
416.     (
417.       growthScore * 0.3 +
418.       roeScore * 0.35 +
419.       marginScore * 0.15 +
420.       promoterScore * 0.2 +
421.       labelBonus
422.     ).toFixed(2)
423.   );
424. }
425. 
426. export function scoreSentiment(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
427.   return sentiment?.score ?? 50;
428. }
429. 
430. export function scoreEarningsSignal(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
```

</details>

```
1. "use client";
2. 
3. import { useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
4. import { usePathname, useRouter } from "next/navigation";
5. import type { StockSearchSuggestion } from "@/lib/types";
6. 
7. type SuggestionApiResponse = {
8.   suggestions: StockSearchSuggestion[];
9.   error?: string;
10. };
11. 
12. type StockSearchBoxProps = {
13.   initialQuery: string;
14.   localSuggestions: StockSearchSuggestion[];
15. };
16. 
17. function normalizeQuery(value: string) {
18.   return value.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
19. }
20. 
21. function matchesSuggestion(suggestion: StockSearchSuggestion, query: string) {
22.   const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
23. 
24.   if (!normalized) {
25.     return false;
26.   }
27. 
28.   return [suggestion.symbol, suggestion.companyName, suggestion.sector, suggestion.industry ?? ""]
29.     .map((value) => value.toUpperCase())
30.     .some((value) => value.includes(normalized));
31. }
32. 
33. function dedupeSuggestions(items: StockSearchSuggestion[]) {
34.   const seen = new Set<string>();
35. 
36.   return items.filter((item) => {
37.     if (seen.has(item.symbol)) {
38.       return false;
39.     }
40. 
41.     seen.add(item.symbol);
42.     return true;
43.   });
44. }
45. 
46. export function StockSearchBox({ initialQuery, localSuggestions }: StockSearchBoxProps) {
47.   const router = useRouter();
48.   const pathname = usePathname();
49.   const blurTimeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
50.   const [query, setQuery] = useState(initialQuery);
51.   const [remoteSuggestions, setRemoteSuggestions] = useState<StockSearchSuggestion[]>([]);
52.   const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
53.   const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
54.   const [errorMessage, setErrorMessage] = useState<string | null>(null);
55.   const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);
56. 
57.   useEffect(() => {
58.     setQuery(initialQuery);
59.     setIsOpen(false);
60.     setActiveIndex(-1);
61.   }, [initialQuery]);
62. 
63.   useEffect(() => {
64.     return () => {
65.       if (blurTimeoutRef.current) {
66.         clearTimeout(blurTimeoutRef.current);
67.       }
68.     };
69.   }, []);
70. 
71.   const localMatches = useMemo(() => {
72.     const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
73. 
74.     if (!normalized) {
75.       return [];
76.     }
77. 
78.     return localSuggestions.filter((suggestion) => matchesSuggestion(suggestion, normalized)).slice(0, 5);
79.   }, [localSuggestions, query]);
80. 
81.   const suggestions = useMemo(() => {
82.     const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
83. 
84.     if (!normalized) {
85.       return [];
86.     }
87. 
88.     return dedupeSuggestions([...localMatches, ...remoteSuggestions]).slice(0, 8);
89.   }, [localMatches, query, remoteSuggestions]);
90. 
91.   useEffect(() => {
92.     const normalized = normalizeQuery(query);
93. 
94.     setActiveIndex(-1);
95.     setErrorMessage(null);
96. 
97.     if (!normalized) {
98.       setRemoteSuggestions([]);
99.       setIsLoading(false);
100.       setIsOpen(false);
101.       return;
102.     }
103. 
104.     if (normalized.length < 2) {
105.       setRemoteSuggestions([]);
106.       setIsLoading(false);
107.       setIsOpen(localMatches.length > 0);
108.       return;
109.     }
110. 
111.     let cancelled = false;
112.     setIsLoading(true);
113. 
114.     const timer = setTimeout(async () => {
115.       try {
116.         const response = await fetch(`/api/search-suggestions?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}`, {
117.           cache: "no-store"
118.         });
119.         const payload = (await response.json()) as SuggestionApiResponse;
120. 
121.         if (cancelled) {
122.           return;
123.         }
124. 
125.         if (!response.ok) {
126.           setRemoteSuggestions([]);
127.           setErrorMessage(payload.error ?? "Live suggestions are unavailable right now.");
128.         } else {
129.           setRemoteSuggestions(payload.suggestions ?? []);
130.         }
131.       } catch {
132.         if (cancelled) {
133.           return;
134.         }
135. 
136.         setRemoteSuggestions([]);
137.         setErrorMessage("Live suggestions are unavailable right now.");
138.       } finally {
139.         if (!cancelled) {
140.           setIsLoading(false);
141.           setIsOpen(true);
142.         }
143.       }
144.     }, 220);
145. 
146.     return () => {
147.       cancelled = true;
148.       clearTimeout(timer);
149.     };
150.   }, [localMatches.length, query]);
151. 
152.   const activeSuggestion = activeIndex >= 0 ? suggestions[activeIndex] : null;
153. 
154.   function clearBlurTimeout() {
155.     if (blurTimeoutRef.current) {
156.       clearTimeout(blurTimeoutRef.current);
157.       blurTimeoutRef.current = null;
158.     }
159.   }
160. 
161.   function analyzeSelection(value: string) {
162.     const normalized = normalizeQuery(value);
163. 
164.     if (!normalized) {
165.       return;
166.     }
167. 
168.     setQuery(normalized);
169.     setIsOpen(false);
170.     router.push(`${pathname}?symbol=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}`);
171.   }
172. 
173.   return (
174.     <form
175.       className="search-form"
176.       method="get"
177.       onSubmit={(event) => {
178.         event.preventDefault();
179.         analyzeSelection(activeSuggestion?.symbol ?? query);
180.       }}
181.     >
182.       <div className="search-autocomplete">
183.         <input
184.           aria-activedescendant={
185.             activeSuggestion ? `stock-search-option-${activeSuggestion.symbol}` : undefined
186.           }
187.           aria-autocomplete="list"
188.           aria-controls="stock-search-suggestions"
189.           aria-expanded={isOpen}
190.           aria-label="Search NSE stock"
191.           autoComplete="off"
192.           className="search-input"
193.           name="symbol"
194.           onBlur={() => {
195.             clearBlurTimeout();
196.             blurTimeoutRef.current = setTimeout(() => setIsOpen(false), 120);
197.           }}
198.           onChange={(event) => {
199.             setQuery(event.target.value);
200.             if (!isOpen && event.target.value.trim()) {
201.               setIsOpen(true);
202.             }
203.           }}
204.           onFocus={() => {
205.             clearBlurTimeout();
206. 
207.             if (normalizeQuery(query)) {
208.               setIsOpen(true);
209.             }
210.           }}
211.           onKeyDown={(event) => {
212.             if (!suggestions.length) {
213.               if (event.key === "Escape") {
214.                 setIsOpen(false);
215.               }
216. 
217.               return;
218.             }
219. 
220.             if (event.key === "ArrowDown") {
221.               event.preventDefault();
222.               setIsOpen(true);
223.               setActiveIndex((current) => Math.min(current + 1, suggestions.length - 1));
224.               return;
225.             }
226. 
227.             if (event.key === "ArrowUp") {
228.               event.preventDefault();
229.               setIsOpen(true);
230.               setActiveIndex((current) => (current <= 0 ? 0 : current - 1));
231.               return;
232.             }
233. 
234.             if (event.key === "Enter" && isOpen && activeSuggestion) {
235.               event.preventDefault();
236.               analyzeSelection(activeSuggestion.symbol);
237.               return;
238.             }
239. 
240.             if (event.key === "Escape") {
241.               setIsOpen(false);
242.             }
243.           }}
244.           placeholder="Search symbol, e.g. RELIANCE, INFY, ASIANPAINT"
245.           type="text"
246.           value={query}
247.         />
248. 
249.         {isOpen && normalizeQuery(query) ? (
250.           <div className="search-suggestions" id="stock-search-suggestions" role="listbox">
251.             {suggestions.map((suggestion, index) => (
252.               <button
253.                 aria-selected={index === activeIndex}
254.                 className={`search-suggestion${index === activeIndex ? " active" : ""}`}
255.                 id={`stock-search-option-${suggestion.symbol}`}
256.                 key={`${suggestion.symbol}-${suggestion.companyName}`}
257.                 onClick={() => analyzeSelection(suggestion.symbol)}
258.                 onMouseDown={(event) => {
259.                   clearBlurTimeout();
260.                   event.preventDefault();
261.                 }}
262.                 role="option"
263.                 type="button"
264.               >
265.                 <span className="search-suggestion-primary">
266.                   <strong className="search-suggestion-symbol">{suggestion.symbol}</strong>
267.                   <span className="search-suggestion-name">{suggestion.companyName}</span>
268.                 </span>
269.                 <span className="search-suggestion-meta">
270.                   {suggestion.sector}
271.                   {suggestion.industry ? ` · ${suggestion.industry}` : ""}
272.                 </span>
273.               </button>
274.             ))}
275. 
276.             {!suggestions.length && !isLoading && !errorMessage ? (
277.               <div className="search-suggestion-status">No matching NSE stocks found.</div>
278.             ) : null}
279. 
280.             {isLoading ? (
281.               <div className="search-suggestion-status">Loading live suggestions...</div>
282.             ) : null}
283. 
284.             {errorMessage ? (
285.               <div className="search-suggestion-status error">{errorMessage}</div>
286.             ) : null}
287.           </div>
288.         ) : null}
289.       </div>
290. 
291.       <button className="search-button" type="submit">
292.         Analyze stock
293.       </button>
294.       {initialQuery ? (
295.         <a className="search-clear" href="/">
296.           Clear
297.         </a>
298.       ) : null}
299.     </form>
300.   );
301. }
```

```
107.   "hold",
108.   "sell",
109.   "target",
110.   "rating",
111.   "upgrade",
112.   "downgrade",
113.   "overweight",
114.   "underweight",
115.   "outperform",
116.   "underperform"
117. ];
118. 
119. function decodeHtmlEntities(value: string) {
120.   return value
121.     .replace(/&amp;/g, "&")
122.     .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
123.     .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
124.     .replace(/&lt;/g, "<")
125.     .replace(/&gt;/g, ">");
126. }
127. 
128. function normalizeSearchSymbol(query: string) {
129.   return query.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
130. }
131. 
132. function parseIndianNumber(value: string | null) {
133.   if (!value) {
134.     return null;
135.   }
136. 
137.   const normalized = value.replace(/,/g, "").trim();
138.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(normalized);
139. 
140.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
141. }
142. 
143. function parsePercent(value: string | null) {
144.   if (!value) {
145.     return null;
146.   }
147. 
148.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(value.replace(/,/g, "").trim());
149. 
150.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
151. }
152. 
153. function textContent(regex: RegExp, text: string) {
154.   const match = regex.exec(text);
155.   return match?.[1] ?? null;
156. }
157. 
158. function titleTone(title: string) {
159.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
160.   const positiveHits = POSITIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
161.   const negativeHits = NEGATIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
162. 
163.   if (positiveHits > negativeHits) {
164.     return "positive" as const;
165.   }
166. 
167.   if (negativeHits > positiveHits) {
168.     return "negative" as const;
169.   }
170. 
171.   return "neutral" as const;
172. }
173. 
174. function headlineCategory(title: string) {
175.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
176. 
177.   if (ANALYST_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
178.     return "analyst" as const;
179.   }
180. 
181.   if (EARNINGS_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
182.     return "earnings" as const;
183.   }
184. 
185.   return "news" as const;
186. }
187. 
188. function sentimentScore(headlines: NewsHeadline[]) {
189.   if (!headlines.length) {
190.     return 50;
191.   }
192. 
193.   const toneTotal = headlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
194.     if (headline.tone === "positive") {
195.       return total + 1;
196.     }
197. 
198.     if (headline.tone === "negative") {
199.       return total - 1;
200.     }
201. 
202.     return total;
203.   }, 0);
204. 
205.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + (toneTotal / headlines.length) * 35));
206. }
207. 
208. function overallSentiment(score: number) {
209.   if (score >= 58) {
210.     return "Positive" as const;
211.   }
212. 
213.   if (score <= 42) {
214.     return "Negative" as const;
215.   }
216. 
217.   return "Neutral" as const;
218. }
219. 
220. async function fetchText(url: string) {
221.   const response = await fetch(url, {
222.     cache: "no-store",
223.     headers: {
224.       Accept: "text/html,application/xml,application/json",
225.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
226.     }
227.   });
228. 
229.   if (!response.ok) {
230.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
231.   }
232. 
233.   return response.text();
234. }
235. 
236. async function fetchJson<T>(url: string) {
237.   const response = await fetch(url, {
238.     cache: "no-store",
239.     headers: {
240.       Accept: "application/json",
241.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
242.     }
243.   });
244. 
245.   if (!response.ok) {
246.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
247.   }
248. 
249.   return (await response.json()) as T;
250. }
251. 
252. function isNseEquityQuote(quote: YahooSearchQuote): quote is YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string } {
253.   return quote.exchange === "NSI" && quote.quoteType === "EQUITY" && Boolean(quote.symbol);
254. }
255. 
256. function toCompanyLookup(quote: YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string }): CompanyLookup {
257.   const normalizedSymbol = normalizeSearchSymbol(quote.symbol);
258. 
259.   return {
260.     symbol: normalizedSymbol,
261.     yahooSymbol: quote.symbol,
262.     companyName: quote.longname ?? quote.shortname ?? normalizedSymbol,
263.     sector: quote.sectorDisp ?? quote.sector ?? "Unknown",
264.     industry: quote.industryDisp ?? quote.industry ?? undefined
265.   };
266. }
267. 
268. function searchRank(lookup: CompanyLookup, normalizedQuery: string) {
269.   const symbol = lookup.symbol;
270.   const companyName = lookup.companyName.toUpperCase();
271. 
272.   if (symbol === normalizedQuery) {
273.     return 0;
274.   }
275. 
276.   if (symbol.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
277.     return 1;
278.   }
279. 
280.   if (companyName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
281.     return 2;
282.   }
283. 
284.   if (companyName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {
285.     return 3;
286.   }
287. 
288.   return 4;
289. }
290. 
291. async function searchNseQuotes(query: string, limit: number) {
292.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
293. 
294.   if (!normalized) {
295.     return [];
296.   }
297. 
298.   const payload = await fetchJson<YahooSearchResponse>(
299.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}&quotesCount=${Math.max(limit * 2, 8)}&newsCount=0&enableFuzzyQuery=true`
300.   );
301.   const matches = (payload.quotes ?? [])
302.     .filter(isNseEquityQuote)
303.     .map(toCompanyLookup)
304.     .sort((left, right) => {
305.       const leftRank = searchRank(left, normalized);
306.       const rightRank = searchRank(right, normalized);
307. 
308.       if (leftRank !== rightRank) {
309.         return leftRank - rightRank;
310.       }
311. 
312.       return left.symbol.localeCompare(right.symbol);
313.     });
314.   const deduped: CompanyLookup[] = [];
315.   const seen = new Set<string>();
316. 
317.   for (const match of matches) {
318.     if (seen.has(match.symbol)) {
319.       continue;
320.     }
321. 
322.     seen.add(match.symbol);
323.     deduped.push(match);
324. 
325.     if (deduped.length >= limit) {
326.       break;
327.     }
328.   }
329. 
330.   return deduped;
331. }
332. 
333. export async function lookupNseSuggestions(
334.   query: string,
335.   limit = 8
336. ): Promise<StockSearchSuggestion[]> {
337.   return (await searchNseQuotes(query, limit)).map(({ symbol, companyName, sector, industry }) => ({
338.     symbol,
339.     companyName,
340.     sector,
```

</details>

```
27. }
```

```
1. @echo off

26. 
27. call :is_port_listening
28. if not errorlevel 1 (
29.   echo The app is already running. Opening browser...
30.   start "" "%APP_URL%"
31.   exit /b 0
32. )
33. 
34. echo Starting the dashboard server in a new window...
35. start "%SERVER_TITLE%" cmd /k "cd /d ""%ROOT_DIR%"" && npm run dev:web"
36. 
37. echo Waiting for the app to become available...
38. set /a ATTEMPTS=0
39. 
40. :wait_for_server
41. call :is_port_listening
42. if not errorlevel 1 goto open_browser
43. 
44. set /a ATTEMPTS+=1
45. if !ATTEMPTS! GEQ 30 (
46.   echo The app did not start in time.
47.   echo Check the "%SERVER_TITLE%" window for errors.
48.   pause
49.   exit /b 1
50. )
51. 
52. timeout /t 2 /nobreak >nul
53. goto wait_for_server
54. 
55. :open_browser
56. echo Opening browser...
57. start "" "%APP_URL%"
58. exit /b 0
59. 
60. :is_port_listening
61. netstat -ano | findstr /r /c:":3000" | findstr /c:"LISTENING" >nul
62. exit /b %errorlevel%
63. 
64.
```
