```
453. }
454. 
455. function pctChange(current: number, previous: number) {
456.   if (!Number.isFinite(current) || !Number.isFinite(previous) || previous === 0) {
457.     return null;
458.   }
459. 
460.   return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100;
461. }
462. 
463. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
464.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
465.     return 0.5;
466.   }
467. 
468.   if (max === min) {
469.     return 0.5;
470.   }
471. 
472.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
473. }
474. 
475. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
476.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
477. }
478. 
479. function percentText(value: number | null) {
480.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
481.     return "n/a";
482.   }
483. 
484.   const prefix = value > 0 ? "+" : "";
485.   return `${prefix}${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
486. }
487. 
488. function ratioText(value: number | null) {
489.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
490.     return "n/a";
491.   }
492. 
493.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}x`;
494. }
495. 
496. function priceText(value: number | null) {
497.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
498.     return "n/a";
499.   }
500. 
501.   return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;
502. }
503. 
504. function compactNumber(value: number | null) {
505.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
506.     return "n/a";
507.   }
508. 
509.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
510.     notation: "compact",
511.     maximumFractionDigits: 1
512.   }).format(value);
513. }
514. 
515. function croreText(value: number | null) {
516.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
517.     return "n/a";
518.   }
519. 
520.   return `${new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
521.     maximumFractionDigits: 0
522.   }).format(value)} Cr`;
523. }
524. 
525. function marketCapBucketFromCrore(value: number | null) {
526.   if (value === null) {
527.     return "Large Cap";
528.   }
529. 
530.   if (value >= 100000) {
531.     return "Large Cap";
532.   }
533. 
534.   if (value >= 30000) {
535.     return "Mid Cap";
536.   }
537. 
538.   return "Small Cap";
539. }
540. 
541. function liquidityTierFromVolume(volume: number | null) {
542.   if (volume === null) {
543.     return "Tier 2";
544.   }
545. 
546.   if (volume >= 5000000) {
547.     return "Tier 1";
548.   }
549. 
550.   if (volume >= 1000000) {
551.     return "Tier 2";
552.   }
553. 
554.   return "Tier 3";
555. }
556. 
557. function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {
558.   if (values.length < period) {
559.     return null;
560.   }
```

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve expanded the model. Next I’m making the data loader backward-compatible and deploy-friendly so old cached/sample files won’t break the new horizon, and so free hosts don’t depend on persistent local disk writes.

```
1467. function buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(
1468.   date: string,
1469.   liveSeries: MarketSeries[],
1470.   benchmark: MarketSeries,
1471.   learningMap?: StopLossLearningMap
1472. ) {
1473.   const recommendations = liveSeries
1474.     .map((series) => {
1475.       const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
1476. 
1477.       if (index === undefined) {
1478.         return null;
1479.       }
1480. 
1481.       const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
1482. 
1483.       if (!metrics) {
1484.         return null;
1485.       }
1486. 
1487.       const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
1488. 
1489.       return {
1490.         symbol: series.symbol,
1491.         companyName: series.companyName,
1492.         sector: series.sector,
1493.         industry: series.industry,
1494.         marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
1495.         liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
1496.         currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
1497.         fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
1498.         sentiment: series.sentiment,
1499.         profiles: {
1500.           single_day: buildTradePlan(
1501.             enrichedMetrics,
1502.             series,
1503.             "single_day",
1504.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.single_day ?? null
1505.           ),
1506.           swing: buildTradePlan(
1507.             enrichedMetrics,
1508.             series,
1509.             "swing",
1510.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.swing ?? null
1511.           ),
1512.           position: buildTradePlan(
1513.             enrichedMetrics,
1514.             series,
1515.             "position",
1516.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.position ?? null
1517.           ),
1518.           long_term: buildTradePlan(
1519.             enrichedMetrics,
1520.             series,
1521.             "long_term",
1522.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.long_term ?? null
1523.           )
1524.         }
1525.       } satisfies StockAnalysis;
1526.     })
1527.     .filter((recommendation): recommendation is StockAnalysis => recommendation !== null);
1528. 
1529.   return applyRanking(recommendations);
1530. }
1531. 
1532. function resolveOutcomeResult(
1533.   openPrice: number,
1534.   targetPrice: number,
1535.   stopLoss: number,
1536.   targetHit: boolean,
1537.   stopHit: boolean
1538. ): OutcomeResult {
1539.   if (targetHit && stopHit) {
1540.     return Math.abs(openPrice - stopLoss) <= Math.abs(targetPrice - openPrice)
1541.       ? "stop_loss_hit"
1542.       : "target_hit";
1543.   }
1544. 
1545.   return targetHit ? "target_hit" : "stop_loss_hit";
1546. }
1547. 
1548. function volumeContextText(currentVolume: number, baselineVolume: number | null) {
1549.   if (baselineVolume === null || !Number.isFinite(baselineVolume) || baselineVolume <= 0) {
1550.     return "";
1551.   }
1552. 
1553.   if (currentVolume >= baselineVolume * 1.25) {
1554.     return " on above-average volume";
1555.   }
1556. 
1557.   if (currentVolume <= baselineVolume * 0.75) {
1558.     return " on light volume";
1559.   }
1560. 
1561.   return " on normal volume";
1562. }
1563. 
1564. function describeOutcomeNote(
1565.   series: MarketSeries,
1566.   batchIndex: number,
1567.   triggerIndex: number,
1568.   result: OutcomeResult,
1569.   entryPrice: number,
1570.   targetPrice: number,
1571.   stopLoss: number
1572. ) {
1573.   const bar = series.bars[triggerIndex];
1574.   const baselineVolume = average(
1575.     series.bars
1576.       .slice(Math.max(0, batchIndex - 10), batchIndex + 1)
1577.       .map((historyBar) => historyBar.volume)
1578.       .filter((volume) => volume > 0)
1579.   );
1580.   const volumeText = volumeContextText(bar.volume, baselineVolume);
1581. 
1582.   if (result === "target_hit") {
1583.     if (bar.open >= targetPrice) {
1584.       return `The stock opened through the target on ${bar.date}, confirming strong follow-through${volumeText}.`;
1585.     }
```

```
1797. 
1798. function latestCommonTradingDate(series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1799.   for (let index = series.bars.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
1800.     const date = series.bars[index].date;
1801. 
1802.     if (benchmark.indexByDate.has(date)) {
1803.       return date;
1804.     }
1805.   }
1806. 
1807.   return null;
1808. }
1809. 
1810. function buildSearchStockForDate(date: string, series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries) {
1811.   const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
1812. 
1813.   if (index === undefined) {
1814.     return null;
1815.   }
1816. 
1817.   const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
1818. 
1819.   if (!metrics) {
1820.     return null;
1821.   }
1822. 
1823.   const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
1824.   const learningMap = deriveStopLossLearning(
1825.     series.symbol === BENCHMARK.symbol ? [] : []
1826.   );
1827.   const buildSearchPlan = (horizon: HorizonId) => {
1828.     const plan = buildTradePlan(enrichedMetrics, series, horizon, learningMap.get(series.symbol)?.[horizon] ?? null);
1829. 
1830.     return {
1831.       ...plan,
1832.       rank: 1,
1833.       isRecommended: (plan.score ?? 0) >= HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].minimumScore
1834.     };
1835.   };
1836. 
1837.   return {
1838.     symbol: series.symbol,
1839.     companyName: series.companyName,
1840.     sector: series.sector,
1841.     industry: series.industry,
1842.     marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
1843.     liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
1844.     currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
1845.     fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
1846.     sentiment: series.sentiment,
1847.     profiles: {
1848.       single_day: buildSearchPlan("single_day"),
1849.       swing: buildSearchPlan("swing"),
1850.       position: buildSearchPlan("position"),
1851.       long_term: buildSearchPlan("long_term")
1852.     }
1853.   } satisfies StockAnalysis;
1854. }
1855. 
1856. function searchVerdict(stock: StockAnalysis) {
1857.   const recommendedHorizons = (Object.keys(HORIZON_SETTINGS) as HorizonId[]).filter(
1858.     (horizon) => stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
1859.   );
1860. 
1861.   if (!recommendedHorizons.length) {
1862.     return {
1863.       recommendedHorizons,
1864.       shouldConsider: false,
1865.       verdict:
1866.         "Do not prioritize this stock right now. Its current technical, fundamental, and sentiment mix is below the recommendation threshold across all supported horizons."
1867.     };
1868.   }
1869. 
1870.   const labels = recommendedHorizons.map((horizon) => HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label.toLowerCase());
1871. 
1872.   return {
1873.     recommendedHorizons,
1874.     shouldConsider: true,
1875.     verdict: `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(", ")} setups. The current score clears the model threshold for those horizons.`
1876.   };
1877. }
1878. 
1879. function fallbackDataSourceInfo(
1880.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1881.   detail: string,
1882.   analyzedSymbols: number
1883. ): DataSourceInfo {
1884.   return {
1885.     mode,
1886.     provider: mode === "cached" ? "Local generated snapshot" : "Sample fallback dataset",
1887.     asOf: new Date().toISOString(),
1888.     detail,
1889.     analyzedSymbols
1890.   };
1891. }
1892. 
1893. async function buildLiveDataset() {
1894.   const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
1895.   const liveSeries = (await Promise.all(MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry)))).filter(
1896.     (series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null
1897.   );
1898. 
1899.   if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
1900.     return null;
1901.   }
1902. 
1903.   const latestDate = benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date;
1904. 
1905.   if (!latestDate) {
1906.     return null;
1907.   }
1908. 
1909.   const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(latestDate, liveSeries, benchmark);
1910. 
1911.   if (currentRecommendations.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
1912.     return null;
1913.   }
1914. 
1915.   const dataset: RecommendationDataset = {
1916.     market: "Indian Equities",
1917.     exchange: "NSE",
1918.     universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,
1919.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
1920.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,
1921.       liveSeries.length
1922.     ),
1923.     profiles: PROFILE_LIST,
1924.     currentBatch: {
1925.       batchDate: latestDate,
1926.       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1927.       recommendations: currentRecommendations
1928.     },
1929.     history: buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark)
1930.   };
1931. 
1932.   await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);
1933. 
1934.   return dataset;
1935. }
1936. 
1937. function annotateFallbackDataset(
1938.   dataset: RecommendationDataset,
1939.   mode: "cached" | "sample",
1940.   detail: string
1941. ): RecommendationDataset {
1942.   return {
1943.     ...dataset,
1944.     dataSource: fallbackDataSourceInfo(mode, detail, dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.length)
1945.   };
1946. }
1947. 
1948. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
1949.   const liveDataset = await buildLiveDataset();
1950. 
1951.   if (liveDataset) {
1952.     return liveDataset;
1953.   }
1954. 
1955.   const cachedDataset = await readGeneratedDataset();
1956. 
1957.   if (cachedDataset) {
1958.     return annotateFallbackDataset(
1959.       cachedDataset,
1960.       "cached",
1961.       `Using the last successful live snapshot from ${cachedDataset.currentBatch.generatedAt}.`
1962.     );
1963.   }
1964. 
1965.   return annotateFallbackDataset(
1966.     await readSampleDataset(),
1967.     "sample",
1968.     "Live market requests failed and no cached snapshot was available, so the demo dataset is being shown."
1969.   );
1970. }
1971. 
1972. export async function analyzeSearchSymbol(query: string): Promise<SearchAnalysisResult | null> {
1973.   const trimmedQuery = query.trim();
1974. 
1975.   if (!trimmedQuery) {
1976.     return null;
1977.   }
1978. 
1979.   try {
1980.     const lookup = await lookupNseSymbol(trimmedQuery);
1981. 
1982.     if (!lookup) {
1983.       return {
1984.         status: "not_found",
1985.         query: trimmedQuery,
1986.         symbol: trimmedQuery.toUpperCase(),
1987.         companyName: trimmedQuery.toUpperCase(),
1988.         sector: "Unknown",
1989.         industry: undefined,
1990.         shouldConsider: false,
1991.         verdict: "The symbol could not be resolved to an NSE-listed equity.",
1992.         recommendedHorizons: [],
1993.         stock: null,
1994.         message: "Try an NSE trading symbol such as RELIANCE, INFY, or ICICIBANK."
1995.       };
1996.     }
1997. 
1998.     const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
1999.     const series = await fetchSeries({
2000.       symbol: lookup.symbol,
2001.       yahooSymbol: lookup.yahooSymbol,
2002.       fallbackCompanyName: lookup.companyName,
2003.       sector: lookup.sector,
2004.       industry: lookup.industry,
2005.       marketCapBucket: "Large Cap",
2006.       liquidityTier: "Tier 2"
2007.     });
2008. 
2009.     if (!benchmark || !series) {
2010.       return {
2011.         status: "error",
2012.         query: trimmedQuery,
2013.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2014.         companyName: lookup.companyName,
2015.         sector: lookup.sector,
2016.         industry: lookup.industry,
2017.         shouldConsider: false,
2018.         verdict: "Live analysis could not be completed for this symbol.",
2019.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2020.         stock: null,
2021.         message: "The live chart or benchmark request failed while evaluating this stock."
2022.       };
2023.     }
2024. 
2025.     const analysisDate = latestCommonTradingDate(series, benchmark);
2026. 
2027.     if (!analysisDate) {
2028.       return {
2029.         status: "error",
2030.         query: trimmedQuery,
2031.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2032.         companyName: lookup.companyName,
2033.         sector: lookup.sector,
2034.         industry: lookup.industry,
2035.         shouldConsider: false,
2036.         verdict: "Live analysis could not be completed for this symbol.",
2037.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2038.         stock: null,
2039.         message: "The stock does not have enough overlapping benchmark history for scoring."
2040.       };
2041.     }
2042. 
2043.     const stock = buildSearchStockForDate(analysisDate, series, benchmark);
2044. 
2045.     if (!stock) {
2046.       return {
2047.         status: "error",
2048.         query: trimmedQuery,
2049.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2050.         companyName: lookup.companyName,
2051.         sector: lookup.sector,
2052.         industry: lookup.industry,
2053.         shouldConsider: false,
2054.         verdict: "Live analysis could not be completed for this symbol.",
2055.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2056.         stock: null,
2057.         message: "There was not enough price history to compute the required signals."
2058.       };
2059.     }
2060.
```

plan.expectedReturnPct;\n+}\n+\n+function isRecommendedPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan | { isRecommended?: boolean }) {\n+  return plan.isRecommended ?? true;\n+}\n+\n+function historyDecisionLabel(result: \"target_hit\" | \"stop_loss_hit\" | \"open\") {\n+  switch (result) {\n+    case \"target_hit\":\n+      return \"Achieved\";\n+    case \"stop_loss_hit\":\n+      return \"Not achieved\";\n+    default:\n+      return \"Open\";\n+  }\n+}\n+\n+function SignalSection({\n+  title,\n+  signals\n+}: {\n+  title: string;\n+  signals?: Signal[];\n+}) {\n+  if (!signals?.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return (\n+    <section className=\"card\">\n+      <h3>{title}</h3>\n+      <div className=\"signal-grid\">\n+        {signals.map((signal) => (\n+          <div key={`${title}-${signal.name}`} className=\"signal-row\">\n+            <span className=\"signal-name\">{signal.name}</span>\n+            <strong>{signal.value}</strong>\n+          </div>\n+        ))}\n+      </div>\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n+\n+function EmptyState({ message }: { message: string }) {\n+  return <div className=\"empty-state\">{message}</div>;\n+}\n+\n+export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {\n+  const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? \"single_day\");\n+  const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>(\"\");\n+  const [selectedHistoryBatchDate, setSelectedHistoryBatchDate] = useState<string>(\n+    data.history[0]?.batchDate ?? \"\"\n+  );\n+\n+  useEffect(() => {\n+    if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {\n+      setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? \"single_day\");\n+    }\n+  }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);\n+\n+  const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];\n+\n+  const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {\n+    const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(\n+      (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])\n+    );\n+    const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));\n+\n+    return recommended.length ? recommended : sorted.slice(0, 5);\n+  }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);\n+\n+  useEffect(() => {\n+    if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {\n+      setSelectedSymbol(\"\");\n+      return;\n+    }\n+\n+    if (!visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {\n+      setSelectedSymbol(visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);\n+    }\n+  }, [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);\n+\n+  useEffect(() => {\n+    if (!data.history.some((batch) => batch.batchDate === selectedHistoryBatchDate)) {\n+      setSelectedHistoryBatchDate(data.history[0]?.batchDate ?? (\n+                      stockHistory.map((row) => (\n+                        <tr key={`${row.symbol}-${row.batchDate}-${row.horizon}`}>\n+                          <td>{row.batchDate}</td>\n+                          <td>\n+                            <span className={`result-chip ${outcomeClass(row.outcome.result)}`}>\n+                              {outcomeLabel(row.outcome.result)}\n+                            </span>\n+                          </td>\n+                          <td>\n+                            <div className=\"table-note\">{row.outcome.notes}</div>\n+                          </td>\n+                          <td>{`${row.outcome.returnPct.toFixed(2)}%`}</td>\n+                          <td>{row.outcome.holdingDays}</td>\n+                          <td>{formatPrice(row.entryPrice)}</td>\n+                          <td>{formatPrice(row.targetPrice)}</td>\n+                          <td>{formatPrice(row.stopLoss)}</td>\n+                        </tr>\n+                      ))\n+                    ) : (\n+                      <tr>\n+                        <td colSpan={8}>\n+                          <EmptyState message=\"No previous reviewed recommendations were available for this stock in the selected horizon.\" />\n+                        </td>\n+                      </tr>\n+                    )}\n+                  </tbody>\n+                </table>\n+              </div>\n+            </section>\n+          </aside>\n+        ) : null}\n+      </div>\n+    </main>\n+  );\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

</details>

<details>
<summary>130 lines</summary>

```
1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. 
4. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
5. 
6. from .analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
7. from .schemas import (
8.     HorizonId,
9.     RecommendationDataset,
10.     RecommendationSummary,
11.     StockAnalysis,
12.     StockDetailResponse,
13. )
14. 
15. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
16. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
17. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
18. 
19. app = FastAPI(
20.     title="Recommendation API",
21.     version="0.1.0",
22.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
23. )
24. 
25. 
26. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
27.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
28.     payload = json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
29.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
30. 
31. 
32. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
33.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
34. 
35.     return RecommendationSummary(
36.         symbol=stock.symbol,
37.         companyName=stock.companyName,
38.         sector=stock.sector,
39.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
40.         horizon=horizon,
41.         score=plan.score,
42.         rank=plan.rank,
43.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
44.         conviction=plan.conviction,
45.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
46.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
47.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
48.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
49.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
50.         summary=plan.summary,
51.     )
52. 
53. 
54. @app.get("/healthz")
55. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
56.     return {"status": "ok"}
57. 
58. 
59. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
60. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
61.     dataset = load_dataset()
62.     ordered_recommendations = sorted(
63.         dataset.currentBatch.recommendations,
64.         key=lambda stock: (
65.             stock.profiles[horizon].score
66.             if stock.profiles[horizon].score is not None
67.             else stock.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct
68.         ),
69.         reverse=True,
70.     )
71.     recommended_rows = [
72.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
73.         for stock in ordered_recommendations
74.         if stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
75.     ]
76.     rows = recommended_rows or [
77.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
78.         for stock in ordered_recommendations[:5]
79.     ]
80.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
81. 
82.     return {
83.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
84.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
85.         "market": dataset.market,
86.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
87.         "universe": dataset.universe,
88.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
89.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
90.         "recommendations": rows,
91.     }
92. 
93. 
94. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
95. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
96.     dataset = load_dataset()
97.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
98. 
99.     return {
100.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
101.         "performance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
102.     }
103. 
104. 
105. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
106. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
107.     dataset = load_dataset()
108.     stock = next(
109.         (
110.             row
111.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
112.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
113.         ),
114.         None,
115.     )
116. 
117.     if stock is None:
118.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
119. 
120.     return StockDetailResponse(
121.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
122.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
123.         market=dataset.market,
124.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
125.         universe=dataset.universe,
126.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
127.         stock=stock,
128.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
129.     )
130.
```

```
1. import unittest
2. 
3. from app.analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
4. from app.main import load_dataset
5. 
6. 
7. class RecommendationAnalyticsTests(unittest.TestCase):
8.     @classmethod
9.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
10.         cls.dataset = load_dataset(prefer_generated=False)
11. 
12.     def test_swing_daily_performance_counts_successes_and_failures(self) -> None:
13.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "swing")
14.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
15. 
16.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 2)
17.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
18.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 3)
19.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 0)
20.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 66.67, places=2)
21. 
22.     def test_position_success_rate_ignores_open_trades(self) -> None:
23.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "position")
24.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
25. 
26.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 1)
27.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
28.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 1)
29.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 2)
30.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 50.0, places=2)
31. 
32.     def test_long_term_daily_performance_is_pending_when_nothing_is_closed(self) -> None:
33.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "long_term")
34.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
35. 
36.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 3)
37.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 0)
38.         self.assertIsNone(latest.successRate)
39.         self.assertIsNone(latest.averageReturnPct)
40. 
41.     def test_stock_history_filters_by_symbol_in_latest_first_order(self) -> None:
42.         history = build_stock_history(self.dataset, "RELIANCE", "swing")
43. 
44.         self.assertEqual(
45.             [entry.batchDate for entry in history],
46.             ["2026-04-14", "2026-04-11", "2026-04-10"],
47.         )
48.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.symbol == "RELIANCE" for entry in history))
49. 
50. 
51. if __name__ == "__main__":
52.     unittest.main()
53.
```

</details>

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\app\\api\\healthz\\route.ts\n+import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n+\n+export function GET() {\n+  return NextResponse.json({\n+    status: \"ok\",\n+    service: \"web\",\n+    runtime: \"nextjs\"\n+  });\n+}\n*** Add File: C:\\copilot\\render.yaml\n+services:\n+  - type: web\n+    name: stock-recommendation-web\n+    env: node\n+    plan: free\n+    rootDir: .\n+    buildCommand: npm install && npm run build:web\n+    startCommand: npm run start:web\n+    autoDeploy: true\n+\n+  - type: web\n+    name: stock-recommendation-api\n+    env: python\n+    plan: free\n+    rootDir: services/api\n+    buildCommand: pip install .\n+    startCommand: uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT\n+    autoDeploy: true\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\README.md\n@@\n-- **MVP horizons:** swing, position, and long-term\n+- **MVP horizons:** single-day, swing, position, and long-term\n@@\n - Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, volatility, lightweight fundamentals, and headline-based sentiment / earnings / analyst signals.\n+- The live model now also applies **stop-loss learning**: repeated recent stop-loss failures reduce future model aggressiveness for similar setups.\n - The app also backtests prior daily batches from the same historical price series so the dashboard can maintain horizon-specific success rates.\n - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.\n-- If live requests fail later, the app falls back to that cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.\n+- If persistent disk writes are not available on the host, the app falls back to **in-memory caching** for the running instance.\n+- If live requests fail later, the app falls back to the cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.\n@@\n 3. The detail view explains:\n     - key factors behind the recommendation\n     - recommended entry price\n     - target price\n     - stop-loss\n     - risk-reward profile\n-    - horizon-specific analysis for swing, position, and long-term modes\n+    - horizon-specific analysis for single-day, swing, position, and long-term modes\n 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, shows whether each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.\n 5. The search panel offers **autocomplete suggestions while you type**, then analyzes the selected NSE stock and says whether it should be **considered or avoided** for each supported horizon.\n+6. Historical stop-loss outcomes now include a clearer explanation of **why the stop-loss was hit**, and the live model uses recent stop-loss history to reduce future aggressiveness for fragile setups.\n@@\n ## Analytics test command\n \n - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.\n+\n+## Deployment and free hosting\n+\n+### Production-readiness changes in this repo\n+\n+- The Next.js app now exposes `GET /api/healthz` for simple uptime checks.\n+- The live dataset loader supports **in-memory runtime caching**, which is friendlier to serverless or ephemeral free hosts.\n+- `render.yaml` is included so you can deploy both the web app and the FastAPI service on Render with minimal setup.\n+\n+### Recommended free hosting options\n+\n+1. **Vercel** for the Next.js app only. This is the easiest choice for the dashboard because it handles Next.js SSR well. In Vercel, set the project root to `apps/web`.\n+2. **Render** if you want both the Next.js app and the FastAPI API hosted together for free. The included `render.yaml` is aimed at this option. Render free web services can sleep when idle, so expect cold starts.\n+3. **Cloudflare Pages** if you want a very low-cost global edge deployment for the web app and are comfortable with more setup than Vercel.\n+\n+For this repository as it exists today, **Vercel** is the simplest free host for the dashboard, while **Render** is the simplest free option if you want to run both services from one repo.\n*** End Patch\n"
```

<summary>23 lines</summary>

```
1. import { Dashboard } from "@/components/dashboard";
2. import type { SearchAnalysisResult } from "@/lib/types";
3. import { analyzeSearchSymbol, loadRecommendationData } from "@/lib/recommendation-data";
4. 
5. export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
6. 
7. type PageProps = {
8.   searchParams?: Promise<{
9.     symbol?: string;
10.   }>;
11. };
12. 
13. export default async function Page({ searchParams }: PageProps) {
14.   const params = (await searchParams) ?? {};
15.   const query = typeof params.symbol === "string" ? params.symbol : "";
16.   const dataset = await loadRecommendationData();
17.   const searchedAnalysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null = query
18.     ? await analyzeSearchSymbol(query, dataset)
19.     : null;
20. 
21.   return <Dashboard data={dataset} searchedAnalysis={searchedAnalysis} />;
22. }
23.
```

</details>

```
1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. from typing import Any
4. 
5. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
6. 
7. from .analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
8. from .schemas import (
9.     HorizonId,
10.     RecommendationDataset,
11.     RecommendationSummary,
12.     StockAnalysis,
13.     StockDetailResponse,
14. )
15. 
16. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
17. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
18. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
19. 
20. app = FastAPI(
21.     title="Recommendation API",
22.     version="0.1.0",
23.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
24. )
25. 
26. 
27. def _clamp(value: float, minimum: float, maximum: float) -> float:
28.     return min(max(value, minimum), maximum)
29. 
30. 
31. def _pct_change(current: float, previous: float) -> float:
32.     if previous == 0:
33.         return 0.0
34. 
35.     return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100
36. 
37. 
38. def _derive_single_day_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
39.     entry_price = round(float(plan["entryPrice"]), 2)
40.     target_pct = _clamp(
41.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["targetPrice"]), entry_price)) * 0.38,
42.         1.2,
43.         3.4,
44.     )
45.     stop_pct = _clamp(
46.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["stopLoss"]), entry_price)) * 0.45,
47.         0.7,
48.         1.9,
49.     )
50.     target_price = round(entry_price * (1 + target_pct / 100), 2)
51.     stop_loss = round(max(entry_price * (1 - stop_pct / 100), entry_price * 0.85), 2)
52. 
53.     return {
54.         **plan,
55.         "score": plan.get("score", 55),
56.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
57.         "entryPrice": entry_price,
58.         "targetPrice": target_price,
59.         "stopLoss": stop_loss,
60.         "expectedReturnPct": round(_pct_change(target_price, entry_price), 2),
61.         "riskReward": round((target_price - entry_price) / max(entry_price - stop_loss, 0.01), 2),
62.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. {plan['summary']}",
63.         "drivers": [
64.             "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
65.             *plan.get("drivers", []),
66.         ][:4],
67.         "technicalSignals": [
68.             {"name": "Compatibility mode", "value": "Derived from swing profile"},
69.             *plan.get("technicalSignals", []),
70.         ][:6],
71.         "riskSignals": plan.get("riskSignals")
72.         or [{"name": "Learning status", "value": "Awaiting refreshed live batch"}],
73.     }
74. 
75. 
76. def _derive_single_day_history(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
77.     outcome = plan["outcome"]
78. 
79.     return {
80.         **plan,
81.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
82.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. {plan['summary']}",
83.         "outcome": {
84.             **outcome,
85.             "notes": f"This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. {outcome['notes']}",
86.         },
87.     }
88. 
89. 
90. def _normalize_payload(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
91.     profile_lookup = {profile["id"]: profile for profile in payload.get("profiles", [])}
92.     payload["profiles"] = [
93.         profile_lookup.get("single_day", {"id": "single_day", "label": "Single-day", "window": "Next trading day"}),
94.         profile_lookup.get("swing", {"id": "swing", "label": "Swing", "window": "5-20 trading days"}),
95.         profile_lookup.get("position", {"id": "position", "label": "Position", "window": "20-60 trading days"}),
96.         profile_lookup.get("long_term", {"id": "long_term", "label": "Long-term", "window": "3-12 months"}),
97.     ]
98. 
99.     for recommendation in payload.get("currentBatch", {}).get("recommendations", []):
100.         profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
101.         if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
102.             profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_plan(profiles["swing"])
103. 
104.     for batch in payload.get("history", []):
105.         for recommendation in batch.get("recommendations", []):
106.             profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
107.             if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
108.                 profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_history(profiles["swing"])
109. 
110.     return payload
111. 
112. 
113. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
114.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
115.     payload = _normalize_payload(json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
116.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
117. 
118. 
119. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
120.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
121. 
122.     return RecommendationSummary(
123.         symbol=stock.symbol,
124.         companyName=stock.companyName,
125.         sector=stock.sector,
126.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
127.         horizon=horizon,
128.         score=plan.score,
129.         rank=plan.rank,
130.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
131.         conviction=plan.conviction,
132.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
133.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
134.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
135.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
136.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
137.         summary=plan.summary,
138.     )
139. 
140. 
141. @app.get("/healthz")
142. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
143.     return {"status": "ok"}
144. 
145. 
146. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
147. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
148.     dataset = load_dataset()
149.     ordered_recommendations = sorted(
150.         dataset.currentBatch.recommendations,
151.         key=lambda stock: (
152.             stock.profiles[horizon].score
153.             if stock.profiles[horizon].score is not None
154.             else stock.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct
155.         ),
156.         reverse=True,
157.     )
158.     recommended_rows = [
159.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
160.         for stock in ordered_recommendations
161.         if stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
162.     ]
163.     rows = recommended_rows or [
164.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
165.         for stock in ordered_recommendations[:5]
166.     ]
167.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
168. 
169.     return {
170.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
171.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
172.         "market": dataset.market,
173.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
174.         "universe": dataset.universe,
175.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
176.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
177.         "recommendations": rows,
178.     }
179. 
180.
```

</details>

```
703. async function generatedFilePath() {
704.   const samplePath = await findFirstReadableFile(sampleFileCandidates());
705.   const directory = samplePath
706.     ? resolveDataDirectoryFromSample(samplePath)
707.     : DATA_DIRECTORY_CANDIDATES[0];
708.

711.   return path.join(directory, GENERATED_FILE_NAME);
712. }
713. 
714. function cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(plan: RecommendationPlan): RecommendationPlan {
715.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS.single_day;
716.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(plan.entryPrice);
717.   const targetPct = clamp(
718.     Math.abs(pctChange(plan.targetPrice, plan.entryPrice) ?? plan.expectedReturnPct) * 0.38,
719.     settings.targetRangePct[0],
720.     settings.targetRangePct[1]
721.   );
722.   const stopPct = clamp(
723.     Math.abs(pctChange(plan.stopLoss, plan.entryPrice) ?? settings.stopRangePct[0]) * 0.45,
724.     settings.stopRangePct[0],
725.     settings.stopRangePct[1]
726.   );
727.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
728.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.85));
729. 
730.   return {
731.     ...plan,
732.     score: plan.score ?? 55,
733.     conviction: plan.conviction === "High" ? "Medium" : plan.conviction,
734.     entryPrice,
735.     targetPrice,
736.     stopLoss,
737.     expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? targetPct),
738.     riskReward: roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01)),
739.     summary: `Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. ${plan.summary}`,
740.     drivers: [
741.       "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
742.       ...plan.drivers
743.     ].slice(0, 4),
744.     technicalSignals: [
745.       { name: "Compatibility mode", value: "Derived from swing profile" },
746.       ...plan.technicalSignals
747.     ].slice(0, 6),
748.     riskSignals: plan.riskSignals ?? [
749.       { name: "Learning status", value: "Awaiting refreshed live batch" }
750.     ]
751.   };
752. }
753. 
754. function cloneSingleDayHistoricalPlanFromFallback(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan): HistoricalRecommendationPlan {
755.   return {
756.     ...plan,
757.     conviction: plan.conviction === "High" ? "Medium" : plan.conviction,
758.     summary: `Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. ${plan.summary}`,
759.     outcome: {
760.       ...plan.outcome,
761.       notes: `This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. ${plan.outcome.notes}`
762.     }
763.   };
764. }
765. 
766. function normalizeRecommendationProfiles(profiles: Record<string, RecommendationPlan>) {
767.   return {
768.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
769.     swing: profiles.swing,
770.     position: profiles.position,
771.     long_term: profiles.long_term
772.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, RecommendationPlan>;
773. }
774. 
775. function normalizeHistoricalProfiles(profiles: Record<string, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>) {
776.   return {
777.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayHistoricalPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
778.     swing: profiles.swing,
779.     position: profiles.position,
780.     long_term: profiles.long_term
781.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>;
782. }
783.

786.     ...dataset,
787.     profiles: HORIZON_ORDER.map(
788.       (horizon) =>
789.         dataset.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === horizon) ?? {
```

```
1. "use client";

28. function formatPrice(value: number) {
29.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
30.     style: "currency",
31.     currency: "INR",
32.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
33.     maximumFractionDigits: 2
34.   }).format(value);

38.   return new Date(value).toLocaleDateString("en-IN");

41. function formatDateTime(value: string) {
42.   return new Date(value).toLocaleString("en-IN");
43. }
44.

46.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
47. }
48. 
49. function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
50.   if (value === null) {
51.     return "Pending";

55. }

69.     case "stop_loss_hit":
70.       return "Not achieved";
71.     default:

106. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
107.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
108.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string>("");
109.   const [selectedHistoryBatchDate, setSelectedHistoryBatchDate] = useState<string>(
110.     data.history[0]?.batchDate ?? ""
111.   );
112. 
113.   useEffect(() => {
114.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
115.       setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
116.     }
117.   }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);
118. 
119.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
120. 
121.   const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {
122.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
123.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
124.     );
125.     const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));
126. 
127.     return recommended.length ? recommended : sorted.slice(0, 5);
128.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);
129. 
130.   useEffect(() => {
131.     if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {
132.       setSelectedSymbol("");
133.       return;
134.     }
135. 
136.     if (!visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {
137.       setSelectedSymbol(visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);
138.     }
139.   }, [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);
140. 
141.   useEffect(() => {
142.     if (!data.history.some((batch) => batch.batchDate === selectedHistoryBatchDate)) {
143.       setSelectedHistoryBatchDate(data.history[0]?.batchDate ?? "");
144.     }
145.   }, [data.history, selectedHistoryBatchDate]);
146. 
147.   const selectedStock = useMemo<StockAnalysis | undefined>(
148.     () => visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? visibleRecommendations[0],
149.     [selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]
150.   );
151. 
152.   const selectedPlan = selectedStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
153.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo<DailyPerformance[]>(
154.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
155.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
156.   );
157.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
158.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
159.     [dailyPerformance]
160.   );
161.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
162.     () =>
163.       selectedStock
164.         ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, selectedStock.symbol, activeHorizon)
165.         : [],
166.     [activeHorizon, data.history, selectedStock]
167.   );
168.   const selectedHistoryBatch =
169.     data.history.find((batch) => batch.batchDate === selectedHistoryBatchDate) ?? data.history[0];
170.   const selectedHistoryRecommendations = useMemo(() => {
171.     if (!selectedHistoryBatch) {
172.       return [];
173.     }
174. 
175.     return [...selectedHistoryBatch.recommendations]
176.       .filter((recommendation) => isRecommendedPlan(recommendation.profiles[activeHorizon]))
177.       .sort(
178.         (left, right) =>
179.           (left.profiles[activeHorizon].rank ?? 999) - (right.profiles[activeHorizon].rank ?? 999)
180.       );
181.   }, [activeHorizon, selectedHistoryBatch]);
182. 
183.   const searchedStock = searchedAnalysis?.stock;
184.   const searchedHorizonPlans = searchedStock
185.     ? data.profiles.map((profile) => ({
186.         horizon: profile.id,
187.         label: profile.label,
188.         plan: searchedStock.profiles[profile.id]
189.       }))
190.     : [];
191.   const localSearchSuggestions = useMemo(
192.     () =>
193.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => ({
194.         symbol: stock.symbol,
195.         companyName: stock.companyName,
196.         sector: stock.sector,
197.         industry: stock.industry
198.       })),
199.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations]
200.   );
201.   const recommendedCount = data.currentBatch.recommendations.filter((stock) =>
202.     isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon])
203.   ).length;
204. 
205.   return (
206.     <main className="shell">
207.       <section className="hero-grid">
208.         <article className="card hero-card">
209.           <span className="eyebrow">Institutional-style NSE dashboard</span>
210.           <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
211.           <p>
212.             This dashboard ranks liquid NSE stocks using technical structure, fundamentals,
213.             headline sentiment, earnings tone, analyst tone, stop-loss learning, and transparent
214.             trade-plan logic.
215.           </p>
216. 
217.           <div className="hero-meta">
218.             <span className="section-chip">{data.exchange}</span>
219.             {activeProfile ? (
220.               <span className="section-chip">
221.                 {activeProfile.label} · {activeProfile.window}
222.               </span>
223.             ) : null}
224.             {data.dataSource ? (
225.               <span className="section-chip">{data.dataSource.mode.toUpperCase()}</span>
226.             ) : null}
227.           </div>
228.         </article>
229. 
230.         <div className="hero-side">
231.           <section className="card run-card surface-card">
232.             <div className="metric-label">Latest batch</div>
233.             <div className="metric-value">{formatDateTime(data.currentBatch.generatedAt)}</div>
234.             <p className="footnote">
235.               {data.exchange} · {data.universe} · batch date {data.currentBatch.batchDate}
236.             </p>
237.             {data.dataSource ? (
238.               <p className="footnote">
239.                 Source: {data.dataSource.mode.toUpperCase()} · {data.dataSource.detail}
240.               </p>
241.             ) : null}
242.           </section>
243. 
244.           <section className="card search-card surface-card">
245.             <div className="section-title">
246.               <div>
247.                 <h2>Search and analyze any NSE stock</h2>
248.                 <div className="section-subtitle">
249.                   Type a symbol or company name to get live suggestions, then select one for
250.                   analysis.
251.                 </div>
252.               </div>
253.             </div>
254. 
255.             <StockSearchBox
256.               initialQuery={searchedAnalysis?.query ?? ""}
257.               localSuggestions={localSearchSuggestions}
258.             />
259.           </section>
260.         </div>
```

```
370.                 {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
371.                   <button
372.                     key={profile.id}
373.                     className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
374.                     onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
375.                     type="button"
376.                   >
377.                     {profile.label} · {profile.window}
378.                   </button>
379.                 ))}
380.               </div>
381.             </div>
382. 
383.             <div className="table-wrap">
384.               <table className="stock-table">
385.                 <thead>
386.                   <tr>
387.                     <th>Symbol</th>
388.                     <th>Company</th>
389.                     <th>Current market price</th>
390.                     <th>Sector</th>
391.                     <th>Conviction</th>
392.                     <th>Entry</th>
393.                     <th>Target</th>
394.                     <th>Stop-loss</th>
395.                     <th>Expected return</th>
396.                     <th>R:R</th>
397.                   </tr>
398.                 </thead>
399.                 <tbody>
400.                   {visibleRecommendations.map((stock) => {
401.                     const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
402. 
403.                     return (
404.                       <tr
405.                         key={stock.symbol}
406.                         className={`stock-row${stock.symbol === selectedStock?.symbol ? " active" : ""}`}
407.                         onClick={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}
408.                       >
409.                         <td>
410.                           <div className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</div>
411.                         </td>
412.                         <td>{stock.companyName}</td>
413.                         <td>{formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)}</td>
414.                         <td>{stock.sector}</td>
415.                         <td>
416.                           <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>
417.                             {plan.conviction}
418.                           </span>
419.                         </td>
420.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)}</td>
421.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</td>
422.                         <td>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</td>
423.                         <td>{plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%</td>
424.                         <td>{plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</td>
425.                       </tr>
426.                     );
427.                   })}
428.                 </tbody>
429.               </table>
430.             </div>
431.           </section>
432. 
433.           <div className="insight-grid">
434.             <section className="card">
435.               <div className="section-title">
436.                 <div>
437.                   <h2>Daily recommendation scorecard</h2>
438.                   <div className="section-subtitle">
439.                     Prior live batches are re-evaluated against subsequent price action.
440.                   </div>
441.                 </div>
442.               </div>
443. 
444.               <div className="table-wrap">
445.                 <table className="stock-table compact-table">
446.                   <thead>
447.                     <tr>
448.                       <th>Batch date</th>
449.                       <th>Published</th>
450.                       <th>Success rate</th>
451.                       <th>Successes</th>
452.                       <th>Failures</th>
453.                       <th>Open</th>
454.                       <th>Avg return</th>
455.                     </tr>
456.                   </thead>
457.                   <tbody>
458.                     {dailyPerformance.map((row) => (
459.                       <tr key={row.batchDate}>
460.                         <td>{row.batchDate}</td>
461.                         <td>{formatDate(row.publishedAt)}</td>
462.                         <td>{formatPercent(row.successRate)}</td>
463.                         <td>{row.successful}</td>
464.                         <td>{row.failed}</td>
465.                         <td>{row.open}</td>
466.                         <td>{formatPercent(row.averageReturnPct)}</td>
467.                       </tr>
468.                     ))}
469.                   </tbody>
470.                 </table>
471.               </div>
472.             </section>
473. 
474.             <section className="card">
475.               <div className="section-title">
476.                 <div>
477.                   <h2>Previous recommendation menu</h2>
478.                   <div className="section-subtitle">
479.                     Inspect whether each earlier call achieved its target or hit its stop-loss.
480.                   </div>
481.                 </div>
482.               </div>
483. 
484.               <div className="history-layout">
485.                 <div className="history-menu">
486.                   {data.history.map((batch) => (
487.                     <button
488.                       key={batch.batchDate}
489.                       className={`history-button${
490.                         batch.batchDate === selectedHistoryBatch?.batchDate ? " active" : ""
491.                       }`}
492.                       onClick={() => setSelectedHistoryBatchDate(batch.batchDate)}
493.                       type="button"
494.                     >
495.                       <strong>{batch.batchDate}</strong>
496.                       <span>{formatDate(batch.publishedAt)}</span>
497.                     </button>
498.                   ))}
499.                 </div>
500. 
501.                 <div className="history-panel">
502.                   {selectedHistoryBatch ? (
503.                     <>
504.                       <div className="section-subtitle history-caption">
505.                         {selectedHistoryBatch.batchDate} · {activeProfile?.label ?? "Active"} horizon
506.                       </div>
507. 
508.                       <div className="table-wrap">
509.                         <table className="stock-table compact-table">
510.                           <thead>
511.                             <tr>
512.                               <th>Symbol</th>
513.                               <th>Company</th>
514.                               <th>Outcome</th>
515.                               <th>Achieved?</th>
516.                               <th>Reason</th>
517.                               <th>Return</th>
518.                               <th>Entry</th>
519.                               <th>Target</th>
520.                               <th>Stop-loss</th>
521.                             </tr>
522.                           </thead>
523.                           <tbody>
524.                             {selectedHistoryRecommendations.length ? (
525.                               selectedHistoryRecommendations.map((recommendation) => {
526.                                 const plan = recommendation.profiles[activeHorizon];
527. 
528.                                 return (
529.                                   <tr key={`${selectedHistoryBatch.batchDate}-${recommendation.symbol}`}>
530.                                     <td>{recommendation.symbol}</td>
531.                                     <td>{recommendation.companyName}</td>
532.                                     <td>
533.                                       <span className={`result-chip ${outcomeClass(plan.outcome.result)}`}>
534.                                         {outcomeLabel(plan.outcome.result)}
535.                                       </span>
536.                                     </td>
537.                                     <td>{historyDecisionLabel(plan.outcome.result)}</td>
538.                                     <td>
539.                                       <div className="table-note">{plan.outcome.notes}</div>
540.                                     </td>
541.                                     <td>{`${plan.outcome.returnPct.toFixed(2)}%`}</td>
542.                                     <td>{formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)}</td>
543.                                     <td>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</td>
544.                                     <td>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</td>
545.                                   </tr>
546.                                 );
547.                               })
548.                             ) : (
549.                               <tr>
550.                                 <td colSpan={9}>
551.                                   <EmptyState message="No recommended stocks were recorded for this batch and horizon." />
552.                                 </td>
553.                               </tr>
554.                             )}
555.                           </tbody>
556.                         </table>
557.                       </div>
558.                     </>
559.                   ) : null}
560.                 </div>
561.               </div>
562.             </section>
563.           </div>
564.         </div>
565. 
566.         {selectedStock && selectedPlan ? (
567.           <aside className="detail-grid detail-rail">
568.             <section className="card detail-hero-card">
569.               <div className="detail-header">
570.                 <div>
571.                   <h2>
572.                     {selectedStock.companyName} ({selectedStock.symbol})
573.                   </h2>
574.                   <div className="detail-subhead">
575.                     {selectedStock.sector}
576.                     {selectedStock.industry ? ` · ${selectedStock.industry}` : ""}
577.                     {" · "}
578.                     {selectedStock.marketCapBucket} · {selectedStock.liquidityTier} · CMP{" "}
579.                     {formatPrice(selectedStock.currentMarketPrice)}
580.                   </div>
581.                 </div>
582. 
583.                 <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(selectedPlan.conviction)}`}>
584.                   {selectedPlan.conviction} conviction
585.                 </span>
586.               </div>
587. 
588.               <p className="summary">{selectedPlan.summary}</p>
589. 
590.               <div className="hero-meta">
591.                 <span className="section-chip">Score {selectedPlan.score?.toFixed(1) ?? "n/a"}</span>
592.                 <span className="section-chip">
593.                   Expected return {selectedPlan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%
594.                 </span>
595.                 <span className="section-chip">R:R {selectedPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</span>
596.               </div>
597. 
598.               {selectedStock.fundamentals ? (
599.                 <p className="footnote">{selectedStock.fundamentals.summary}</p>
600.               ) : null}
601.             </section>
602. 
603.             <section className="card">
604.               <h3>Trade plan</h3>
605.               <div className="stat-grid">
606.                 <article className="stat-card">
607.                   <div className="metric-label">Entry price</div>
608.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.entryPrice)}</strong>
609.                 </article>
610.                 <article className="stat-card">
611.                   <div className="metric-label">Target price</div>
612.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.targetPrice)}</strong>
613.                 </article>
614.                 <article className="stat-card">
615.                   <div className="metric-label">Stop-loss</div>
616.                   <strong>{formatPrice(selectedPlan.stopLoss)}</strong>
617.                 </article>
618.                 <article className="stat-card">
619.                   <div className="metric-label">Risk-reward</div>
620.                   <strong>{selectedPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}</strong>
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 263m 20s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**It’s much closer to production-ready now, but I couldn’t complete build/test sign-off here because this environment is missing `pwsh`.**

I updated the app to be **cleaner, responsive, and deployment-friendlier**:
- **UI:** reorganized the dashboard into clearer sections, improved the dark color system, and made it responsive across desktop, tablet, and mobile.
- **Model:** added a **single-day** horizon, added clearer **why stop-loss hit** reasoning in history, and added **stop-loss learning** so recent stop-loss patterns reduce future model aggressiveness.
- **Runtime/deployment:** added **in-memory caching** for hosts with ephemeral filesystems, added **`/api/healthz`** for the web app, added **`render.yaml`**, and updated the README with hosting guidance.

**Free hosting options:**
1. **Vercel** — best/easiest for the Next.js dashboard.
2. **Render** — easiest if you want to host both the dashboard and FastAPI; `render.yaml` is included.
3. **Cloudflare Pages** — good alternative for the web app if you want edge hosting and don’t mind more setup.

To fully sign it off, run:
`npm run build:web`  
`npm run test:api`

<sub>⏱️ 337m 42s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Right now it considers 10 NSE stocks plus 1 benchmark.**

The live stock universe is:
`RELIANCE, TCS, HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, INFY, SBIN, BHARTIARTL, LT, ITC, HINDUNILVR`
and it uses **Nifty 50** as the benchmark.

**If by resources you mean data sources, it currently uses 3 main live inputs:**
1. **Yahoo Finance** chart/search data
2. **Screener.in** fundamentals
3. **Google News RSS** sentiment/headline data

So the app is **not yet scanning the full NSE market**; it is still using a curated liquid watchlist.

<sub>⏱️ 342m 3s</sub>

### 👤 User

how about tradingview and other platforms

---

<sub>⏱️ 342m 53s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**TradingView is great for charting and alerts, but I would not use it as the core production data engine.** For a serious setup, use **TradingView for visualization**, and use **official broker/data APIs** for live prices and execution.

**Best use of each platform:**
1. **TradingView** — charts, indicators, watchlists, Pine-based signal visualization, alerts/webhooks
2. **Zerodha Kite / Upstox / Fyers / Angel One** — broker-connected live data and actual order execution
3. **Licensed market data providers / official exchange-friendly sources** — stable production-grade price feeds
4. **News/sentiment providers** — real news APIs for sentiment instead of only RSS/headline heuristics

**My recommendation for this app:**
- Keep the **Next.js dashboard**
- Add **TradingView-style charts** in the UI
- Use **broker APIs** for live market/execution layer
- Keep the **recommendation engine separate** on the backend
- Avoid depending only on scraped/free sources like Yahoo/Screener/Google News if you want real production reliability

**About other platforms:** sites like **TickerTape, Trendlyne, Moneycontrol** are useful for research, but they are usually better as reference tools than as a stable production backend unless they provide official APIs and licensing.

<sub>⏱️ 345m 7s</sub>

### ✅ `sql`

**Query ready todos**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.status\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\n  AND NOT EXISTS (\n    SELECT 1\n    FROM todo_deps td\n    JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n    WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n  )\nORDER BY t.created_at;"
}
```

</details>

```
1 row(s) returned:

| id | title | status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| build-backtesting | Build backtesting and evaluation | pending |
```

```
1. "use client";
2. 
3. import Link from "next/link";
4. import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";
5. import type { ReactNode } from "react";
6. 
7. const NAV_ITEMS = [
8.   {
9.     href: "/",
10.     label: "Dashboard"
11.   },
12.   {
13.     href: "/history",
14.     label: "Archive"
15.   }
16. ];
17. 
18. export function AppShell({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
19.   const pathname = usePathname();
20. 
21.   return (
22.     <div className="app-shell">
23.       <header className="app-shell-header">
24.         <div className="app-shell-inner">
25.           <div className="app-brand">
26.             <span className="app-brand-kicker">NSE research console</span>
27.             <div className="app-brand-title">Stock recommendation workspace</div>
28.           </div>
29. 
30.           <nav className="app-nav" aria-label="Primary">
31.             {NAV_ITEMS.map((item) => {
32.               const isActive =
33.                 item.href === "/"
34.                   ? pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith("/stocks/")
35.                   : pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`);
36. 
37.               return (
38.                 <Link
39.                   key={item.href}
40.                   href={item.href}
41.                   className={`app-nav-link${isActive ? " active" : ""}`}
42.                 >
43.                   {item.label}
44.                 </Link>
45.               );
46.             })}
47.           </nav>
48.         </div>
49.       </header>
50. 
51.       <div className="app-shell-body">{children}</div>
52.     </div>
53.   );
54. }
55.
```

</details>

```
507. .page-links,
508. .panel-actions {
509.   display: flex;
510.   flex-wrap: wrap;
511.   gap: 10px;
512. }
513. 
514. .panel-actions {
515.   margin-top: 16px;
516. }
517. 
518. .status-card .mini-metric-grid {
519.   margin-top: 18px;
520. }
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 345m 55s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found one thing to be careful about: the stylesheet has a lot of newer layout primitives, so I’m checking which classes the current dashboard is actually using before I insert the TradingView panel. That avoids stepping on any user-side layout work.
