"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\recommendation-data.ts\n@@\n function rangePosition(current: number, low: number, high: number) {\n   if (high <= low) {\n     return null;\n   }\n \n   return ((current - low) / (high - low)) * 100;\n }\n+\n+function recentExtreme(values: number[], lookback: number, mode: \"high\" | \"low\") {\n+  if (values.length <= lookback) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const window = values.slice(-(lookback + 1), -1);\n+\n+  if (!window.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return mode === \"high\" ? Math.max(...window) : Math.min(...window);\n+}\n+\n+function movingAverageSlope(values: number[], period: number, lookback: number) {\n+  if (values.length < period + lookback) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const currentAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values, period);\n+  const priorAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values.slice(0, -lookback), period);\n+\n+  if (currentAverage === null || priorAverage === null) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return pctChange(currentAverage, priorAverage);\n+}\n+\n+function averageBarRangePct(bars: PriceBar[]) {\n+  const percentages = bars\n+    .map((bar) => (bar.close !== 0 ? ((bar.high - bar.low) / bar.close) * 100 : null))\n+    .filter((value): value is number => value !== null && Number.isFinite(value));\n+\n+  return average(percentages);\n+}\n+\n+function rangeCompressionRatio(bars: PriceBar[], shortPeriod: number, longPeriod: number) {\n+  if (bars.length < longPeriod) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const shortAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-shortPeriod));\n+  const longAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-longPeriod));\n+\n+  if (shortAverage === null || longAverage === null || longAverage === 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return shortAverage / longAverage;\n+}\n+\n+function closeLocationPct(bar: PriceBar) {\n+  const range = bar.high - bar.low;\n+\n+  if (range <= 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return ((bar.close - bar.low) / range) * 100;\n+}\n+\n+function candleBodyPct(bar: PriceBar) {\n+  const range = bar.high - bar.low;\n+\n+  if (range <= 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return (Math.abs(bar.close - bar.open) / range) * 100;\n+}\n+\n+function upperWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {\n+  const range = bar.high - bar.low;\n+\n+  if (range <= 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return ((bar.high - Math.max(bar.open, bar.close)) / range) * 100;\n+}\n+\n+function lowerWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {\n+  const range = bar.high - bar.low;\n+\n+  if (range <= 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return ((Math.min(bar.open, bar.close) - bar.low) / range) * 100;\n+}\n \n function tradingDate(timestamp: number) {\n   return new Date(timestamp * 1000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);\n }\n*** End Patch\n"

\"20D breakout\"\n+            : horizon === \"position\"\n+              ? \"55D breakout\"\n+              : \"120D breakout\",\n+      value:\n+        horizon === \"single_day\" || horizon === \"swing\"\n+          ? percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)\n+          : horizon === \"position\"\n+            ? percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)\n+            : percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)\n+    },\n+    {\n+      name: \"Range compression (5D/20D)\",\n+      value: metrics.rangeCompression5v20 !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangeCompression5v20).toFixed(2)}x` : \"n/a\"\n     }\n   ];\n*** End Patch\n"

1569.       ...signals

1573. 
1574. function buildTradePlan(
1575.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
1576.   entry: MarketSeries,
1577.   horizon: HorizonId,
1578.   learning: StopLossLearning | null = null
1579. ): PlanTemplate {
1580.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1581.   const technicalBreakdown = technicalScoreBreakdown(metrics, entry, horizon);
1582.   const rawScore = blendedModelScore(entry, metrics, horizon, technicalBreakdown.technicalScore);
1583.   const technicalScore = technicalBreakdown.technicalScore;
1584.   const chartScore = technicalBreakdown.chartScore;
1585.   const factorScore = technicalBreakdown.factorScore;
1586.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
1587.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1588.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1589.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1590.   const riskFeedback = stopLossFragilityFeedback(metrics, horizon, learning);
1591.   const score = roundMetric(clamp(rawScore - riskFeedback.penalty, 0, 100));
1592.   const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);
1593.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);
1594.   const targetPct = Math.max(
1595.     projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),
1596.     atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple
1597.   );
1598.   const stopPct = Math.max(
1599.     projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),
1600.     atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple
1601.   );
1602.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
1603.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));
1604.   const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;
1605.   const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));
1606.   const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);
1607.   const strengthLabel = horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics, horizon);
1608.   const returnLabel = horizonMomentumLabel(metrics, horizon);
1609.   const summaryBase = horizonSummary(entry, metrics, horizon);
1610.   const summary =
1611.     riskFeedback.reasons.length && riskFeedback.penalty > 0
1612.       ? `${summaryBase} Risk controls trimmed the setup because ${riskFeedback.reasons[0]}.`
1613.       : summaryBase;
1614.   const chartLabel =
1615.     (horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && (metrics.breakout20Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1616.       ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)} versus the recent 20-day high`
1617.       : horizon === "position" && (metrics.breakout55Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1618.         ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)} versus the recent 55-day high`
1619.         : horizon === "long_term" && (metrics.breakout120Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1620.           ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)} versus the recent 120-day high`
1621.           : `price has not cleanly cleared the recent breakout zone yet`;
1622. 
1623.   const drivers = [
1624.     `${HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label} score ${signalScoreText(score)} blends technical ${signalScoreText(technicalScore)}, fundamental ${signalScoreText(fundamentalScore)}, and sentiment ${signalScoreText(sentimentScore)} inputs.`,
1625.     `Chart analysis contributes ${signalScoreText(chartScore)} to the technical layer: ${chartLabel}, close location is ${metrics.closeLocationPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.closeLocationPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"} of the session range, and candle body strength is ${metrics.candleBodyPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.candleBodyPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"}.`,
1626.     `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${strengthLabel}, while RSI (14) is ${metrics.rsi14 !== null ? roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : "n/a"}.`,
1627.     `Volume is running at ${ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average with 20D volatility at ${percentText(metrics.volatility20)}.`,
1628.     entry.fundamentals
```

<sub>⏱️ 359m 25s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\README.md.**

C:\copilot\README.md (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>87 lines</summary>

````
1. # Indian equities recommendation platform
2. 
3. This repository now includes a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. The dashboard analyzes a live NSE watchlist using recent market history, produces ranked multi-horizon recommendations, and explains each idea with an entry, target, and stop-loss instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.
4. 
5. ## Chosen product scope
6. 
7. - **User:** analyst or serious retail investor
8. - **Market:** liquid NSE equities, starting from an expanded large-cap live watchlist
9. - **Delivery:** web dashboard
10. - **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution
11. - **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan
12. - **MVP horizons:** single-day, swing, position, and long-term
13. - **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists
14. 
15. ## Chosen stack
16. 
17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Current repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## Live recommendation flow
37. 
38. - The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for a liquid NSE watchlist plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.
39. - Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, volatility, **chart-structure analysis** (breakouts, candle quality, range behavior, and moving-average slope), lightweight fundamentals, and headline-based sentiment / earnings / analyst signals.
40. - The live model now also applies **stop-loss learning**: repeated recent stop-loss failures reduce future model aggressiveness for similar setups.
41. - The app also backtests prior daily batches from the same historical price series so the dashboard can maintain horizon-specific success rates.
42. - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.
43. - If persistent disk writes are not available on the host, the app falls back to **in-memory caching** for the running instance.
44. - If live requests fail later, the app falls back to the cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.
45. 
46. ## MVP user experience
47. 
48. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch with a broader live shortlist.
49. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
50. 3. The detail view explains:
51.     - key factors behind the recommendation
52.     - recommended entry price
53.     - target price
54.     - stop-loss
55.     - risk-reward profile
56.     - horizon-specific analysis for single-day, swing, position, and long-term modes
57. 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, explains why each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.
58. 5. The search panel offers **autocomplete suggestions while you type**, then analyzes the selected NSE stock and says whether it should be **considered or avoided** for each supported horizon.
59. 6. Historical stop-loss outcomes now include a clearer explanation of **why the stop-loss was hit**, and the live model uses recent stop-loss history to reduce future aggressiveness for fragile setups.
60. 
61. ## Windows quick start
62. 
63. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
64. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
65. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
66. - The run card in the app shows whether the current batch is coming from **LIVE**, **CACHED**, or **SAMPLE** data.
67. 
68. ## Analytics test command
69. 
70. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
71. 
72. ## Deployment and free hosting
73. 
74. ### Production-readiness changes in this repo
75. 
76. - The Next.js app now exposes `GET /api/healthz` for simple uptime checks.
77. - The live dataset loader supports **in-memory runtime caching**, which is friendlier to serverless or ephemeral free hosts.
78. - `render.yaml` is included so you can deploy both the web app and the FastAPI service on Render with minimal setup.
79. 
80. ### Recommended free hosting options
81. 
82. 1. **Vercel** for the Next.js app only. This is the easiest choice for the dashboard because it handles Next.js SSR well. In Vercel, set the project root to `apps/web`.
83. 2. **Render** if you want both the Next.js app and the FastAPI API hosted together for free. The included `render.yaml` is aimed at this option. Render free web services can sleep when idle, so expect cold starts.
84. 3. **Cloudflare Pages** if you want a very low-cost global edge deployment for the web app and are comfortable with more setup than Vercel.
85. 
86. For this repository as it exists today, **Vercel** is the simplest free host for the dashboard, while **Render** is the simplest free option if you want to run both services from one repo.
87.
````

</details>

6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
17.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
18.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
19.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
20. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
21. 
22. ## Proposed approach
23. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
24. 
25. 1. Product definition
26.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
27.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
28.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.

<sub>⏱️ 360m 49s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Done — the live recommendation engine now uses **actual chart-structure analysis** in the score, not just basic price/volume trend, and the home dashboard now shows a **10-name shortlist**.

**What changed**
1. **Technical model upgraded:** recommendations now factor in breakout position vs recent highs, candle quality, close-in-range, wick rejection, range compression, and moving-average slope. Those signals also feed the stop-loss fragility penalty, so weak chart setups are pushed down more aggressively.
2. **Stop-loss explanations improved:** history items now give more chart-aware reasons like gap-down failure, prior-low breakdown, or failed rebound behavior instead of generic stop-hit text.
3. **Dashboard expanded:** the home workspace now surfaces **10 names** per horizon, with clear **Consider** vs **Watch** labeling when the model needs to fill the shortlist beyond the strongest setups.
4. **Universe/documentation updated:** the live NSE universe is broadened beyond the earlier small set, and the docs/plan now reflect the chart-analysis model and larger shortlist behavior.

**Files updated**
- `apps\web\lib\recommendation-data.ts`
- `apps\web\components\dashboard.tsx`
- `apps\web\components\stock-detail.tsx`
- `README.md`
- session `plan.md`

<sub>⏱️ 362m 16s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md.**

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
18.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
19.   - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
20.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
21.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
22. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
23. 
24. ## Proposed approach
25. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
26. 
27. 1. Product definition
28.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
29.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
30.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
31.    - Chosen MVP scope:
32.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
33.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
34.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
35.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
36.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
37.      - Supported horizons in the MVP: swing, position, and long-term; intraday deferred to a later high-frequency phase.
38. 
39. 2. Data foundation
40.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
41.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
42.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
43.    - Current implementation note: the MVP now ingests live Yahoo Finance chart data plus a cached generated snapshot fallback.
44. 
45. 3. Research feature layer
46.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
47.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
48.    - Current implementation note: the live model now combines technical factors with lightweight fundamentals and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone heuristics.
49. 
50. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
51.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
52.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
53.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
54.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
55.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
56.    - Current implementation note: the first live model uses momentum, moving-average trend, relative strength versus Nifty 50, RSI, volume, and volatility to produce ranked recommendations.
57. 
58. 5. Evaluation and controls
59.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
60.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
61.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
62. 
63. 6. Explainability and delivery
64.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
65.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
66.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
67.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
68.    - Current implementation note: the dashboard includes a searchable stock analyzer and a recommendation-history menu with achieved/not-achieved outcome inspection.
69. 
70. 7. UI template recommendation
71.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
72.    - Chosen starting point: Next.js with TypeScript and shadcn/ui-style dashboard patterns for a clean analyst-oriented UI.
73.    - Layout suggestion:
74.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.

1. "use client";

27. type StockDetailProps = {
28.   data: RecommendationDataset;
29.   analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;
30.   requestedSymbol: string;
31. };
32. 
33. function defaultHorizon(data: RecommendationDataset, analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null) {
34.   const preferred = analysis?.recommendedHorizons?.[0];
35. 
36.   if (preferred && data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === preferred)) {
37.     return preferred;
38.   }
39. 
40.   return data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day";
41. }
42. 
43. function DetailRow({
44.   left,
45.   right
46. }: {
47.   left: ReactNode | null;
48.   right?: ReactNode | null;
49. }) {
50.   if (!left && !right) {
51.     return null;
52.   }
53. 
54.   if (!left || !right) {
55.     return <div className="detail-row single">{left ?? right}</div>;
56.   }
57. 
58.   return (
59.     <div className="detail-row">
60.       {left}
61.       {right}
62.     </div>
63.   );
64. }
65. 
66. export function StockDetail({ data, analysis, requestedSymbol }: StockDetailProps) {
67.   const fallbackStock = data.currentBatch.recommendations.find(
68.     (stock) => normalizeSymbol(stock.symbol) === normalizeSymbol(requestedSymbol)
69.   );
70.   const stock = (analysis?.stock ?? fallbackStock) as StockAnalysis | null;
71.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(() => defaultHorizon(data, analysis));
72.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
73.     () => (stock ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, stock.symbol, activeHorizon) : []),
74.     [activeHorizon, data.history, stock]
75.   );
76. 
77.   useEffect(() => {
78.     const nextDefault = defaultHorizon(data, analysis);
79. 
80.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
81.       setActiveHorizon(nextDefault);
82.     }
83.   }, [activeHorizon, analysis, data.profiles]);
84. 
85.   if (!stock) {
86.     return (
87.       <main className="shell">
88.         <section className="card masthead-card tone-risk">
89.           <div className="panel-banner">
90.             <div className="masthead-copy">
91.               <span className="section-eyebrow">Stock workspace</span>
92.               <h1>Stock not available</h1>
93.               <p>
94.                 I could not build a stock detail page for <strong>{requestedSymbol.toUpperCase()}</strong>{" "}
95.                 from the current live dataset.
96.               </p>
97.             </div>
98.             <div className="page-links">
99.               <Link className="secondary-link" href="/">
100.                 Back to dashboard
101.               </Link>
102.             </div>
103.           </div>
104. 
105.           <EmptyState message={analysis?.message ?? "Try searching another NSE symbol from the dashboard."} />
106.         </section>
107.       </main>
108.     );
109.   }
110.

117.   }));
118.

121.       ? analysis.shouldConsider
122.         ? "success"

140.     />
141.   );

145.       subtitle="Headline tone, earnings cues, and external narrative signals."

147.       tone="sentiment"
148.     />
149.   );
150.   const riskCard = (

152.       title="Risk controls"

172.     />

174.   const fundamentalsSnapshotCard = stock.fundamentals ? (

364.                 <Link className="secondary-link" href="/">
365.                   Back to dashboard
366.                 </Link>
367.                 <Link className="secondary-link" href="/history">
368.                   Open archive page
369.                 </Link>
370.               </div>
371.             </div>
372. 
373.             <ul className="list">
374.               {selectedPlan.drivers.map((driver) => (
375.                 <li key={driver}>{driver}</li>
376.               ))}
377.             </ul>
378. 
379.             {stock.fundamentals ? <p className="footnote">{stock.fundamentals.summary}</p> : null}
380.           </section>
381. 
382.           <DetailRow left={technicalCard} right={fundamentalsSnapshotCard} />
383.           <DetailRow left={fundamentalAnalysisCard} right={sentimentSnapshotCard} />
384.           <DetailRow left={sentimentAnalysisCard} right={headlinesCard} />
385.           <DetailRow left={riskCard} right={earningsCard} />
386.           <DetailRow left={analystCard} right={researchContextCard} />
387.         </section>
388.       </section>
389. 
390.       <section className="section-cluster">
391.         <SectionHeader
392.           eyebrow="Historical outcomes"
393.           title="How earlier calls played out"
394.           subtitle="These cards show whether the target was achieved, the stop-loss was hit, or the trade remains open."
395.           tone="history"
396.         />
397. 
398.         <section className="card tone-history">
399.           <div className="history-result-grid">
400.             {stockHistory.length ? (
401.               stockHistory.map((item, index) => (
402.                 <StockOutcomeCard
403.                   key={`${item.batchDate}-${item.outcome.result}-${index}`}
404.                   item={item}
405.                 />
406.               ))
407.             ) : (
408.               <EmptyState message="This stock does not yet have a stored recommendation history." />
409.             )}
410.           </div>
411.         </section>
412.       </section>
413.     </main>
414.   );
415. }
416.
```

</details>

1. import type {
2.   DailyPerformance,
3.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
4.   HorizonId,
5.   RecommendationPlan,
6.   Signal,
7.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
8. } from "@/lib/types";
9. 
10. export type AccentTone =
11.   | "brand"
12.   | "search"
13.   | "overview"
14.   | "technical"
15.   | "fundamental"
16.   | "sentiment"
17.   | "risk"
18.   | "history";
19. 
20. export function formatPrice(value: number) {
21.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
22.     style: "currency",
23.     currency: "INR",
24.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
25.     maximumFractionDigits: 2
26.   }).format(value);
27. }
28. 
29. export function formatNumber(value: number, maximumFractionDigits = 0) {
30.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
31.     maximumFractionDigits
32.   }).format(value);
33. }
34. 
35. export function formatCrore(value: number | null) {
36.   if (value === null) {
37.     return "n/a";
38.   }
39. 
40.   return `${formatNumber(value)} Cr`;
41. }
42. 
43. export function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
44.   if (value === null) {
45.     return "Pending";
46.   }
47. 
48.   return `${value.toFixed(2)}%`;
49. }
50. 
51. export function formatDate(value: string) {
52.   return new Date(value).toLocaleDateString("en-IN");
53. }
54. 
55. export function formatDateTime(value: string) {
56.   return new Date(value).toLocaleString("en-IN");
57. }
58. 
59. export function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {
60.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
61. }
62. 
63. export function isRecommendedPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan | { isRecommended?: boolean }) {
64.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
65. }
66. 
67. export function historyDecisionLabel(result: "target_hit" | "stop_loss_hit" | "open") {
68.   switch (result) {
69.     case "target_hit":
70.       return "Achieved";
71.     case "stop_loss_hit":
72.       return "Not achieved";
73.     default:
74.       return "Open";
75.   }
76. }
77. 
78. export function successRateTone(successRate: number | null) {
79.   if (successRate === null) {
80.     return "neutral";
81.   }
82. 
83.   if (successRate >= 60) {
84.     return "success";
85.   }
86. 
87.   if (successRate < 40) {
88.     return "danger";
89.   }
90. 
91.   return "neutral";
92. }
93. 
94. export function compactUniverseLabel(value: string) {
95.   return value.replace(/\s*\([^)]*\)\s*$/, "");
96. }
97. 
98. export function universeQualifier(value: string) {
99.   const match = value.match(/\(([^)]+)\)/);
100.   return match?.[1] ?? value;
101. }
102. 
103. export function normalizeSymbol(value: string) {
104.   return value.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
105. }
106. 
107. export function SectionHeader({
108.   eyebrow,
109.   title,
110.   subtitle,
111.   tone = "brand"
112. }: {
113.   eyebrow: string;
114.   title: string;
115.   subtitle: string;
116.   tone?: AccentTone;
117. }) {
118.   return (
119.     <div className={`section-header tone-${tone}`}>
120.       <span className="section-eyebrow">{eyebrow}</span>
121.       <h2>{title}</h2>
122.       <p className="section-copy">{subtitle}</p>
123.     </div>
124.   );
125. }
126. 
127. export function MetricTile({
128.   label,
129.   value,
130.   footnote
131. }: {
132.   label: string;
133.   value: string;
134.   footnote?: string;
135. }) {
136.   return (
137.     <article className="metric-tile">
138.       <span className="metric-label">{label}</span>
139.       <strong>{value}</strong>
140.       {footnote ? <span className="metric-footnote">{footnote}</span> : null}
141.     </article>
142.   );
143. }
144. 
145. export function SignalSection({
146.   title,
147.   subtitle,
148.   signals,
149.   tone
150. }: {
151.   title: string;
152.   subtitle: string;
153.   signals?: Signal[];
154.   tone: AccentTone;
155. }) {
156.   if (!signals?.length) {
157.     return null;
158.   }
159. 
160.   return (
161.     <section className={`card category-panel tone-${tone}`}>
162.       <div className="panel-title">
163.         <span className="panel-kicker">{title}</span>
164.         <h3>{title}</h3>
165.         <p>{subtitle}</p>
166.       </div>
167.       <div className="signal-tile-grid">
168.         {signals.map((signal) => (
169.           <article key={`${title}-${signal.name}`} className="signal-tile">
170.             <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
171.             <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
172.           </article>
173.         ))}
174.       </div>
175.     </section>
176.   );
177. }
178. 
179. export function EmptyState({ message }: { message: string }) {
180.   return <div className="empty-state">{message}</div>;
181. }
182. 
183. export function DailyPerformanceCard({ item }: { item: DailyPerformance }) {
184.   const tone = successRateTone(item.successRate);
185. 
186.   return (
187.     <article className={`performance-card ${tone}`}>
188.       <div className="performance-card-top">
189.         <div>
190.           <span className="panel-kicker">Trading day</span>
191.           <h3>{formatDate(item.batchDate)}</h3>
192.         </div>
193.         <span className={`result-chip ${tone}`}>
194.           {item.successRate === null
195.             ? "Pending"
196.             : item.successRate >= 60
197.               ? "Strong"
198.               : item.successRate < 40
199.                 ? "Weak"
200.                 : "Mixed"}
201.         </span>
202.       </div>
203. 
204.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
205.         <MetricTile label="Success rate" value={formatPercent(item.successRate)} />
206.         <MetricTile label="Closed calls" value={String(item.closed)} />
207.         <MetricTile label="Winners" value={String(item.successful)} />
208.         <MetricTile label="Stops" value={String(item.failed)} />
209.       </div>
210.     </article>
211.   );
212. }
213. 
214. export function HistoryRecommendationCard({
215.   stock,
216.   activeHorizon
217. }: {
218.   stock: HistoricalStockRecommendation;
219.   activeHorizon: HorizonId;
220. }) {
221.   const plan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
222. 
223.   return (
224.     <article className="history-result-card">
225.       <div className="history-result-top">
226.         <div>
227.           <span className="panel-kicker">Recommendation</span>
228.           <h3>
229.             {stock.companyName} <span className="inline-meta">({stock.symbol})</span>
230.           </h3>
231.           <p>{stock.sector}</p>
232.         </div>
233.         <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>{plan.conviction}</span>
234.       </div>
235. 
236.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
237.         <MetricTile label="Entry" value={formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)} />
238.         <MetricTile label="Target" value={formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)} />
239.         <MetricTile label="Stop-loss" value={formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)} />
240.         <MetricTile label="Score" value={plan.score?.toFixed(1) ?? "n/a"} />
241.       </div>
242. 
243.       <p className="idea-summary">{plan.summary}</p>
244.     </article>
245.   );
246. }
247. 
248. export function StockOutcomeCard({ item }: { item: StockPerformanceHistoryEntry }) {
249.   return (
250.     <article className={`stock-outcome-card ${item.outcome.result}`}>
251.       <div className="history-result-top">
252.         <div>
253.           <span className="panel-kicker">Previous recommendation</span>
254.           <h3>{formatDate(item.batchDate)}</h3>
255.           <p>
256.             Entry {formatPrice(item.entryPrice)} · Target {formatPrice(item.targetPrice)} · Stop{" "}
257.             {formatPrice(item.stopLoss)}
258.           </p>
259.         </div>
260.         <span
261.           className={`result-chip ${
262.             item.outcome.result === "target_hit"
263.               ? "success"
264.               : item.outcome.result === "stop_loss_hit"
265.                 ? "danger"
266.                 : "neutral"
267.           }`}
268.         >
269.           {historyDecisionLabel(item.outcome.result)}
270.         </span>
271.       </div>
272. 
273.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
274.         <MetricTile label="Outcome" value={`${item.outcome.returnPct.toFixed(2)}%`} />
275.         <MetricTile label="Holding days" value={String(item.outcome.holdingDays)} />
276.         <MetricTile
277.           label={item.outcome.result === "open" ? "Last evaluated" : "Exit date"}
278.           value={formatDate(item.outcome.evaluatedOn)}
279.         />
280.       </div>
281. 
282.       <p className="idea-summary">{item.outcome.notes}</p>
283.     </article>
284.   );
285. }
286.
```

<sub>⏱️ 362m 29s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The current page does have a general “why” list, but it isn’t broken out by **technical / fundamental / sentiment / risk cause**. I’m splitting the reasoning by analysis layer so each section says what pushed the stock up or down and why.

42.   plan: RecommendationPlan;
43.   href: string;
44. }) {
45.   const statusTone = isRecommendedPlan(plan) ? "success" : "neutral";
46.   const statusLabel = isRecommendedPlan(plan) ? "Consider" : "Watch";
47. 
48.   return (
49.     <article className="idea-card">
50.       <div className="idea-card-top">
51.         <div className="idea-card-copy">
52.           <div className="idea-symbol-row">
53.             <span className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</span>
54.             <span className="idea-sector">{stock.sector}</span>
55.           </div>
56.           <h3>{stock.companyName}</h3>
57.           <p>
58.             {stock.industry ? `${stock.industry} · ` : ""}
59.             {stock.marketCapBucket} · {stock.liquidityTier}
60.           </p>
61.         </div>
62.         <div className="idea-card-badges">
63.           {typeof plan.rank === "number" ? <span className="rank-chip">#{plan.rank}</span> : null}
64.           <span className={`result-chip ${statusTone}`}>{statusLabel}</span>
65.           <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>{plan.conviction}</span>
66.         </div>
67.       </div>
68. 
69.       <div className="idea-metric-grid">
70.         <MetricTile label="CMP" value={formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)} />
71.         <MetricTile label="Entry" value={formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)} />
72.         <MetricTile label="Target" value={formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)} />
73.         <MetricTile label="Stop-loss" value={formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)} />
74.         <MetricTile label="Expected return" value={`${plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%`} />
75.         <MetricTile label="Risk / reward" value={plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} />
76.       </div>
77. 
78.       <p className="idea-summary">{plan.summary}</p>
79. 
80.       <div className="idea-card-actions">
81.         <span className="idea-card-note">Open the dedicated stock workspace for full analysis.</span>
82.         <Link className="action-link" href={href}>
83.           Open stock page
84.         </Link>
85.       </div>
86.     </article>
87.   );
88. }
89. 
90. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
91.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
92.   const targetVisibleIdeas = 10;
93. 
94.   useEffect(() => {
95.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
96.       setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
97.     }
98.   }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);
99. 
100.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
101.   const localSearchSuggestions = useMemo(
102.     () =>
103.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => ({
104.         symbol: stock.symbol,
105.         companyName: stock.companyName,
106.         sector: stock.sector,
107.         industry: stock.industry
108.       })),
109.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations]
110.   );
111. 
112.   const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {
113.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
114.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
115.     );
116.     const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));
117. 
118.     if (sorted.length <= targetVisibleIdeas) {
119.       return sorted;
120.     }
121. 
122.     if (recommended.length >= targetVisibleIdeas) {
123.       return recommended.slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas);
124.     }
125. 
126.     const seen = new Set(recommended.map((stock) => stock.symbol));
127.     const watchlistFill = sorted.filter((stock) => !seen.has(stock.symbol));
128. 
129.     return [...recommended, ...watchlistFill].slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas);
130.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations, targetVisibleIdeas]);
131. 
132.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo(
133.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
134.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
135.   );
136.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
137.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
138.     [dailyPerformance]
139.   );
140. 
141.   const spotlightStock = visibleRecommendations[0];
142.   const spotlightPlan = spotlightStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
143.   const searchedStock = searchedAnalysis?.stock;
144.   const searchedHorizonPlans = searchedStock
145.     ? data.profiles.map((profile) => ({
146.         horizon: profile.id,
147.         label: profile.label,
148.         plan: searchedStock.profiles[profile.id]
149.       }))
150.     : [];
151. 
152.   const recommendedCount = data.currentBatch.recommendations.filter((stock) =>
153.     isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon])
154.   ).length;
155.   const latestCompletedDate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.batchDate
156.     ? formatDate(performanceSummary.latestCompleted.batchDate)
157.     : null;
158.   const sourceProvider = data.dataSource?.provider ?? "Live recommendation loader";
159. 
160.   const overviewCards = [
161.     {
162.       label: "Universe",
163.       value: compactUniverseLabel(data.universe),
164.       footnote: universeQualifier(data.universe)
165.     },
166.     {
167.       label: "Active horizon",
168.       value: activeProfile?.label ?? "n/a",
169.       footnote: activeProfile?.window ?? ""
170.     },
171.     {
172.       label: "Shortlist names",
173.       value: String(visibleRecommendations.length),
174.       footnote: `${recommendedCount} model-qualified ideas`
175.     },
176.     {
177.       label: "Analyzed stocks",
178.       value: String(data.currentBatch.recommendations.length),
179.       footnote: "Current live coverage"
180.     },
181.     {
182.       label: "Latest completed success rate",
183.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? null),
184.       footnote: latestCompletedDate ?? "No closed batch yet"
185.     },
186.     {
187.       label: "Average closed return",
188.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct),
189.       footnote: "Across completed batches"
190.     }
191.   ];
192. 
193.   return (
194.     <main className="shell">
195.       <section className="card masthead-card tone-brand">
196.         <div className="masthead-grid">
197.           <div className="masthead-copy">
198.             <span className="section-eyebrow">Institutional-style NSE dashboard</span>
199.             <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
200.             <p>
201.               The home dashboard now stays stable: use it to scan the current shortlist, inspect
202.               batch health, and jump into dedicated stock or archive workspaces when you need deep
203.               analysis.
204.             </p>
205.           </div>
206. 
207.           <div className="masthead-summary">
208.             <MetricTile label="Latest batch" value={formatDateTime(data.currentBatch.generatedAt)} />
209.             <MetricTile label="Market scope" value={`${data.exchange} · ${compactUniverseLabel(data.universe)}`} />
210.             <MetricTile
211.               label="Source mode"
212.               value={`${data.dataSource?.mode.toUpperCase() ?? "LIVE"} · ${sourceProvider}`}
213.               footnote={data.currentBatch.batchDate}
214.             />
215.           </div>
216.         </div>
217.       </section>
218. 
219.       <section className="section-cluster">
220.         <SectionHeader
221.           eyebrow="Screening"
222.           title="Search and market snapshot"
223.           subtitle="Use autocomplete to analyze any stock, then jump into the separate detail workspace only when you want a full breakdown."
224.           tone="search"
225.         />
226. 
227.         <div className="top-panels">
228.           <section className="card search-card tone-search">
229.             <div className="panel-title">
230.               <span className="panel-kicker">Stock lookup</span>
231.               <h3>Search and analyze any NSE stock</h3>
232.               <p>Type a symbol or company name, select a suggestion, and review the model verdict.</p>
233.             </div>
234. 
235.             <StockSearchBox
236.               initialQuery={searchedAnalysis?.query ?? ""}
237.               localSuggestions={localSearchSuggestions}
238.             />
239.           </section>
240. 
241.           <section className="card status-card tone-overview">
242.             <div className="panel-title">
243.               <span className="panel-kicker">Batch snapshot</span>
244.               <h3>Live market context</h3>
245.               <p>Quick context for the currently rendered batch and active recommendation profile.</p>
246.             </div>
247. 
248.             <div className="mini-metric-grid">
249.               <MetricTile label="Exchange" value={data.exchange} />
250.               <MetricTile label="Active profile" value={activeProfile?.label ?? "n/a"} />
251.               <MetricTile label="Window" value={activeProfile?.window ?? "n/a"} />
252.               <MetricTile
253.                 label="Shortlist size"
254.                 value={String(visibleRecommendations.length)}
255.                 footnote={`${recommendedCount} recommended`}
256.               />
257.             </div>
258.           </section>
259.         </div>
260.       </section>
261. 
262.       {searchedAnalysis ? (
263.         <section className="section-cluster">
264.           <SectionHeader
265.             eyebrow="Search verdict"
266.             title="Searched stock analysis"
267.             subtitle="This verdict uses the same live scoring engine as the ranked shortlist, then lets you open a dedicated stock page for full context."
268.             tone="search"
269.           />
270. 
271.           <section className="card search-analysis-card tone-search">
272.             <div className="panel-banner">
273.               <div>
274.                 <span className="panel-kicker">Recommendation verdict</span>
275.                 <h3>{searchedAnalysis.query.toUpperCase()}</h3>
276.               </div>
277.               <span
278.                 className={`result-chip ${
279.                   searchedAnalysis.status === "analyzed"
280.                     ? searchedAnalysis.shouldConsider
281.                       ? "success"
282.                       : "danger"
283.                     : "neutral"
284.                 }`}
285.               >
286.                 {searchedAnalysis.status === "analyzed"
287.                   ? searchedAnalysis.shouldConsider
288.                     ? "Consider"
289.                     : "Avoid for now"
290.                   : searchedAnalysis.status === "not_found"
291.                     ? "Not found"
292.                     : "Analysis unavailable"}
293.               </span>
294.             </div>
295. 
296.             <p className="summary">{searchedAnalysis.verdict}</p>
297.             {searchedAnalysis.message ? <p className="footnote">{searchedAnalysis.message}</p> : null}
298. 
299.             {searchedStock ? (
300.               <>
301.                 <div className="detail-subhead">
302.                   {searchedStock.companyName} ({searchedStock.symbol}) · {searchedStock.sector}
303.                   {searchedStock.industry ? ` · ${searchedStock.industry}` : ""} · CMP{" "}
304.                   {formatPrice(searchedStock.currentMarketPrice)}
305.                 </div>
306. 
307.                 <div className="search-result-grid">
308.                   {searchedHorizonPlans.map(({ horizon, label, plan }) => (
309.                     <article key={horizon} className="stat-card tone-overview">
310.                       <span className="panel-kicker">{label}</span>
311.                       <strong>{isRecommendedPlan(plan) ? "Consider" : "Do not consider"}</strong>
312.                       <div className="search-plan-line">
313.                         Score {plan.score?.toFixed(1) ?? "n/a"} · {plan.conviction} conviction
314.                       </div>
315.                       <div className="search-plan-line">
316.                         Entry {formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)} · Target {formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}
317.                       </div>
318.                       <div className="search-plan-line">
319.                         Stop {formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)} · R:R {plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)}
320.                       </div>
321.                     </article>
322.                   ))}
323.                 </div>
324. 
325.                 <div className="panel-actions">
326.                   <Link className="action-link" href={`/stocks/${searchedStock.symbol}`}>
327.                     Open full stock workspace
328.                   </Link>
329.                 </div>
330.               </>
331.             ) : null}
332.           </section>
333.         </section>
334.       ) : null}
335. 
336.       <section className="section-cluster">
337.         <SectionHeader
338.           eyebrow="Market summary"
339.           title="Key coverage metrics"
340.           subtitle="A quick operating readout for coverage, active profile, and recent recommendation quality."
341.           tone="overview"
342.         />
343. 
344.         <section className="overview-grid">
345.           {overviewCards.map((card) => (
346.             <article key={card.label} className="card overview-card tone-overview">
347.               <div className="metric-label">{card.label}</div>
348.               <div className="metric-value">{card.value}</div>
349.               <div className="footnote">{card.footnote}</div>
350.             </article>
351.           ))}
352.         </section>
353.       </section>
354. 
355.       <section className="section-cluster">
356.         <SectionHeader
357.           eyebrow="Recommendation workspace"
358.           title="Live ideas with dedicated detail pages"
359.           subtitle="Clicking a stock now opens its own workspace instead of changing unrelated panels lower on the dashboard."
360.           tone="brand"
361.         />
362. 
363.         <section className="workspace-grid">
364.           <section className="card tone-brand">
365.             <div className="panel-title panel-title-split">
366.               <div>
367.                 <span className="panel-kicker">Ranked shortlist</span>
368.                 <h3>Today&apos;s ranked ideas</h3>
369.                 <p>
370.                   Use the horizon switcher to reshuffle the top 10 names, then open any stock page
371.                   for deep research.
372.                 </p>
373.               </div>
374. 
375.               <div className="pills">
376.                 {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
377.                   <button
378.                     key={profile.id}
379.                     className={`pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
380.                     onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
381.                     type="button"
382.                   >
383.                     {profile.label}
384.                   </button>
385.                 ))}
386.               </div>
387.             </div>
388. 
389.             <div className="idea-list">
390.               {visibleRecommendations.map((stock) => (
391.                 <RecommendationIdeaCard
392.                   key={stock.symbol}
393.                   stock={stock}
394.                   plan={stock.profiles[activeHorizon]}
395.                   href={`/stocks/${stock.symbol}`}
396.                 />
397.               ))}
398.             </div>
399.           </section>
400. 
401.           <aside className="spotlight-rail">
402.             {spotlightStock && spotlightPlan ? (
403.               <section className="card tone-brand">
404.                 <div className="panel-title">
405.                   <span className="panel-kicker">Top idea for this horizon</span>
406.                   <h3>
407.                     {spotlightStock.companyName} <span className="inline-meta">({spotlightStock.symbol})</span>
408.                   </h3>
409.                   <p>
410.                     {spotlightStock.sector}
411.                     {spotlightStock.industry ? ` · ${spotlightStock.industry}` : ""}
412.                     {" · "}
413.                     CMP {formatPrice(spotlightStock.currentMarketPrice)}
414.                   </p>
415.                 </div>
416. 
417.                 <div className="mini-metric-grid">
418.                   <MetricTile label="Score" value={spotlightPlan.score?.toFixed(1) ?? "n/a"} />
419.                   <MetricTile label="Expected return" value={`${spotlightPlan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%`} />
420.                   <MetricTile label="Risk / reward" value={spotlightPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} />
```

</details>

1. from typing import Literal
2. 
3. from pydantic import BaseModel

6. Conviction = Literal["High", "Medium", "Low"]
7. OutcomeResult = Literal["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit", "open"]
8. 
9.

11.     name: str
12.     value: str
13. 
14.

16.     result: OutcomeResult
17.     evaluatedOn: str
18.     holdingDays: int
19.     returnPct: float
20.     notes: str
21. 
22. 
23. class RecommendationPlan(BaseModel):

25.     rank: int | None = None
26.     isRecommended: bool = True
27.     conviction: Conviction
28.     entryPrice: float
29.     targetPrice: float
30.     stopLoss: float
31.     expectedReturnPct: float
32.     riskReward: float
33.     summary: str
34.     drivers: list[str]
35.     technicalSignals: list[Signal]
36.     fundamentalSignals: list[Signal]
37.     sentimentSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
38.     earningsSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
39.     analystSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
40.     riskSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
41.     newsContext: list[str]
42. 
43. 
44. class HistoricalRecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
45.     score: float | None = None
46.     rank: int | None = None
47.     isRecommended: bool = True
48.     conviction: Conviction
49.     entryPrice: float
50.     targetPrice: float
51.     stopLoss: float
52.     summary: str
53.     outcome: RecommendationOutcome
54. 
55.

57.     symbol: str
58.     companyName: str
59.     sector: str
60.     marketCapBucket: str
61.     liquidityTier: str
62.     currentMarketPrice: float
63.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, RecommendationPlan]
64. 
65. 
66. class HistoricalStockRecommendation(BaseModel):
67.     symbol: str
68.     companyName: str
69.     sector: str
70.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan]
71. 
72.

74.     id: HorizonId
75.     label: str
76.     window: str
77. 
78. 
79. class RecommendationBatch(BaseModel):
80.     batchDate: str
81.     generatedAt: str
82.     recommendations: list[StockAnalysis]
83. 
84. 
85. class HistoricalBatch(BaseModel):
86.     batchDate: str
87.     publishedAt: str
88.     recommendations: list[HistoricalStockRecommendation]
89. 
90.

92.     market: str
93.     exchange: str
94.     universe: str
95.     profiles: list[HorizonProfile]
96.     currentBatch: RecommendationBatch
97.     history: list[HistoricalBatch]
98. 
99.

101.     symbol: str
102.     companyName: str
103.     sector: str
104.     currentMarketPrice: float
105.     horizon: HorizonId
106.     score: float | None = None
107.     rank: int | None = None
108.     isRecommended: bool = True
109.     conviction: Conviction

111.     targetPrice: float
112.     stopLoss: float
113.     expectedReturnPct: float
114.     riskReward: float
115.     summary: str
116. 
117.

121.     total: int
122.     closed: int
123.     open: int
124.     successful: int

1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. from typing import Any
4. 
5. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
6.

9.     HorizonId,
10.     RecommendationDataset,
11.     RecommendationSummary,
12.     StockAnalysis,
13.     StockDetailResponse,
14. )
15. 
16. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
17. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
18. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
19. 
20. app = FastAPI(
21.     title="Recommendation API",
22.     version="0.1.0",
23.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
24. )
25. 
26. 
27. def _clamp(value: float, minimum: float, maximum: float) -> float:
28.     return min(max(value, minimum), maximum)
29. 
30. 
31. def _pct_change(current: float, previous: float) -> float:
32.     if previous == 0:
33.         return 0.0
34. 
35.     return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100
36. 
37. 
38. def _derive_single_day_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
39.     entry_price = round(float(plan["entryPrice"]), 2)
40.     target_pct = _clamp(
41.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["targetPrice"]), entry_price)) * 0.38,
42.         1.2,
43.         3.4,
44.     )
45.     stop_pct = _clamp(
46.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["stopLoss"]), entry_price)) * 0.45,
47.         0.7,
48.         1.9,
49.     )
50.     target_price = round(entry_price * (1 + target_pct / 100), 2)
51.     stop_loss = round(max(entry_price * (1 - stop_pct / 100), entry_price * 0.85), 2)
52. 
53.     return {
54.         **plan,
55.         "score": plan.get("score", 55),
56.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
57.         "entryPrice": entry_price,
58.         "targetPrice": target_price,
59.         "stopLoss": stop_loss,
60.         "expectedReturnPct": round(_pct_change(target_price, entry_price), 2),
61.         "riskReward": round((target_price - entry_price) / max(entry_price - stop_loss, 0.01), 2),
62.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. {plan['summary']}",
63.         "drivers": [
64.             "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
65.             *plan.get("drivers", []),
66.         ][:4],
67.         "technicalSignals": [
68.             {"name": "Compatibility mode", "value": "Derived from swing profile"},
69.             *plan.get("technicalSignals", []),
70.         ][:6],
71.         "riskSignals": plan.get("riskSignals")
72.         or [{"name": "Learning status", "value": "Awaiting refreshed live batch"}],
73.     }
74. 
75. 
76. def _derive_single_day_history(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
77.     outcome = plan["outcome"]
78. 
79.     return {
80.         **plan,
81.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
82.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. {plan['summary']}",
83.         "outcome": {
84.             **outcome,
85.             "notes": f"This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. {outcome['notes']}",
86.         },
87.     }
88. 
89. 
90. def _normalize_payload(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
91.     profile_lookup = {profile["id"]: profile for profile in payload.get("profiles", [])}
92.     payload["profiles"] = [
93.         profile_lookup.get("single_day", {"id": "single_day", "label": "Single-day", "window": "Next trading day"}),
94.         profile_lookup.get("swing", {"id": "swing", "label": "Swing", "window": "5-20 trading days"}),
95.         profile_lookup.get("position", {"id": "position", "label": "Position", "window": "20-60 trading days"}),
96.         profile_lookup.get("long_term", {"id": "long_term", "label": "Long-term", "window": "3-12 months"}),
97.     ]
98. 
99.     for recommendation in payload.get("currentBatch", {}).get("recommendations", []):
100.         profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
101.         if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
102.             profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_plan(profiles["swing"])
103. 
104.     for batch in payload.get("history", []):
105.         for recommendation in batch.get("recommendations", []):
106.             profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
107.             if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
108.                 profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_history(profiles["swing"])
109. 
110.     return payload
111. 
112. 
113. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
114.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
115.     payload = _normalize_payload(json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
116.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
117. 
118. 
119. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
120.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
121. 
122.     return RecommendationSummary(
123.         symbol=stock.symbol,
124.         companyName=stock.companyName,
125.         sector=stock.sector,
126.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
127.         horizon=horizon,
128.         score=plan.score,
129.         rank=plan.rank,
130.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
131.         conviction=plan.conviction,
132.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
133.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
134.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
135.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
136.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
137.         summary=plan.summary,
138.     )
139. 
140. 
141. @app.get("/healthz")
142. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
143.     return {"status": "ok"}
144. 
145. 
146. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
147. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
148.     dataset = load_dataset()
149.     ordered_recommendations = sorted(
150.         dataset.currentBatch.recommendations,
151.         key=lambda stock: (
152.             stock.profiles[horizon].score
153.             if stock.profiles[horizon].score is not None
154.             else stock.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct
155.         ),
156.         reverse=True,
157.     )
158.     recommended_rows = [
159.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
160.         for stock in ordered_recommendations
161.         if stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
162.     ]
163.     rows = recommended_rows or [
164.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
165.         for stock in ordered_recommendations[:5]
166.     ]
167.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
168. 
169.     return {
170.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
171.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
172.         "market": dataset.market,
173.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
174.         "universe": dataset.universe,
175.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
176.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
177.         "recommendations": rows,
178.     }
179. 
180.

897. function cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(plan: RecommendationPlan): RecommendationPlan {
898.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS.single_day;
899.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(plan.entryPrice);
900.   const targetPct = clamp(
901.     Math.abs(pctChange(plan.targetPrice, plan.entryPrice) ?? plan.expectedReturnPct) * 0.38,

910.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));

2265. async function buildLiveDataset() {
2266.   const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
2267.   const liveSeries = (await Promise.all(MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry)))).filter(
2268.     (series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null
2269.   );
2270. 
2271.   if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
2272.     return null;
2273.   }
2274.

2276. 
2277.   if (!latestDate) {
2278.     return null;
2279.   }
2280. 
2281.   const history = buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark);
2282.   const learningMap = deriveStopLossLearning(history);
2283.   const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(
2284.     latestDate,
2285.     liveSeries,
2286.     benchmark,
2287.     learningMap
2288.   );
2289. 
2290.   if (currentRecommendations.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
2291.     return null;
2292.   }
2293. 
2294.   const dataset: RecommendationDataset = normalizeDataset({
2295.     market: "Indian Equities",
2296.     exchange: "NSE",
2297.     universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,
2298.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
2299.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,
2300.       liveSeries.length
2301.     ),
2302.     profiles: PROFILE_LIST,
2303.     currentBatch: {
2304.       batchDate: latestDate,
2305.       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
2306.       recommendations: currentRecommendations
2307.     },
2308.     history
2309.   });
2310.   const persisted = await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);
2311.   const finalDataset = {
2312.     ...dataset,
2313.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
2314.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.${persisted ? " Snapshot persisted locally." : " Persistent filesystem cache was unavailable, so the app is using in-memory caching for this runtime."}`,
2315.       liveSeries.length
2316.     )
2317.   };
2318. 
2319.   return rememberDataset(finalDataset);
2320. }

2333. export async function loadRecommendationData(): Promise<RecommendationDataset> {
2334.   const memoryDataset = readDatasetMemoryCache();
2335. 
2336.   if (memoryDataset) {
2337.     return memoryDataset;
2338.   }

2347.

2352.         "cached",

2354.       )
2355.     );
2356.   }
2357.

2359.     annotateFallbackDataset(

2367. export async function analyzeSearchSymbol(
2368.   query: string,
2369.   referenceDataset?: RecommendationDataset
2370. ): Promise<SearchAnalysisResult | null> {
2371.   const trimmedQuery = query.trim();
2372. 
2373.   if (!trimmedQuery) {
2374.     return null;
2375.   }
2376. 
2377.   try {
2378.     const lookup = await lookupNseSymbol(trimmedQuery);
2379. 
2380.     if (!lookup) {
2381.       return {
2382.         status: "not_found",
2383.         query: trimmedQuery,
2384.         symbol: trimmedQuery.toUpperCase(),
2385.         companyName: trimmedQuery.toUpperCase(),
2386.         sector: "Unknown",
2387.         industry: undefined,
2388.         shouldConsider: false,
2389.         verdict: "The symbol could not be resolved to an NSE-listed equity.",
2390.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2391.         stock: null,
2392.         message: "Try an NSE trading symbol such as RELIANCE, INFY, or ICICIBANK."
2393.       };
2394.     }
2395. 
2396.     const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
2397.     const series = await fetchSeries({
2398.       symbol: lookup.symbol,
2399.       yahooSymbol: lookup.yahooSymbol,
2400.       fallbackCompanyName: lookup.companyName,
2401.       sector: lookup.sector,
2402.       industry: lookup.industry,
2403.       marketCapBucket: "Large Cap",
2404.       liquidityTier: "Tier 2"
2405.     });
2406. 
2407.     if (!benchmark || !series) {
2408.       return {
2409.         status: "error",
2410.         query: trimmedQuery,
2411.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2412.         companyName: lookup.companyName,
2413.         sector: lookup.sector,
2414.         industry: lookup.industry,
2415.         shouldConsider: false,
2416.         verdict: "Live analysis could not be completed for this symbol.",
2417.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2418.         stock: null,
2419.         message: "The live chart or benchmark request failed while evaluating this stock."
2420.       };
2421.     }
2422. 
2423.     const analysisDate = latestCommonTradingDate(series, benchmark);
2424. 
2425.     if (!analysisDate) {
2426.       return {
2427.         status: "error",
2428.         query: trimmedQuery,
2429.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2430.         companyName: lookup.companyName,
2431.         sector: lookup.sector,
2432.         industry: lookup.industry,
2433.         shouldConsider: false,
2434.         verdict: "Live analysis could not be completed for this symbol.",
2435.         recommendedHorizons: [],
2436.         stock: null,
2437.         message: "The stock does not have enough overlapping benchmark history for scoring."
2438.       };
2439.     }
2440. 
2441.     const learningSource = referenceDataset ?? (await readGeneratedDataset());
2442.     const stock = buildSearchStockForDate(
2443.       analysisDate,
2444.       series,
2445.       benchmark,
2446.       learningSource ? deriveStopLossLearning(learningSource.history) : undefined
2447.     );
2448. 
2449.     if (!stock) {
2450.       return {
2451.         status: "error",
2452.         query: trimmedQuery,
2453.         symbol: lookup.symbol,
2454.         companyName: lookup.companyName,

949. function normalizeRecommendationProfiles(profiles: Record<string, RecommendationPlan>) {
950.   return {
951.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
952.     swing: profiles.swing,
953.     position: profiles.position,
954.     long_term: profiles.long_term
955.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, RecommendationPlan>;
956. }
957. 
958. function normalizeHistoricalProfiles(profiles: Record<string, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>) {
959.   return {
960.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayHistoricalPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
961.     swing: profiles.swing,
962.     position: profiles.position,
963.     long_term: profiles.long_term
964.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>;
965. }
966. 
967. function normalizeDataset(dataset: RecommendationDataset): RecommendationDataset {
968.   return {
969.     ...dataset,
970.     profiles: HORIZON_ORDER.map(
971.       (horizon) =>
972.         dataset.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === horizon) ?? {
973.           id: horizon,
974.           label: HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label,
975.           window: HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].window
976.         }
977.     ),
978.     currentBatch: {
979.       ...dataset.currentBatch,
980.       recommendations: dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
981.         ...recommendation,
982.         profiles: normalizeRecommendationProfiles(recommendation.profiles as Record<string, RecommendationPlan>)
983.       }))
984.     },
985.     history: dataset.history.map((batch) => ({
986.       ...batch,
987.       recommendations: batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
988.         ...recommendation,
989.         profiles: normalizeHistoricalProfiles(
990.           recommendation.profiles as Record<string, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>
991.         )
992.       }))
993.     }))
994.   };
995. }

520. const SAMPLE_FILE_NAME = "sample-recommendations.json";

522. const LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE = "1y";
523. const HISTORY_BATCH_COUNT = 8;
524. const MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS = 10;
525. 
526. function roundMetric(value: number) {
527.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
528. }
529. 
530. function roundPrice(value: number) {
531.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
532. }
533. 
534. function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number) {
535.   return Math.min(Math.max(value, min), max);
536. }
537. 
538. function average(values: number[]) {
539.   if (!values.length) {
540.     return null;
541.   }
542. 
543.   return values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / values.length;
544. }
545. 
546. function pctChange(current: number, previous: number) {
547.   if (!Number.isFinite(current) || !Number.isFinite(previous) || previous === 0) {
548.     return null;
549.   }
550. 
551.   return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100;
552. }
553. 
554. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
555.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
556.     return 0.5;
557.   }
558. 
559.   if (max === min) {
560.     return 0.5;
561.   }
562. 
563.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
564. }
565. 
566. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
567.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
568. }
569. 
570. function percentText(value: number | null) {
571.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
572.     return "n/a";
573.   }
574. 
575.   const prefix = value > 0 ? "+" : "";
576.   return `${prefix}${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
577. }
578. 
579. function ratioText(value: number | null) {
580.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
581.     return "n/a";
582.   }
583. 
584.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}x`;
585. }
586. 
587. function priceText(value: number | null) {
588.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
589.     return "n/a";
590.   }
591. 
592.   return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;
593. }
594. 
595. function compactNumber(value: number | null) {
596.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
597.     return "n/a";
598.   }
599. 
600.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
601.     notation: "compact",
602.     maximumFractionDigits: 1
603.   }).format(value);
604. }
605. 
606. function croreText(value: number | null) {
