430. function rememberDataset(dataset: RecommendationDataset) {
431.   datasetMemoryCache = {
432.     dataset,
433.     cachedAt: Date.now()
434.   };
435. 
436.   return dataset;
437. }
438. 
439. function horizonMomentumValue(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
440.   switch (horizon) {
441.     case "single_day":
442.       return metrics.sessionChangePct;
443.     case "swing":
444.       return metrics.return20;
445.     case "position":
446.       return metrics.return60;
447.     case "long_term":
448.       return metrics.return120;
449.   }
450. }
451. 
452. function horizonMomentumLabel(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
453.   return percentText(horizonMomentumValue(metrics, horizon));
454. }
455. 
456. function horizonMomentumSignalName(horizon: HorizonId) {
457.   switch (horizon) {
458.     case "single_day":
459.       return "Session change";
460.     case "swing":
461.       return "20D momentum";
462.     case "position":
463.       return "60D momentum";
464.     case "long_term":
465.       return "120D momentum";
466.   }
467. }
468. 
469. function horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
470.   switch (horizon) {
471.     case "single_day":
472.     case "swing":
473.       return metrics.relativeStrength20;
474.     case "position":
475.       return metrics.relativeStrength60;
476.     case "long_term":
477.       return metrics.relativeStrength120;
478.   }
479. }
480. 
481. function horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
482.   return percentText(horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon));
483. }
484. 
485. function horizonRelativeStrengthSignalName(horizon: HorizonId) {
486.   switch (horizon) {
487.     case "single_day":
488.       return "Next-day relative strength";
489.     case "swing":
490.       return "20D relative strength";
491.     case "position":
492.       return "60D relative strength";
493.     case "long_term":
494.       return "120D relative strength";
495.   }
496. }
497. 
498. function horizonSummary(entry: MarketSeries, metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
499.   const momentumLabel = horizonMomentumLabel(metrics, horizon);
500.   const strengthLabel = horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics, horizon);
501.   const newsTone = entry.sentiment?.overall?.toLowerCase() ?? "neutral";
502. 
503.   switch (horizon) {
504.     case "single_day":
505.       return `${metrics.companyName} is shaping up as a next-session tactical idea with ${momentumLabel} session strength, ${strengthLabel} short-term relative strength versus Nifty 50, and a ${newsTone} news tone.`;
506.     case "swing":
507.       return `${metrics.companyName} is holding a constructive short-term trend with ${momentumLabel} recent momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus Nifty 50, and a ${newsTone} news tone.`;
508.     case "position":
509.       return `${metrics.companyName} ranks well on the medium-term trend stack, combining ${momentumLabel} price follow-through, ${strengthLabel} benchmark outperformance, and improving fundamental support.`;
510.     case "long_term":
511.       return `${metrics.companyName} remains a longer-duration accumulation candidate with ${momentumLabel} multi-month momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus the index, and supportive fundamental quality markers.`;
512.   }
513. }
514. 
515. const DATA_DIRECTORY_CANDIDATES = [
516.   path.join(process.cwd(), "data"),
517.   path.join(process.cwd(), "..", "..", "data")
518. ];
519. 
520. const SAMPLE_FILE_NAME = "sample-recommendations.json";
```

1788. function applyRanking(recommendations: StockAnalysis[]) {
1789.   const ranked = recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
1790.     ...recommendation,
1791.     profiles: {
1792.       single_day: { ...recommendation.profiles.single_day },
1793.       swing: { ...recommendation.profiles.swing },
1794.       position: { ...recommendation.profiles.position },
1795.       long_term: { ...recommendation.profiles.long_term }
1796.     }
1797.   }));
1798. 
1799.   for (const horizon of HORIZON_ORDER) {
1800.     const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1801.     const ordered = [...ranked].sort(
1802.       (left, right) =>
1803.         (right.profiles[horizon].score ?? right.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct) -
1804.         (left.profiles[horizon].score ?? left.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct)
1805.     );
1806.     const thresholdCount = ordered.filter(
1807.       (stock, index) =>
1808.         index < settings.recommendationCount &&
1809.         (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore
1810.     ).length;
1811.     const forcedCount = Math.min(settings.minimumRecommendationCount, ordered.length);
1812. 
1813.     ordered.forEach((stock, index) => {
1814.       stock.profiles[horizon].rank = index + 1;
1815.       stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended =
1816.         (index < settings.recommendationCount &&
1817.           (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore) ||
1818.         (thresholdCount < forcedCount && index < forcedCount);
1819.     });
1820.   }
1821. 
1822.   return ranked;
1823. }

That matters because a confirmed breakout usually means nearby supply has already been absorbed, which improves follow-through odds.`;\n+    } else if (breakout >= -1.5) {\n+      breakoutImpact = \"neutral\";\n+      breakoutDetail = `Price is ${percentText(breakout)} below the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. The setup is close to a trigger zone, but it still needs a clean push through resistance before the move becomes more reliable.`;\n+    } else {\n+      breakoutImpact = \"negative\";\n+      breakoutDetail = `Price is still ${percentText(breakout)} below the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. That keeps the stock under resistance, so upside continuation is less dependable until that ceiling is cleared.`;\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  const closeLocation = metrics.closeLocationPct;\n+  const upperWick = metrics.upperWickPct;\n+  const candleBody = metrics.candleBodyPct;\n+  let candleImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let candleDetail =\n+    \"The latest candle structure is mixed, so the model is not treating the most recent session as a decisive control candle.\";\n+\n+  if (closeLocation !== null && upperWick !== null && candleBody !== null) {\n+    if (closeLocation >= 65 && upperWick <= 25 && candleBody >= 45) {\n+      candleImpact = \"positive\";\n+      candleDetail = `The latest candle closed near the top of the range (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%) with only ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because strong closes show buyers kept control into the end of the session.`;\n+    } else if (closeLocation < 42 || upperWick > 45) {\n+      candleImpact = \"negative\";\n+      candleDetail = `The latest candle finished weakly (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% close location) with ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because sellers were able to push price away from intraday highs, which often leads to failed follow-through.`;\n+    } else {\n+      candleDetail = `The latest candle closed at ${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% of its range with a ${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}% real body. That gives the setup some balance, but not enough to treat the last bar as a strong confirmation candle on its own.`;\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  const relativeStrength = horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon);\n+  const volumeRatio = metrics.volumeRatio;\n+  let participationImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let participationDetail =\n+    \"Participation signals are mixed, so the model is not assuming broad institutional sponsorship behind the current move yet.\";\n+\n+  if (relativeStrength !== null && volumeRatio !== null) {\n+    if (relativeStrength > 0 && volumeRatio >= 1.05) {\n+      participationImpact = \"positive\";\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} and volume is ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because breakouts work better when the stock is already outperforming the benchmark and participation is expanding.`;\n+    } else if (relativeStrength < 0 || volumeRatio < 0.9) {\n+      participationImpact = \"negative\";\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} while volume is only ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because weaker participation makes rallies easier to fade and harder to sustain.`;\n+    } else {\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  return [\n+    { area: \"technical\", impact: breakoutImpact, title: \"Breakout position\", detail: breakoutDetail },\n+    { area: \"technical\", impact: candleImpact, title: \"Candle quality\", detail: candleDetail },\n+    { area: \"technical\", impact: participationImpact, title: \"Participation\", detail: participationDetail }\n+  ];\n+}\n+\n+function buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+  if (!entry.fundamentals) {\n+    return [\n+      {\n+        area: \"fundamental\",\n+        impact: \"neutral\",\n+        title: \"Fundamental coverage\",\n+        detail:\n+          \"Live fundamental fields were not available for this stock today, so the recommendation is leaning more heavily on chart structure, liquidity, and risk controls than on business-quality data.\"\n+      }\n+    ];\n+  }\n+\n+  const growth = entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct;\n+  const roe = entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct;\n+  const promoterHolding = entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct;\n+  const netMargin = entry.fundamentals.netMarginPct;\n+\n+  let qualityImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let qualityDetail =\n+    \"Fundamental quality is mixed, so the business backdrop is not strongly amplifying or blocking the technical setup.\";\n+\n+  if ((growth ?? 0) >= 12 || (roe ?? 0) >= 15) {\n+    qualityImpact = \"positive\";\n+    qualityDetail = `The business shows ${growth !== null ? `${roundMetric(growth).toFixed(2)}% five-year sales growth` : \"stable sales growth\"} and ${roe !== null ? `${roundMetric(roe).toFixed(2)}% ROE` : \"reasonable returns on equity\"}. That matters because weaker alignment or profitability leaves less business support underneath the trade setup.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  return [\n+    { area: \"fundamental\", impact: qualityImpact, title: \"Business quality\", detail: qualityDetail },\n+    { area: \"fundamental\", impact: ownershipImpact, title: \"Ownership and profitability\", detail: ownershipDetail }\n+  ];\n+}\n+\n+function buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(\n+  entry: MarketSeries,\n+  earningsScore: number,\n+  analystScore: number\n+): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+  if (!entry.sentiment) {\n+    return [\n+      {\n+        area: \"sentiment\",\n+        impact: \"neutral\",\n+        title: \"Headline coverage\",\n+        detail:\n+          \"No strong recent news, earnings, or analyst headline cluster was found, so sentiment is not materially changing the recommendation in either direction.\"\n+      }\n+    ];\n+  }\n+\n+  const sentiment = entry.sentiment;\n+  const headlineBalance = sentiment.positiveCount - sentiment.negativeCount;\n+  let toneImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let toneDetail =\n+    \"Headline tone is balanced, so sentiment is acting more like background context than like a hard catalyst.\";\n+\n+  if (headlineBalance > 0 && sentiment.score >= 0) {\n+    toneImpact = \"positive\";\n+    toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because earnings or analyst disappointment can override otherwise decent technical structure.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  return [\n+    { area: \"sentiment\", impact: toneImpact, title: \"Narrative tone\", detail: toneDetail },\n+    { area: \"sentiment\", impact: catalystImpact, title: \"Earnings and analyst catalyst\", detail: catalystDetail }\n+  ];\n+}\n+\n+function buildRiskAnalysisDrivers(\n+  metrics: SnapshotMetrics,\n+  horizon: HorizonId,\n+  riskFeedback: { penalty: number; reasons: string[]; signals: Signal[] },\n+  entryPrice: number,\n+  stopLoss: number,\n+  riskReward: number\n+): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+  const stopDistancePct = Math.abs(pctChange(stopLoss, entryPrice) ?? 0);\n+  const volatility = metrics.volatility20;\n+  const fragilityImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = riskFeedback.penalty > 0 ? \"negative\" : \"positive\";\n+  const fragilityDetail =\n+    riskFeedback.penalty > 0 && riskFeedback.reasons.length\n+      ? `The model removed ${riskFeedback.penalty.toFixed(1)} points for fragility because ${riskFeedback.reasons.join(\"; \")}. roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : \"n/a\"}.`,\n-    `Volume is running at ${ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average with 20D volatility at ${percentText(metrics.volatility20)}.`,\n-    entry.fundamentals\n-      ? `Fundamentals show ${entry.fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel ?? \"mixed\"} sales growth (${entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct).toFixed(2)}%` : \"n/a\"}) and ${entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel ?? \"mixed\"} ROE (${entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct).toFixed(2)}%` : \"n/a\"}).`\n-      : `Fundamental data is unavailable, so the model leans more heavily on price structure and liquidity.`\n-  ];\n-\n-  if (riskFeedback.penalty > 0 && riskFeedback.reasons.length) {\n-    drivers.push(\n-      `Stop-loss learning trims the score by ${riskFeedback.penalty.toFixed(1)} points because ${riskFeedback.reasons.join(\"; \")}.`\n-    );\n-  } else {\n-    drivers.push(\"Risk controls found no material recent stop-loss pattern that required a score penalty.\");\n-  }\n+  const analysisDrivers = [\n+    ...buildTechnicalAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon),\n+    ...buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry),\n+    ...buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(entry, earningsScore, analystScore),\n+    ...buildRiskAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon, riskFeedback, entryPrice, stopLoss, riskReward)\n+  ];\n+  const drivers = analysisDrivers.map((driver) => `${driver.title}: ${driver.detail}`).slice(0, 8);\n \n   const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [\n@@\n     summary,\n     drivers,\n+    analysisDrivers,\n     technicalSignals,\n     fundamentalSignals,\n     sentimentSignals,\n@@\n function searchVerdict(stock: StockAnalysis) {\n   const recommendedHorizons = HORIZON_ORDER.filter((horizon) => stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended);\n+  const focusHorizon =\n+    recommendedHorizons[0] ??\n+    [...HORIZON_ORDER].sort(\n+      (left, right) =>\n+        (stock.profiles[right].score ?? stock.profiles[right].expectedReturnPct) -\n+        (stock.profiles[left].score ?? stock.profiles[left].expectedReturnPct)\n+    )[0];\n+  const focusPlan = stock.profiles[focusHorizon];\n+  const positiveReason = focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.impact === \"positive\");\n+  const cautionReason =\n+    focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.area === \"risk\" && driver.impact === \"negative\") ??\n+    focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.impact === \"negative\");\n \n   if (!recommendedHorizons.length) {\n     return {\n       recommendedHorizons,\n       shouldConsider: false,\n-      verdict:\n-        \"Do not prioritize this stock right now. Its current technical, fundamental, and sentiment mix is below the recommendation threshold across all supported horizons.\"\n+      verdict: cautionReason\n+        ? `Do not prioritize this stock right now. The biggest drag is ${cautionReason.title.toLowerCase()}: ${cautionReason.detail}`\n+        : \"Do not prioritize this stock right now. Its current technical, fundamental, and sentiment mix is below the recommendation threshold across all supported horizons.\"\n     };\n   }\n \n   const labels = recommendedHorizons.map((horizon) => HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label.toLowerCase());\n \n   return {\n     recommendedHorizons,\n     shouldConsider: true,\n-    verdict: `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(\", \")} setups. The current score clears the model threshold for those horizons.`\n+    verdict: positiveReason\n+      ? `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(\", \")} setups. The main support is ${positiveReason.title.toLowerCase()}: ${positiveReason.detail}${cautionReason ? ` Main caution: ${cautionReason.detail}` : \"\"}`\n+      : `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(\", \")} setups. The current score clears the model threshold for those horizons.`\n   };\n }\n*** End Patch\n"

7.   EmptyState,
8.   MetricTile,
9.   SectionHeader,

27. type StockDetailProps = {
28.   data: RecommendationDataset;
29.   analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;
30.   requestedSymbol: string;
31. };

33. function defaultHorizon(data: RecommendationDataset, analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null) {
34.   const preferred = analysis?.recommendedHorizons?.[0];
35. 
36.   if (preferred && data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === preferred)) {
37.     return preferred;
38.   }
39. 
40.   return data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day";
41. }
42. 
43. function DetailRow({
44.   left,
45.   right
46. }: {
47.   left: ReactNode | null;
48.   right?: ReactNode | null;
49. }) {
50.   if (!left && !right) {
51.     return null;
52.   }
53. 
54.   if (!left || !right) {
55.     return <div className="detail-row single">{left ?? right}</div>;
56.   }
57. 
58.   return (
59.     <div className="detail-row">
60.       {left}
61.       {right}
62.     </div>
63.   );
64. }
65. 
66. export function StockDetail({ data, analysis, requestedSymbol }: StockDetailProps) {
67.   const fallbackStock = data.currentBatch.recommendations.find(
68.     (stock) => normalizeSymbol(stock.symbol) === normalizeSymbol(requestedSymbol)
69.   );
70.   const stock = (analysis?.stock ?? fallbackStock) as StockAnalysis | null;
71.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(() => defaultHorizon(data, analysis));
72.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
73.     () => (stock ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, stock.symbol, activeHorizon) : []),
74.     [activeHorizon, data.history, stock]
75.   );
76. 
77.   useEffect(() => {
78.     const nextDefault = defaultHorizon(data, analysis);
79. 
80.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
81.       setActiveHorizon(nextDefault);
82.     }
83.   }, [activeHorizon, analysis, data.profiles]);
84. 
85.   if (!stock) {
86.     return (
87.       <main className="shell">
88.         <section className="card masthead-card tone-risk">
89.           <div className="panel-banner">
90.             <div className="masthead-copy">
91.               <span className="section-eyebrow">Stock workspace</span>
92.               <h1>Stock not available</h1>
93.               <p>
94.                 I could not build a stock detail page for <strong>{requestedSymbol.toUpperCase()}</strong>{" "}
95.                 from the current live dataset.
96.               </p>
97.             </div>
98.             <div className="page-links">
99.               <Link className="secondary-link" href="/">
100.                 Back to dashboard
101.               </Link>
102.             </div>
103.           </div>
104. 
105.           <EmptyState message={analysis?.message ?? "Try searching another NSE symbol from the dashboard."} />
106.         </section>
107.       </main>
108.     );
109.   }
110. 
111.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
112.   const selectedPlan = stock.profiles[activeHorizon];
113. 
114.   const horizonPlans = data.profiles.map((profile) => ({
115.     ...profile,
116.     plan: stock.profiles[profile.id]
117.   }));
118. 
119.   const verdictTone =
120.     analysis?.status === "analyzed"
121.       ? analysis.shouldConsider
122.         ? "success"
123.         : "danger"
124.       : "neutral";
125.   const primaryVerdict = analysis?.status === "analyzed" ? analysis.verdict : selectedPlan.summary;
126.   const technicalCard = (
127.     <SignalSection
128.       title="Technical analysis"
129.       subtitle="Momentum, chart structure, breakout, candle-quality, and trend readings used in the model."
130.       signals={selectedPlan.technicalSignals}
131.       tone="technical"
132.     />
133.   );
134.   const fundamentalAnalysisCard = (
135.     <SignalSection
136.       title="Fundamental analysis"
137.       subtitle="Business quality and valuation context from the fundamentals layer."
138.       signals={selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals}
139.       tone="fundamental"
140.     />
141.   );
142.   const sentimentAnalysisCard = (

144.       title="Sentiment and news"
145.       subtitle="Headline tone, earnings cues, and external narrative signals."
146.       signals={selectedPlan.sentimentSignals}
147.       tone="sentiment"
148.     />
149.   );
150.   const riskCard = (
151.     <SignalSection
152.       title="Risk controls"
153.       subtitle="Stop-loss learning, fragility checks, and downside guardrails."
154.       signals={selectedPlan.riskSignals}
155.       tone="risk"
156.     />
157.   );
158.   const earningsCard = (

164.     />
165.   );

172.     />

174.   const fundamentalsSnapshotCard = stock.fundamentals ? (

178.         <h3>Business context</h3>

180.       </div>
181.

1. import type {
2.   DailyPerformance,
3.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
4.   HorizonId,
5.   RecommendationPlan,
6.   Signal,
7.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
8. } from "@/lib/types";
9. 
10. export type AccentTone =
11.   | "brand"
12.   | "search"
13.   | "overview"
14.   | "technical"
15.   | "fundamental"
16.   | "sentiment"
17.   | "risk"
18.   | "history";
19. 
20. export function formatPrice(value: number) {
21.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
22.     style: "currency",
23.     currency: "INR",
24.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
25.     maximumFractionDigits: 2
26.   }).format(value);
27. }
28. 
29. export function formatNumber(value: number, maximumFractionDigits = 0) {
30.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
31.     maximumFractionDigits
32.   }).format(value);
33. }
34. 
35. export function formatCrore(value: number | null) {
36.   if (value === null) {
37.     return "n/a";
38.   }
39. 
40.   return `${formatNumber(value)} Cr`;
41. }
42. 
43. export function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
44.   if (value === null) {
45.     return "Pending";
46.   }
47. 
48.   return `${value.toFixed(2)}%`;
49. }
50. 
51. export function formatDate(value: string) {
52.   return new Date(value).toLocaleDateString("en-IN");
53. }
54. 
55. export function formatDateTime(value: string) {
56.   return new Date(value).toLocaleString("en-IN");
57. }
58. 
59. export function convictionClass(conviction: RecommendationPlan["conviction"]) {
60.   return conviction.toLowerCase();
61. }
62. 
63. export function isRecommendedPlan(plan: RecommendationPlan | { isRecommended?: boolean }) {
64.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
65. }
66. 
67. export function historyDecisionLabel(result: "target_hit" | "stop_loss_hit" | "open") {
68.   switch (result) {
69.     case "target_hit":
70.       return "Achieved";
71.     case "stop_loss_hit":
72.       return "Not achieved";
73.     default:
74.       return "Open";
75.   }
76. }
77. 
78. export function successRateTone(successRate: number | null) {
79.   if (successRate === null) {
80.     return "neutral";
81.   }
82. 
83.   if (successRate >= 60) {
84.     return "success";
85.   }
86. 
87.   if (successRate < 40) {
88.     return "danger";
89.   }
90. 
91.   return "neutral";
92. }
93. 
94. export function compactUniverseLabel(value: string) {
95.   return value.replace(/\s*\([^)]*\)\s*$/, "");
96. }
97. 
98. export function universeQualifier(value: string) {
99.   const match = value.match(/\(([^)]+)\)/);
100.   return match?.[1] ?? value;
101. }
102. 
103. export function normalizeSymbol(value: string) {
104.   return value.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
105. }
106. 
107. export function SectionHeader({
108.   eyebrow,
109.   title,
110.   subtitle,
111.   tone = "brand"
112. }: {
113.   eyebrow: string;
114.   title: string;
115.   subtitle: string;
116.   tone?: AccentTone;
117. }) {
118.   return (
119.     <div className={`section-header tone-${tone}`}>
120.       <span className="section-eyebrow">{eyebrow}</span>
121.       <h2>{title}</h2>
122.       <p className="section-copy">{subtitle}</p>
123.     </div>
124.   );
125. }
126. 
127. export function MetricTile({
128.   label,
129.   value,
130.   footnote
131. }: {
132.   label: string;
133.   value: string;
134.   footnote?: string;
135. }) {
136.   return (
137.     <article className="metric-tile">
138.       <span className="metric-label">{label}</span>
139.       <strong>{value}</strong>
140.       {footnote ? <span className="metric-footnote">{footnote}</span> : null}
141.     </article>
142.   );
143. }
144. 
145. export function SignalSection({
146.   title,
147.   subtitle,
148.   signals,
149.   tone
150. }: {
151.   title: string;
152.   subtitle: string;
153.   signals?: Signal[];
154.   tone: AccentTone;
155. }) {
156.   if (!signals?.length) {
157.     return null;
158.   }
159. 
160.   return (
161.     <section className={`card category-panel tone-${tone}`}>
162.       <div className="panel-title">
163.         <span className="panel-kicker">{title}</span>
164.         <h3>{title}</h3>
165.         <p>{subtitle}</p>
166.       </div>
167.       <div className="signal-tile-grid">
168.         {signals.map((signal) => (
169.           <article key={`${title}-${signal.name}`} className="signal-tile">
170.             <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
171.             <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
172.           </article>
173.         ))}
174.       </div>
175.     </section>
176.   );
177. }
178. 
179. export function EmptyState({ message }: { message: string }) {
180.   return <div className="empty-state">{message}</div>;
181. }
182. 
183. export function DailyPerformanceCard({ item }: { item: DailyPerformance }) {
184.   const tone = successRateTone(item.successRate);
185. 
186.   return (
187.     <article className={`performance-card ${tone}`}>
188.       <div className="performance-card-top">
189.         <div>
190.           <span className="panel-kicker">Trading day</span>
191.           <h3>{formatDate(item.batchDate)}</h3>
192.         </div>
193.         <span className={`result-chip ${tone}`}>
194.           {item.successRate === null
195.             ? "Pending"
196.             : item.successRate >= 60
197.               ? "Strong"
198.               : item.successRate < 40
199.                 ? "Weak"
200.                 : "Mixed"}
201.         </span>
202.       </div>
203. 
204.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
205.         <MetricTile label="Success rate" value={formatPercent(item.successRate)} />
206.         <MetricTile label="Closed calls" value={String(item.closed)} />
207.         <MetricTile label="Winners" value={String(item.successful)} />
208.         <MetricTile label="Stops" value={String(item.failed)} />
209.       </div>
210.     </article>
211.   );
212. }
213.

239.         <MetricTile label="Stop-loss" value={formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)} />
240.         <MetricTile label="Score" value={plan.score?.toFixed(1) ?? "n/a"} />
241.       </div>
242. 
243.       <p className="idea-summary">{plan.summary}</p>
244.     </article>
245.   );
246. }
247. 
248. export function StockOutcomeCard({ item }: { item: StockPerformanceHistoryEntry }) {

250.     <article className={`stock-outcome-card ${item.outcome.result}`}>

256.             Entry {formatPrice(item.entryPrice)} · Target {formatPrice(item.targetPrice)} · Stop{" "}
257.             {formatPrice(item.stopLoss)}
258.           </p>
259.         </div>
260.         <span

28.   data: RecommendationDataset;
29.   analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;

33. type UiTone = "brand" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral";
34. 
35. function defaultHorizon(data: RecommendationDataset, analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null) {
36.   const preferred = analysis?.recommendedHorizons?.[0];
37. 
38.   if (preferred && data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === preferred)) {
39.     return preferred;
40.   }
41. 
42.   return data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day";
43. }
44. 
45. function DetailRow({
46.   left,
47.   right
48. }: {
49.   left: ReactNode | null;
50.   right?: ReactNode | null;
51. }) {
52.   if (!left && !right) {
53.     return null;
54.   }
55. 
56.   if (!left || !right) {
57.     return <div className="detail-row single">{left ?? right}</div>;
58.   }
59. 
60.   return (
61.     <div className="detail-row">
62.       {left}
63.       {right}
64.     </div>
65.   );
66. }
67. 
68. function scoreInsight(score: number | undefined) {
69.   if (score === undefined || !Number.isFinite(score)) {
70.     return {
71.       label: "Awaiting model score",
72.       tone: "neutral" as UiTone,
73.       progress: 0
74.     };
75.   }
76. 
77.   if (score >= 70) {
78.     return { label: "Strong Confidence", tone: "success" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
79.   }
80. 
81.   if (score >= 55) {
82.     return { label: "Moderate Confidence", tone: "brand" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
83.   }
84. 
85.   if (score >= 45) {
86.     return { label: "Cautious Setup", tone: "warning" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
87.   }
88. 
89.   return { label: "Low Confidence", tone: "danger" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
90. }
91. 
92. function riskRewardInsight(riskReward: number) {
93.   if (riskReward >= 1.5) {
94.     return { label: "Good Risk/Reward", tone: "success" as UiTone };
95.   }
96. 
97.   if (riskReward >= 1) {
98.     return { label: "Balanced Risk/Reward", tone: "warning" as UiTone };
99.   }
100. 
101.   return { label: "Weak Risk/Reward", tone: "danger" as UiTone };
102. }
103. 
104. function priceTrendInsight(change: number | null | undefined) {
105.   if (change === null || change === undefined || !Number.isFinite(change)) {
106.     return { label: "Session trend unavailable", tone: "neutral" as UiTone };
107.   }
108. 
109.   if (change > 0) {
110.     return { label: `↑ +${change.toFixed(2)}%`, tone: "success" as UiTone };
111.   }
112. 
113.   if (change < 0) {
114.     return { label: `↓ -${Math.abs(change).toFixed(2)}%`, tone: "danger" as UiTone };
115.   }
116. 
117.   return { label: "→ 0.00%", tone: "neutral" as UiTone };
118. }
119.

It is needed so you can understand the cause-and-effect logic behind the recommendation instead of relying only on raw scores.\",\n+    technical:\n+      \"Technical analysis tracks trend, breakout level, candle quality, and participation. It is needed because price and volume reveal whether buyers are truly taking control or whether the move is still weak under resistance.\",\n+    technicalReasoning:\n+      \"This reasoning panel explains which chart conditions are helping or hurting the setup, why they matter, and how they change the chance of follow-through or failure.\",\n+    fundamental:\n+      \"Fundamental analysis checks business quality, growth, profitability, and ownership context. It is needed because strong businesses usually support trends better, while weak quality can make rallies fade faster.\",\n+    sentiment:\n+      \"Sentiment analysis tracks headline tone, earnings cues, and analyst narrative. It is needed because supportive or damaging narrative can accelerate or cap a stock move even when the chart looks strong.\",\n+    risk:\n+      \"Risk controls measure fragility, recent stop-loss patterns, volatility pressure, and payoff quality. They are needed so the model can reduce exposure to attractive-looking setups that often fail in practice.\",\n+    earnings:\n+      \"The earnings lens isolates earnings-related headlines. It is needed because results surprises and management commentary can quickly change the next leg of a stock move.\",\n+    analyst:\n+      \"The analyst lens tracks broker and revision-style headlines. It is needed because upgrades, downgrades, and estimate changes can strengthen or weaken conviction behind the move.\",\n+    fundamentalsSnapshot:\n+      \"This snapshot shows the raw business context feeding the fundamental layer. It helps verify whether the company quality underneath the chart is strong enough to support the setup.\",\n+    sentimentSnapshot:\n+      \"This snapshot summarizes the current narrative balance. It helps show whether the stock is getting positive support, mixed chatter, or negative pressure from the news flow.\",\n+    headlines:\n+      \"These are the recent headlines feeding the sentiment layer. They are needed because catalysts often explain why a move is accelerating, stalling, or becoming fragile.\",\n+    context:\n+      \"This panel collects market and news context around the setup. It helps connect the recommendation to the broader environment instead of viewing the stock in isolation.\"\n+  } as const;\n+  const scoreNavigatorCards = [\n+    {\n+      area: \"technical\" as const,\n+      label: \"Technical score\",\n+      value: signalValue(selectedPlan.technicalSignals, \"Technical score\"),\n+      note: \"Jump to the chart-structure reasoning panel.\"\n+    },\n+    {\n+      area: \"fundamental\" as const,\n+      label: \"Fundamental score\",\n+      value: signalValue(selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals, \"Fundamental score\"),\n+      note: \"Jump to the business-quality reasoning panel.\"\n+    },\n+    {\n+      area: \"sentiment\" as const,\n+      label: \"Sentiment score\",\n+      value: signalValue(selectedPlan.sentimentSignals, \"Sentiment score\"),\n+      note: \"Jump to the news and catalyst reasoning panel.\"\n+    },\n+    {\n+      area: \"risk\" as const,\n+      label: \"Risk penalty\",\n+      value: signalValue(selectedPlan.riskSignals, \"Fragility penalty\"),\n+      note: \"Jump to the downside and stop-loss reasoning panel.\"\n+    }\n+  ];\n \n   const horizonPlans = data.profiles.map((profile) => ({\n     ...profile,\n     plan: stock.profiles[profile.id]\n   }));\n+\n+  const jumpToReasoning = (area: ReasoningArea) => {\n+    if (typeof window === \"undefined\") {\n+      return;\n+    }\n+\n+    const panelId = REASONING_PANEL_IDS[area];\n+    setFocusedReasoning(area);\n+    window.history.replaceState(null, \"\", `#${panelId}`);\n+    document.getElementById(panelId)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: \"smooth\", block: \"start\" });\n+  };\n*** End Patch\n"

282.       "This panel explains the model call in plain language. It is needed so you can understand the cause-and-effect logic behind the recommendation instead of relying only on raw scores.",
283.     technical:
284.       "Technical analysis tracks trend, breakout level, candle quality, and participation. It is needed because price and volume reveal whether buyers are truly taking control or whether the move is still weak under resistance.",
285.     technicalReasoning:
286.       "This reasoning panel explains which chart conditions are helping or hurting the setup, why they matter, and how they change the chance of follow-through or failure.",
287.     fundamental:
288.       "Fundamental analysis checks business quality, growth, profitability, and ownership context. It is needed because strong businesses usually support trends better, while weak quality can make rallies fade faster.",
289.     sentiment:
290.       "Sentiment analysis tracks headline tone, earnings cues, and analyst narrative. It is needed because supportive or damaging narrative can accelerate or cap a stock move even when the chart looks strong.",
291.     risk:
292.       "Risk controls measure fragility, recent stop-loss patterns, volatility pressure, and payoff quality. They are needed so the model can reduce exposure to attractive-looking setups that often fail in practice.",
293.     earnings:
294.       "The earnings lens isolates earnings-related headlines. It is needed because results surprises and management commentary can quickly change the next leg of a stock move.",
295.     analyst:
296.       "The analyst lens tracks broker and revision-style headlines. It is needed because upgrades, downgrades, and estimate changes can strengthen or weaken conviction behind the move.",
297.     fundamentalsSnapshot:
298.       "This snapshot shows the raw business context feeding the fundamental layer. It helps verify whether the company quality underneath the chart is strong enough to support the setup.",
299.     sentimentSnapshot:
300.       "This snapshot summarizes the current narrative balance. It helps show whether the stock is getting positive support, mixed chatter, or negative pressure from the news flow.",
301.     headlines:
302.       "These are the recent headlines feeding the sentiment layer. They are needed because catalysts often explain why a move is accelerating, stalling, or becoming fragile.",
303.     context:
304.       "This panel collects market and news context around the setup. It helps connect the recommendation to the broader environment instead of viewing the stock in isolation."
305.   } as const;
306.   const scoreNavigatorCards = [
307.     {
308.       area: "technical" as const,
309.       label: "Technical score",
310.       value: signalValue(selectedPlan.technicalSignals, "Technical score"),
311.       note: "Jump to the chart-structure reasoning panel."
312.     },
313.     {
314.       area: "fundamental" as const,
315.       label: "Fundamental score",
316.       value: signalValue(selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals, "Fundamental score"),
317.       note: "Jump to the business-quality reasoning panel."
318.     },
319.     {
320.       area: "sentiment" as const,
321.       label: "Sentiment score",
322.       value: signalValue(selectedPlan.sentimentSignals, "Sentiment score"),
323.       note: "Jump to the news and catalyst reasoning panel."
324.     },
325.     {
326.       area: "risk" as const,
327.       label: "Risk penalty",
328.       value: signalValue(selectedPlan.riskSignals, "Fragility penalty"),
329.       note: "Jump to the downside and stop-loss reasoning panel."
330.     }
331.   ];
332. 
333.   const horizonPlans = data.profiles.map((profile) => ({
334.     ...profile,
335.     plan: stock.profiles[profile.id]
336.   }));
337. 
338.   const jumpToReasoning = (area: ReasoningArea) => {
339.     if (typeof window === "undefined") {
340.       return;
341.     }
342. 
343.     const panelId = REASONING_PANEL_IDS[area];
344.     setFocusedReasoning(area);
345.     window.history.replaceState(null, "", `#${panelId}`);
346.     document.getElementById(panelId)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" });
347.   };
348. 
349.   const verdictTone =
350.     analysis?.status === "analyzed"
351.       ? analysis.shouldConsider
352.         ? "success"
353.         : "danger"
354.       : "neutral";
355.   const primaryVerdict = analysis?.status === "analyzed" ? analysis.verdict : selectedPlan.summary;
356.   const technicalCard = (
357.     <SignalSection
358.       title="Technical analysis"
359.       subtitle="Momentum, chart structure, breakout, candle-quality, and trend readings used in the model."
360.       signals={selectedPlan.technicalSignals}
361.       tone="technical"
362.       info={panelInfo.technical}
363.     />
364.   );
365.   const fundamentalAnalysisCard = (
366.     <SignalSection
367.       title="Fundamental analysis"
368.       subtitle="Business quality and valuation context from the fundamentals layer."
369.       signals={selectedPlan.fundamentalSignals}
370.       tone="fundamental"
371.       info={panelInfo.fundamental}
372.     />
373.   );
374.   const sentimentAnalysisCard = (
375.     <SignalSection
376.       title="Sentiment and news"
377.       subtitle="Headline tone, earnings cues, and external narrative signals."
378.       signals={selectedPlan.sentimentSignals}
379.       tone="sentiment"
380.       info={panelInfo.sentiment}
381.     />
382.   );
383.   const riskCard = (
384.     <SignalSection
385.       title="Risk controls"
386.       subtitle="Stop-loss learning, fragility checks, and downside guardrails."
387.       signals={selectedPlan.riskSignals}
388.       tone="risk"
389.       info={panelInfo.risk}
390.     />
391.   );
392.   const earningsCard = (
393.     <SignalSection
394.       title="Earnings lens"
395.       subtitle="Recent earnings-related cues extracted from the research layer."
396.       signals={selectedPlan.earningsSignals}
397.       tone="fundamental"
398.       info={panelInfo.earnings}
399.     />
400.   );
401.   const analystCard = (
402.     <SignalSection
403.       title="Analyst lens"
404.       subtitle="Revision and broker-tone style cues reflected in the model overlays."
405.       signals={selectedPlan.analystSignals}
406.       tone="sentiment"
407.       info={panelInfo.analyst}
408.     />
409.   );
410.   const technicalReasoningCard = (
411.     <ReasoningPanel
412.       panelId={REASONING_PANEL_IDS.technical}
413.       title="Technical reasoning"
414.       subtitle="What the chart is doing, why it matters, and how it changes the odds of follow-through."
415.       drivers={technicalDrivers}
416.       tone="technical"
417.       info={panelInfo.technicalReasoning}
418.       active={focusedReasoning === "technical"}
419.     />
420.   );
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 385m 9s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md.**

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 6-24)

<details>
<summary>19 lines</summary>

```
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
18.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
19.   - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
20.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
21.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
22. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
23. 
24. ## Proposed approach
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 386m 9s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Done — the stock page now has a proper **score-to-reasoning flow**.

**What changed**
1. **Explainability navigator:** technical, fundamental, sentiment, and risk score tiles now jump to anchored reasoning panels.
2. **Reasoning panels:** each panel explains the **logic**, not just the score — what is helping or hurting the stock, **why it matters**, and **how it can impact the move**.
3. **Hover help:** the analysis panels now have hover info tooltips that explain **what the panel is**, **why it is needed**, and **how it affects the stock**.
4. **Highlight on jump:** when you click a score tile, the destination reasoning panel is brought into view and visually highlighted.
5. **Data model alignment:** the new reasoning structure is also reflected in the API schema.

**Updated files**
- `apps\web\components\stock-detail.tsx`
- `apps\web\components\market-ui.tsx`
- `apps\web\app\globals.css`
- `apps\web\lib\types.ts`
- `apps\web\lib\recommendation-data.ts`
- `services\api\app\schemas.py`
- `services\api\app\main.py`

{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.status\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\nAND NOT EXISTS (\n  SELECT 1\n  FROM todo_deps td\n  JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n  WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n)\nORDER BY t.id;"
}

1. "use client";
2. 
3. import Link from "next/link";
4. import type { ReactNode } from "react";
5. import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
6. import {
7.   EmptyState,
8.   InfoTooltip,
9.   MetricTile,
10.   ReasoningPanel,
11.   SectionHeader,
12.   SignalSection,
13.   StockOutcomeCard,
14.   formatCrore,
15.   formatNumber,
16.   formatPrice,
17.   isRecommendedPlan,
18.   normalizeSymbol
19. } from "@/components/market-ui";
20. import { buildStockPerformanceHistory } from "@/lib/analytics";
21. import type {
22.   AnalysisDriver,
23.   HorizonId,
24.   RecommendationPlan,
25.   RecommendationDataset,
26.   SearchAnalysisResult,
27.   Signal,
28.   StockAnalysis,
29.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
30. } from "@/lib/types";
31. 
32. type StockDetailProps = {
33.   data: RecommendationDataset;
34.   analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;
35.   requestedSymbol: string;
36. };
37. 
38. type UiTone = "brand" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral";
39. type ReasoningArea = AnalysisDriver["area"];
40. 
41. const REASONING_PANEL_IDS: Record<ReasoningArea, string> = {
42.   technical: "reasoning-technical",
43.   fundamental: "reasoning-fundamental",
44.   sentiment: "reasoning-sentiment",
45.   risk: "reasoning-risk"
46. };
47. 
48. function defaultHorizon(data: RecommendationDataset, analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null) {
49.   const preferred = analysis?.recommendedHorizons?.[0];
50. 
51.   if (preferred && data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === preferred)) {
52.     return preferred;
53.   }
54. 
55.   return data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day";
56. }
57. 
58. function DetailRow({
59.   left,
60.   right
61. }: {
62.   left: ReactNode | null;
63.   right?: ReactNode | null;
64. }) {
65.   if (!left && !right) {
66.     return null;
67.   }
68. 
69.   if (!left || !right) {
70.     return <div className="detail-row single">{left ?? right}</div>;
71.   }
72. 
73.   return (
74.     <div className="detail-row">
75.       {left}
76.       {right}
77.     </div>
78.   );
79. }
80. 
81. function signalValue(signals: Signal[] | undefined, name: string) {
82.   return signals?.find((signal) => signal.name === name)?.value ?? "n/a";
83. }
84. 
85. function reasoningDriversFor(plan: RecommendationPlan, area: ReasoningArea): AnalysisDriver[] {
86.   const explicitDrivers = plan.analysisDrivers?.filter((driver) => driver.area === area);
87. 
88.   if (explicitDrivers?.length) {
89.     return explicitDrivers;
90.   }
91. 
92.   return [
93.     {
94.       area,
95.       impact: "neutral",
96.       title: "Saved model rationale",
97.       detail:
98.         plan.drivers[0] ??
99.         "This saved snapshot predates the richer reasoning view. Use the evidence tiles below until the next live batch refresh."
100.     }
101.   ];
102. }
103. 
104. function scoreInsight(score: number | undefined) {
105.   if (score === undefined || !Number.isFinite(score)) {
106.     return {
107.       label: "Awaiting model score",
108.       tone: "neutral" as UiTone,
109.       progress: 0
110.     };
111.   }
112. 
113.   if (score >= 70) {
114.     return { label: "Strong Confidence", tone: "success" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
115.   }
116. 
117.   if (score >= 55) {
118.     return { label: "Moderate Confidence", tone: "brand" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
119.   }
120. 
121.   if (score >= 45) {
122.     return { label: "Cautious Setup", tone: "warning" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
123.   }
124. 
125.   return { label: "Low Confidence", tone: "danger" as UiTone, progress: Math.min(score, 100) };
126. }
127. 
128. function riskRewardInsight(riskReward: number) {
129.   if (riskReward >= 1.5) {
130.     return { label: "Good Risk/Reward", tone: "success" as UiTone };
131.   }
132. 
133.   if (riskReward >= 1) {
134.     return { label: "Balanced Risk/Reward", tone: "warning" as UiTone };
135.   }
136. 
137.   return { label: "Weak Risk/Reward", tone: "danger" as UiTone };
138. }
139. 
140. function priceTrendInsight(change: number | null | undefined) {
141.   if (change === null || change === undefined || !Number.isFinite(change)) {
142.     return { label: "Session trend unavailable", tone: "neutral" as UiTone };
143.   }
144. 
145.   if (change > 0) {
146.     return { label: `↑ +${change.toFixed(2)}%`, tone: "success" as UiTone };
147.   }
148. 
149.   if (change < 0) {
150.     return { label: `↓ -${Math.abs(change).toFixed(2)}%`, tone: "danger" as UiTone };
151.   }
152. 
153.   return { label: "→ 0.00%", tone: "neutral" as UiTone };
154. }
155. 
156. function actionLabel(profileLabel: string, isRecommended: boolean) {
157.   return isRecommended ? `Buy for ${profileLabel}` : `Watch ${profileLabel} setup`;
158. }
159. 
160. function decisionLabel(isRecommended: boolean) {
161.   return isRecommended ? "Consider" : "Avoid";
162. }
163. 
164. function TradeMetricCard({
165.   label,
166.   value,
167.   note,
168.   indicator,
169.   tone,
170.   progress
171. }: {
172.   label: string;
173.   value: string;
174.   note: string;
175.   indicator: string;
176.   tone: UiTone | "entry" | "target" | "stop";
177.   progress?: {
178.     value: number;
179.     tone: UiTone;
180.   };
181. }) {
182.   return (
183.     <article className={`trade-metric-card ${tone}${progress ? " has-progress" : ""}`}>
184.       <div className="trade-metric-top">
185.         <span className="trade-metric-indicator">{indicator}</span>
186.         <span className="trade-metric-label">{label}</span>
187.       </div>
188.       <strong className="trade-metric-value">{value}</strong>
189.       <span className="trade-metric-note">{note}</span>
190.       {progress ? (
191.         <div className={`score-progress ${progress.tone}`}>
192.           <span style={{ width: `${progress.value}%` }} />
193.         </div>
194.       ) : null}
195.     </article>
196.   );
197. }
198. 
199. export function StockDetail({ data, analysis, requestedSymbol }: StockDetailProps) {
200.   const fallbackStock = data.currentBatch.recommendations.find(
201.     (stock) => normalizeSymbol(stock.symbol) === normalizeSymbol(requestedSymbol)
202.   );
203.   const stock = (analysis?.stock ?? fallbackStock) as StockAnalysis | null;
204.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(() => defaultHorizon(data, analysis));
205.   const [focusedReasoning, setFocusedReasoning] = useState<ReasoningArea | null>(null);
206.   const stockHistory = useMemo<StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[]>(
207.     () => (stock ? buildStockPerformanceHistory(data.history, stock.symbol, activeHorizon) : []),
208.     [activeHorizon, data.history, stock]
209.   );
210. 
211.   useEffect(() => {
212.     const nextDefault = defaultHorizon(data, analysis);
213. 
214.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
215.       setActiveHorizon(nextDefault);
216.     }
217.   }, [activeHorizon, analysis, data.profiles]);
218. 
219.   useEffect(() => {
220.     if (typeof window === "undefined") {
221.       return;
222.     }
223. 
224.     const matchedEntry = Object.entries(REASONING_PANEL_IDS).find(
225.       ([, panelId]) => window.location.hash === `#${panelId}`
226.     );
227. 
228.     if (!matchedEntry) {
229.       return;
230.     }
231. 
232.     const [area, panelId] = matchedEntry as [ReasoningArea, string];
233.     setFocusedReasoning(area);
234. 
235.     window.requestAnimationFrame(() => {
236.       document.getElementById(panelId)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" });
237.     });
238.   }, [activeHorizon, stock?.symbol]);
239. 
240.   if (!stock) {
241.     return (
242.       <main className="shell">
243.         <section className="card masthead-card tone-risk">
244.           <div className="panel-banner">
245.             <div className="masthead-copy">
246.               <span className="section-eyebrow">Stock workspace</span>
247.               <h1>Stock not available</h1>
248.               <p>
249.                 I could not build a stock detail page for <strong>{requestedSymbol.toUpperCase()}</strong>{" "}
250.                 from the current live dataset.
251.               </p>
252.             </div>
253.             <div className="page-links">
254.               <Link className="secondary-link" href="/">
255.                 Back to dashboard
256.               </Link>
257.             </div>
258.           </div>
259. 
260.           <EmptyState message={analysis?.message ?? "Try searching another NSE symbol from the dashboard."} />
```

</details>

23.     style: "currency",
24.     currency: "INR",
25.     minimumFractionDigits: 2,
26.     maximumFractionDigits: 2
27.   }).format(value);
28. }
29. 
30. export function formatNumber(value: number, maximumFractionDigits = 0) {
31.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
32.     maximumFractionDigits
33.   }).format(value);
34. }
35. 
36. export function formatCrore(value: number | null) {
37.   if (value === null) {
38.     return "n/a";
39.   }
40. 
41.   return `${formatNumber(value)} Cr`;
42. }
43. 
44. export function formatPercent(value: number | null) {
45.   if (value === null) {
46.     return "Pending";
47.   }
48. 
49.   return `${value.toFixed(2)}%`;
50. }
51. 
52. export function formatDate(value: string) {
53.   return new Date(value).toLocaleDateString("en-IN");
54. }
55. 
56. export function formatDateTime(value: string) {
57.   return new Date(value).toLocaleString("en-IN");
58. }
59.

62. }

65.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
66. }
67.

71.       return "Achieved";
72.     case "stop_loss_hit":
73.       return "Not achieved";
74.     default:
75.       return "Open";
76.   }
77. }
78. 
79. export function successRateTone(successRate: number | null) {
80.   if (successRate === null) {
81.     return "neutral";
82.   }
83. 
84.   if (successRate >= 60) {
85.     return "success";
86.   }
87. 
88.   if (successRate < 40) {
89.     return "danger";
90.   }
91. 
92.   return "neutral";
93. }
94. 
95. export function compactUniverseLabel(value: string) {
96.   return value.replace(/\s*\([^)]*\)\s*$/, "");

98. 
99. export function universeQualifier(value: string) {
100.   const match = value.match(/\(([^)]+)\)/);
101.   return match?.[1] ?? value;
102. }

105.   return value.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");

145.

152. }) {
153.   return (

<sub>⏱️ 388m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the gap: earnings and analyst still only expose signal tiles, not their own reasoning objects. I’m extending the model so those two layers generate explicit “why this scored that way” explanations and making their score tiles jump to those explanations.

1816. 
1817. function buildTradePlan(
1818.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
1819.   entry: MarketSeries,
1820.   horizon: HorizonId,
1821.   learning: StopLossLearning | null = null
1822. ): PlanTemplate {
1823.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1824.   const technicalBreakdown = technicalScoreBreakdown(metrics, entry, horizon);
1825.   const rawScore = blendedModelScore(entry, metrics, horizon, technicalBreakdown.technicalScore);
1826.   const technicalScore = technicalBreakdown.technicalScore;
1827.   const chartScore = technicalBreakdown.chartScore;
1828.   const factorScore = technicalBreakdown.factorScore;
1829.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
1830.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1831.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1832.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1833.   const riskFeedback = stopLossFragilityFeedback(metrics, horizon, learning);
1834.   const score = roundMetric(clamp(rawScore - riskFeedback.penalty, 0, 100));
1835.   const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);
1836.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);
1837.   const targetPct = Math.max(
1838.     projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),
1839.     atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple
1840.   );
1841.   const stopPct = Math.max(
1842.     projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),
1843.     atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple
1844.   );
1845.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
1846.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));
1847.   const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;
1848.   const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));
1849.   const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);
1850.   const strengthLabel = horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics, horizon);
1851.   const returnLabel = horizonMomentumLabel(metrics, horizon);
1852.   const summaryBase = horizonSummary(entry, metrics, horizon);
1853.   const summary =
1854.     riskFeedback.reasons.length && riskFeedback.penalty > 0
1855.       ? `${summaryBase} Risk controls trimmed the setup because ${riskFeedback.reasons[0]}.`
1856.       : summaryBase;
1857.   const chartLabel =
1858.     (horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && (metrics.breakout20Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1859.       ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)} versus the recent 20-day high`
1860.       : horizon === "position" && (metrics.breakout55Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1861.         ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)} versus the recent 55-day high`
1862.         : horizon === "long_term" && (metrics.breakout120Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1863.           ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)} versus the recent 120-day high`
1864.           : `price has not cleanly cleared the recent breakout zone yet`;
1865. 
1866.   const analysisDrivers = [
1867.     ...buildTechnicalAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon),
1868.     ...buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry),
1869.     ...buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(entry, earningsScore, analystScore),
1870.     ...buildRiskAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon, riskFeedback, entryPrice, stopLoss, riskReward)
1871.   ];
1872.   const drivers = analysisDrivers.map((driver) => `${driver.title}: ${driver.detail}`).slice(0, 8);
1873. 
1874.   const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [
1875.     { name: "Adjusted score", value: signalScoreText(score) },
1876.     { name: "Raw blended score", value: signalScoreText(rawScore) },
1877.     { name: "Technical score", value: signalScoreText(technicalScore) },
1878.     { name: "Factor trend score", value: signalScoreText(factorScore) },
1879.     { name: "Chart structure score", value: signalScoreText(chartScore) },
1880.     {
1881.       name: horizonMomentumSignalName(horizon),
1882.       value: returnLabel
1883.     },
1884.     {
1885.       name: horizonRelativeStrengthSignalName(horizon),
1886.       value: strengthLabel
1887.     },
1888.     {
1889.       name: "Price vs 50DMA",
1890.       value: metrics.sma50 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma50)) : "n/a"
1891.     },
1892.     {
1893.       name: "Price vs 200DMA",
1894.       value: metrics.sma200 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma200)) : "n/a"
1895.     },
1896.     {
1897.       name: "Candle close location",
1898.       value: metrics.closeLocationPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.closeLocationPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1899.     },
1900.     {
1901.       name: "Candle body strength",
1902.       value: metrics.candleBodyPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.candleBodyPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1903.     },
1904.     {
1905.       name:
1906.         horizon === "single_day"
1907.           ? "20D breakout"
1908.           : horizon === "swing"
1909.             ? "20D breakout"
1910.             : horizon === "position"
1911.               ? "55D breakout"
1912.               : "120D breakout",
1913.       value:
1914.         horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing"
1915.           ? percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)
1916.           : horizon === "position"
1917.             ? percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)
1918.             : percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)
1919.     },
1920.     {
1921.       name: "Range compression (5D/20D)",
1922.       value: metrics.rangeCompression5v20 !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangeCompression5v20).toFixed(2)}x` : "n/a"
1923.     }
1924.   ];
1925. 
1926.   const fundamentalSignals: Signal[] = entry.fundamentals
1927.     ? [
1928.         { name: "Fundamental score", value: signalScoreText(fundamentalScore) },
1929.         { name: "Market cap", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.marketCapCrore) },
1930.         { name: "Revenue", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.revenueCrore) },
1931.         { name: "Profit", value: croreText(entry.fundamentals.profitCrore) },
1932.         {
1933.           name: "5Y sales growth",
1934.           value:
1935.             entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct !== null
1936.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct).toFixed(2)}%`
1937.               : "n/a"
1938.         },
1939.         {
1940.           name: "ROE",
1941.           value:
1942.             entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct !== null
1943.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct).toFixed(2)}%`
1944.               : "n/a"
1945.         },
1946.         {
1947.           name: "Promoter holding",
1948.           value:
1949.             entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct !== null
1950.               ? `${roundMetric(entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct).toFixed(2)}%`
1951.               : "n/a"
1952.         }
1953.       ]
1954.     : [
1955.         { name: "Fundamental score", value: signalScoreText(fundamentalScore) },
1956.         { name: "Market cap bucket", value: entry.marketCapBucket },
1957.         { name: "Liquidity tier", value: entry.liquidityTier },
1958.         { name: "20D volatility", value: percentText(metrics.volatility20) },
1959.         {
1960.           name: "52W range position",
1961.           value:
1962.             metrics.rangePosition !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangePosition).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1963.         }
1964.       ];
1965. 
1966.   const sentimentSignals: Signal[] = entry.sentiment
1967.     ? [
1968.         { name: "Sentiment score", value: signalScoreText(sentimentScore) },
1969.         { name: "Overall tone", value: entry.sentiment.overall },
1970.         {
1971.           name: "Headline balance",
1972.           value: `${entry.sentiment.positiveCount} positive / ${entry.sentiment.negativeCount} negative`
1973.         },
1974.         { name: "Neutral headlines", value: `${entry.sentiment.neutralCount}` }
1975.       ]
1976.     : [{ name: "Sentiment score", value: signalScoreText(sentimentScore) }];
1977. 
1978.   const earningsSignals: Signal[] = [
1979.     { name: "Earnings headline score", value: signalScoreText(earningsScore) },
1980.     {
1981.       name: "Earnings mentions",
1982.       value: `${entry.sentiment?.earningsMentionCount ?? 0} recent headlines`
1983.     }
1984.   ];
1985. 
1986.   const analystSignals: Signal[] = [
1987.     { name: "Analyst tone score", value: signalScoreText(analystScore) },
1988.     {
1989.       name: "Analyst mentions",
1990.       value: `${entry.sentiment?.analystMentionCount ?? 0} recent headlines`
1991.     }
1992.   ];
1993. 
1994.   const marketContext = [
1995.     `Latest session change: ${percentText(metrics.sessionChangePct)} while Nifty 50 moved ${percentText(metrics.benchmarkSessionChangePct)}.`,
1996.     `Current price ${priceText(metrics.currentPrice)} versus 52-week range ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekLow)} to ${priceText(metrics.fiftyTwoWeekHigh)}.`,
1997.     `Current volume ${compactNumber(metrics.currentVolume)} versus 20-day average ${compactNumber(metrics.avgVolume20)}.`,
1998.     ...(entry.sentiment?.headlines.slice(0, 3).map((headline) => `${headline.source}: ${headline.title}`) ?? [])
1999.   ];
2000. 
2001.   return {
2002.     score,
2003.     conviction,
2004.     entryPrice,
2005.     targetPrice,
2006.     stopLoss,
2007.     expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(expectedReturnPct),
2008.     riskReward,
2009.     summary,
2010.     drivers,
2011.     analysisDrivers,
2012.     technicalSignals,
2013.     fundamentalSignals,
2014.     sentimentSignals,
2015.     earningsSignals,
2016.     analystSignals,
2017.     riskSignals: riskFeedback.signals,
2018.     newsContext: marketContext
```

</details>

462.           />
463.         </div>
464.         <h3>Business context</h3>
465.         <p>Key business and profitability markers from the fundamentals layer.</p>
466.       </div>
467. 
468.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
469.         <MetricTile label="Market cap" value={formatCrore(stock.fundamentals.marketCapCrore)} />
470.         <MetricTile label="Revenue" value={formatCrore(stock.fundamentals.revenueCrore)} />
471.         <MetricTile label="Profit" value={formatCrore(stock.fundamentals.profitCrore)} />
472.         <MetricTile
473.           label="ROE"
474.           value={
475.             stock.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct !== null
476.               ? `${stock.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct.toFixed(1)}%`
477.               : "n/a"
478.           }
479.         />
480.       </div>
481.     </section>
482.   ) : null;
483.   const sentimentSnapshotCard = stock.sentiment ? (
484.     <section className="card tone-sentiment">
485.       <div className="panel-title">
486.         <div className="panel-title-heading">
487.           <span className="panel-kicker">Sentiment snapshot</span>
488.           <InfoTooltip label="Sentiment snapshot information" content={panelInfo.sentimentSnapshot} />
489.         </div>
490.         <h3>Headline tone summary</h3>
491.         <p>Quick counts from the headline-based sentiment layer.</p>
492.       </div>
493. 
494.       <div className="mini-metric-grid">
495.         <MetricTile label="Overall tone" value={stock.sentiment.overall} />
496.         <MetricTile label="Sentiment score" value={stock.sentiment.score.toFixed(1)} />
497.         <MetricTile label="Positive headlines" value={formatNumber(stock.sentiment.positiveCount)} />
498.         <MetricTile label="Negative headlines" value={formatNumber(stock.sentiment.negativeCount)} />
499.       </div>
500.     </section>
501.   ) : null;
502.   const headlinesCard = stock.sentiment?.headlines?.length ? (
503.     <section className="card tone-sentiment">
504.       <div className="panel-title">
505.         <div className="panel-title-heading">
506.           <span className="panel-kicker">Recent headlines</span>
507.           <InfoTooltip label="Recent headlines information" content={panelInfo.headlines} />
508.         </div>
509.         <h3>Latest sentiment inputs</h3>
510.         <p>Recent headlines that influenced the narrative layer.</p>
511.       </div>
512. 
513.       <div className="headline-list">
514.         {stock.sentiment.headlines.slice(0, 6).map((headline, index) => (
515.           <article key={`${headline.title}-${index}`} className="headline-item">
516.             <strong>{headline.title}</strong>
517.             <span className="headline-meta">
518.               {headline.source} · {headline.category} · {headline.tone}
519.             </span>
520.           </article>
521.         ))}
522.       </div>
523.     </section>
524.   ) : null;
525.   const researchContextCard = selectedPlan.newsContext?.length ? (
526.     <section className="card category-panel tone-history">
527.       <div className="panel-title">
528.         <div className="panel-title-heading">
529.           <span className="panel-kicker">Research context</span>
530.           <InfoTooltip label="Research context information" content={panelInfo.context} />
531.         </div>
532.         <h3>Headline and market context</h3>
533.         <p>Supporting notes that provide extra narrative behind the current setup.</p>
534.       </div>
535.       <ul className="list">
536.         {selectedPlan.newsContext.map((note, index) => (
537.           <li key={`${note}-${index}`}>{note}</li>
538.         ))}
539.       </ul>
540.     </section>
541.   ) : null;
542. 
543.   return (
544.     <main className="shell">
545.       <section className="card masthead-card tone-brand">
546.         <div className="page-utility-row">
547.           <div className="page-links">
548.             <Link className="secondary-link" href="/">
549.               Dashboard
550.             </Link>
551.             <Link className="secondary-link" href="/history">
552.               Archive
553.             </Link>
554.           </div>
555.         </div>
556. 
557.         <div className="trade-hero-layout">
558.           <div className="trade-hero-copy">
559.             <span className="section-eyebrow">Dedicated stock workspace</span>
560.             <h1 className="trade-hero-title">{activeProfileLabel} trade setup</h1>
561.             <div className="trade-company-line">
562.               <span className="trade-symbol-pill">{stock.symbol}</span>
563.               <span>{stock.companyName}</span>
564.               <span>{stock.sector}</span>
565.               {stock.industry ? <span>{stock.industry}</span> : null}
566.               <span>{stock.marketCapBucket}</span>
567.               <span>{stock.liquidityTier}</span>
568.             </div>
569.             <div className="trade-price-strip">
570.               <div className="trade-price-block">
571.                 <span className="trade-price-label">Current price</span>
572.                 <strong>{formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)}</strong>
573.               </div>
574.               <span className={`trade-status-pill ${selectedPriceTrend.tone}`}>
575.                 {selectedPriceTrend.label}
576.               </span>
577.               <span className={`trade-status-pill ${selectedScoreInsight.tone}`}>
578.                 {selectedScoreInsight.label}
579.               </span>
580.             </div>
581.             <p className="summary">{primaryVerdict}</p>
582.           </div>
583. 
584.           <div className="trade-hero-actions">
585.             <span className={`trade-decision-chip ${verdictTone}`}>{comparisonDecisionText}</span>
586.             <div className="trade-cta-group">
587.               <a className="hero-primary-cta" href="#trade-setup">
588.                 {primaryActionText}
589.               </a>
590.               <a className="hero-secondary-cta" href="#trade-setup">
591.                 View Trade Setup
592.               </a>
593.             </div>
594.             <p className="trade-cta-note">
595.               {selectedPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} - {selectedRiskRewardInsight.label}
596.             </p>
597.           </div>
598.         </div>
599. 
600.         <div className="horizon-tabs" role="tablist" aria-label="Horizon selector">
601.           {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
602.             <button
603.               key={profile.id}
604.               className={`horizon-tab${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
605.               onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
606.               role="tab"
607.               aria-selected={profile.id === activeHorizon}
608.               type="button"
609.             >
610.               <span className="horizon-tab-title">{profile.label}</span>
611.               <span className="horizon-tab-meta">{profile.window}</span>
612.             </button>
613.           ))}
614.         </div>
615.       </section>
616. 
617.       <section className="section-cluster">
618.         <SectionHeader
619.           eyebrow="Trade plan"
620.           title="Read the setup in one scan"
```

</details>
