560.   const closeLocation = metrics.closeLocationPct;
561.   const upperWick = metrics.upperWickPct;
562.   const candleBody = metrics.candleBodyPct;
563.   let candleImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
564.   let candleDetail =
565.     "The latest candle structure is mixed, so the model is not treating the most recent session as a decisive control candle.";
566. 
567.   if (closeLocation !== null && upperWick !== null && candleBody !== null) {
568.     if (closeLocation >= 65 && upperWick <= 25 && candleBody >= 45) {
569.       candleImpact = "positive";
570.       candleDetail = `The latest candle closed near the top of the range (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%) with only ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because strong closes show buyers kept control into the end of the session.`;
571.     } else if (closeLocation < 42 || upperWick > 45) {
572.       candleImpact = "negative";
573.       candleDetail = `The latest candle finished weakly (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% close location) with ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because sellers were able to push price away from intraday highs, which often leads to failed follow-through.`;
574.     } else {
575.       candleDetail = `The latest candle closed at ${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% of its range with a ${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}% real body. That gives the setup some balance, but not enough to treat the last bar as a strong confirmation candle on its own.`;
576.     }
577.   }
578. 
579.   const relativeStrength = horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon);
580.   const volumeRatio = metrics.volumeRatio;
581.   let participationImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
582.   let participationDetail =
583.     "Participation signals are mixed, so the model is not assuming broad institutional sponsorship behind the current move yet.";
584. 
585.   if (relativeStrength !== null && volumeRatio !== null) {
586.     if (relativeStrength > 0 && volumeRatio >= 1.05) {
587.       participationImpact = "positive";
588.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} and volume is ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because breakouts work better when the stock is already outperforming the benchmark and participation is expanding.`;
589.     } else if (relativeStrength < 0 || volumeRatio < 0.9) {
590.       participationImpact = "negative";
591.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} while volume is only ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because weaker participation makes rallies easier to fade and harder to sustain.`;
592.     } else {
593.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;
594.     }
595.   }
596. 
597.   return [
598.     { area: "technical", impact: breakoutImpact, title: "Breakout position", detail: breakoutDetail },
599.     { area: "technical", impact: candleImpact, title: "Candle quality", detail: candleDetail },
600.     { area: "technical", impact: participationImpact, title: "Participation", detail: participationDetail }
601.   ];
602. }
603. 
604. function buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries): AnalysisDriver[] {
605.   if (!entry.fundamentals) {
606.     return [
607.       {
608.         area: "fundamental",
609.         impact: "neutral",
610.         title: "Fundamental coverage",
611.         detail:
612.           "Live fundamental fields were not available for this stock today, so the recommendation is leaning more heavily on chart structure, liquidity, and risk controls than on business-quality data."
613.       }
614.     ];
615.   }
616. 
617.   const growth = entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct;
618.   const roe = entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct;
619.   const promoterHolding = entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct;
620.   const netMargin = entry.fundamentals.netMarginPct;
621. 
622.   let qualityImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
623.   let qualityDetail =
624.     "Fundamental quality is mixed, so the business backdrop is not strongly amplifying or blocking the technical setup.";
625. 
626.   if ((growth ?? 0) >= 12 || (roe ?? 0) >= 15) {
627.     qualityImpact = "positive";
628.     qualityDetail = `The business shows ${growth !== null ? `${roundMetric(growth).toFixed(2)}% five-year sales growth` : "stable sales growth"} and ${roe !== null ? `${roundMetric(roe).toFixed(2)}% ROE` : "reasonable returns on equity"}. That matters because strong growth plus healthy returns makes technical strength more credible over longer holding periods.`;
629.   } else if ((growth !== null && growth < 5) || (roe !== null && roe < 10)) {
630.     qualityImpact = "negative";
631.     qualityDetail = `The business only shows ${growth !== null ? `${roundMetric(growth).toFixed(2)}% five-year sales growth` : "limited visible growth"} and ${roe !== null ? `${roundMetric(roe).toFixed(2)}% ROE` : "unclear returns on equity"}. That matters because weak operating quality makes price breakouts more vulnerable to mean reversion.`;
632.   }
633. 
634.   let ownershipImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
635.   let ownershipDetail =
636.     "Ownership and profitability markers are steady, so they are supporting stability but are not acting as a major upside catalyst by themselves.";
637. 
638.   if ((promoterHolding ?? 0) >= 50 || (netMargin ?? 0) >= 10) {
639.     ownershipImpact = "positive";
640.     ownershipDetail = `Promoter holding is ${promoterHolding !== null ? `${roundMetric(promoterHolding).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"} and net margin is ${netMargin !== null ? `${roundMetric(netMargin).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}. That matters because stronger insider alignment and profitability reduce the chance that the move is being carried only by short-term speculation.`;
641.   } else if ((promoterHolding !== null && promoterHolding < 40) || (netMargin !== null && netMargin < 5)) {
642.     ownershipImpact = "negative";
643.     ownershipDetail = `Promoter holding is ${promoterHolding !== null ? `${roundMetric(promoterHolding).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"} and net margin is ${netMargin !== null ? `${roundMetric(netMargin).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}. That matters because weaker alignment or profitability leaves less business support underneath the trade setup.`;
644.   }
645. 
646.   return [
647.     { area: "fundamental", impact: qualityImpact, title: "Business quality", detail: qualityDetail },
648.     { area: "fundamental", impact: ownershipImpact, title: "Ownership and profitability", detail: ownershipDetail }
649.   ];
650. }
651. 
652. function buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(
653.   entry: MarketSeries,
654.   earningsScore: number,
655.   analystScore: number
656. ): AnalysisDriver[] {
657.   if (!entry.sentiment) {
658.     return [
659.       {
660.         area: "sentiment",
661.         impact: "neutral",
662.         title: "Headline coverage",
663.         detail:
664.           "No strong recent news, earnings, or analyst headline cluster was found, so sentiment is not materially changing the recommendation in either direction."
665.       }
666.     ];
667.   }
668. 
669.   const sentiment = entry.sentiment;
670.   const headlineBalance = sentiment.positiveCount - sentiment.negativeCount;
671.   let toneImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
672.   let toneDetail =
673.     "Headline tone is balanced, so sentiment is acting more like background context than like a hard catalyst.";
674. 
675.   if (headlineBalance > 0 && sentiment.score >= 0) {
676.     toneImpact = "positive";
677.     toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because positive narrative support makes investors more willing to keep adding on strength.`;
678.   } else if (headlineBalance < 0 || sentiment.score < 0) {
679.     toneImpact = "negative";
680.     toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because a negative narrative can quickly cap upside even when the chart looks constructive.`;
681.   }
682. 
683.   const overlayCount = sentiment.earningsMentionCount + sentiment.analystMentionCount;
684.   const overlayScore = Math.max(earningsScore, analystScore);
685.   let catalystImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
686.   let catalystDetail =
687.     "Recent coverage does not show a strong earnings or analyst revision cluster, so narrative momentum is present but not being driven by a clear catalyst.";
688. 
689.   if (overlayCount > 0 && overlayScore >= 60) {
690.     catalystImpact = "positive";
691.     catalystDetail = `The research feed found ${sentiment.earningsMentionCount} earnings-related and ${sentiment.analystMentionCount} analyst-related headlines. That matters because revisions and earnings follow-through often create the catalyst that keeps a breakout moving after the initial price move.`;
692.   } else if (overlayCount > 0 && overlayScore <= 45) {
693.     catalystImpact = "negative";
694.     catalystDetail = `The research feed found ${sentiment.earningsMentionCount} earnings-related and ${sentiment.analystMentionCount} analyst-related headlines, but their tone is not supportive. That matters because earnings or analyst disappointment can override otherwise decent technical structure.`;
695.   }
696. 
697.   return [
698.     { area: "sentiment", impact: toneImpact, title: "Narrative tone", detail: toneDetail },
699.     { area: "sentiment", impact: catalystImpact, title: "Earnings and analyst catalyst", detail: catalystDetail }
700.   ];
701. }
702. 
703. function buildRiskAnalysisDrivers(
704.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
705.   horizon: HorizonId,
706.   riskFeedback: { penalty: number; reasons: string[]; signals: Signal[] },
707.   entryPrice: number,
708.   stopLoss: number,
709.   riskReward: number
710. ): AnalysisDriver[] {
711.   const stopDistancePct = Math.abs(pctChange(stopLoss, entryPrice) ?? 0);
712.   const volatility = metrics.volatility20;
713.   const fragilityImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = riskFeedback.penalty > 0 ? "negative" : "positive";
714.   const fragilityDetail =
715.     riskFeedback.penalty > 0 && riskFeedback.reasons.length
716.       ? `The model removed ${riskFeedback.penalty.toFixed(1)} points for fragility because ${riskFeedback.reasons.join("; ")}. That matters because this engine is designed to reduce exposure to setups that resemble recent stop-loss failures.`
717.       : "No material recent stop-loss pattern forced a penalty, so the setup is not currently being treated as structurally fragile by the learning layer.";
718. 
719.   let payoffImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
720.   let payoffDetail = `The plan risks about ${roundMetric(stopDistancePct).toFixed(2)}% to pursue a ${riskReward.toFixed(2)} risk/reward profile. That gives the setup a balanced payoff structure, but it is not unusually asymmetric.`;
721. 
722.   if (riskReward >= 2) {
723.     payoffImpact = "positive";
724.     payoffDetail = `The plan risks about ${roundMetric(stopDistancePct).toFixed(2)}% to pursue a ${riskReward.toFixed(2)} risk/reward profile. That matters because the model prefers setups where the potential upside remains meaningfully larger than the planned downside.`;
725.   } else if (riskReward < 1.4) {
726.     payoffImpact = "negative";
727.     payoffDetail = `The plan risks about ${roundMetric(stopDistancePct).toFixed(2)}% to pursue only a ${riskReward.toFixed(2)} risk/reward profile. That matters because thinner payoff asymmetry makes it harder for the setup to absorb normal volatility without disappointing.`;
728.   }
729. 
730.   const volatilityImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] =
731.     volatility !== null && (horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && volatility > 3.5
732.       ? "negative"
733.       : "neutral";
734.   const volatilityDetail =
735.     volatility !== null
736.       ? volatilityImpact === "negative"
737.         ? `20-day volatility is ${percentText(volatility)}. That matters because higher short-term volatility increases the chance of a stop-loss being hit before the thesis has time to play out.`
738.         : `20-day volatility is ${percentText(volatility)}. That gives the trade enough movement to work, without automatically classifying it as an unstable setup.`
739.       : "Short-term volatility could not be estimated from the available bars, so the risk layer is leaning more on structure and stop-loss learning.";
740.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 393m 3s</sub>

### 👤 User

and in my dashboard it is showing one stock even after switching from day , swing, longterm. its always showing avoid. if its avoidable why it is in the list

---

<sub>⏱️ 393m 46s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the likely cause: the home shortlist was being padded with lower-ranked **watch** names to keep ten names visible, so an “Avoid” stock could still appear in the same list as recommendations. I’m separating those states so only actual **Consider** names stay in the recommendation list.

---

<sub>⏱️ 393m 50s</sub>

### ✅ `sql`

**Query ready todos**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Query ready todos",
  "query": "SELECT t.id, t.title, t.status\nFROM todos t\nWHERE t.status = 'pending'\nAND NOT EXISTS (\n  SELECT 1\n  FROM todo_deps td\n  JOIN todos dep ON dep.id = td.depends_on\n  WHERE td.todo_id = t.id AND dep.status != 'done'\n)\nORDER BY t.id;"
}
```

</details>

```
1 row(s) returned:

| id | title | status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| build-backtesting | Build backtesting and evaluation | pending |

1. "use client";

3. import Link from "next/link";

6.   MetricTile,
7.   SectionHeader,

10.   formatDate,
11.   formatDateTime,

27. type DashboardProps = {
28.   data: RecommendationDataset;

31. 
32. function planScore(plan: RecommendationPlan) {
33.   return plan.score ?? plan.expectedReturnPct;
34. }
35. 
36. function RecommendationIdeaCard({
37.   stock,
38.   plan,
39.   href
40. }: {
41.   stock: StockAnalysis;
42.   plan: RecommendationPlan;
43.   href: string;
44. }) {
45.   const statusTone = isRecommendedPlan(plan) ? "success" : "neutral";
46.   const statusLabel = isRecommendedPlan(plan) ? "Consider" : "Watch";
47. 
48.   return (
49.     <article className="idea-card">
50.       <div className="idea-card-top">
51.         <div className="idea-card-copy">
52.           <div className="idea-symbol-row">
53.             <span className="symbol">{stock.symbol}</span>
54.             <span className="idea-sector">{stock.sector}</span>
55.           </div>
56.           <h3>{stock.companyName}</h3>
57.           <p>
58.             {stock.industry ? `${stock.industry} · ` : ""}
59.             {stock.marketCapBucket} · {stock.liquidityTier}
60.           </p>
61.         </div>
62.         <div className="idea-card-badges">
63.           {typeof plan.rank === "number" ? <span className="rank-chip">#{plan.rank}</span> : null}
64.           <span className={`result-chip ${statusTone}`}>{statusLabel}</span>
65.           <span className={`badge ${convictionClass(plan.conviction)}`}>{plan.conviction}</span>
66.         </div>
67.       </div>
68. 
69.       <div className="idea-metric-grid">
70.         <MetricTile label="CMP" value={formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)} />
71.         <MetricTile label="Entry" value={formatPrice(plan.entryPrice)} />
72.         <MetricTile label="Target" value={formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)} />
73.         <MetricTile label="Stop-loss" value={formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)} />
74.         <MetricTile label="Expected return" value={`${plan.expectedReturnPct.toFixed(2)}%`} />
75.         <MetricTile label="Risk / reward" value={plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} />
76.       </div>
77. 
78.       <p className="idea-summary">{plan.summary}</p>
79. 
80.       <div className="idea-card-actions">
81.         <span className="idea-card-note">Open the dedicated stock workspace for full analysis.</span>
82.         <Link className="action-link" href={href}>
83.           Open stock page
84.         </Link>
85.       </div>
86.     </article>
87.   );
88. }
89. 
90. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
91.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
92.   const targetVisibleIdeas = 10;
93. 
94.   useEffect(() => {
95.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
96.       setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
97.     }
98.   }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);
99. 
100.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
101.   const localSearchSuggestions = useMemo(
102.     () =>
103.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => ({
104.         symbol: stock.symbol,
105.         companyName: stock.companyName,
106.         sector: stock.sector,
107.         industry: stock.industry
108.       })),
109.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations]
110.   );
111. 
112.   const visibleRecommendations = useMemo(() => {
113.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
114.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
115.     );
116.     const recommended = sorted.filter((stock) => isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon]));
117. 
118.     if (sorted.length <= targetVisibleIdeas) {
119.       return sorted;
120.     }
121. 
122.     if (recommended.length >= targetVisibleIdeas) {
123.       return recommended.slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas);
124.     }
125. 
126.     const seen = new Set(recommended.map((stock) => stock.symbol));
127.     const watchlistFill = sorted.filter((stock) => !seen.has(stock.symbol));
128. 
129.     return [...recommended, ...watchlistFill].slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas);
130.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations, targetVisibleIdeas]);
131. 
132.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo(
133.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
134.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
135.   );
136.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
137.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
138.     [dailyPerformance]
139.   );
140. 
141.   const spotlightStock = visibleRecommendations[0];
142.   const spotlightPlan = spotlightStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
143.   const searchedStock = searchedAnalysis?.stock;
144.   const searchedHorizonPlans = searchedStock
145.     ? data.profiles.map((profile) => ({
146.         horizon: profile.id,
147.         label: profile.label,
148.         plan: searchedStock.profiles[profile.id]
149.       }))
150.     : [];
151. 
152.   const recommendedCount = data.currentBatch.recommendations.filter((stock) =>
153.     isRecommendedPlan(stock.profiles[activeHorizon])
154.   ).length;
155.   const latestCompletedDate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.batchDate
156.     ? formatDate(performanceSummary.latestCompleted.batchDate)
157.     : null;
158.   const sourceProvider = data.dataSource?.provider ?? "Live recommendation loader";
159. 
160.   const overviewCards = [
161.     {
162.       label: "Universe",
163.       value: compactUniverseLabel(data.universe),
164.       footnote: universeQualifier(data.universe)
165.     },
166.     {
167.       label: "Active horizon",
168.       value: activeProfile?.label ?? "n/a",
169.       footnote: activeProfile?.window ?? ""
170.     },
171.     {
172.       label: "Shortlist names",
173.       value: String(visibleRecommendations.length),
174.       footnote: `${recommendedCount} model-qualified ideas`
175.     },
176.     {
177.       label: "Analyzed stocks",
178.       value: String(data.currentBatch.recommendations.length),
179.       footnote: "Current live coverage"
180.     },
181.     {
182.       label: "Latest completed success rate",
183.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? null),
184.       footnote: latestCompletedDate ?? "No closed batch yet"
185.     },
186.     {
187.       label: "Average closed return",
188.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct),
189.       footnote: "Across completed batches"
190.     }
191.   ];
192. 
193.   return (
194.     <main className="shell">
195.       <section className="card masthead-card tone-brand">
196.         <div className="masthead-grid">
197.           <div className="masthead-copy">
198.             <span className="section-eyebrow">Institutional-style NSE dashboard</span>
199.             <h1>Indian equities recommendation console</h1>
200.             <p>
201.               The home dashboard now stays stable: use it to scan the current shortlist, inspect
202.               batch health, and jump into dedicated stock or archive workspaces when you need deep
203.               analysis.
204.             </p>
205.           </div>
206. 
207.           <div className="masthead-summary">
208.             <MetricTile label="Latest batch" value={formatDateTime(data.currentBatch.generatedAt)} />
209.             <MetricTile label="Market scope" value={`${data.exchange} · ${compactUniverseLabel(data.universe)}`} />
210.             <MetricTile
211.               label="Source mode"
212.               value={`${data.dataSource?.mode.toUpperCase() ?? "LIVE"} · ${sourceProvider}`}
213.               footnote={data.currentBatch.batchDate}
214.             />
215.           </div>
216.         </div>
217.       </section>
218. 
219.       <section className="section-cluster">
220.         <SectionHeader
221.           eyebrow="Screening"
222.           title="Search and market snapshot"
223.           subtitle="Use autocomplete to analyze any stock, then jump into the separate detail workspace only when you want a full breakdown."
224.           tone="search"
225.         />
226. 
227.         <div className="top-panels">
228.           <section className="card search-card tone-search">
229.             <div className="panel-title">
230.               <span className="panel-kicker">Stock lookup</span>
231.               <h3>Search and analyze any NSE stock</h3>
232.               <p>Type a symbol or company name, select a suggestion, and review the model verdict.</p>
233.             </div>
234. 
235.             <StockSearchBox
236.               initialQuery={searchedAnalysis?.query ?? ""}
237.               localSuggestions={localSearchSuggestions}
238.             />
239.           </section>
240. 
241.           <section className="card status-card tone-overview">
242.             <div className="panel-title">
243.               <span className="panel-kicker">Batch snapshot</span>
244.               <h3>Live market context</h3>
245.               <p>Quick context for the currently rendered batch and active recommendation profile.</p>
246.             </div>
247. 
248.             <div className="mini-metric-grid">
249.               <MetricTile label="Exchange" value={data.exchange} />
250.               <MetricTile label="Active profile" value={activeProfile?.label ?? "n/a"} />
251.               <MetricTile label="Window" value={activeProfile?.window ?? "n/a"} />
252.               <MetricTile
253.                 label="Shortlist size"
254.                 value={String(visibleRecommendations.length)}
255.                 footnote={`${recommendedCount} recommended`}
256.               />
257.             </div>
258.           </section>
259.         </div>
260.       </section>

437.                 <h3>Review previous recommendation batches</h3>
438.                 <p>
439.                   The batch archive and scorecards now live on their own page so the home dashboard stays focused on current-market work.
440.                 </p>
441.               </div>
442. 
443.               <div className="action-grid">
444.                 <MetricTile
445.                   label="Latest completed success rate"
446.                   value={formatPercent(performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? null)}
447.                   footnote={latestCompletedDate ?? "No closed batch yet"}
448.                 />
449.                 <MetricTile
450.                   label="Average daily success"
451.                   value={formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate)}
452.                   footnote="Across completed batches"
453.                 />
454.               </div>
455. 
456.               <div className="panel-actions">
457.                 <Link className="action-link" href="/history">
458.                   Open archive page
459.                 </Link>
460.               </div>
461.             </section>
462.           </aside>
463.         </section>
464.       </section>
465.     </main>
466.   );
467. }
468.
```

</details>

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\recommendation-data.ts\n@@\n-function buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(\n-  entry: MarketSeries,\n-  earningsScore: number,\n-  analystScore: number\n-): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+function buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries): AnalysisDriver[] {\n   if (!entry.sentiment) {\n     return [\n       {\n         area: \"sentiment\",\n         impact: \"neutral\",\n         title: \"Headline coverage\",\n         detail:\n-          \"No strong recent news, earnings, or analyst headline cluster was found, so sentiment is not materially changing the recommendation in either direction.\"\n+          \"No strong recent news cluster was found, so the broad sentiment layer is not materially changing the recommendation in either direction.\"\n       }\n     ];\n   }\n \n   const sentiment = entry.sentiment;\n   const headlineBalance = sentiment.positiveCount - sentiment.negativeCount;\n@@\n   } else if (headlineBalance < 0 || sentiment.score < 0) {\n     toneImpact = \"negative\";\n     toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because a negative narrative can quickly cap upside even when the chart looks constructive.`;\n   }\n \n-  const overlayCount = sentiment.earningsMentionCount + sentiment.analystMentionCount;\n-  const overlayScore = Math.max(earningsScore, analystScore);\n-  let catalystImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n-  let catalystDetail =\n-    \"Recent coverage does not show a strong earnings or analyst revision cluster, so narrative momentum is present but not being driven by a clear catalyst.\";\n+  const coverageCount = sentiment.positiveCount + sentiment.neutralCount + sentiment.negativeCount;\n+  let coverageImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let coverageDetail =\n+    \"Headline coverage is light, so the narrative layer is acting as background context rather than as a strong driver of the move.\";\n \n-  if (overlayCount > 0 && overlayScore >= 60) {\n-    catalystImpact = \"positive\";\n-    catalystDetail = `The research feed found ${sentiment.earningsMentionCount} earnings-related and ${sentiment.analystMentionCount} analyst-related headlines. That matters because revisions and earnings follow-through often create the catalyst that keeps a breakout moving after the initial price move.`;\n-  } else if (overlayCount > 0 && overlayScore <= 45) {\n-    catalystImpact = \"negative\";\n-    catalystDetail = `The research feed found ${sentiment.earningsMentionCount} earnings-related and ${sentiment.analystMentionCount} analyst-related headlines, but their tone is not supportive. That matters because earnings or analyst disappointment can override otherwise decent technical structure.`;\n+  if (coverageCount >= 5 && headlineBalance > 1) {\n+    coverageImpact = \"positive\";\n+    coverageDetail = `The feed captured ${coverageCount} recent headlines with a clearly positive skew. That matters because heavier supportive coverage tends to keep the stock in focus and can reinforce buying interest after an initial move.`;\n+  } else if (coverageCount >= 5 && headlineBalance < -1) {\n+    coverageImpact = \"negative\";\n+    coverageDetail = `The feed captured ${coverageCount} recent headlines with a negative skew. That matters because heavier critical coverage can pressure sentiment and make traders quicker to sell strength.`;\n   }\n \n   return [\n     { area: \"sentiment\", impact: toneImpact, title: \"Narrative tone\", detail: toneDetail },\n-    { area: \"sentiment\", impact: catalystImpact, title: \"Earnings and analyst catalyst\", detail: catalystDetail }\n+    { area: \"sentiment\", impact: coverageImpact, title: \"Headline coverage\", detail: coverageDetail }\n   ];\n }\n+\n+function categoryToneCounts(\n+  sentiment: MarketSeries[\"sentiment\"],\n+  category: \"earnings\" | \"analyst\"\n+) {\n+  const headlines = sentiment?.headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === category) ?? [];\n+\n+  return {\n+    total: headlines.length,\n+    positive: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === \"positive\").length,\n+    negative: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === \"negative\").length,\n+    neutral: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === \"neutral\").length\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function buildEarningsAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries, earningsScore: number): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+  const counts = categoryToneCounts(entry.sentiment ?? null, \"earnings\");\n+\n+  if (!entry.sentiment || counts.total === 0) {\n+    return [\n+      {\n+        area: \"earnings\",\n+        impact: \"neutral\",\n+        title: \"Earnings coverage\",\n+        detail:\n+          \"No recent earnings-related headline cluster was found, so the earnings lens is not materially lifting or dragging the stock today.\"\n+      }\n+    ];\n+  }\n+\n+  let toneImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let toneDetail =\n+    \"Earnings-related headlines are mixed, so the model is treating earnings as context rather than as a decisive catalyst right now.\";\n+\n+  if (earningsScore >= 60 || counts.positive > counts.negative) {\n+    toneImpact = \"positive\";\n+    toneDetail = `The earnings lens scored ${signalScoreText(earningsScore)} because recent earnings-related coverage is supportive (${counts.positive} positive vs ${counts.negative} negative headlines). That matters because constructive results or guidance often keep buyers engaged after the initial move.`;\n+  } else if (earningsScore <= 45 || counts.negative > counts.positive) {\n+    toneImpact = \"negative\";\n+    toneDetail = `The earnings lens scored ${signalScoreText(earningsScore)} because recent earnings-related coverage is weak (${counts.positive} positive vs ${counts.negative} negative headlines). That matters because disappointing results or cautious guidance can quickly limit upside or trigger de-rating.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  let visibilityImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} earnings-related headlines. That is enough to monitor, but not enough on its own to dominate the stock call.`;\n+\n+  if (counts.total >= 3 && toneImpact === \"positive\") {\n+    visibilityImpact = \"positive\";\n+    visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} recent earnings-related headlines. That matters because repeated supportive earnings coverage usually means the market is still digesting the results as a bullish catalyst.`;\n+  } else if (counts.total >= 3 && toneImpact === \"negative\") {\n+    visibilityImpact = \"negative\";\n+    visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} recent earnings-related headlines. That matters because repeated negative earnings coverage can keep pressure on the stock even if the chart tries to stabilize.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  return [\n+    { area: \"earnings\", impact: toneImpact, title: \"Earnings tone\", detail: toneDetail },\n+    { area: \"earnings\", impact: visibilityImpact, title: \"Earnings visibility\", detail: visibilityDetail }\n+  ];\n+}\n+\n+function buildAnalystAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries, analystScore: number): AnalysisDriver[] {\n+  const counts = categoryToneCounts(entry.sentiment ?? null, \"analyst\");\n+\n+  if (!entry.sentiment || counts.total === 0) {\n+    return [\n+      {\n+        area: \"analyst\",\n+        impact: \"neutral\",\n+        title: \"Analyst coverage\",\n+        detail:\n+          \"No recent analyst or revision-style headline cluster was found, so the analyst lens is not materially changing the stock score today.\"\n+      }\n+    ];\n+  }\n+\n+  let toneImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let toneDetail =\n+    \"Analyst coverage is mixed, so the model is not treating broker tone as a decisive source of conviction right now.\";\n+\n+  if (analystScore >= 60 || counts.positive > counts.negative) {\n+    toneImpact = \"positive\";\n+    toneDetail = `The analyst lens scored ${signalScoreText(analystScore)} because revision-style coverage is supportive (${counts.positive} positive vs ${counts.negative} negative headlines). That matters because upgrades and target-price improvements can attract incremental institutional attention.`;\n+  } else if (analystScore <= 45 || counts.negative > counts.positive) {\n+    toneImpact = \"negative\";\n+    toneDetail = `The analyst lens scored ${signalScoreText(analystScore)} because revision-style coverage is weak (${counts.positive} positive vs ${counts.negative} negative headlines). That matters because downgrades or cautious broker tone often cap rallies and reduce conviction.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  let visibilityImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} analyst-related headlines. That gives some context, but it is not a dominant driver by itself.`;\n+\n+  if (counts.total >= 2 && toneImpact === \"positive\") {\n+    visibilityImpact = \"positive\";\n+    visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} analyst-related headlines with supportive tone. That matters because repeated positive broker coverage can reinforce the market narrative behind the stock.`;\n+  } else if (counts.total >= 2 && toneImpact === \"negative\") {\n+    visibilityImpact = \"negative\";\n+    visibilityDetail = `The model found ${counts.total} analyst-related headlines with weak tone. That matters because repeated cautious broker commentary can make the market less willing to chase the stock higher.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  return [\n+    { area: \"analyst\", impact: toneImpact, title: \"Analyst tone\", detail: toneDetail },\n+    { area: \"analyst\", impact: visibilityImpact, title: \"Analyst visibility\", detail: visibilityDetail }\n+  ];\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 398m 36s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 8-24)

<details>
<summary>17 lines</summary>

```
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - a score-to-reasoning navigator in the stock workspace so technical, fundamental, sentiment, and risk scores jump to anchored explanation panels,
18.   - hover help on the analysis panels so each card explains what it is, why it is needed, and how it can impact the stock,
19.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
20.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
21.   - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
22.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
23.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
24. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 399m 10s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Fixed — **“Avoid” stocks no longer appear in the main recommendation list**.

The dashboard now keeps the main list to **only stocks that cleared the active horizon threshold**. If a stock is still scanned but not recommended, it goes into a separate **“Watchlist candidates”** section with explicit below-threshold labeling, so it’s clear why it’s there.

I also completed the score-explanation gap: **Earnings lens** and **Analyst lens** now have their own reasoning panels, and clicking their score tiles jumps to an explanation of **why that score was assigned, what signals drove it, and how it can affect the stock**. The same click-through behavior now applies to the main analysis scores as well.

### 👤 User

You are reviewing a stock analysis and prediction system.

Check whether the system includes ALL of the following features. If anything is missing, incomplete, or weak, clearly identify the gaps and suggest what needs to be added or improved.

FEATURE CHECKLIST:

1. Fundamental Analysis:

* Balance sheet, P&L, cash flow analysis
* Key ratios (PE, PB, ROE, ROCE, Debt/Equity)
* Free cash flow and earnings growth
* Sector-wise comparison
* Fundamental scoring

2. Technical Analysis:

* SMA (50 & 200)
* RSI
* MACD
* Bollinger Bands
* Volume analysis
* Candlestick pattern detection
* Trend classification (bullish/bearish)

3. News Analysis:

* Data from NSE, MoneyControl, Google News
* Stock-wise tagging
* Sector-wise grouping
* News relevance ranking

4. Sentiment Analysis:

* Positive/Negative/Neutral classification
* Stock-level sentiment score
* Sector sentiment
* Market-wide sentiment

5. AI / LLM Capabilities:

* Growth vs shrink prediction
* Expected price estimation
* Clear reasoning (fundamental + technical + news based)
* Ability to switch between OpenAI and local LLM

6. Market Coverage:

* Covers all NIFTY 500 (or ~750 stocks)
* Daily analysis

7. Predictions:

* List of gainers and losers
* Confidence score
* Explanation for each prediction

8. Portfolio Features:

* Portfolio optimization
* Suggested allocation
* Risk-return balancing

9. Compliance:

* Sector exposure limits
* Stock weight limits
* Rule validation

10. Risk Analysis:

* Volatility
* Drawdown
* Risk scoring

11. Dashboard:

* View all stocks
* Filters (sector, sentiment, growth)
* Individual stock view

12. Automation:

* Scheduled data fetching
* Fully automated pipeline

13. Data Crawling:

* Multi-source scraping
* Data cleaning and structuring

14. Logging & Monitoring:

* API success/failure logs
* Error tracking
* System health monitoring

15. API Architecture:

* Microservices
* Independent APIs
* Swagger support

INSTRUCTIONS:

* First, list what is already present.
* Then list missing or weak areas.
* Then suggest concrete improvements or additional features.
* Be practical and system-design focused.

}

<sub>⏱️ 424m 59s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\copilot\README.md.**

C:\copilot\README.md (lines 1-200)

<details>
<summary>87 lines</summary>

````
1. # Indian equities recommendation platform
2. 
3. This repository now includes a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. The dashboard analyzes a live NSE watchlist using recent market history, produces ranked multi-horizon recommendations, and explains each idea with an entry, target, and stop-loss instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.
4. 
5. ## Chosen product scope
6. 
7. - **User:** analyst or serious retail investor
8. - **Market:** liquid NSE equities, starting from an expanded large-cap live watchlist
9. - **Delivery:** web dashboard
10. - **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution
11. - **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan
12. - **MVP horizons:** single-day, swing, position, and long-term
13. - **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists
14. 
15. ## Chosen stack
16. 
17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Current repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## Live recommendation flow
37. 
38. - The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for a liquid NSE watchlist plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.
39. - Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, volatility, **chart-structure analysis** (breakouts, candle quality, range behavior, and moving-average slope), lightweight fundamentals, and headline-based sentiment / earnings / analyst signals.
40. - The live model now also applies **stop-loss learning**: repeated recent stop-loss failures reduce future model aggressiveness for similar setups.
41. - The app also backtests prior daily batches from the same historical price series so the dashboard can maintain horizon-specific success rates.
42. - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.
43. - If persistent disk writes are not available on the host, the app falls back to **in-memory caching** for the running instance.
44. - If live requests fail later, the app falls back to the cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.
45. 
46. ## MVP user experience
47. 
48. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch with a broader live shortlist.
49. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
50. 3. The detail view explains:
51.     - key factors behind the recommendation
52.     - recommended entry price
53.     - target price
54.     - stop-loss
55.     - risk-reward profile
56.     - horizon-specific analysis for single-day, swing, position, and long-term modes
57. 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, explains why each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.
58. 5. The search panel offers **autocomplete suggestions while you type**, then analyzes the selected NSE stock and says whether it should be **considered or avoided** for each supported horizon.
59. 6. Historical stop-loss outcomes now include a clearer explanation of **why the stop-loss was hit**, and the live model uses recent stop-loss history to reduce future aggressiveness for fragile setups.
60. 
61. ## Windows quick start
62. 
63. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
64. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
65. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
66. - The run card in the app shows whether the current batch is coming from **LIVE**, **CACHED**, or **SAMPLE** data.
67. 
68. ## Analytics test command
69. 
70. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
71. 
72. ## Deployment and free hosting
73. 
74. ### Production-readiness changes in this repo
75. 
76. - The Next.js app now exposes `GET /api/healthz` for simple uptime checks.
77. - The live dataset loader supports **in-memory runtime caching**, which is friendlier to serverless or ephemeral free hosts.
78. - `render.yaml` is included so you can deploy both the web app and the FastAPI service on Render with minimal setup.
79. 
80. ### Recommended free hosting options
81. 
82. 1. **Vercel** for the Next.js app only. This is the easiest choice for the dashboard because it handles Next.js SSR well. In Vercel, set the project root to `apps/web`.
83. 2. **Render** if you want both the Next.js app and the FastAPI API hosted together for free. The included `render.yaml` is aimed at this option. Render free web services can sleep when idle, so expect cold starts.
84. 3. **Cloudflare Pages** if you want a very low-cost global edge deployment for the web app and are comfortable with more setup than Vercel.
85. 
86. For this repository as it exists today, **Vercel** is the simplest free host for the dashboard, while **Render** is the simplest free option if you want to run both services from one repo.
87.
````

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md.**

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>116 lines</summary>

```
1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - a score-to-reasoning navigator in the stock workspace so technical, fundamental, sentiment, and risk scores jump to anchored explanation panels,
18.   - dedicated earnings and analyst reasoning panels, with clickable score tiles that explain why those scores moved and how they can impact the stock,
19.   - hover help on the analysis panels so each card explains what it is, why it is needed, and how it can impact the stock,
20.   - a threshold-separated home workspace where below-threshold names are shown only in a clearly labeled watchlist section instead of inside the active recommendation list,
21.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
22.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
23.   - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
24.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
25.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
26. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
27. 
28. ## Proposed approach
29. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
30. 
31. 1. Product definition
32.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
33.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
34.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
35.    - Chosen MVP scope:
36.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
37.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
38.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
39.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
40.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
41.      - Supported horizons in the MVP: swing, position, and long-term; intraday deferred to a later high-frequency phase.
42. 
43. 2. Data foundation
44.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
45.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
46.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
47.    - Current implementation note: the MVP now ingests live Yahoo Finance chart data plus a cached generated snapshot fallback.
48. 
49. 3. Research feature layer
50.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
51.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.
52.    - Current implementation note: the live model now combines technical factors with lightweight fundamentals and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone heuristics.
53. 
54. 4. Modeling and recommendation logic
55.    - Start with a strong baseline ranking model before trying more complex ML.
56.    - Score stocks on expected return, downside risk, and confidence.
57.    - Convert scores into recommendations with portfolio and exposure constraints.
58.    - Add a trade-plan layer that proposes entry, target, and stop-loss levels using transparent rules such as volatility bands, support and resistance, liquidity filters, and minimum risk-reward thresholds.
59.    - Support separate strategy profiles for swing, position, and long-term recommendations, with room to add intraday once a higher-frequency data stack exists.
60.    - Current implementation note: the first live model uses momentum, moving-average trend, relative strength versus Nifty 50, RSI, volume, and volatility to produce ranked recommendations.
61. 
62. 5. Evaluation and controls
63.    - Backtest against benchmark indexes and simpler factor strategies.
64.    - Track hit rate, Sharpe-like measures, drawdown, turnover, sector concentration, and feature drift.
65.    - Add guardrails so the system can reject weak-signal situations instead of always producing a confident pick.
66. 
67. 6. Explainability and delivery
68.    - Show the top drivers behind every recommendation.
69.    - Expose results through an API and a simple dashboard with audit history.
70.    - Persist each trading-day recommendation batch so users can compare today's output with prior runs.
71.    - Design a stock detail view that explains the recommendation with factor breakdowns, supporting charts, recent news context, and the generated entry, target, and stop-loss plan.
72.    - Current implementation note: the dashboard includes a searchable stock analyzer and a recommendation-history menu with achieved/not-achieved outcome inspection.
73. 
74. 7. UI template recommendation
75.    - Prefer a web-based analyst dashboard rather than a generic consumer app layout.
76.    - Chosen starting point: Next.js with TypeScript and shadcn/ui-style dashboard patterns for a clean analyst-oriented UI.
77.    - Layout suggestion:
78.      - Left navigation for dashboard, recommendations, watchlists, backtests, and settings.
79.      - Main table for today's ranked stocks with sortable signals and confidence.
80.      - Right-side drawer or dedicated detail page for a clicked stock.
81.    - Stock detail page should include:
82.      - Recommendation summary card.
83.      - Horizon selector so the same stock can show swing, position, and long-term variants when available.
84.      - Entry, target, and stop-loss card with risk-reward ratio.
85.      - Tabs for technical analysis, fundamentals, sentiment or news, and historical recommendation performance.
86.      - Price chart with annotated levels and model signals.
87. 
88. ## Draft todo list
89. - `define-scope`: confirm the product shape, target user, stock universe, horizon, and whether the MVP is research-only or execution-oriented.
90. - `choose-stack`: pick the app architecture, storage, data orchestration, model serving pattern, and deployment target.
91. - `design-data-model`: define source adapters, schemas, entity identifiers, snapshot strategy, and feature contracts.
92. - `build-ingestion`: implement market and fundamentals ingestion with validation and scheduling.
93. - `build-features`: create the first reusable factor and regime features.
94. - `train-baseline-model`: implement baseline ranking and scoring logic with clear offline metrics.
95. - `build-trade-plan-engine`: generate transparent entry, exit, and stop-loss levels from model output and market structure rules.
96. - `build-backtesting`: add benchmark comparison, risk analytics, and experiment reporting.
97. - `build-recommendation-api`: expose ranked recommendations, explanations, and audit metadata.
98. - `build-operator-ui`: create a lightweight research dashboard with a recommendation table and stock detail view showing analysis, charts, entry, target, and stop-loss.
99. - `add-monitoring`: track data freshness, model drift, recommendation quality, and retraining triggers.
100. 
101. ## Notes and considerations
102. - "Like BlackRock" should be interpreted as institutional rigor, risk management, and explainability, not as copying any proprietary strategy.
103. - The working assumption is a research and recommendation system for Indian equities that analyzes fresh market data each trading day and emits a ranked recommendation list with rationale, confidence, and risk flags.
104. - Entry, exit, and stop-loss levels should come from explicit and auditable logic, not a hidden black-box output, so users can see why each trade plan was proposed.
105. - Personalized financial advice, brokerage execution, and regulatory workflows should be deferred until the core research engine proves value.
106. - A simpler baseline often outperforms an overfit deep model in early versions; prioritize data quality and evaluation discipline first.
107. - Initial market assumption: start with liquid NSE-listed equities or a benchmark-derived universe such as Nifty 500 to reduce data and liquidity edge cases.
108. - Revised scope assumption: the platform should be designed for multiple horizons, but daily-data MVP coverage should focus on swing, position, and long-term profiles first.
109. - Intraday recommendations require separate high-frequency data ingestion, tighter execution assumptions, and different backtesting logic, so they should be treated as a later phase rather than bundled into the first daily-data release.
110. - Chosen foundation stack:
111.   - Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript with a clean dashboard UI.
112.   - API and analytics services: FastAPI + Pydantic in Python.
113.   - Operational storage: PostgreSQL for users, recommendation batches, and audit history.
114.   - Research storage: Parquet or DuckDB for snapshots, factor data, and backtesting workloads.
115.   - Scheduling: market-calendar-aware daily batch jobs, starting with end-of-day runs.
116.
```

</details>

```
1. import json
2. from pathlib import Path
3. from typing import Any
4. 
5. from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
6. 
7. from .analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
8. from .schemas import (
9.     HorizonId,
10.     RecommendationDataset,
11.     RecommendationSummary,
12.     StockAnalysis,
13.     StockDetailResponse,
14. )
15. 
16. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
17. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
18. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
19. 
20. app = FastAPI(
21.     title="Recommendation API",
22.     version="0.1.0",
23.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
24. )
25. 
26. 
27. def _clamp(value: float, minimum: float, maximum: float) -> float:
28.     return min(max(value, minimum), maximum)
29. 
30. 
31. def _pct_change(current: float, previous: float) -> float:
32.     if previous == 0:
33.         return 0.0
34. 
35.     return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100
36. 
37. 
38. def _derive_single_day_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
39.     entry_price = round(float(plan["entryPrice"]), 2)
40.     target_pct = _clamp(
41.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["targetPrice"]), entry_price)) * 0.38,
42.         1.2,
43.         3.4,
44.     )
45.     stop_pct = _clamp(
46.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["stopLoss"]), entry_price)) * 0.45,
47.         0.7,
48.         1.9,
49.     )
50.     target_price = round(entry_price * (1 + target_pct / 100), 2)
51.     stop_loss = round(max(entry_price * (1 - stop_pct / 100), entry_price * 0.85), 2)
52. 
53.     return {
54.         **plan,
55.         "score": plan.get("score", 55),
56.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
57.         "entryPrice": entry_price,
58.         "targetPrice": target_price,
59.         "stopLoss": stop_loss,
60.         "expectedReturnPct": round(_pct_change(target_price, entry_price), 2),
61.         "riskReward": round((target_price - entry_price) / max(entry_price - stop_loss, 0.01), 2),
62.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. {plan['summary']}",
63.         "drivers": [
64.             "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
65.             *plan.get("drivers", []),
66.         ][:4],
67.         "analysisDrivers": [
68.             {
69.                 "area": "risk",
70.                 "impact": "neutral",
71.                 "title": "Compatibility mode",
72.                 "detail": "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the current single-day reasoning model.",
73.             },
74.             *plan.get("analysisDrivers", []),
75.         ][:6],
76.         "technicalSignals": [
77.             {"name": "Compatibility mode", "value": "Derived from swing profile"},
78.             *plan.get("technicalSignals", []),
79.         ][:6],
80.         "riskSignals": plan.get("riskSignals")
81.         or [{"name": "Learning status", "value": "Awaiting refreshed live batch"}],
82.     }
83. 
84. 
85. def _derive_single_day_history(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
86.     outcome = plan["outcome"]
87. 
88.     return {
89.         **plan,
90.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
91.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. {plan['summary']}",
92.         "outcome": {
93.             **outcome,
94.             "notes": f"This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. {outcome['notes']}",
95.         },
96.     }
97. 
98. 
99. def _normalize_payload(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
100.     profile_lookup = {profile["id"]: profile for profile in payload.get("profiles", [])}
101.     payload["profiles"] = [
102.         profile_lookup.get("single_day", {"id": "single_day", "label": "Single-day", "window": "Next trading day"}),
103.         profile_lookup.get("swing", {"id": "swing", "label": "Swing", "window": "5-20 trading days"}),
104.         profile_lookup.get("position", {"id": "position", "label": "Position", "window": "20-60 trading days"}),
105.         profile_lookup.get("long_term", {"id": "long_term", "label": "Long-term", "window": "3-12 months"}),
106.     ]
107. 
108.     for recommendation in payload.get("currentBatch", {}).get("recommendations", []):
109.         profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
110.         if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
111.             profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_plan(profiles["swing"])
112. 
113.     for batch in payload.get("history", []):
114.         for recommendation in batch.get("recommendations", []):
115.             profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
116.             if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
117.                 profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_history(profiles["swing"])
118. 
119.     return payload
120. 
121. 
122. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
123.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
124.     payload = _normalize_payload(json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
125.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
126. 
127. 
128. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
129.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
130. 
131.     return RecommendationSummary(
132.         symbol=stock.symbol,
133.         companyName=stock.companyName,
134.         sector=stock.sector,
135.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
136.         horizon=horizon,
137.         score=plan.score,
138.         rank=plan.rank,
139.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
140.         conviction=plan.conviction,
141.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
142.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
143.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
144.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
145.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
146.         summary=plan.summary,
147.     )
148. 
149. 
150. @app.get("/healthz")
151. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
152.     return {"status": "ok"}
153. 
154. 
155. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
156. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
157.     dataset = load_dataset()
158.     ordered_recommendations = sorted(
159.         dataset.currentBatch.recommendations,
160.         key=lambda stock: (
161.             stock.profiles[horizon].score
162.             if stock.profiles[horizon].score is not None
163.             else stock.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct
164.         ),
165.         reverse=True,
166.     )
167.     recommended_rows = [
168.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
169.         for stock in ordered_recommendations
170.         if stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
171.     ]
172.     rows = recommended_rows or [
173.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
174.         for stock in ordered_recommendations[:5]
175.     ]
176.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
177. 
178.     return {
179.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
180.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
181.         "market": dataset.market,
182.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
183.         "universe": dataset.universe,
184.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
185.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
186.         "recommendations": rows,
187.     }
188. 
189. 
190. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
191. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
192.     dataset = load_dataset()
193.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
194. 
195.     return {
196.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
197.         "performance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
198.     }
199. 
200. 
201. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
202. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
203.     dataset = load_dataset()
204.     stock = next(
205.         (
206.             row
207.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
208.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
209.         ),
210.         None,
211.     )
212. 
213.     if stock is None:
214.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
215. 
216.     return StockDetailResponse(
217.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
218.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
219.         market=dataset.market,
220.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
221.         universe=dataset.universe,
222.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
223.         stock=stock,
224.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
225.     )
226.
```

</details>

```
1. from typing import Literal
2. 
3. from pydantic import BaseModel
4. 
5. HorizonId = Literal["single_day", "swing", "position", "long_term"]
6. Conviction = Literal["High", "Medium", "Low"]
7. OutcomeResult = Literal["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit", "open"]
8. AnalysisArea = Literal["technical", "fundamental", "sentiment", "earnings", "analyst", "risk"]
9. AnalysisImpact = Literal["positive", "negative", "neutral"]
10. 
11. 
12. class Signal(BaseModel):
13.     name: str
14.     value: str
15. 
16. 
17. class AnalysisDriver(BaseModel):
18.     area: AnalysisArea
19.     impact: AnalysisImpact
20.     title: str
21.     detail: str
22. 
23. 
24. class RecommendationOutcome(BaseModel):
25.     result: OutcomeResult
26.     evaluatedOn: str
27.     holdingDays: int
28.     returnPct: float
29.     notes: str
30. 
31. 
32. class RecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
33.     score: float | None = None
34.     rank: int | None = None
35.     isRecommended: bool = True
36.     conviction: Conviction
37.     entryPrice: float
38.     targetPrice: float
39.     stopLoss: float
40.     expectedReturnPct: float
41.     riskReward: float
42.     summary: str
43.     drivers: list[str]
44.     analysisDrivers: list[AnalysisDriver] | None = None
45.     technicalSignals: list[Signal]
46.     fundamentalSignals: list[Signal]
47.     sentimentSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
48.     earningsSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
49.     analystSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
50.     riskSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
51.     newsContext: list[str]
52. 
53. 
54. class HistoricalRecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
55.     score: float | None = None
56.     rank: int | None = None
57.     isRecommended: bool = True
58.     conviction: Conviction
59.     entryPrice: float
60.     targetPrice: float
61.     stopLoss: float
62.     summary: str
63.     outcome: RecommendationOutcome
64. 
65. 
66. class StockAnalysis(BaseModel):
67.     symbol: str
68.     companyName: str
69.     sector: str
70.     marketCapBucket: str
71.     liquidityTier: str
72.     currentMarketPrice: float
73.     latestSessionChangePct: float | None = None
74.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, RecommendationPlan]
75. 
76. 
77. class HistoricalStockRecommendation(BaseModel):
78.     symbol: str
79.     companyName: str
80.     sector: str
81.     profiles: dict[HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan]
82. 
83. 
84. class HorizonProfile(BaseModel):

86.     label: str
87.     window: str
88. 
89. 
90. class RecommendationBatch(BaseModel):
91.     batchDate: str
92.     generatedAt: str
93.     recommendations: list[StockAnalysis]
94. 
95. 
96. class HistoricalBatch(BaseModel):
97.     batchDate: str
98.     publishedAt: str
99.     recommendations: list[HistoricalStockRecommendation]
100. 
101. 
102. class RecommendationDataset(BaseModel):

104.     exchange: str
105.     universe: str
106.     profiles: list[HorizonProfile]
107.     currentBatch: RecommendationBatch
108.     history: list[HistoricalBatch]
109. 
110. 
111. class RecommendationSummary(BaseModel):

115.     currentMarketPrice: float
116.     horizon: HorizonId

124.     expectedReturnPct: float

134.     open: int

119. function decodeHtmlEntities(value: string) {
120.   return value
121.     .replace(/&amp;/g, "&")
122.     .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
123.     .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
124.     .replace(/&lt;/g, "<")
125.     .replace(/&gt;/g, ">");

127. 
128. function normalizeSearchSymbol(query: string) {
129.   return query.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
130. }
131. 
132. function parseIndianNumber(value: string | null) {
133.   if (!value) {
134.     return null;
135.   }
136. 
137.   const normalized = value.replace(/,/g, "").trim();
138.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(normalized);
139. 
140.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
141. }
142.

158. function titleTone(title: string) {
159.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
160.   const positiveHits = POSITIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
161.   const negativeHits = NEGATIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
162. 
163.   if (positiveHits > negativeHits) {
164.     return "positive" as const;
165.   }
166. 
167.   if (negativeHits > positiveHits) {
168.     return "negative" as const;
169.   }
170. 
171.   return "neutral" as const;
172. }
173. 
174. function headlineCategory(title: string) {
175.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
176. 
177.   if (ANALYST_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
178.     return "analyst" as const;
179.   }
180. 
181.   if (EARNINGS_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
182.     return "earnings" as const;
183.   }
184. 
185.   return "news" as const;
186. }
187. 
188. function sentimentScore(headlines: NewsHeadline[]) {
189.   if (!headlines.length) {
190.     return 50;
191.   }
192. 
193.   const toneTotal = headlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
194.     if (headline.tone === "positive") {
195.       return total + 1;
196.     }
197. 
198.     if (headline.tone === "negative") {
199.       return total - 1;
200.     }
201. 
202.     return total;
203.   }, 0);
204. 
205.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + (toneTotal / headlines.length) * 35));
206. }
207. 
208. function overallSentiment(score: number) {
209.   if (score >= 58) {

215.   }

218. }
