293. 
294.   if (!normalized) {
295.     return [];
296.   }
297. 
298.   const payload = await fetchJson<YahooSearchResponse>(
299.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}&quotesCount=${Math.max(limit * 2, 8)}&newsCount=0&enableFuzzyQuery=true`
300.   );
301.   const matches = (payload.quotes ?? [])
302.     .filter(isNseEquityQuote)
303.     .map(toCompanyLookup)
304.     .sort((left, right) => {
305.       const leftRank = searchRank(left, normalized);
306.       const rightRank = searchRank(right, normalized);
307. 
308.       if (leftRank !== rightRank) {
309.         return leftRank - rightRank;
310.       }
311. 
312.       return left.symbol.localeCompare(right.symbol);
313.     });
314.   const deduped: CompanyLookup[] = [];
315.   const seen = new Set<string>();
316. 
317.   for (const match of matches) {
318.     if (seen.has(match.symbol)) {
319.       continue;
320.     }
321. 
322.     seen.add(match.symbol);
323.     deduped.push(match);
324. 
325.     if (deduped.length >= limit) {
326.       break;
327.     }
328.   }
329. 
330.   return deduped;
331. }
332. 
333. export async function lookupNseSuggestions(
334.   query: string,
335.   limit = 8
336. ): Promise<StockSearchSuggestion[]> {
337.   return (await searchNseQuotes(query, limit)).map(({ symbol, companyName, sector, industry }) => ({
338.     symbol,
339.     companyName,
340.     sector,
341.     industry
342.   }));
343. }
344. 
345. export async function lookupNseSymbol(query: string): Promise<CompanyLookup | null> {
346.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
347. 
348.   if (!normalized) {
349.     return null;
350.   }
351. 
352.   const matches = await searchNseQuotes(normalized, 6);
353. 
354.   return matches.find((match) => match.symbol === normalized) ?? matches[0] ?? null;
355. }
356. 
357. async function fetchScreenerFundamentals(symbol: string) {
358.   try {
359.     const html = await fetchText(`https://www.screener.in/company/${encodeURIComponent(symbol)}/consolidated/`);
360.     const description = decodeHtmlEntities(
361.       textContent(/<meta name="description" content="([^"]+)"/i, html) ?? ""
362.     );
363. 
364.     if (!description) {
365.       return null;
366.     }
367. 
368.     const marketCapCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Mkt Cap:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Crore/i, description));
369.     const marketCapChange1YPct = parsePercent(
370.       textContent(/Mkt Cap:[^(]+\((?:up|down)\s*([\d.]+)% in 1 year\)/i, description)
371.     );
372.     const revenueCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Revenue:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
373.     const profitCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Profit:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
374.     const salesGrowthLabel = textContent(/company has delivered a ([a-z]+) sales growth/i, description);
375.     const salesGrowth5YPct = parsePercent(textContent(/sales growth of ([\d.]+)% over past five years/i, description));
376.     const returnOnEquityLabel = textContent(/company has a ([a-z]+) return on equity/i, description);
377.     const returnOnEquityPct = parsePercent(textContent(/return on equity of ([\d.]+)%/i, description));
378.     const promoterHoldingPct = parsePercent(textContent(/Promoter Holding:\s*([\d.]+)%/i, description));
379.     const netMarginPct =
380.       revenueCrore && profitCrore ? Number(((profitCrore / revenueCrore) * 100).toFixed(2)) : null;
381. 
382.     return {
383.       source: "Screener.in",
384.       summary: description,
385.       marketCapCrore,
386.       marketCapChange1YPct,
387.       revenueCrore,
388.       profitCrore,
389.       netMarginPct,
390.       salesGrowth5YPct,
391.       salesGrowthLabel,
392.       returnOnEquityPct,
393.       returnOnEquityLabel,
394.       promoterHoldingPct
395.     } satisfies FundamentalSnapshot;
396.   } catch {
397.     return null;
398.   }
399. }
400. 
401. async function fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(companyName: string, symbol: string) {
402.   try {
403.     const query = `"${companyName}" OR ${symbol} NSE stock when:30d`;
404.     const xml = await fetchText(
405.       `https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN:en`
406.     );
407.     const headlines: NewsHeadline[] = [];
408. 
409.     for (const match of xml.matchAll(/<item>([\s\S]*?)<\/item>/g)) {
410.       const item = match[1];
411.       const rawTitle = textContent(/<title>([\s\S]*?)<\/title>/i, item);
412. 
413.       if (!rawTitle) {
414.         continue;
415.       }
416. 
417.       const title = decodeHtmlEntities(rawTitle).replace(/\s+-\s+[^-]+$/, "").trim();
418.       const source = decodeHtmlEntities(textContent(/<source[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/source>/i, item) ?? "Google News");
419.       const publishedAt = textContent(/<pubDate>([\s\S]*?)<\/pubDate>/i, item) ?? "";
420.       const tone = titleTone(title);
421.       const category = headlineCategory(title);
422. 
423.       headlines.push({
424.         title,
425.         source,
426.         publishedAt,
427.         tone,
428.         category
429.       });
430. 
431.       if (headlines.length >= 6) {
432.         break;
433.       }
434.     }
435. 
436.     if (!headlines.length) {
437.       return null;
438.     }
439. 
440.     const score = Number(sentimentScore(headlines).toFixed(2));
441. 
442.     return {
443.       query,
444.       overall: overallSentiment(score),
445.       score,
446.       positiveCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "positive").length,
447.       neutralCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "neutral").length,
448.       negativeCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "negative").length,
449.       earningsMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings").length,
450.       analystMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "analyst").length,
451.       headlines
452.     } satisfies SentimentSnapshot;
453.   } catch {
454.     return null;
455.   }
456. }
457. 
458. export async function fetchCompanyResearch(lookup: CompanyLookup): Promise<CompanyResearch> {
459.   const [fundamentals, sentiment] = await Promise.all([
460.     fetchScreenerFundamentals(lookup.symbol),
461.     fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(lookup.companyName, lookup.symbol)
462.   ]);
463. 
464.   return {
465.     companyName: lookup.companyName,
466.     sector: lookup.sector,
467.     industry: lookup.industry,
468.     fundamentals,
469.     sentiment
470.   };
471. }
472. 
473. export function scoreFundamentals(fundamentals: FundamentalSnapshot | null) {
474.   if (!fundamentals) {
475.     return 50;
476.   }
477. 
478.   const growthScore =
479.     fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct === null
480.       ? 50
481.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 35 + fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct * 3));
482.   const roeScore =
483.     fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct === null
484.       ? 50
485.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct * 3));
486.   const marginScore =
487.     fundamentals.netMarginPct === null
488.       ? 50
489.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 30 + fundamentals.netMarginPct * 2.5));
490.   const promoterScore =
491.     fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct === null
492.       ? 50
493.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct));
494.   const labelBonus =
495.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "strong" || fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "high" ? 7 : 0) +
496.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "high" || fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "good" ? 6 : 0) -
497.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "poor" ? 8 : 0) -
498.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "low" ? 8 : 0);
499. 
500.   return Number(
501.     (
502.       growthScore * 0.3 +
503.       roeScore * 0.35 +
504.       marginScore * 0.15 +
505.       promoterScore * 0.2 +
506.       labelBonus
507.     ).toFixed(2)
508.   );
509. }
510. 
511. export function scoreSentiment(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
512.   return sentiment?.score ?? 50;
513. }
514. 
515. export function scoreEarningsSignal(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
516.   if (!sentiment) {
517.     return 50;
518.   }
519. 
520.   const earningsHeadlines = sentiment.headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings");
```

</details>

1. "use client";
2.

7.   compactUniverseLabel,

9.   formatDateTime,
10.   formatNumber,
11.   formatPercent,
12.   formatPrice,

14. } from "@/components/market-ui";

18.   HorizonId,
19.   RecommendationDataset,
20.   RecommendationPlan,
21.   SearchAnalysisResult,
22.   StockAnalysis

25. type DashboardProps = {
26.   data: RecommendationDataset;
27.   searchedAnalysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;
28. };
29. 
30. type SignalState = "consider" | "watch" | "avoid";
31. type Tone = "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral";
32. 
33. const HORIZON_THRESHOLDS: Record<HorizonId, number> = {
34.   single_day: 54,
35.   swing: 56,
36.   position: 58,
37.   long_term: 60
38. };
39. 
40. function clampProgress(value: number) {
41.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, value));
42. }
43. 
44. function planScore(plan: RecommendationPlan) {
45.   return plan.score ?? plan.expectedReturnPct;
46. }
47. 
48. function signalStateFor(plan: RecommendationPlan, horizon: HorizonId): SignalState {
49.   const threshold = HORIZON_THRESHOLDS[horizon];
50.   const score = plan.score ?? 0;
51. 
52.   if (isRecommendedPlan(plan) && score >= threshold) {
53.     return "consider";
54.   }
55. 
56.   if (score >= threshold - 8 || plan.riskReward >= 1.2) {
57.     return "watch";
58.   }
59. 
60.   return "avoid";
61. }
62. 
63. function signalLabel(state: SignalState) {
64.   switch (state) {
65.     case "consider":
66.       return "Tradable";
67.     case "watch":
68.       return "Watch";
69.     default:
70.       return "Avoid";
71.   }
72. }
73. 
74. function actionLabel(state: SignalState, profileLabel: string) {
75.   switch (state) {
76.     case "consider":
77.       return `Buy (${profileLabel})`;
78.     case "watch":
79.       return "Review Watch Setup";
80.     default:
81.       return "View Analysis";
82.   }
83. }
84. 
85. function confidenceMeta(plan: RecommendationPlan, horizon: HorizonId) {
86.   const threshold = HORIZON_THRESHOLDS[horizon];
87.   const score = plan.score ?? 0;
88. 
89.   if (score >= threshold + 12) {
90.     return {
91.       label: "High Probability Trade",
92.       tone: "success" as Tone,
93.       progress: clampProgress(score),
94.       threshold
95.     };
96.   }
97. 
98.   if (score >= threshold) {
99.     return {
100.       label: "Tradable",
101.       tone: "success" as Tone,
102.       progress: clampProgress(score),
103.       threshold
104.     };
105.   }
106. 
107.   if (score >= threshold - 8) {
108.     return {
109.       label: "Watch",
110.       tone: "warning" as Tone,
111.       progress: clampProgress(score),
112.       threshold
113.     };
114.   }
115. 
116.   return {
117.     label: "Avoid",
118.     tone: "danger" as Tone,
119.     progress: clampProgress(score),
120.     threshold
121.   };
122. }
123. 
124. function riskRewardMeta(value: number) {
125.   if (value > 1.5) {
126.     return { tone: "success" as Tone, label: "Good" };
127.   }
128. 
129.   if (value >= 1) {
130.     return { tone: "warning" as Tone, label: "Balanced" };
131.   }
132. 
133.   return { tone: "danger" as Tone, label: "Weak" };
134. }
135. 
136. function trendMeta(value?: number | null) {
137.   if (value === null || value === undefined) {
138.     return { tone: "neutral" as Tone, label: "Flat", move: "n/a" };
139.   }
140. 
141.   if (value > 0) {
142.     return { tone: "success" as Tone, label: "Up", move: `↑ +${value.toFixed(2)}%` };
143.   }
144. 
145.   if (value < 0) {
146.     return { tone: "danger" as Tone, label: "Down", move: `↓ ${value.toFixed(2)}%` };
147.   }
148. 
149.   return { tone: "neutral" as Tone, label: "Flat", move: "→ 0.00%" };
150. }
151. 
152. function liveStatusMeta(mode?: string) {
153.   switch (mode) {
154.     case "live":
155.       return { tone: "success" as Tone, label: "Live" };
156.     case "cache":
157.       return { tone: "warning" as Tone, label: "Cached" };
158.     case "sample":
159.       return { tone: "neutral" as Tone, label: "Offline" };
160.     default:
161.       return { tone: "neutral" as Tone, label: "Ready" };
162.   }
163. }
164. 
165. function ScoreMeter({
166.   tone,
167.   progress,
168.   threshold,
169.   label
170. }: {
171.   tone: Tone;
172.   progress: number;
173.   threshold: number;
174.   label?: string;
175. }) {
176.   return (
177.     <div className="dashboard-score-meter" aria-hidden="true">
178.       <div className="dashboard-score-track">
179.         <span
180.           className={`dashboard-score-fill ${tone}`}
181.           style={{ width: `${progress}%` }}
182.         />
183.         <span className="dashboard-score-threshold" style={{ left: `${threshold}%` }} />
184.       </div>
185.       {label ? <span className="dashboard-score-baseline">{label}</span> : null}
186.     </div>
187.   );
188. }
189. 
190. function SearchResultBanner({
191.   searchedAnalysis,
192.   stockHref,
193.   activeHorizon,
194.   profileLabel
195. }: {
196.   searchedAnalysis: SearchAnalysisResult;
197.   stockHref: string | null;
198.   activeHorizon: HorizonId;
199.   profileLabel: string;
200. }) {
201.   const searchedStock = searchedAnalysis.stock;
202.   const quickPlan = searchedStock ? searchedStock.profiles[activeHorizon] : null;
203.   const searchedState =
204.     searchedAnalysis.status === "analyzed" && quickPlan
205.       ? signalStateFor(quickPlan, activeHorizon)
206.       : "watch";
207.   const searchTone =
208.     searchedAnalysis.status !== "analyzed"
209.       ? "neutral"
210.       : searchedAnalysis.shouldConsider
211.         ? "consider"
212.         : "avoid";
213. 
214.   return (
215.     <section className={`dashboard-search-banner ${searchTone}`}>
216.       <div className="dashboard-search-banner-copy">
217.         <span className="dashboard-mini-label">Search verdict</span>
218.         <div className="dashboard-search-banner-top">
219.           <h2>
220.             {searchedStock ? `${searchedStock.companyName} (${searchedStock.symbol})` : searchedAnalysis.query}
```

1. import type {
2.   DailyPerformance,
3.   HistoricalBatch,
4.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
5.   HorizonId,
6.   OutcomeResult,
7.   RecommendationDataset,
8.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
9. } from "@/lib/types";
10. 
11. const CLOSED_RESULTS: OutcomeResult[] = ["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit"];
12. 
13. function roundMetric(value: number) {
14.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
15. }
16. 
17. function isClosedResult(result: OutcomeResult) {
18.   return CLOSED_RESULTS.includes(result);
19. }
20. 
21. function isSuccessfulResult(result: OutcomeResult) {
22.   return result === "target_hit";
23. }
24. 
25. function isRecommendedPlan(plan: { isRecommended?: boolean }) {
26.   return plan.isRecommended ?? true;
27. }
28. 
29. function planAverageReturn(plans: HistoricalRecommendationPlan[]) {
30.   const closedPlans = plans.filter(
31.     (plan) => isRecommendedPlan(plan) && isClosedResult(plan.outcome.result)
32.   );
33. 
34.   if (!closedPlans.length) {
35.     return null;
36.   }
37. 
38.   const average =
39.     closedPlans.reduce((total, plan) => total + plan.outcome.returnPct, 0) / closedPlans.length;
40. 
41.   return roundMetric(average);
42. }
43. 
44. export function buildDailyPerformance(
45.   history: RecommendationDataset["history"],
46.   horizon: HorizonId
47. ): DailyPerformance[] {
48.   return [...history]
49.     .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))
50.     .map((batch) => {
51.       const plans = batch.recommendations
52.         .map((recommendation) => recommendation.profiles[horizon])
53.         .filter((plan) => isRecommendedPlan(plan));
54.       const successful = plans.filter((plan) => isSuccessfulResult(plan.outcome.result)).length;
55.       const failed = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === "stop_loss_hit").length;
56.       const open = plans.filter((plan) => plan.outcome.result === "open").length;
57.       const closed = successful + failed;
58. 
59.       return {
60.         batchDate: batch.batchDate,
61.         publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,
62.         total: plans.length,
63.         closed,
64.         open,
65.         successful,
66.         failed,
67.         successRate: closed ? roundMetric((successful / closed) * 100) : null,
68.         averageReturnPct: planAverageReturn(plans)
69.       };
70.     });
71. }
72. 
73. export function buildPerformanceSummary(rows: DailyPerformance[]) {
74.   const completedRows = rows.filter((row) => row.successRate !== null);
75.   const latestCompleted = completedRows[0] ?? null;
76. 
77.   if (!completedRows.length) {
78.     return {
79.       latestCompleted,
80.       averageSuccessRate: null,
81.       averageClosedReturnPct: null
82.     };
83.   }
84. 
85.   const averageSuccessRate =
86.     completedRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.successRate ?? 0), 0) / completedRows.length;
87. 
88.   const returnRows = completedRows.filter((row) => row.averageReturnPct !== null);
89.   const averageClosedReturnPct = returnRows.length
90.     ? returnRows.reduce((total, row) => total + (row.averageReturnPct ?? 0), 0) / returnRows.length
91.     : null;
92. 
93.   return {
94.     latestCompleted,
95.     averageSuccessRate: roundMetric(averageSuccessRate),
96.     averageClosedReturnPct:
97.       averageClosedReturnPct === null ? null : roundMetric(averageClosedReturnPct)
98.   };
99. }
100. 
101. export function buildStockPerformanceHistory(
102.   history: HistoricalBatch[],
103.   symbol: string,
104.   horizon: HorizonId
105. ): StockPerformanceHistoryEntry[] {
106.   return [...history]
107.     .sort((left, right) => right.batchDate.localeCompare(left.batchDate))
108.     .flatMap((batch) =>
109.       batch.recommendations
110.         .filter((recommendation) => recommendation.symbol === symbol)
111.         .filter((recommendation) => isRecommendedPlan(recommendation.profiles[horizon]))
112.         .map((recommendation) => ({
113.           batchDate: batch.batchDate,
114.           publishedAt: batch.publishedAt,
115.           symbol: recommendation.symbol,
116.           companyName: recommendation.companyName,
117.           sector: recommendation.sector,
118.           horizon,
119.           conviction: recommendation.profiles[horizon].conviction,
120.           entryPrice: recommendation.profiles[horizon].entryPrice,
121.           targetPrice: recommendation.profiles[horizon].targetPrice,
122.           stopLoss: recommendation.profiles[horizon].stopLoss,
123.           summary: recommendation.profiles[horizon].summary,
124.           outcome: recommendation.profiles[horizon].outcome
125.         }))
126.     );
127. }
128. 
129. export function outcomeLabel(result: OutcomeResult) {
130.   switch (result) {
131.     case "target_hit":
132.       return "Target hit";
133.     case "stop_loss_hit":
134.       return "Stop-loss hit";
135.     default:
136.       return "Open";
137.   }
138. }
139. 
140. export function outcomeClass(result: OutcomeResult) {
141.   switch (result) {
142.     case "target_hit":
143.       return "success";
144.     case "stop_loss_hit":
145.       return "danger";
146.     default:
147.       return "neutral";
148.   }
149. }
150.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 428m 15s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Overall:** this is an **explainable MVP research console**, not a full checklist-complete stock analysis platform. It already has a solid recommendation UI and a usable scoring engine, but **many institutional features are still partial or missing**.

## Already present

- **Fundamental layer (lightweight):**
  - Pulls limited fundamentals from **Screener.in**
  - Uses revenue, profit, sales growth, ROE, promoter holding, margin-derived fields
  - Has a **fundamental score**
  - Shows fundamental reasoning on the stock page

- **Technical layer (good MVP coverage):**
  - **SMA 50 / 200**
  - **RSI**
  - Volume ratio / volume context
  - Volatility
  - Breakout vs recent highs
  - Candle structure metrics (body, wick, close location)
  - Trend slope / moving-average slope
  - Technical scoring + reasoning

- **News / sentiment layer (basic but real):**
  - **Google News RSS** ingestion
  - Stock-level headline tagging
  - Positive / neutral / negative tone classification
  - **Stock-level sentiment score**
  - Separate **earnings** and **analyst** signal scores
  - Reasoning panels for sentiment / earnings / analyst

- **Prediction / recommendation layer:**
  - Ranked recommendations by horizon
  - Confidence-style outputs via **score** and **conviction**
  - Entry, target, stop-loss, expected return, risk/reward
  - Explanation for recommendations

- **Risk / explainability:**
  - Volatility
  - Fragility / stop-loss learning
  - Risk scoring/penalty logic
  - Historical outcome tracking

- **Dashboard / UX:**
  - Dashboard shortlist
  - Search + autocomplete
  - Individual stock detail page
  - History/archive page
  - Explanatory hover help and score-to-reasoning navigation

- **API / deployment basics:**
  - **FastAPI + Pydantic**
  - Versioned recommendation endpoints
  - Health endpoints
  - FastAPI gives **Swagger/OpenAPI docs** by default
  - Web + API deployable separately via `render.yaml`

---

## Missing or weak areas

| Checklist area | Status | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| **1. Fundamental analysis** | **Partial** | No full **balance sheet / P&L / cash flow** modeling. No structured **PE, PB, ROCE, Debt/Equity**. No **free cash flow**. No **earnings growth** model. No **sector-wise comparison** engine. |
| **2. Technical analysis** | **Partial** | Has SMA, RSI, volume, candle structure. Missing **MACD**, **Bollinger Bands**, proper **candlestick pattern detection** (engulfing/doji/hammer etc.), and explicit **bullish/bearish trend classification** labels. |
| **3. News analysis** | **Weak/Partial** | Uses **Google News** only. No **NSE** or **MoneyControl** ingestion. No **sector-wise grouping**. No true **news relevance ranking** beyond light filtering. |
| **4. Sentiment analysis** | **Partial** | Has stock-level sentiment. Missing **sector sentiment** and **market-wide sentiment** aggregation. |
| **5. AI / LLM capabilities** | **Mostly missing** | No actual **growth vs shrink prediction model**, no learned **price estimation model**, no **OpenAI vs local LLM switch**. Reasoning exists, but it is rule-based/factor-based, not LLM-driven. |
| **6. Market coverage** | **Missing for target scale** | Current live universe is about **20 large-cap stocks**, not **NIFTY 500 / 750 stocks**. |
| **7. Predictions** | **Partial** | Has ranked recommendations, confidence-like score, and explanations. Missing explicit **gainers list**, **losers list**, and downside/bearish prediction mode. |
| **8. Portfolio features** | **Missing** | No **portfolio optimization**, **allocation suggestions**, or **risk-return balancing** engine. |
| **9. Compliance** | **Missing** | No **sector caps**, **stock weight caps**, or **rule validation** layer. |
| **10. Risk analysis** | **Partial** | Has volatility and risk penalty logic. Missing **drawdown**, richer risk analytics, and a more formal multi-factor **risk scoring framework**. |
| **11. Dashboard** | **Partial** | Has dashboard + stock detail view, but not a full **view-all-stocks table** with **sector / sentiment / growth filters**. |
| **12. Automation** | **Weak/Missing** | Designed for daily analysis, but no real **scheduler** or fully automated batch pipeline is implemented. |
| **13. Data crawling** | **Partial** | Multi-source exists in a small way (**Yahoo + Screener + Google News**), with basic parsing/cleaning. Missing broader source coverage, raw-data storage, dedup, retry, source confidence, and robust pipelines. |
| **14. Logging & monitoring** | **Weak** | Has basic **health checks** only. No structured **API success/failure logs**, no **error tracking**, no real monitoring dashboards. |
| **15. API architecture** | **Partial** | Separate web and API services exist, and Swagger is effectively present via FastAPI. But this is **not a microservices architecture** yet; it is a single API service. |

---

## Concrete improvements

1. **Build a proper data foundation**
   - Add structured ingestion tables for:
     - `income_statement`
     - `balance_sheet`
     - `cash_flow`
     - `ratios`
     - `news_items`
     - `daily_prices`
   - Keep **raw + cleaned** copies of source data.

2. **Expand market coverage to NIFTY 500**
   - Replace the hardcoded watchlist with a **universe master table**.
   - Add batch processing for all covered stocks.
   - Persist daily analysis results instead of recomputing only on page load.

3. **Upgrade fundamentals from “snapshot” to “engine”**
   - Compute:
     - PE, PB, ROE, ROCE, Debt/Equity
     - free cash flow
     - earnings CAGR / revenue CAGR
     - sector percentile ranks
   - Add **sector-relative scoring** instead of absolute-only scoring.

4. **Complete the technical stack**
   - Add:
     - **MACD**
     - **Bollinger Bands**
     - explicit **trend classification**
     - proper **candlestick pattern detection**
   - Keep current candle-structure logic, but complement it with named patterns.

5. **Strengthen news + sentiment**
   - Add adapters for:
     - **NSE corporate announcements**
     - **MoneyControl** or equivalent source if terms allow
   - Add:
     - stock/entity tagging
     - sector grouping
     - source trust weighting
     - duplicate clustering
     - relevance ranking by recency + source + symbol match + category

6. **Add sector and market-wide intelligence**
   - Compute:
     - sector sentiment
     - market-wide sentiment
     - sector momentum / breadth
   - Use these as overlays on stock recommendations.

7. **Introduce a real prediction layer**
   - Separate **ranking logic** from **prediction logic**.
   - Add:
     - upside/downside classifier
     - expected return model
     - probability/confidence calibration
   - Keep the current rule-based reasoning, but support a learned model behind it.

8. **Add LLM provider abstraction**
   - Create a provider interface like:
     - `OpenAIProvider`
     - `OllamaProvider` / local LLM provider
   - Use it only for reasoning/summarization first, not primary scoring.
   - Make provider switchable via config/env.

9. **Add portfolio + compliance services**
   - Portfolio optimizer:
     - mean-variance / risk-parity / score-weighted
   - Compliance rules:
     - max sector weight
     - max stock weight
     - liquidity filter
     - concentration checks
   - Add a validation report before portfolio output is accepted.

10. **Deepen risk analytics**
    - Add:
      - max drawdown
      - rolling volatility
      - beta
      - VaR / expected shortfall
      - liquidity risk
    - Turn risk into a formal **risk model**, not just a fragility penalty.

11. **Upgrade dashboard from shortlist to research terminal**
    - Add:
      - full stock table
      - filters for sector / sentiment / growth / score / risk
      - top gainers / losers views
      - sector dashboard
      - portfolio view

12. **Automate the pipeline**
    - Add a scheduled worker:
      - end-of-day fetch
      - feature generation
      - scoring
      - persistence
      - alerting on failures
    - Use cron / scheduled job / Prefect / Airflow / GitHub Actions depending on hosting.

13. **Add logging and monitoring**
    - Structured logs
    - Error tracking (e.g. Sentry)
    - Data freshness checks
    - Source success/failure metrics
    - API latency and job-run monitoring

14. **Move toward service separation**
    - Practical split:
      1. ingestion service
      2. scoring/research service
      3. serving API
      4. optional portfolio/compliance service
    - Keep FastAPI docs/OpenAPI, but formalize service boundaries.

**Bottom line:** the current system is a **good explainable recommendation MVP**, but it is **far from covering the full checklist**. The biggest gaps are **full fundamentals, missing technical indicators, news-source breadth, NIFTY 500 scale, portfolio/compliance, automation, and monitoring**.

---

<sub>⏱️ 432m 52s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

For **v1**, I’d keep it tight and ship **8 practical changes**, not the full checklist.

1. **Expand coverage to Nifty 100/200 first, not 500.** Add a proper universe master and daily batch persistence. Current ~20-stock coverage is too small for a real product, but jumping straight to 500 will slow you down.
2. **Upgrade fundamentals from “light snapshot” to structured factors.** Add **PE, PB, ROE, ROCE, Debt/Equity, earnings growth, free cash flow/operating cash flow**, and make the score partly **sector-relative**.
3. **Complete the core technical stack.** Keep your current SMA/RSI/volume/candle logic, but add **MACD**, **Bollinger Bands**, explicit **bullish/neutral/bearish trend labels**, and named **candlestick pattern detection**.
4. **Strengthen the news layer.** Add **NSE announcements** first, then **MoneyControl** if legally/technically acceptable. Add **stock tagging, sector grouping, and relevance ranking**.
5. **Add real evaluation/backtesting.** This is the most important missing v1 capability. Track **hit rate, average return, max drawdown, horizon-wise performance, and confidence calibration**.
6. **Make daily automation real.** Add one scheduled **EOD batch worker** that fetches data, computes features, scores stocks, and stores the output. Right now the system is live/cached, but not yet a full automated pipeline.
7. **Upgrade the dashboard into a research console.** Add a **full stock table**, filters for **sector / sentiment / score / growth**, and explicit **top gainers / losers** views. Keep recommended vs watchlist clearly separated.
8. **Add production basics.** Structured **logs**, source success/failure tracking, freshness checks, retry handling, and a simple monitoring view beyond health checks.

**For v1 architecture**, I would keep it simple: **Next.js UI + one FastAPI API + one scheduled batch worker + persisted storage** for daily runs. Do **not** split into microservices yet.

**Defer to v2:** portfolio optimization, allocation/compliance rules, OpenAI vs local LLM switching, market-wide sentiment engine, and full Nifty 500 scale. Those are valuable, but they are not the highest-value v1 work.

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 1-40)

<details>
<summary>40 lines</summary>

```
1. # Stock recommendation system plan
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. Build an institutional-style stock recommendation platform inspired by firms like BlackRock: a system that ingests market and company data, ranks investment candidates, explains why they rank well, and enforces risk controls. Based on the clarified scope, the first version should support Indian equities, refresh from current internet-based market data on trading days, and produce recurring recommendations after its daily analysis run. When a user clicks a stock, the product should show the analysis behind the recommendation plus a proposed entry price, exit target, and stop-loss level.
5. 
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - a score-to-reasoning navigator in the stock workspace so technical, fundamental, sentiment, and risk scores jump to anchored explanation panels,
18.   - dedicated earnings and analyst reasoning panels, with clickable score tiles that explain why those scores moved and how they can impact the stock,
19.   - hover help on the analysis panels so each card explains what it is, why it is needed, and how it can impact the stock,
20.   - a threshold-separated home workspace where below-threshold names are shown only in a clearly labeled watchlist section instead of inside the active recommendation list,
21.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
22.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
23.   - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
24.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
25.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`.
26. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
27. 
28. ## Proposed approach
29. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
30. 
31. 1. Product definition
32.    - Define the target user, market universe, holding horizon, and delivery channel.
33.    - Decide whether the output is a ranked watchlist, portfolio suggestions, or execution-ready trades.
34.    - Structure the product around multiple horizon profiles instead of a single recommendation type.
35.    - Chosen MVP scope:
36.      - Target user: an analyst or serious retail investor reviewing institutional-style research, not a casual tips feed.
37.      - Market universe: liquid NSE equities, starting with a Nifty 500-derived universe.
38.      - Delivery: web dashboard with sortable recommendations and a stock detail page.
39.      - Recommendation mode: research-first and decision-support oriented, not direct broker execution.
40.      - Daily batch behavior: run after market close on supported trading days and publish a next-session trade plan.
```

</details>

3. import {
4.   fetchCompanyResearch,
5.   lookupNseSymbol,
6.   scoreAnalystSignal,
7.   scoreEarningsSignal,
8.   scoreFundamentals,
9.   scoreSentiment
10. } from "@/lib/company-research";

12.   AnalysisDriver,
13.   DataSourceInfo,
14.   HistoricalBatch,
15.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
16.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
17.   HorizonId,
18.   HorizonProfile,
19.   OutcomeResult,
20.   RecommendationDataset,
21.   RecommendationOutcome,
22.   RecommendationPlan,
23.   SearchAnalysisResult,
24.   Signal,
25.   StockAnalysis
26. } from "@/lib/types";

44.   minimumScore: number;
45.   targetRangePct: [number, number];
46.   stopRangePct: [number, number];
47.   atrTargetMultiple: number;
48.   atrStopMultiple: number;
49.   maxHoldDays: number;
50. };
51.

55.   open: number;
56.   high: number;
57.   low: number;

61.

68.   regularMarketDayLow?: number;

85.   chart?: {
86.     result?: Array<{
87.       meta?: ChartMeta;
88.       timestamp?: number[];

855. 
856. function roundMetric(value: number) {
857.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
858. }
859. 
860. function roundPrice(value: number) {
861.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
862. }
863. 
864. function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number) {
865.   return Math.min(Math.max(value, min), max);
866. }
867. 
868. function average(values: number[]) {
869.   if (!values.length) {
870.     return null;
871.   }
872. 
873.   return values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / values.length;
874. }
875. 
876. function pctChange(current: number, previous: number) {
877.   if (!Number.isFinite(current) || !Number.isFinite(previous) || previous === 0) {
878.     return null;
879.   }
880. 
881.   return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100;
882. }
883. 
884. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
885.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
886.     return 0.5;
887.   }
888. 
889.   if (max === min) {
890.     return 0.5;
891.   }
892. 
893.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
894. }
895. 
896. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
897.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
898. }
899. 
900. function percentText(value: number | null) {
901.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
902.     return "n/a";

904.

946. function marketCapBucketFromCrore(value: number | null) {
947.   if (value === null) {
948.     return "Large Cap";
949.   }
950. 
951.   if (value >= 100000) {
952.     return "Large Cap";
953.   }
954. 
955.   if (value >= 30000) {
956.     return "Mid Cap";
957.   }
958. 
959.   return "Small Cap";
960. }
961. 
962. function liquidityTierFromVolume(volume: number | null) {
963.   if (volume === null) {
964.     return "Tier 2";
965.   }
966. 
967.   if (volume >= 5000000) {
968.     return "Tier 1";
969.   }
970. 
971.   if (volume >= 1000000) {
972.     return "Tier 2";
973.   }
974. 
975.   return "Tier 3";
976. }
977. 
978. function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {
979.   if (values.length < period) {
980.     return null;
981.   }
982. 
983.   return average(values.slice(-period));
984. }
985. 
986. function annualizedVolatility(closes: number[], period: number) {
987.   if (closes.length < period + 1) {
988.     return null;
989.   }
990. 
991.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
992.   const returns = window.slice(1).map((close, index) => (close - window[index]) / window[index]);
993.   const mean = average(returns);
994. 
995.   if (mean === null) {
996.     return null;
997.   }
998. 
999.   const variance =
1000.     returns.reduce((total, value) => total + (value - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;
1001. 
1002.   return Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100;
1003. }
1004. 
1005. function relativeStrengthIndex(closes: number[], period: number) {
1006.   if (closes.length <= period) {
1007.     return null;
1008.   }
1009. 
1010.   let gains = 0;
1011.   let losses = 0;
1012.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
1013. 
1014.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
1015.     const change = window[index] - window[index - 1];
1016. 
1017.     if (change > 0) {
1018.       gains += change;
1019.     } else {
1020.       losses += Math.abs(change);
1021.     }
1022.   }
1023. 
1024.   if (losses === 0) {
1025.     return 100;
1026.   }
1027. 
1028.   const averageGain = gains / period;
1029.   const averageLoss = losses / period;
1030.   const relativeStrength = averageGain / averageLoss;
1031. 
1032.   return 100 - 100 / (1 + relativeStrength);
1033. }
1034. 
1035. function averageTrueRange(bars: PriceBar[], period: number) {
1036.   if (bars.length <= period) {
1037.     return null;
1038.   }
1039. 
1040.   const window = bars.slice(-(period + 1));
1041.   const trueRanges: number[] = [];
1042. 
1043.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
1044.     const current = window[index];
1045.     const previous = window[index - 1];
1046.     const trueRange = Math.max(
1047.       current.high - current.low,
1048.       Math.abs(current.high - previous.close),
1049.       Math.abs(current.low - previous.close)
1050.     );
1051.     trueRanges.push(trueRange);
1052.   }
1053. 
1054.   return average(trueRanges);
1055. }
1056.

1058.   if (high <= low) {
1059.     return null;
1060.   }

2615. async function buildLiveDataset() {
2616.   const benchmark = await fetchSeries(BENCHMARK);
2617.   const liveSeries = (await Promise.all(MARKET_UNIVERSE.map((entry) => fetchSeries(entry)))).filter(
2618.     (series): series is MarketSeries => series !== null
2619.   );
2620. 
2621.   if (!benchmark || liveSeries.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
2622.     return null;

2624. 
2625.   const latestDate = benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date;
2626. 
2627.   if (!latestDate) {
2628.     return null;
2629.   }
2630. 
2631.   const history = buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries, benchmark);
2632.   const learningMap = deriveStopLossLearning(history);
2633.   const currentRecommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(
2634.     latestDate,
2635.     liveSeries,
2636.     benchmark,
2637.     learningMap
2638.   );
2639. 
2640.   if (currentRecommendations.length < MINIMUM_LIVE_SYMBOLS) {
2641.     return null;
2642.   }
2643. 
2644.   const dataset: RecommendationDataset = normalizeDataset({
2645.     market: "Indian Equities",
2646.     exchange: "NSE",
2647.     universe: `Liquid NSE watchlist (${liveSeries.length} live symbols)`,
2648.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
2649.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.`,
2650.       liveSeries.length
2651.     ),
2652.     profiles: PROFILE_LIST,
2653.     currentBatch: {
2654.       batchDate: latestDate,
2655.       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
2656.       recommendations: currentRecommendations
2657.     },
2658.     history
2659.   });
2660.   const persisted = await writeGeneratedDataset(dataset);
2661.   const finalDataset = {
2662.     ...dataset,
2663.     dataSource: liveDataSourceInfo(
2664.       `${liveSeries.length}/${MARKET_UNIVERSE.length} live symbols refreshed successfully with benchmark ${BENCHMARK.symbol}.${persisted ? " Snapshot persisted locally." : " Persistent filesystem cache was unavailable, so the app is using in-memory caching for this runtime."}`,
2665.       liveSeries.length
2666.     )
2667.   };
2668. 
2669.   return rememberDataset(finalDataset);
2670. }

1065. function recentExtreme(values: number[], lookback: number, mode: "high" | "low") {
1066.   if (values.length <= lookback) {
1067.     return null;
1068.   }
1069. 
1070.   const window = values.slice(-(lookback + 1), -1);
1071. 
1072.   if (!window.length) {
1073.     return null;
1074.   }
1075. 
1076.   return mode === "high" ? Math.max(...window) : Math.min(...window);
1077. }
1078. 
1079. function movingAverageSlope(values: number[], period: number, lookback: number) {
1080.   if (values.length < period + lookback) {
1081.     return null;
1082.   }
1083. 
1084.   const currentAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values, period);
1085.   const priorAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values.slice(0, -lookback), period);
1086. 
1087.   if (currentAverage === null || priorAverage === null) {
1088.     return null;
1089.   }
1090. 
1091.   return pctChange(currentAverage, priorAverage);
1092. }
1093. 
1094. function averageBarRangePct(bars: PriceBar[]) {
1095.   const percentages = bars
1096.     .map((bar) => (bar.close !== 0 ? ((bar.high - bar.low) / bar.close) * 100 : null))
1097.     .filter((value): value is number => value !== null && Number.isFinite(value));
1098. 
1099.   return average(percentages);
1100. }
1101. 
1102. function rangeCompressionRatio(bars: PriceBar[], shortPeriod: number, longPeriod: number) {
1103.   if (bars.length < longPeriod) {
1104.     return null;
1105.   }
1106. 
1107.   const shortAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-shortPeriod));
1108.   const longAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-longPeriod));
1109. 
1110.   if (shortAverage === null || longAverage === null || longAverage === 0) {
1111.     return null;
1112.   }
1113. 
1114.   return shortAverage / longAverage;
1115. }
1116. 
1117. function closeLocationPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1118.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1119. 
1120.   if (range <= 0) {
1121.     return null;
1122.   }
1123. 
1124.   return ((bar.close - bar.low) / range) * 100;
1125. }
1126. 
1127. function candleBodyPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1128.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1129. 
1130.   if (range <= 0) {
1131.     return null;
1132.   }
1133. 
1134.   return (Math.abs(bar.close - bar.open) / range) * 100;
1135. }
1136. 
1137. function upperWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1138.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1139. 
1140.   if (range <= 0) {
1141.     return null;
1142.   }
1143. 
1144.   return ((bar.high - Math.max(bar.open, bar.close)) / range) * 100;
1145. }
1146. 
1147. function lowerWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1148.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1149. 
1150.   if (range <= 0) {
1151.     return null;
1152.   }
1153. 
1154.   return ((Math.min(bar.open, bar.close) - bar.low) / range) * 100;
1155. }
1156. 
1157. function tradingDate(timestamp: number) {
1158.   return new Date(timestamp * 1000).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
1159. }
1160. 
1161. function publishedTimestamp(date: string) {
1162.   return `${date}T15:45:00+05:30`;
1163. }
1164. 
1165. function signalScoreText(value: number) {
1166.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(1)}/100`;
1167. }
1168. 
1169. function companyNameFromMeta(meta: ChartMeta, fallbackCompanyName: string) {
1170.   return meta.longName ?? meta.shortName ?? fallbackCompanyName;
1171. }
1172. 
1173. function convictionFromScore(score: number) {
1174.   if (score >= 74) {
1175.     return "High";
1176.   }
1177. 
1178.   if (score >= 60) {
1179.     return "Medium";
1180.   }
1181. 
1182.   return "Low";
1183. }
1184. 
1185. function weightedScore(parts: Array<[number, number]>) {
1186.   const total = parts.reduce((sum, [weight, score]) => sum + weight * score, 0);
1187.   return roundMetric(total * 100);
1188. }

1190. function resolveDataDirectoryFromSample(samplePath: string) {
1191.   return path.dirname(samplePath);
1192. }
1193. 
1194. async function findFirstReadableFile(candidates: string[]) {
1195.   for (const candidate of candidates) {
1196.     try {
1197.       await readFile(candidate, "utf-8");
1198.       return candidate;
1199.     } catch {
1200.       continue;
1201.     }
1202.   }
1203. 
1204.   return null;
1205. }
1206. 
1207. async function readJsonFile<T>(filePath: string) {
1208.   const payload = await readFile(filePath, "utf-8");
1209.   return JSON.parse(payload) as T;
1210. }
1211. 
1212. function sampleFileCandidates() {
1213.   return DATA_DIRECTORY_CANDIDATES.map((directory) => path.join(directory, SAMPLE_FILE_NAME));
1214. }
1215. 
1216. async function generatedFilePath() {
1217.   const samplePath = await findFirstReadableFile(sampleFileCandidates());
1218.   const directory = samplePath
1219.     ? resolveDataDirectoryFromSample(samplePath)
1220.     : DATA_DIRECTORY_CANDIDATES[0];
1221. 
1222.   await mkdir(directory, { recursive: true });
1223. 
1224.   return path.join(directory, GENERATED_FILE_NAME);
1225. }
1226. 
1227. function cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(plan: RecommendationPlan): RecommendationPlan {
1228.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS.single_day;
1229.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(plan.entryPrice);
1230.   const targetPct = clamp(
1231.     Math.abs(pctChange(plan.targetPrice, plan.entryPrice) ?? plan.expectedReturnPct) * 0.38,
1232.     settings.targetRangePct[0],
1233.     settings.targetRangePct[1]
1234.   );
1235.   const stopPct = clamp(
1236.     Math.abs(pctChange(plan.stopLoss, plan.entryPrice) ?? settings.stopRangePct[0]) * 0.45,
1237.     settings.stopRangePct[0],
1238.     settings.stopRangePct[1]
1239.   );
1240.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
1241.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.85));
1242. 
1243.   return {
1244.     ...plan,
1245.     score: plan.score ?? 55,
1246.     conviction: plan.conviction === "High" ? "Medium" : plan.conviction,
1247.     entryPrice,
1248.     targetPrice,
1249.     stopLoss,
1250.     expectedReturnPct: roundMetric(pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? targetPct),
1251.     riskReward: roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01)),
1252.     summary: `Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. ${plan.summary}`,
1253.     drivers: [
1254.       "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
1255.       ...plan.drivers
1256.     ].slice(0, 4),
1257.     analysisDrivers: [
1258.       {
1259.         area: "risk",
1260.         impact: "neutral",
1261.         title: "Compatibility mode",
1262.         detail:
1263.           "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the current single-day reasoning model."
1264.       },
1265.       ...(plan.analysisDrivers ?? [])
1266.     ].slice(0, 6),
1267.     technicalSignals: [
1268.       { name: "Compatibility mode", value: "Derived from swing profile" },
1269.       ...plan.technicalSignals
1270.     ].slice(0, 6),
1271.     riskSignals: plan.riskSignals ?? [
1272.       { name: "Learning status", value: "Awaiting refreshed live batch" }
1273.     ]
1274.   };
1275. }
1276. 
1277. function cloneSingleDayHistoricalPlanFromFallback(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan): HistoricalRecommendationPlan {
1278.   return {
1279.     ...plan,
1280.     conviction: plan.conviction === "High" ? "Medium" : plan.conviction,
1281.     summary: `Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. ${plan.summary}`,
1282.     outcome: {
1283.       ...plan.outcome,
1284.       notes: `This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. ${plan.outcome.notes}`
1285.     }
1286.   };
1287. }
1288. 
1289. function normalizeRecommendationProfiles(profiles: Record<string, RecommendationPlan>) {
1290.   return {
1291.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
1292.     swing: profiles.swing,
1293.     position: profiles.position,
1294.     long_term: profiles.long_term
1295.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, RecommendationPlan>;
1296. }
1297. 
1298. function normalizeHistoricalProfiles(profiles: Record<string, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>) {
1299.   return {
1300.     single_day: profiles.single_day ?? cloneSingleDayHistoricalPlanFromFallback(profiles.swing),
1301.     swing: profiles.swing,
1302.     position: profiles.position,
1303.     long_term: profiles.long_term
1304.   } satisfies Record<HorizonId, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>;
1305. }
1306. 
1307. function normalizeDataset(dataset: RecommendationDataset): RecommendationDataset {
1308.   return {
1309.     ...dataset,
1310.     profiles: HORIZON_ORDER.map(
1311.       (horizon) =>
1312.         dataset.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === horizon) ?? {
1313.           id: horizon,
1314.           label: HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label,
1315.           window: HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].window
1316.         }
1317.     ),
1318.     currentBatch: {
1319.       ...dataset.currentBatch,
1320.       recommendations: dataset.currentBatch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
1321.         ...recommendation,
1322.         profiles: normalizeRecommendationProfiles(recommendation.profiles as Record<string, RecommendationPlan>)
1323.       }))
1324.     },
1325.     history: dataset.history.map((batch) => ({
1326.       ...batch,
1327.       recommendations: batch.recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
1328.         ...recommendation,
1329.         profiles: normalizeHistoricalProfiles(
1330.           recommendation.profiles as Record<string, HistoricalRecommendationPlan>
1331.         )
1332.       }))
1333.     }))
1334.   };
1335. }
1336. 
1337. async function readSampleDataset() {
1338.   const samplePath = await findFirstReadableFile(sampleFileCandidates());
1339. 
1340.   if (!samplePath) {
1341.     throw new Error("Unable to locate sample-recommendations.json");
1342.   }
1343. 
1344.   return normalizeDataset(await readJsonFile<RecommendationDataset>(samplePath));
1345. }
1346. 
1347. async function readGeneratedDataset() {
1348.   try {
1349.     return normalizeDataset(await readJsonFile<RecommendationDataset>(await generatedFilePath()));
1350.   } catch {
1351.     return null;
1352.   }
1353. }
1354. 
1355. async function writeGeneratedDataset(dataset: RecommendationDataset) {
1356.   try {
1357.     await writeFile(await generatedFilePath(), JSON.stringify(dataset, null, 2), "utf-8");
1358.     return true;
1359.   } catch {
1360.     return false;
1361.   }
1362. }
1363. 
1364. async function fetchChartPayload(yahooSymbol: string) {
1365.   const encodedSymbol = encodeURIComponent(yahooSymbol);
1366.   const urls = [
1367.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`,
1368.     `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`
```

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\recommendation-data.ts\n@@\n function average(values: number[]) {\n   if (!values.length) {\n     return null;\n   }\n \n   return values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / values.length;\n }\n+\n+function minimumLiveSymbolsRequired() {\n+  return Math.max(25, Math.floor(MARKET_UNIVERSE.length * MINIMUM_LIVE_COVERAGE_RATIO));\n+}\n+\n+async function mapWithConcurrency<T, TResult>(\n+  items: T[],\n+  concurrency: number,\n+  mapper: (item: T, index: number) => Promise<TResult>\n+) {\n+  const results = new Array<TResult>(items.length);\n+  let nextIndex = 0;\n+\n+  async function worker() {\n+    while (true) {\n+      const index = nextIndex;\n+      nextIndex += 1;\n+\n+      if (index >= items.length) {\n+        return;\n+      }\n+\n+      results[index] = await mapper(items[index], index);\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  const workerCount = Math.max(1, Math.min(concurrency, items.length));\n+  await Promise.all(Array.from({ length: workerCount }, () => worker()));\n+\n+  return results;\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"
```

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\README.md\n@@\n-- **Market:** liquid NSE equities, starting from an expanded large-cap live watchlist\n+- **Market:** liquid NSE equities, now starting from a dedicated **Nifty 100 universe master**\n@@\n-- The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for a liquid NSE watchlist plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.\n+- The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for the **Nifty 100 universe** plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.\n@@\n - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.\n+- Each successful daily batch is also archived to `data/daily-batches/<batch-date>.json` for persistent run history.\n - If persistent disk writes are not available on the host, the app falls back to **in-memory caching** for the running instance.\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\rc18763\\.copilot\\session-state\\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\\plan.md\n@@\n - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\\copilot`.\n-- The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a liquid NSE watchlist, ranks stocks per horizon, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.\n+- The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a Nifty 100 universe master, ranks stocks per horizon, archives daily batch snapshots, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.\n@@\n   - a threshold-separated home workspace where below-threshold names are shown only in a clearly labeled watchlist section instead of inside the active recommendation list,\n   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,\n   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,\n-  - an expanded live NSE watchlist and a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,\n+  - a Nifty 100 universe master with safer live-fetch scaling and daily batch snapshot persistence, plus a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,\n*** End Patch\n"

1. import unittest
2. 
3. from app.analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
4. from app.main import load_dataset
5. 
6. 
7. class RecommendationAnalyticsTests(unittest.TestCase):
8.     @classmethod
9.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
10.         cls.dataset = load_dataset(prefer_generated=False)
11. 
12.     def test_swing_daily_performance_counts_successes_and_failures(self) -> None:
13.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "swing")
14.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
15. 
16.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 2)
17.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
18.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 3)
19.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 0)
20.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 66.67, places=2)
21. 
22.     def test_position_success_rate_ignores_open_trades(self) -> None:
23.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "position")
24.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
25. 
26.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 1)
27.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
28.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 1)
29.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 2)
30.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 50.0, places=2)
31. 
32.     def test_long_term_daily_performance_is_pending_when_nothing_is_closed(self) -> None:
33.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "long_term")
34.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
35. 
36.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 3)
37.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 0)
38.         self.assertIsNone(latest.successRate)
39.         self.assertIsNone(latest.averageReturnPct)
40. 
41.     def test_stock_history_filters_by_symbol_in_latest_first_order(self) -> None:
42.         history = build_stock_history(self.dataset, "RELIANCE", "swing")
43. 
44.         self.assertEqual(
45.             [entry.batchDate for entry in history],
46.             ["2026-04-14", "2026-04-11", "2026-04-10"],
47.         )
48.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.symbol == "RELIANCE" for entry in history))
49. 
50.     def test_dataset_normalization_adds_single_day_profile(self) -> None:
51.         self.assertIn("single_day", self.dataset.currentBatch.recommendations[0].profiles)
52.         self.assertEqual(self.dataset.profiles[0].id, "single_day")
53. 
54. 
55. if __name__ == "__main__":
56.     unittest.main()
57.
```

C:\copilot\README.md (lines 60-100)

<details>

```
60. 6. Historical stop-loss outcomes now include a clearer explanation of **why the stop-loss was hit**, and the live model uses recent stop-loss history to reduce future aggressiveness for fragile setups.
61. 
62. ## Windows quick start
63. 
64. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
65. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
66. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
67. - The run card in the app shows whether the current batch is coming from **LIVE**, **CACHED**, or **SAMPLE** data.
68. 
69. ## Analytics test command
70. 
71. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
72. 
73. ## Deployment and free hosting
74. 
75. ### Production-readiness changes in this repo
76. 
77. - The Next.js app now exposes `GET /api/healthz` for simple uptime checks.
78. - The live dataset loader supports **in-memory runtime caching**, which is friendlier to serverless or ephemeral free hosts.
79. - `render.yaml` is included so you can deploy both the web app and the FastAPI service on Render with minimal setup.
80. 
81. ### Recommended free hosting options
82. 
83. 1. **Vercel** for the Next.js app only. This is the easiest choice for the dashboard because it handles Next.js SSR well. In Vercel, set the project root to `apps/web`.
84. 2. **Render** if you want both the Next.js app and the FastAPI API hosted together for free. The included `render.yaml` is aimed at this option. Render free web services can sleep when idle, so expect cold starts.
85. 3. **Cloudflare Pages** if you want a very low-cost global edge deployment for the web app and are comfortable with more setup than Vercel.
86. 
87. For this repository as it exists today, **Vercel** is the simplest free host for the dashboard, while **Render** is the simplest free option if you want to run both services from one repo.
88.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

C:\copilot\services\api\app\analytics.py (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>98 lines</summary>

```
1. from .schemas import (
2.     DailyPerformance,
3.     HistoricalBatch,
4.     HorizonId,
5.     OutcomeResult,
6.     RecommendationDataset,
7.     StockPerformanceHistoryEntry,
8. )
9. 
10. CLOSED_RESULTS: set[OutcomeResult] = {"target_hit", "stop_loss_hit"}
11. 
12. 
13. def _round_metric(value: float | None) -> float | None:
14.     if value is None:
15.         return None
16. 
17.     return round(value, 2)
18. 
19. 
20. def _is_closed_result(result: OutcomeResult) -> bool:
21.     return result in CLOSED_RESULTS
22. 
23. 
24. def _is_recommended(plan: object) -> bool:
25.     return getattr(plan, "isRecommended", True)
26. 
27. 
28. def build_daily_performance(dataset: RecommendationDataset, horizon: HorizonId) -> list[DailyPerformance]:
29.     rows: list[DailyPerformance] = []
30. 
31.     for batch in sorted(dataset.history, key=lambda item: item.batchDate, reverse=True):
32.         plans = [
33.             recommendation.profiles[horizon]
34.             for recommendation in batch.recommendations
35.             if _is_recommended(recommendation.profiles[horizon])
36.         ]
37.         successful = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "target_hit")
38.         failed = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "stop_loss_hit")
39.         open_count = sum(1 for plan in plans if plan.outcome.result == "open")
40.         closed = successful + failed
41.         closed_returns = [
42.             plan.outcome.returnPct for plan in plans if _is_closed_result(plan.outcome.result)
43.         ]
44. 
45.         rows.append(
46.             DailyPerformance(
47.                 batchDate=batch.batchDate,
48.                 publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,
49.                 total=len(plans),
50.                 closed=closed,
51.                 open=open_count,
52.                 successful=successful,
53.                 failed=failed,
54.                 successRate=_round_metric((successful / closed) * 100) if closed else None,
55.                 averageReturnPct=(
56.                     _round_metric(sum(closed_returns) / len(closed_returns))
57.                     if closed_returns
58.                     else None
59.                 ),
60.             )
61.         )
62. 
63.     return rows
64. 
65. 
66. def build_stock_history(
67.     dataset: RecommendationDataset, symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId
68. ) -> list[StockPerformanceHistoryEntry]:
69.     rows: list[StockPerformanceHistoryEntry] = []
70.     requested_symbol = symbol.upper()
71. 
72.     for batch in sorted(dataset.history, key=lambda item: item.batchDate, reverse=True):
73.         for recommendation in batch.recommendations:
74.             if recommendation.symbol != requested_symbol:
75.                 continue
76. 
77.             plan = recommendation.profiles[horizon]
78.             if not _is_recommended(plan):
79.                 continue
80.             rows.append(
81.                 StockPerformanceHistoryEntry(
82.                     batchDate=batch.batchDate,
83.                     publishedAt=batch.publishedAt,
84.                     symbol=recommendation.symbol,
85.                     companyName=recommendation.companyName,
86.                     sector=recommendation.sector,
87.                     horizon=horizon,
88.                     conviction=plan.conviction,
89.                     entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
90.                     targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
91.                     stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
92.                     summary=plan.summary,
93.                     outcome=plan.outcome,
94.                 )
95.             )
96. 
97.     return rows
98.
```

</details>

1. import type {

8. export type CompanyLookup = {

10.   yahooSymbol: string;
11.   companyName: string;
12.   sector: string;

15.

28.   longname?: string;

38. };
