40. const POSITIVE_WORDS = [
41.   "beat",
42.   "beats",
43.   "bullish",
44.   "buy",
45.   "gains",
46.   "gain",
47.   "growth",
48.   "improves",
49.   "improve",
50.   "outperform",
51.   "positive",
52.   "rally",
53.   "rebound",
54.   "record",
55.   "robust",
56.   "strong",
57.   "surge",
58.   "safe bet",
59.   "upgrade",
60.   "upgrades",
61.   "accumulate"
62. ];
63. 
64. const NEGATIVE_WORDS = [
65.   "bearish",
66.   "concern",
67.   "crash",
68.   "cut",
69.   "cuts",
70.   "decline",
71.   "declines",
72.   "downgrade",
73.   "downgrades",
74.   "drop",
75.   "drops",
76.   "fall",
77.   "falls",
78.   "lower",
79.   "miss",
80.   "misses",
81.   "pressure",
82.   "sell",
83.   "slump",
84.   "slumps",
85.   "underperform",
86.   "warning",
87.   "weak"
88. ];
89. 
90. const EARNINGS_KEYWORDS = [
91.   "earnings",
92.   "results",
93.   "quarter",
94.   "q1",
95.   "q2",
96.   "q3",
97.   "q4",
98.   "revenue",
99.   "profit",
100.   "margin"
101. ];
102. 
103. const ANALYST_KEYWORDS = [
104.   "analyst",
105.   "brokerage",
106.   "buy",
107.   "hold",
108.   "sell",
109.   "target",
110.   "rating",
111.   "upgrade",
112.   "downgrade",
113.   "overweight",
114.   "underweight",
115.   "outperform",
116.   "underperform"
117. ];
118. 
119. function decodeHtmlEntities(value: string) {
120.   return value
121.     .replace(/&amp;/g, "&")
122.     .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
123.     .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
124.     .replace(/&lt;/g, "<")
125.     .replace(/&gt;/g, ">");
126. }
127. 
128. function normalizeSearchSymbol(query: string) {
129.   return query.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");
130. }
131. 
132. function parseIndianNumber(value: string | null) {
133.   if (!value) {
134.     return null;
135.   }
136. 
137.   const normalized = value.replace(/,/g, "").trim();
138.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(normalized);
139. 
140.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
141. }
142. 
143. function parsePercent(value: string | null) {
144.   if (!value) {
145.     return null;
146.   }
147. 
148.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(value.replace(/,/g, "").trim());
149. 
150.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
151. }
152. 
153. function textContent(regex: RegExp, text: string) {
154.   const match = regex.exec(text);
155.   return match?.[1] ?? null;
156. }
157. 
158. function titleTone(title: string) {
159.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
160.   const positiveHits = POSITIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
161.   const negativeHits = NEGATIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
162. 
163.   if (positiveHits > negativeHits) {
164.     return "positive" as const;
165.   }
166. 
167.   if (negativeHits > positiveHits) {
168.     return "negative" as const;
169.   }
170. 
171.   return "neutral" as const;
172. }
173. 
174. function headlineCategory(title: string) {
175.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
176. 
177.   if (ANALYST_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
178.     return "analyst" as const;
179.   }
180. 
181.   if (EARNINGS_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
182.     return "earnings" as const;
183.   }
184. 
185.   return "news" as const;
186. }
187. 
188. function sentimentScore(headlines: NewsHeadline[]) {
189.   if (!headlines.length) {
190.     return 50;
191.   }
192. 
193.   const toneTotal = headlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
194.     if (headline.tone === "positive") {
195.       return total + 1;
196.     }
197. 
198.     if (headline.tone === "negative") {
199.       return total - 1;
200.     }
201. 
202.     return total;
203.   }, 0);
204. 
205.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + (toneTotal / headlines.length) * 35));
206. }
207. 
208. function overallSentiment(score: number) {
209.   if (score >= 58) {
210.     return "Positive" as const;
211.   }
212. 
213.   if (score <= 42) {
214.     return "Negative" as const;
215.   }
216. 
217.   return "Neutral" as const;
218. }
219. 
220. async function fetchText(url: string) {
221.   const response = await fetch(url, {
222.     cache: "no-store",
223.     headers: {
224.       Accept: "text/html,application/xml,application/json",
225.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
226.     }
227.   });
228. 
229.   if (!response.ok) {
230.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
231.   }
232. 
233.   return response.text();
234. }
235. 
236. async function fetchJson<T>(url: string) {
237.   const response = await fetch(url, {
238.     cache: "no-store",
239.     headers: {
240.       Accept: "application/json",
241.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
242.     }
243.   });
244. 
245.   if (!response.ok) {
246.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
247.   }
248. 
249.   return (await response.json()) as T;
250. }
251. 
252. function isNseEquityQuote(quote: YahooSearchQuote): quote is YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string } {
253.   return quote.exchange === "NSI" && quote.quoteType === "EQUITY" && Boolean(quote.symbol);
254. }
255. 
256. function toCompanyLookup(quote: YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string }): CompanyLookup {
257.   const normalizedSymbol = normalizeSearchSymbol(quote.symbol);
258. 
259.   return {
260.     symbol: normalizedSymbol,
261.     yahooSymbol: quote.symbol,
262.     companyName: quote.longname ?? quote.shortname ?? normalizedSymbol,
263.     sector: quote.sectorDisp ?? quote.sector ?? "Unknown",
264.     industry: quote.industryDisp ?? quote.industry ?? undefined
265.   };
266. }
267. 
268. function searchRank(lookup: CompanyLookup, normalizedQuery: string) {
269.   const symbol = lookup.symbol;
270.   const companyName = lookup.companyName.toUpperCase();
271. 
272.   if (symbol === normalizedQuery) {
273.     return 0;
274.   }
275. 
276.   if (symbol.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
277.     return 1;
278.   }
279. 
280.   if (companyName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
281.     return 2;
282.   }
283. 
284.   if (companyName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {
285.     return 3;
286.   }
287. 
288.   return 4;
289. }
290. 
291. async function searchNseQuotes(query: string, limit: number) {
292.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
293. 
294.   if (!normalized) {
295.     return [];
296.   }
297. 
298.   const payload = await fetchJson<YahooSearchResponse>(
299.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}&quotesCount=${Math.max(limit * 2, 8)}&newsCount=0&enableFuzzyQuery=true`
300.   );
301.   const matches = (payload.quotes ?? [])
302.     .filter(isNseEquityQuote)
303.     .map(toCompanyLookup)
304.     .sort((left, right) => {
305.       const leftRank = searchRank(left, normalized);
306.       const rightRank = searchRank(right, normalized);
307. 
308.       if (leftRank !== rightRank) {
309.         return leftRank - rightRank;
310.       }
311. 
312.       return left.symbol.localeCompare(right.symbol);
313.     });
314.   const deduped: CompanyLookup[] = [];
315.   const seen = new Set<string>();
316. 
317.   for (const match of matches) {
318.     if (seen.has(match.symbol)) {
319.       continue;
320.     }
321. 
322.     seen.add(match.symbol);
323.     deduped.push(match);
324. 
325.     if (deduped.length >= limit) {
326.       break;
327.     }
328.   }
329. 
330.   return deduped;
331. }
332. 
333. export async function lookupNseSuggestions(
334.   query: string,
335.   limit = 8
336. ): Promise<StockSearchSuggestion[]> {
337.   return (await searchNseQuotes(query, limit)).map(({ symbol, companyName, sector, industry }) => ({
338.     symbol,
339.     companyName,
340.     sector,
341.     industry
342.   }));
343. }
344. 
345. export async function lookupNseSymbol(query: string): Promise<CompanyLookup | null> {
346.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
347. 
348.   if (!normalized) {
349.     return null;
350.   }
351. 
352.   const matches = await searchNseQuotes(normalized, 6);
353. 
354.   return matches.find((match) => match.symbol === normalized) ?? matches[0] ?? null;
355. }
356. 
357. async function fetchScreenerFundamentals(symbol: string) {
358.   try {
359.     const html = await fetchText(`https://www.screener.in/company/${encodeURIComponent(symbol)}/consolidated/`);
360.     const description = decodeHtmlEntities(
361.       textContent(/<meta name="description" content="([^"]+)"/i, html) ?? ""
362.     );
363. 
364.     if (!description) {
365.       return null;
366.     }
367. 
368.     const marketCapCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Mkt Cap:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Crore/i, description));
369.     const marketCapChange1YPct = parsePercent(
370.       textContent(/Mkt Cap:[^(]+\((?:up|down)\s*([\d.]+)% in 1 year\)/i, description)
371.     );
372.     const revenueCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Revenue:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
373.     const profitCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Profit:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
374.     const salesGrowthLabel = textContent(/company has delivered a ([a-z]+) sales growth/i, description);
375.     const salesGrowth5YPct = parsePercent(textContent(/sales growth of ([\d.]+)% over past five years/i, description));
376.     const returnOnEquityLabel = textContent(/company has a ([a-z]+) return on equity/i, description);
377.     const returnOnEquityPct = parsePercent(textContent(/return on equity of ([\d.]+)%/i, description));
378.     const promoterHoldingPct = parsePercent(textContent(/Promoter Holding:\s*([\d.]+)%/i, description));
379.     const netMarginPct =
380.       revenueCrore && profitCrore ? Number(((profitCrore / revenueCrore) * 100).toFixed(2)) : null;
381. 
382.     return {
383.       source: "Screener.in",
384.       summary: description,
385.       marketCapCrore,
386.       marketCapChange1YPct,
387.       revenueCrore,
388.       profitCrore,
389.       netMarginPct,
390.       salesGrowth5YPct,
391.       salesGrowthLabel,
392.       returnOnEquityPct,
393.       returnOnEquityLabel,
394.       promoterHoldingPct
395.     } satisfies FundamentalSnapshot;
396.   } catch {
397.     return null;
398.   }
399. }
400. 
401. async function fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(companyName: string, symbol: string) {
402.   try {
403.     const query = `"${companyName}" OR ${symbol} NSE stock when:30d`;
404.     const xml = await fetchText(
405.       `https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN:en`
406.     );
407.     const headlines: NewsHeadline[] = [];
408. 
409.     for (const match of xml.matchAll(/<item>([\s\S]*?)<\/item>/g)) {
410.       const item = match[1];
411.       const rawTitle = textContent(/<title>([\s\S]*?)<\/title>/i, item);
412. 
413.       if (!rawTitle) {
414.         continue;
415.       }
416. 
417.       const title = decodeHtmlEntities(rawTitle).replace(/\s+-\s+[^-]+$/, "").trim();
418.       const source = decodeHtmlEntities(textContent(/<source[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/source>/i, item) ?? "Google News");
419.       const publishedAt = textContent(/<pubDate>([\s\S]*?)<\/pubDate>/i, item) ?? "";
420.       const tone = titleTone(title);
421.       const category = headlineCategory(title);
422. 
423.       headlines.push({
424.         title,
425.         source,
426.         publishedAt,
427.         tone,
428.         category
429.       });
430. 
431.       if (headlines.length >= 6) {
432.         break;
433.       }
434.     }
435. 
436.     if (!headlines.length) {
437.       return null;
438.     }
439. 
440.     const score = Number(sentimentScore(headlines).toFixed(2));
441. 
442.     return {
443.       query,
444.       overall: overallSentiment(score),
445.       score,
446.       positiveCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "positive").length,
447.       neutralCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "neutral").length,
448.       negativeCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "negative").length,
449.       earningsMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings").length,
450.       analystMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "analyst").length,
451.       headlines
452.     } satisfies SentimentSnapshot;
453.   } catch {
454.     return null;
455.   }
456. }
457. 
458. export async function fetchCompanyResearch(lookup: CompanyLookup): Promise<CompanyResearch> {
459.   const [fundamentals, sentiment] = await Promise.all([
460.     fetchScreenerFundamentals(lookup.symbol),
461.     fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(lookup.companyName, lookup.symbol)
462.   ]);
463. 
464.   return {
465.     companyName: lookup.companyName,
466.     sector: lookup.sector,
467.     industry: lookup.industry,
468.     fundamentals,
469.     sentiment
470.   };
471. }
472. 
473. export function scoreFundamentals(fundamentals: FundamentalSnapshot | null) {
474.   if (!fundamentals) {
475.     return 50;
476.   }
477. 
478.   const growthScore =
479.     fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct === null
480.       ? 50
481.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 35 + fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct * 3));
482.   const roeScore =
483.     fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct === null
484.       ? 50
485.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct * 3));
486.   const marginScore =
487.     fundamentals.netMarginPct === null
488.       ? 50
489.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 30 + fundamentals.netMarginPct * 2.5));
490.   const promoterScore =
491.     fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct === null
492.       ? 50
493.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct));
494.   const labelBonus =
495.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "strong" || fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "high" ? 7 : 0) +
496.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "high" || fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "good" ? 6 : 0) -
497.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "poor" ? 8 : 0) -
498.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "low" ? 8 : 0);
499. 
500.   return Number(
501.     (
502.       growthScore * 0.3 +
503.       roeScore * 0.35 +
504.       marginScore * 0.15 +
505.       promoterScore * 0.2 +
506.       labelBonus
507.     ).toFixed(2)
508.   );
509. }
510. 
511. export function scoreSentiment(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
512.   return sentiment?.score ?? 50;
513. }
514. 
515. export function scoreEarningsSignal(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
516.   if (!sentiment) {
517.     return 50;
518.   }
519. 
520.   const earningsHeadlines = sentiment.headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings");
521. 
522.   if (!earningsHeadlines.length) {
523.     return 50;
524.   }
525. 
526.   const averageTone =
527.     earningsHeadlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
528.       if (headline.tone === "positive") {
529.         return total + 1;
530.       }
531. 
532.       if (headline.tone === "negative") {
533.         return total - 1;
534.       }
535. 
536.       return total;
537.     }, 0) / earningsHeadlines.length;
538. 
539.   return Number(Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + averageTone * 30)).toFixed(2));
540. }
541. 
542. export function scoreAnalystSignal(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
543.   if (!sentiment) {
544.     return 50;
545.   }
546. 
547.   const analystHeadlines = sentiment.headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "analyst");
548. 
549.   if (!analystHeadlines.length) {
550.     return 50;
551.   }
552. 
553.   const averageTone =
554.     analystHeadlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
555.       if (headline.tone === "positive") {
556.         return total + 1;
557.       }
558. 
559.       if (headline.tone === "negative") {
560.         return total - 1;
561.       }
562. 
563.       return total;
564.     }, 0) / analystHeadlines.length;
565. 
566.   return Number(Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + averageTone * 35)).toFixed(2));
567. }
568.
```

</details>

214.           return (
215.             <article key={`${title}-${signal.name}`} className="signal-tile">
216.               <span className="signal-name">{signal.name}</span>
217.               <strong>{signal.value}</strong>
218.             </article>
219.           );
220.         })}
221.       </div>
222.     </section>
223.   );
224. }
225. 
226. function driverImpactLabel(impact: AnalysisDriver["impact"]) {
227.   switch (impact) {
228.     case "positive":
229.       return "Support";
230.     case "negative":
231.       return "Caution";
232.     default:
233.       return "Neutral";
234.   }
235. }
236. 
237. function driverImpactTone(impact: AnalysisDriver["impact"]) {
238.   switch (impact) {
239.     case "positive":
240.       return "success";
241.     case "negative":
242.       return "danger";
243.     default:
244.       return "neutral";
245.   }
246. }
247. 
248. export function ReasoningPanel({
249.   panelId,
250.   title,
251.   subtitle,
252.   drivers,
253.   tone,
254.   info,
255.   active = false
256. }: {
257.   panelId: string;
258.   title: string;
259.   subtitle: string;
260.   drivers?: AnalysisDriver[];
261.   tone: AccentTone;
262.   info?: string;
263.   active?: boolean;
264. }) {
265.   if (!drivers?.length) {
266.     return null;
267.   }
268. 
269.   return (
270.     <section className={`card category-panel tone-${tone} reasoning-panel${active ? " active" : ""}`} id={panelId}>
271.       <div className="panel-title">
272.         <div className="panel-title-heading">
273.           <span className="panel-kicker">{title}</span>
274.           {info ? <InfoTooltip label={`${title} information`} content={info} /> : null}
275.         </div>
276.         <h3>{title}</h3>
277.         <p>{subtitle}</p>
278.       </div>
279.       <div className="reasoning-list">
280.         {drivers.map((driver) => (
281.           <article key={`${panelId}-${driver.title}`} className="reasoning-item">
282.             <div className="reasoning-item-top">
283.               <strong>{driver.title}</strong>
284.               <span className={`result-chip ${driverImpactTone(driver.impact)}`}>
285.                 {driverImpactLabel(driver.impact)}
286.               </span>
287.             </div>
288.             <p>{driver.detail}</p>
289.           </article>
290.         ))}
291.       </div>
292.     </section>
293.   );
294. }
295. 
296. export function EmptyState({ message }: { message: string }) {
297.   return <div className="empty-state">{message}</div>;
298. }
299. 
300. export function DailyPerformanceCard({ item }: { item: DailyPerformance }) {
301.   const tone = successRateTone(item.successRate);
302. 
303.   return (
304.     <article className={`performance-card ${tone}`}>
305.       <div className="performance-card-top">
306.         <div>
307.           <span className="panel-kicker">Trading day</span>
308.           <h3>{formatDate(item.batchDate)}</h3>
309.         </div>
310.         <span className={`result-chip ${tone}`}>
311.           {item.successRate === null
312.             ? "Pending"
313.             : item.successRate >= 60
314.               ? "Strong"
315.               : item.successRate < 40
316.                 ? "Weak"
317.                 : "Mixed"}
318.         </span>
319.       </div>
320.
```

</details>

356. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
357.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
358.   const targetVisibleIdeas = 10;
359. 
360.   useEffect(() => {
361.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
362.       setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
363.     }
364.   }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);
365. 
366.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
367.   const profileLabel = activeProfile?.label ?? "Single-day";
368.   const sourceProvider = data.dataSource?.provider ?? "Live recommendation loader";
369.   const watchlistName = compactUniverseLabel(data.universe);
370.   const liveStatus = liveStatusMeta(data.dataSource?.mode);
371. 
372.   const localSearchSuggestions = useMemo(
373.     () =>
374.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => ({
375.         symbol: stock.symbol,
376.         companyName: stock.companyName,
377.         sector: stock.sector,
378.         industry: stock.industry
379.       })),
380.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations]
381.   );
382. 
383.   const recommendationBuckets = useMemo(() => {
384.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
385.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
386.     );
387.     const actionable = sorted.filter(
388.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) !== "avoid"
389.     );
390.     const tradableCount = actionable.filter(
391.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) === "consider"
392.     ).length;
393.     const watchCount = actionable.filter(
394.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) === "watch"
395.     ).length;
396. 
397.     return {
398.       visibleRecommendations: actionable.slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas),
399.       tradableCount,
400.       watchCount
401.     };
402.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);
403.   const visibleRecommendations = recommendationBuckets.visibleRecommendations;
404.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string | null>(visibleRecommendations[0]?.symbol ?? null);
405. 
406.   useEffect(() => {
407.     if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {
408.       setSelectedSymbol(null);
409.       return;
410.     }
411. 
412.     const searchedSymbol = searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol;
413.     const searchedVisible = searchedSymbol
414.       ? visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === searchedSymbol)?.symbol
415.       : null;
416. 
417.     if (!selectedSymbol || !visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {
418.       setSelectedSymbol(searchedVisible ?? visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);
419.     }
420.   }, [searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol, selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);
421. 
422.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo(
423.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
424.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
425.   );
426.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
427.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
428.     [dailyPerformance]
429.   );
430. 
431.   const spotlightStock =
432.     visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? visibleRecommendations[0];
433.   const spotlightPlan = spotlightStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
434.   const spotlightConfidence = spotlightPlan ? confidenceMeta(spotlightPlan, activeHorizon) : null;
435.   const spotlightTrend = trendMeta(spotlightStock?.latestSessionChangePct);
436.   const spotlightRisk = spotlightPlan ? riskRewardMeta(spotlightPlan.riskReward) : null;
437. 
438.   const recommendedCount = recommendationBuckets.tradableCount;
439.   const watchCount = recommendationBuckets.watchCount;
440. 
441.   const quickSearchSymbols = useMemo(() => {
442.     const items = [
443.       searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol,
444.       ...visibleRecommendations.slice(0, 4).map((stock) => stock.symbol)
445.     ].filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value));
446. 
447.     return [...new Set(items)].slice(0, 5);
448.   }, [searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol, visibleRecommendations]);
449. 
450.   const contextItems = [
451.     { label: "Exchange", value: data.exchange },
452.     { label: "Watchlist", value: watchlistName },
453.     { label: "Horizon", value: profileLabel },
454.     { label: "Data source", value: sourceProvider },
455.     { label: "Last updated", value: formatDateTime(data.currentBatch.generatedAt) },
456.     { label: "Status", value: liveStatus.label, tone: liveStatus.tone }
457.   ];
458. 
459.   const overviewCards = [
460.     {
461.       icon: "EX",
462.       label: "Exchange",
463.       tooltip: "The active market being screened in this dashboard.",
464.       value: data.exchange,
465.       footnote: watchlistName
466.     },
467.     {
468.       icon: "HZ",
469.       label: "Active horizon",
470.       tooltip: "The recommendation window controlling score thresholds and trade plans.",
471.       value: profileLabel,
472.       footnote: activeProfile?.window ?? "Model profile"
473.     },
474.     {
475.       icon: "WL",
476.       label: "Watchlist stocks",
477.       tooltip: "The shortlist shown in the live table for fast scanning.",
478.       value: formatNumber(visibleRecommendations.length),
479.       footnote: `${recommendedCount} tradable · ${watchCount} watch`
480.     },
481.     {
482.       icon: "AN",
483.       label: "Analyzed stocks",
484.       tooltip: "Names processed in the current recommendation batch.",
485.       value: formatNumber(data.currentBatch.recommendations.length),
486.       footnote: data.currentBatch.batchDate
487.     },
488.     {
489.       icon: "AR",
490.       label: "Avg return",
491.       tooltip: "Average return across completed recommendation batches.",
492.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct),
493.       footnote: `Success ${formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate)}`
494.     }
495.   ];
496. 
497.   const archiveSuccessRate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate;
498.   const archiveReturn = performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct;
499.   const latestCompletedDate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.batchDate
500.     ? formatDate(performanceSummary.latestCompleted.batchDate)
501.     : "No closed batch yet";
502. 
503.   return (
504.     <main className="shell dashboard-redesign-shell">
505.       <section className="dashboard-context-bar" aria-label="Active dashboard context">
506.         {contextItems.map((item) => (
507.           <div className="dashboard-context-item" key={item.label}>
508.             <span className="dashboard-context-label">{item.label}</span>
509.             <strong className={item.tone ? `dashboard-context-value ${item.tone}` : "dashboard-context-value"}>
510.               {item.value}
511.             </strong>
512.           </div>
513.         ))}
514.       </section>
515. 
516.       <section className="dashboard-hero">
517.         <div className="dashboard-hero-copy">
518.           <span className="dashboard-mini-label">NSE equities workspace</span>
519.           <h1>Scan ideas, compare trade plans, and act fast.</h1>
520.           <p>
```

</details>

1810. ): PlanTemplate {
1811.   const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
1812.   const technicalBreakdown = technicalScoreBreakdown(metrics, entry, horizon);
1813.   const rawScore = blendedModelScore(entry, metrics, horizon, technicalBreakdown.technicalScore);
1814.   const technicalScore = technicalBreakdown.technicalScore;
1815.   const chartScore = technicalBreakdown.chartScore;
1816.   const factorScore = technicalBreakdown.factorScore;
1817.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
1818.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1819.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1820.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1821.   const riskFeedback = stopLossFragilityFeedback(metrics, horizon, learning);
1822.   const score = roundMetric(clamp(rawScore - riskFeedback.penalty, 0, 100));
1823.   const atrPct = metrics.atrPct ?? (settings.targetRangePct[0] * 0.45);
1824.   const entryPrice = roundPrice(metrics.currentPrice);
1825.   const targetPct = Math.max(
1826.     projectedPct(score, settings.targetRangePct),
1827.     atrPct * settings.atrTargetMultiple
1828.   );
1829.   const stopPct = Math.max(
1830.     projectedPct(100 - score, settings.stopRangePct),
1831.     atrPct * settings.atrStopMultiple
1832.   );
1833.   const targetPrice = roundPrice(entryPrice * (1 + targetPct / 100));
1834.   const stopLoss = roundPrice(Math.max(entryPrice * (1 - stopPct / 100), entryPrice * 0.7));
1835.   const expectedReturnPct = pctChange(targetPrice, entryPrice) ?? 0;
1836.   const riskReward = roundMetric((targetPrice - entryPrice) / Math.max(entryPrice - stopLoss, 0.01));
1837.   const conviction = convictionFromScore(score);
1838.   const strengthLabel = horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics, horizon);
1839.   const returnLabel = horizonMomentumLabel(metrics, horizon);
1840.   const summaryBase = horizonSummary(entry, metrics, horizon);
1841.   const summary =
1842.     riskFeedback.reasons.length && riskFeedback.penalty > 0
1843.       ? `${summaryBase} Risk controls trimmed the setup because ${riskFeedback.reasons[0]}.`
1844.       : summaryBase;
1845.   const chartLabel =
1846.     (horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && (metrics.breakout20Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1847.       ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)} versus the recent 20-day high`
1848.       : horizon === "position" && (metrics.breakout55Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1849.         ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)} versus the recent 55-day high`
1850.         : horizon === "long_term" && (metrics.breakout120Pct ?? -1) >= 0
1851.           ? `price is trading ${percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)} versus the recent 120-day high`
1852.           : `price has not cleanly cleared the recent breakout zone yet`;
1853. 
1854.   const analysisDrivers = [
1855.     ...buildTechnicalAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon),
1856.     ...buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry),
1857.     ...buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(entry),
1858.     ...buildEarningsAnalysisDrivers(entry, earningsScore),
1859.     ...buildAnalystAnalysisDrivers(entry, analystScore),
1860.     ...buildRiskAnalysisDrivers(metrics, horizon, riskFeedback, entryPrice, stopLoss, riskReward)
1861.   ];
1862.   const drivers = analysisDrivers.map((driver) => `${driver.title}: ${driver.detail}`).slice(0, 8);
1863. 
1864.   const technicalSignals: Signal[] = [
1865.     { name: "Adjusted score", value: signalScoreText(score) },
1866.     { name: "Raw blended score", value: signalScoreText(rawScore) },
1867.     { name: "Technical score", value: signalScoreText(technicalScore) },
1868.     { name: "Factor trend score", value: signalScoreText(factorScore) },
1869.     { name: "Chart structure score", value: signalScoreText(chartScore) },
1870.     {
1871.       name: horizonMomentumSignalName(horizon),
1872.       value: returnLabel
1873.     },
1874.     {
1875.       name: horizonRelativeStrengthSignalName(horizon),
1876.       value: strengthLabel
1877.     },
1878.     {
1879.       name: "Price vs 50DMA",
1880.       value: metrics.sma50 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma50)) : "n/a"
1881.     },
1882.     {
1883.       name: "Price vs 200DMA",
1884.       value: metrics.sma200 ? percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma200)) : "n/a"
1885.     },
1886.     {
1887.       name: "Candle close location",
1888.       value: metrics.closeLocationPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.closeLocationPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1889.     },
1890.     {
1891.       name: "Candle body strength",
1892.       value: metrics.candleBodyPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.candleBodyPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1893.     },
1894.     {
1895.       name:
1896.         horizon === "single_day"
1897.           ? "20D breakout"
1898.           : horizon === "swing"
1899.             ? "20D breakout"
1900.             : horizon === "position"
1901.               ? "55D breakout"
1902.               : "120D breakout",
1903.       value:
1904.         horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing"
1905.           ? percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct)
1906.           : horizon === "position"
1907.             ? percentText(metrics.breakout55Pct)
1908.             : percentText(metrics.breakout120Pct)
1909.     },
1910.     {
1911.       name: "Range compression (5D/20D)",
1912.       value: metrics.rangeCompression5v20 !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.rangeCompression5v20).toFixed(2)}x` : "n/a"
1913.     }

2012. function applyRanking(recommendations: StockAnalysis[]) {
2013.   const ranked = recommendations.map((recommendation) => ({
2014.     ...recommendation,
2015.     profiles: {
2016.       single_day: { ...recommendation.profiles.single_day },
2017.       swing: { ...recommendation.profiles.swing },
2018.       position: { ...recommendation.profiles.position },
2019.       long_term: { ...recommendation.profiles.long_term }
2020.     }
2021.   }));
2022. 
2023.   for (const horizon of HORIZON_ORDER) {
2024.     const settings = HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon];
2025.     const ordered = [...ranked].sort(
2026.       (left, right) =>
2027.         (right.profiles[horizon].score ?? right.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct) -
2028.         (left.profiles[horizon].score ?? left.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct)
2029.     );
2030.     const thresholdCount = ordered.filter(
2031.       (stock, index) =>
2032.         index < settings.recommendationCount &&
2033.         (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore
2034.     ).length;
2035.     const forcedCount = Math.min(settings.minimumRecommendationCount, ordered.length);
2036. 
2037.     ordered.forEach((stock, index) => {
2038.       stock.profiles[horizon].rank = index + 1;
2039.       stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended =
2040.         (index < settings.recommendationCount &&
2041.           (stock.profiles[horizon].score ?? 0) >= settings.minimumScore) ||
2042.         (thresholdCount < forcedCount && index < forcedCount);
2043.     });
2044.   }
2045. 
2046.   return ranked;
2047. }

2310. function toHistoricalRecommendation(
2311.   recommendation: StockAnalysis,
2312.   batchDate: string,
2313.   seriesBySymbol: Map<string, MarketSeries>
2314. ): HistoricalStockRecommendation {
2315.   const series = seriesBySymbol.get(recommendation.symbol);

2318.     throw new Error(`Missing series for ${recommendation.symbol}`);
2319.   }
2320. 
2321.   const buildHistoricalPlan = (horizon: HorizonId): HistoricalRecommendationPlan => {
2322.     const livePlan = recommendation.profiles[horizon];
2323. 
2324.     return {
2325.       score: livePlan.score,
2326.       rank: livePlan.rank,
2327.       isRecommended: livePlan.isRecommended,

2330.       targetPrice: livePlan.targetPrice,
2331.       stopLoss: livePlan.stopLoss,
2332.       summary: livePlan.summary,
2333.       outcome: evaluateOutcome(
2334.         series,
2335.         batchDate,

2345.     symbol: recommendation.symbol,

2349.       single_day: buildHistoricalPlan("single_day"),

2357. function historicalBatchDates(benchmark: MarketSeries) {
2358.   return benchmark.bars
2359.     .slice(-(HISTORY_BATCH_COUNT + 1), -1)
2360.     .map((bar) => bar.date)
2361.     .filter((date) => date !== benchmark.bars.at(-1)?.date);
2362. }
2363. 
2364. function buildHistoricalBatches(liveSeries: MarketSeries[], benchmark: MarketSeries) {
2365.   const seriesBySymbol = new Map(liveSeries.map((series) => [series.symbol, series]));
2366. 
2367.   return historicalBatchDates(benchmark).map((date) => {
2368.     const recommendations = buildCurrentRecommendationsForDate(date, liveSeries, benchmark).map(
2369.       (recommendation) => toHistoricalRecommendation(recommendation, date, seriesBySymbol)
2370.     );
2371. 
2372.     return {
2373.       batchDate: date,
2374.       publishedAt: publishedTimestamp(date),
2375.       recommendations
2376.     } satisfies HistoricalBatch;
2377.   });
2378. }

2402. function buildSearchStockForDate(
2403.   date: string,
2404.   series: MarketSeries,
2405.   benchmark: MarketSeries,
2406.   learningMap?: StopLossLearningMap
2407. ) {
2408.   const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
2409. 
2410.   if (index === undefined) {
2411.     return null;
2412.   }
2413. 
2414.   const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
2415. 
2416.   if (!metrics) {
2417.     return null;
2418.   }
2419. 
2420.   const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
2421.   const buildSearchPlan = (horizon: HorizonId) => {
2422.     const plan = buildTradePlan(
2423.       enrichedMetrics,
2424.       series,
2425.       horizon,
2426.       learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.[horizon] ?? null
2427.     );
2428. 
2429.     return {
2430.       ...plan,
2431.       rank: 1,
2432.       isRecommended: (plan.score ?? 0) >= HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].minimumScore
2433.     };
2434.   };
2435. 
2436.   return {
2437.     symbol: series.symbol,
2438.     companyName: series.companyName,
2439.     sector: series.sector,
2440.     industry: series.industry,
2441.     marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
2442.     liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
2443.     currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
2444.     latestSessionChangePct: enrichedMetrics.sessionChangePct,
2445.     fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
2446.     sentiment: series.sentiment,
2447.     profiles: {
2448.       single_day: buildSearchPlan("single_day"),
2449.       swing: buildSearchPlan("swing"),
2450.       position: buildSearchPlan("position"),
2451.       long_term: buildSearchPlan("long_term")
2452.     }
2453.   } satisfies StockAnalysis;
2454. }
2455. 
2456. function searchVerdict(stock: StockAnalysis) {
2457.   const recommendedHorizons = HORIZON_ORDER.filter((horizon) => stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended);
2458.   const focusHorizon =
2459.     recommendedHorizons[0] ??
2460.     [...HORIZON_ORDER].sort(
2461.       (left, right) =>
2462.         (stock.profiles[right].score ?? stock.profiles[right].expectedReturnPct) -
2463.         (stock.profiles[left].score ?? stock.profiles[left].expectedReturnPct)
2464.     )[0];
2465.   const focusPlan = stock.profiles[focusHorizon];
2466.   const positiveReason = focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.impact === "positive");
2467.   const cautionReason =
2468.     focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.area === "risk" && driver.impact === "negative") ??
2469.     focusPlan.analysisDrivers?.find((driver) => driver.impact === "negative");
2470. 
2471.   if (!recommendedHorizons.length) {
2472.     return {
2473.       recommendedHorizons,
2474.       shouldConsider: false,
2475.       verdict: cautionReason
2476.         ? `Do not prioritize this stock right now. The biggest drag is ${cautionReason.title.toLowerCase()}: ${cautionReason.detail}`
2477.         : "Do not prioritize this stock right now. Its current technical, fundamental, and sentiment mix is below the recommendation threshold across all supported horizons."
2478.     };
2479.   }
2480. 
2481.   const labels = recommendedHorizons.map((horizon) => HORIZON_SETTINGS[horizon].label.toLowerCase());
2482. 
2483.   return {
2484.     recommendedHorizons,
2485.     shouldConsider: true,
2486.     verdict: positiveReason
2487.       ? `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(", ")} setups. The main support is ${positiveReason.title.toLowerCase()}: ${positiveReason.detail}${cautionReason ? ` Main caution: ${cautionReason.detail}` : ""}`
2488.       : `Consider this stock for ${labels.join(", ")} setups. The current score clears the model threshold for those horizons.`
2489.   };
2490. }

1642. function blendedModelScore(
1643.   entry: MarketSeries,
1644.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
1645.   horizon: HorizonId,
1646.   precomputedTechnicalScore?: number
1647. ) {
1648.   const technicalScore =
1649.     precomputedTechnicalScore ?? technicalScoreBreakdown(metrics, entry, horizon).technicalScore;
1650.   const fundamentalScore = scoreFundamentals(entry.fundamentals ?? null);
1651.   const sentimentScore = scoreSentiment(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1652.   const earningsScore = scoreEarningsSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1653.   const analystScore = scoreAnalystSignal(entry.sentiment ?? null);
1654. 
1655.   switch (horizon) {
1656.     case "single_day":
1657.       return roundMetric(
1658.         technicalScore * 0.8 +
1659.           fundamentalScore * 0.04 +
1660.           sentimentScore * 0.08 +
1661.           earningsScore * 0.04 +
1662.           analystScore * 0.04
1663.       );
1664.     case "swing":
1665.       return roundMetric(
1666.         technicalScore * 0.72 +
1667.           fundamentalScore * 0.08 +
1668.           sentimentScore * 0.1 +
1669.           earningsScore * 0.05 +
1670.           analystScore * 0.05
1671.       );
1672.     case "position":
1673.       return roundMetric(
1674.         technicalScore * 0.6 +
1675.           fundamentalScore * 0.18 +
1676.           sentimentScore * 0.1 +
1677.           earningsScore * 0.06 +
1678.           analystScore * 0.06
1679.       );
1680.     case "long_term":
1681.       return roundMetric(
1682.         technicalScore * 0.52 +
1683.           fundamentalScore * 0.24 +
1684.           sentimentScore * 0.08 +
1685.           earningsScore * 0.08 +
1686.           analystScore * 0.08
1687.       );
1688.   }
1689. }
1690. 
1691. function projectedPct(score: number, [minPct, maxPct]: [number, number]) {
1692.   const normalizedScore = scaleRange(score, 45, 85);
1693.   return minPct + (maxPct - minPct) * normalizedScore;
1694. }
1695. 
1696. function stopLossFragilityFeedback(
1697.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
1698.   horizon: HorizonId,
1699.   learning: StopLossLearning | null
1700. ) {
1701.   let fragilityPenalty = 0;
1702.   const reasons: string[] = [];
1703.   const signals: Signal[] = [];
1704. 
1705.   if ((metrics.volumeRatio ?? 1) < 0.95) {
1706.     fragilityPenalty += horizon === "single_day" ? 1.8 : 1.2;
1707.     reasons.push("volume confirmation is weak");
1708.     signals.push({ name: "Volume confirmation", value: ratioText(metrics.volumeRatio) });
1709.   }
1710. 
1711.   if ((metrics.volatility20 ?? 0) > (horizon === "single_day" ? 30 : horizon === "swing" ? 38 : 44)) {
1712.     fragilityPenalty += horizon === "single_day" ? 2.2 : 1.6;
1713.     reasons.push("volatility is elevated");
1714.     signals.push({ name: "20D volatility", value: percentText(metrics.volatility20) });
1715.   }
1716. 
1717.   if ((metrics.rsi14 ?? 50) > 72) {
1718.     fragilityPenalty += 1.4;
1719.     reasons.push("RSI is stretched");
1720.     signals.push({
1721.       name: "RSI stretch",
1722.       value: metrics.rsi14 !== null ? roundMetric(metrics.rsi14).toFixed(1) : "n/a"
1723.     });
1724.   }
1725. 
1726.   if ((horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && metrics.sma20 && metrics.currentPrice < metrics.sma20) {
1727.     fragilityPenalty += 1.8;
1728.     reasons.push("price is below 20DMA support");
1729.     signals.push({
1730.       name: "Price vs 20DMA",
1731.       value: percentText(pctChange(metrics.currentPrice, metrics.sma20))
1732.     });
1733.   }
1734. 
1735.   if ((metrics.relativeStrength20 ?? 0) < 0) {
1736.     fragilityPenalty += horizon === "single_day" ? 1.8 : 1.2;
1737.     reasons.push("short-term relative strength is negative");
1738.     signals.push({
1739.       name: "Short-term relative strength",
1740.       value: percentText(metrics.relativeStrength20)
1741.     });
1742.   }
1743. 
1744.   if ((metrics.upperWickPct ?? 0) > 45) {
1745.     fragilityPenalty += horizon === "single_day" ? 1.8 : 1.2;
1746.     reasons.push("the latest candle was rejected near resistance");
1747.     signals.push({
1748.       name: "Upper wick",
1749.       value: metrics.upperWickPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.upperWickPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1750.     });
1751.   }
1752. 
1753.   if ((metrics.closeLocationPct ?? 50) < 42) {
1754.     fragilityPenalty += 1.4;
1755.     reasons.push("the latest close finished in the lower half of the range");
1756.     signals.push({
1757.       name: "Close location",
1758.       value: metrics.closeLocationPct !== null ? `${roundMetric(metrics.closeLocationPct).toFixed(1)}%` : "n/a"
1759.     });
1760.   }
1761. 
1762.   if ((horizon === "single_day" || horizon === "swing") && (metrics.breakout20Pct ?? 0) < 0) {
1763.     fragilityPenalty += 1.6;
1764.     reasons.push("price is still below recent resistance");
1765.     signals.push({ name: "20D breakout", value: percentText(metrics.breakout20Pct) });
1766.   }
1767. 
1768.   if ((horizon === "position" || horizon === "long_term") && (metrics.sma50Slope10Pct ?? 0) < 0) {
1769.     fragilityPenalty += 1.5;
1770.     reasons.push("trend slope is flattening");
1771.     signals.push({ name: "50DMA slope", value: percentText(metrics.sma50Slope10Pct) });
1772.   }
1773. 
1774.   if (horizon === "single_day" && (metrics.sessionChangePct ?? 0) < 0) {
1775.     fragilityPenalty += 1.8;
1776.     reasons.push("the latest session faded into the close");
1777.     signals.push({ name: "Latest session", value: percentText(metrics.sessionChangePct) });
1778.   }
1779. 
1780.   const learningPenalty = learning?.penalty ?? 0;
1781.   const totalPenalty = roundMetric(clamp(fragilityPenalty + learningPenalty, 0, 15));
1782.   const learningNote =
1783.     learning?.note ??
1784.     (learningPenalty
1785.       ? `Recent ${profileFor(horizon).label.toLowerCase()} stop-loss history increases caution.`
1786.       : null);
1787. 
1788.   return {
1789.     penalty: totalPenalty,
1790.     reasons: learningNote ? [...reasons, learningNote] : reasons,
1791.     signals: [
1792.       { name: "Fragility penalty", value: `${roundMetric(fragilityPenalty).toFixed(1)} pts` },
1793.       { name: "Learning penalty", value: `${roundMetric(learningPenalty).toFixed(1)} pts` },
1794.       ...(learning
1795.         ? [
1796.             { name: "Recent stop-losses", value: `${learning.recentStopLosses}` },
1797.             { name: "Quick stop-losses", value: `${learning.quickStopLosses}` }
1798.           ]
1799.         : []),
1800.       ...signals
1801.     ].slice(0, 6)
1802.   };
1803. }

1806.   metrics: SnapshotMetrics,
1807.   entry: MarketSeries,
1808.   horizon: HorizonId,
1809.   learning: StopLossLearning | null = null
1810. ): PlanTemplate {

2050.   date: string,
2051.   liveSeries: MarketSeries[],
2052.   benchmark: MarketSeries,
2053.   learningMap?: StopLossLearningMap
2054. ) {
2055.   const recommendations = liveSeries
2056.     .map((series) => {
2057.       const index = series.indexByDate.get(date);
2058. 
2059.       if (index === undefined) {
2060.         return null;
2061.       }
2062. 
2063.       const metrics = snapshotMetrics(series, benchmark, index);
2064. 
2065.       if (!metrics) {
2066.         return null;
2067.       }
2068. 
2069.       const enrichedMetrics = enrichRelativeStrength(metrics);
2070. 
2071.       return {
2072.         symbol: series.symbol,
2073.         companyName: series.companyName,
2074.         sector: series.sector,
2075.         industry: series.industry,
2076.         marketCapBucket: series.marketCapBucket,
2077.         liquidityTier: series.liquidityTier,
2078.         currentMarketPrice: roundPrice(enrichedMetrics.currentPrice),
2079.         latestSessionChangePct: enrichedMetrics.sessionChangePct,
2080.         fundamentals: series.fundamentals,
2081.         sentiment: series.sentiment,
2082.         profiles: {
2083.           single_day: buildTradePlan(
2084.             enrichedMetrics,
2085.             series,
2086.             "single_day",
2087.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.single_day ?? null
2088.           ),
2089.           swing: buildTradePlan(
2090.             enrichedMetrics,
2091.             series,
2092.             "swing",
2093.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.swing ?? null
2094.           ),
2095.           position: buildTradePlan(
2096.             enrichedMetrics,
2097.             series,
2098.             "position",
2099.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.position ?? null
2100.           ),
2101.           long_term: buildTradePlan(
2102.             enrichedMetrics,
2103.             series,
2104.             "long_term",
2105.             learningMap?.get(series.symbol)?.long_term ?? null
2106.           )
2107.         }
2108.       } satisfies StockAnalysis;
2109.     })
2110.     .filter((recommendation): recommendation is StockAnalysis => recommendation !== null);
2111. 
2112.   return applyRanking(recommendations);
2113. }
2114. 
2115. function resolveOutcomeResult(
2116.   openPrice: number,
2117.   targetPrice: number,
2118.   stopLoss: number,
2119.   targetHit: boolean,
2120.   stopHit: boolean
2121. ): OutcomeResult {
2122.   if (targetHit && stopHit) {
2123.     return Math.abs(openPrice - stopLoss) <= Math.abs(targetPrice - openPrice)
2124.       ? "stop_loss_hit"
2125.       : "target_hit";
2126.   }
2127. 
2128.   return targetHit ? "target_hit" : "stop_loss_hit";
2129. }
2130. 
2131. function volumeContextText(currentVolume: number, baselineVolume: number | null) {
2132.   if (baselineVolume === null || !Number.isFinite(baselineVolume) || baselineVolume <= 0) {
2133.     return "";
2134.   }
2135. 
2136.   if (currentVolume >= baselineVolume * 1.25) {
2137.     return " on above-average volume";
2138.   }
2139. 
2140.   if (currentVolume <= baselineVolume * 0.75) {
2141.     return " on light volume";
2142.   }
2143. 
2144.   return " on normal volume";
2145. }
```

</details>

680. 
681.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : 10;
682. })();
683. 
684. function roundMetric(value: number) {
685.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
686. }
687. 
688. function roundPrice(value: number) {
689.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
690. }
691. 
692. function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number) {
693.   return Math.min(Math.max(value, min), max);
694. }
695. 
696. function average(values: number[]) {
697.   if (!values.length) {
698.     return null;
699.   }
700. 
701.   return values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / values.length;
702. }
703. 
704. function minimumLiveSymbolsRequired() {
705.   return Math.max(25, Math.floor(MARKET_UNIVERSE.length * MINIMUM_LIVE_COVERAGE_RATIO));
706. }
707. 
708. async function mapWithConcurrency<T, TResult>(
709.   items: T[],
710.   concurrency: number,
711.   mapper: (item: T, index: number) => Promise<TResult>
712. ) {
713.   const results = new Array<TResult>(items.length);
714.   let nextIndex = 0;
715. 
716.   async function worker() {
717.     while (true) {
718.       const index = nextIndex;
719.       nextIndex += 1;
720. 
721.       if (index >= items.length) {
722.         return;
723.       }
724. 
725.       results[index] = await mapper(items[index], index);
726.     }
727.   }
728. 
729.   const workerCount = Math.max(1, Math.min(concurrency, items.length));
730.   await Promise.all(Array.from({ length: workerCount }, () => worker()));
731. 
732.   return results;
733. }
734. 
735. function pctChange(current: number, previous: number) {
736.   if (!Number.isFinite(current) || !Number.isFinite(previous) || previous === 0) {
737.     return null;
738.   }
739. 
740.   return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100;
741. }
742. 
743. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
744.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
745.     return 0.5;
746.   }
747. 
748.   if (max === min) {
749.     return 0.5;
750.   }
751. 
752.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
753. }
754. 
755. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
756.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
757. }
758. 
759. function percentText(value: number | null) {
760.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
761.     return "n/a";
762.   }
763. 
764.   const prefix = value > 0 ? "+" : "";
765.   return `${prefix}${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
766. }
767. 
768. function ratioText(value: number | null) {
769.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
770.     return "n/a";
771.   }
772. 
773.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}x`;
774. }
775. 
776. function priceText(value: number | null) {
777.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
778.     return "n/a";
779.   }
780. 
781.   return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;
782. }
783. 
784. function compactNumber(value: number | null) {
785.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
786.     return "n/a";
787.   }
788. 
789.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
790.     notation: "compact",
791.     maximumFractionDigits: 1
792.   }).format(value);
793. }
794. 
795. function croreText(value: number | null) {
796.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
797.     return "n/a";
798.   }
799. 
800.   return `${new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
801.     maximumFractionDigits: 0
802.   }).format(value)} Cr`;
803. }
804. 
805. function marketCapBucketFromCrore(value: number | null) {
806.   if (value === null) {
807.     return "Large Cap";
808.   }
809. 
810.   if (value >= 100000) {
811.     return "Large Cap";
812.   }
813. 
814.   if (value >= 30000) {
815.     return "Mid Cap";
816.   }
817. 
818.   return "Small Cap";
819. }
820. 
821. function liquidityTierFromVolume(volume: number | null) {
822.   if (volume === null) {
823.     return "Tier 2";
824.   }
825. 
826.   if (volume >= 5000000) {
827.     return "Tier 1";
828.   }
829. 
830.   if (volume >= 1000000) {
831.     return "Tier 2";
832.   }
833. 
834.   return "Tier 3";
835. }
836. 
837. function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {
838.   if (values.length < period) {
839.     return null;
840.   }
841. 
842.   return average(values.slice(-period));
843. }
844. 
845. function annualizedVolatility(closes: number[], period: number) {
846.   if (closes.length < period + 1) {
847.     return null;
848.   }
849. 
850.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
851.   const returns = window.slice(1).map((close, index) => (close - window[index]) / window[index]);
852.   const mean = average(returns);
853. 
854.   if (mean === null) {
855.     return null;
856.   }
857. 
858.   const variance =
859.     returns.reduce((total, value) => total + (value - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;
860. 
861.   return Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100;
862. }
863. 
864. function relativeStrengthIndex(closes: number[], period: number) {
865.   if (closes.length <= period) {
866.     return null;
867.   }
868. 
869.   let gains = 0;
870.   let losses = 0;
871.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
872. 
873.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
874.     const change = window[index] - window[index - 1];
875. 
876.     if (change > 0) {
877.       gains += change;
878.     } else {
879.       losses += Math.abs(change);
880.     }
881.   }
882. 
883.   if (losses === 0) {
884.     return 100;
885.   }
886. 
887.   const averageGain = gains / period;
888.   const averageLoss = losses / period;
889.   const relativeStrength = averageGain / averageLoss;
890. 
891.   return 100 - 100 / (1 + relativeStrength);
892. }
893. 
894. function averageTrueRange(bars: PriceBar[], period: number) {
895.   if (bars.length <= period) {
896.     return null;
897.   }
898. 
899.   const window = bars.slice(-(period + 1));
900.   const trueRanges: number[] = [];
```

401.   const relativeStrength = horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon);

414.     } else {
415.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;
416.     }

426. function buildFundamentalAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries): AnalysisDriver[] {
427.   if (!entry.fundamentals) {
428.     return [
429.       {
430.         area: "fundamental",
431.         impact: "neutral",
432.         title: "Fundamental coverage",
433.         detail:
434.           "Live fundamental fields were not available for this stock today, so the recommendation is leaning more heavily on chart structure, liquidity, and risk controls than on business-quality data."
435.       }
436.     ];
437.   }
438. 
439.   const growth = entry.fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct;
440.   const roe = entry.fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct;
441.   const promoterHolding = entry.fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct;
442.   const netMargin = entry.fundamentals.netMarginPct;
443. 
444.   let qualityImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
445.   let qualityDetail =
446.     "Fundamental quality is mixed, so the business backdrop is not strongly amplifying or blocking the technical setup.";
447. 
448.   if ((growth ?? 0) >= 12 || (roe ?? 0) >= 15) {
449.     qualityImpact = "positive";
450.     qualityDetail = `The business shows ${growth !== null ? `${roundMetric(growth).toFixed(2)}% five-year sales growth` : "stable sales growth"} and ${roe !== null ? `${roundMetric(roe).toFixed(2)}% ROE` : "reasonable returns on equity"}. That matters because strong growth plus healthy returns makes technical strength more credible over longer holding periods.`;
451.   } else if ((growth !== null && growth < 5) || (roe !== null && roe < 10)) {
452.     qualityImpact = "negative";
453.     qualityDetail = `The business only shows ${growth !== null ? `${roundMetric(growth).toFixed(2)}% five-year sales growth` : "limited visible growth"} and ${roe !== null ? `${roundMetric(roe).toFixed(2)}% ROE` : "unclear returns on equity"}. That matters because weak operating quality makes price breakouts more vulnerable to mean reversion.`;
454.   }
455. 
456.   let ownershipImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
457.   let ownershipDetail =
458.     "Ownership and profitability markers are steady, so they are supporting stability but are not acting as a major upside catalyst by themselves.";
459. 
460.   if ((promoterHolding ?? 0) >= 50 || (netMargin ?? 0) >= 10) {
461.     ownershipImpact = "positive";
462.     ownershipDetail = `Promoter holding is ${promoterHolding !== null ? `${roundMetric(promoterHolding).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"} and net margin is ${netMargin !== null ? `${roundMetric(netMargin).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}. That matters because stronger insider alignment and profitability reduce the chance that the move is being carried only by short-term speculation.`;
463.   } else if ((promoterHolding !== null && promoterHolding < 40) || (netMargin !== null && netMargin < 5)) {
464.     ownershipImpact = "negative";
465.     ownershipDetail = `Promoter holding is ${promoterHolding !== null ? `${roundMetric(promoterHolding).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"} and net margin is ${netMargin !== null ? `${roundMetric(netMargin).toFixed(2)}%` : "n/a"}. That matters because weaker alignment or profitability leaves less business support underneath the trade setup.`;
466.   }
467. 
468.   return [
469.     { area: "fundamental", impact: qualityImpact, title: "Business quality", detail: qualityDetail },
470.     { area: "fundamental", impact: ownershipImpact, title: "Ownership and profitability", detail: ownershipDetail }
471.   ];
472. }
473. 
474. function buildSentimentAnalysisDrivers(entry: MarketSeries): AnalysisDriver[] {
475.   if (!entry.sentiment) {
476.     return [
477.       {
478.         area: "sentiment",

491.     "Headline tone is balanced, so sentiment is acting more like background context than like a hard catalyst.";
492.

494.     toneImpact = "positive";
495.     toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because positive narrative support makes investors more willing to keep adding on strength.`;
496.   } else if (headlineBalance < 0 || sentiment.score < 0) {
497.     toneImpact = "negative";
498.     toneDetail = `Headline tone is ${sentiment.overall.toLowerCase()} with ${sentiment.positiveCount} positive versus ${sentiment.negativeCount} negative headlines. That matters because a negative narrative can quickly cap upside even when the chart looks constructive.`;
499.   }
500. 
501.   const coverageCount = sentiment.positiveCount + sentiment.neutralCount + sentiment.negativeCount;
502.   let coverageImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
503.   let coverageDetail =
504.     "Headline coverage is light, so the narrative layer is acting as background context rather than as a strong driver of the move.";
505. 
506.   if (coverageCount >= 5 && headlineBalance > 1) {
507.     coverageImpact = "positive";
508.     coverageDetail = `The feed captured ${coverageCount} recent headlines with a clearly positive skew. That matters because heavier supportive coverage tends to keep the stock in focus and can reinforce buying interest after an initial move.`;
509.   } else if (coverageCount >= 5 && headlineBalance < -1) {
510.     coverageImpact = "negative";
511.     coverageDetail = `The feed captured ${coverageCount} recent headlines with a negative skew. That matters because heavier critical coverage can pressure sentiment and make traders quicker to sell strength.`;
512.   }
513. 
514.   return [
515.     { area: "sentiment", impact: toneImpact, title: "Narrative tone", detail: toneDetail },
516.     { area: "sentiment", impact: coverageImpact, title: "Headline coverage", detail: coverageDetail }
517.   ];
518. }

1. from typing import Literal
2. 
3. from pydantic import BaseModel
4. 
5. HorizonId = Literal["single_day", "swing", "position", "long_term"]
6. Conviction = Literal["High", "Medium", "Low"]
7. OutcomeResult = Literal["target_hit", "stop_loss_hit", "open"]
8. AnalysisArea = Literal["technical", "fundamental", "sentiment", "earnings", "analyst", "risk"]
9. AnalysisImpact = Literal["positive", "negative", "neutral"]
10. 
11. 
12. class Signal(BaseModel):
13.     name: str
14.     value: str
15. 
16. 
17. class AnalysisDriver(BaseModel):
18.     area: AnalysisArea
19.     impact: AnalysisImpact
20.     title: str
21.     detail: str
22. 
23. 
24. class RecommendationOutcome(BaseModel):
25.     result: OutcomeResult
26.     evaluatedOn: str
27.     holdingDays: int
28.     returnPct: float
29.     notes: str
30. 
31. 
32. class RecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
33.     score: float | None = None
34.     rank: int | None = None
35.     isRecommended: bool = True
36.     conviction: Conviction
37.     entryPrice: float
38.     targetPrice: float
39.     stopLoss: float
40.     expectedReturnPct: float
41.     riskReward: float
42.     summary: str
43.     drivers: list[str]
44.     analysisDrivers: list[AnalysisDriver] | None = None
45.     technicalSignals: list[Signal]
46.     fundamentalSignals: list[Signal]
47.     sentimentSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
48.     earningsSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
49.     analystSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
50.     riskSignals: list[Signal] | None = None
51.     newsContext: list[str]
52. 
53. 
54. class HistoricalRecommendationPlan(BaseModel):
55.     score: float | None = None
56.     rank: int | None = None
57.     isRecommended: bool = True
58.     conviction: Conviction
59.     entryPrice: float
60.     targetPrice: float
61.     stopLoss: float
62.     summary: str

1. import json

9.     HorizonId,

11.     RecommendationSummary,
12.     StockAnalysis,
13.     StockDetailResponse,
14. )
15. 
16. DATA_DIRECTORY = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "data"
17. GENERATED_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "generated-recommendations.json"
18. SAMPLE_DATA_PATH = DATA_DIRECTORY / "sample-recommendations.json"
19. 
20. app = FastAPI(
21.     title="Recommendation API",
22.     version="0.1.0",
23.     description="Explainable API for Indian equities recommendation batches.",
24. )
25. 
26. 
27. def _clamp(value: float, minimum: float, maximum: float) -> float:
28.     return min(max(value, minimum), maximum)
29. 
30. 
31. def _pct_change(current: float, previous: float) -> float:
32.     if previous == 0:
33.         return 0.0
34. 
35.     return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100
36. 
37. 
38. def _derive_single_day_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
39.     entry_price = round(float(plan["entryPrice"]), 2)
40.     target_pct = _clamp(
41.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["targetPrice"]), entry_price)) * 0.38,
42.         1.2,
43.         3.4,
44.     )
45.     stop_pct = _clamp(
46.         abs(_pct_change(float(plan["stopLoss"]), entry_price)) * 0.45,
47.         0.7,
48.         1.9,
49.     )
50.     target_price = round(entry_price * (1 + target_pct / 100), 2)
51.     stop_loss = round(max(entry_price * (1 - stop_pct / 100), entry_price * 0.85), 2)
52. 
53.     return {
54.         **plan,
55.         "score": plan.get("score", 55),
56.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
57.         "entryPrice": entry_price,
58.         "targetPrice": target_price,
59.         "stopLoss": stop_loss,
60.         "expectedReturnPct": round(_pct_change(target_price, entry_price), 2),
61.         "riskReward": round((target_price - entry_price) / max(entry_price - stop_loss, 0.01), 2),
62.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day setup derived from the saved swing profile. {plan['summary']}",
63.         "drivers": [
64.             "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the single-day model.",
65.             *plan.get("drivers", []),
66.         ][:4],
67.         "analysisDrivers": [
68.             {
69.                 "area": "risk",
70.                 "impact": "neutral",
71.                 "title": "Compatibility mode",
72.                 "detail": "This single-day view was synthesized from the saved swing setup because the cached dataset predates the current single-day reasoning model.",
73.             },
74.             *plan.get("analysisDrivers", []),
75.         ][:6],
76.         "technicalSignals": [
77.             {"name": "Compatibility mode", "value": "Derived from swing profile"},
78.             *plan.get("technicalSignals", []),
79.         ][:6],
80.         "riskSignals": plan.get("riskSignals")
81.         or [{"name": "Learning status", "value": "Awaiting refreshed live batch"}],
82.     }
83. 
84. 
85. def _derive_single_day_history(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
86.     outcome = plan["outcome"]
87. 
88.     return {
89.         **plan,
90.         "conviction": "Medium" if plan.get("conviction") == "High" else plan.get("conviction"),
91.         "summary": f"Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result. {plan['summary']}",
92.         "outcome": {
93.             **outcome,
94.             "notes": f"This single-day history row reuses the saved swing outcome because the cached dataset predates the single-day model. {outcome['notes']}",
95.         },
96.     }
97. 
98. 
99. def _normalize_payload(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
100.     profile_lookup = {profile["id"]: profile for profile in payload.get("profiles", [])}
101.     payload["profiles"] = [
102.         profile_lookup.get("single_day", {"id": "single_day", "label": "Single-day", "window": "Next trading day"}),
103.         profile_lookup.get("swing", {"id": "swing", "label": "Swing", "window": "5-20 trading days"}),
104.         profile_lookup.get("position", {"id": "position", "label": "Position", "window": "20-60 trading days"}),
105.         profile_lookup.get("long_term", {"id": "long_term", "label": "Long-term", "window": "3-12 months"}),
106.     ]
107. 
108.     for recommendation in payload.get("currentBatch", {}).get("recommendations", []):
109.         profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
110.         if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
111.             profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_plan(profiles["swing"])
112. 
113.     for batch in payload.get("history", []):
114.         for recommendation in batch.get("recommendations", []):
115.             profiles = recommendation.get("profiles", {})
116.             if "single_day" not in profiles and "swing" in profiles:
117.                 profiles["single_day"] = _derive_single_day_history(profiles["swing"])
118. 
119.     return payload
120. 
121. 
122. def load_dataset(prefer_generated: bool = True) -> RecommendationDataset:
123.     data_path = GENERATED_DATA_PATH if prefer_generated and GENERATED_DATA_PATH.exists() else SAMPLE_DATA_PATH
124.     payload = _normalize_payload(json.loads(data_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
125.     return RecommendationDataset.model_validate(payload)
126. 
127. 
128. def build_summary(stock: StockAnalysis, horizon: HorizonId) -> RecommendationSummary:
129.     plan = stock.profiles[horizon]
130. 
131.     return RecommendationSummary(
132.         symbol=stock.symbol,
133.         companyName=stock.companyName,
134.         sector=stock.sector,
135.         currentMarketPrice=stock.currentMarketPrice,
136.         horizon=horizon,
137.         score=plan.score,
138.         rank=plan.rank,
139.         isRecommended=plan.isRecommended,
140.         conviction=plan.conviction,
141.         entryPrice=plan.entryPrice,
142.         targetPrice=plan.targetPrice,
143.         stopLoss=plan.stopLoss,
144.         expectedReturnPct=plan.expectedReturnPct,
145.         riskReward=plan.riskReward,
146.         summary=plan.summary,
147.     )
148. 
149. 
150. @app.get("/healthz")
151. def healthcheck() -> dict[str, str]:
152.     return {"status": "ok"}
153. 
154. 
155. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations")
156. def list_recommendations(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
157.     dataset = load_dataset()
158.     ordered_recommendations = sorted(
159.         dataset.currentBatch.recommendations,
160.         key=lambda stock: (
161.             stock.profiles[horizon].score
162.             if stock.profiles[horizon].score is not None
163.             else stock.profiles[horizon].expectedReturnPct
164.         ),
165.         reverse=True,
166.     )
167.     recommended_rows = [
168.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
169.         for stock in ordered_recommendations
170.         if stock.profiles[horizon].isRecommended
171.     ]
172.     rows = recommended_rows or [
173.         build_summary(stock, horizon).model_dump()
174.         for stock in ordered_recommendations[:5]
175.     ]
176.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
177. 
178.     return {
179.         "batchDate": dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
180.         "generatedAt": dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
181.         "market": dataset.market,
182.         "exchange": dataset.exchange,
183.         "universe": dataset.universe,
184.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
185.         "dailyPerformance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
186.         "recommendations": rows,
187.     }
188. 
189. 
190. @app.get("/api/v1/performance/daily")
191. def get_daily_performance(horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> dict[str, object]:
192.     dataset = load_dataset()
193.     performance = build_daily_performance(dataset, horizon)
194. 
195.     return {
196.         "selectedHorizon": horizon,
197.         "performance": [row.model_dump() for row in performance],
198.     }
199. 
200. 
201. @app.get("/api/v1/recommendations/{symbol}")
202. def get_recommendation(symbol: str, horizon: HorizonId = "swing") -> StockDetailResponse:
203.     dataset = load_dataset()
204.     stock = next(
205.         (
206.             row
207.             for row in dataset.currentBatch.recommendations
208.             if row.symbol == symbol.upper()
209.         ),
210.         None,
211.     )
212. 
213.     if stock is None:
214.         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Unknown stock symbol: {symbol}")
215. 
216.     return StockDetailResponse(
217.         generatedAt=dataset.currentBatch.generatedAt,
218.         batchDate=dataset.currentBatch.batchDate,
219.         market=dataset.market,
220.         exchange=dataset.exchange,
221.         universe=dataset.universe,
222.         selectedHorizon=horizon,
223.         stock=stock,
224.         history=build_stock_history(dataset, symbol, horizon),
225.     )
226.
```

1. "use client";
2. 
3. import Link from "next/link";
4. import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
5. import { buildStockPerformanceHistory } from "@/lib/analytics";
6. import {
7.   EmptyState,
8.   InfoTooltip,
9.   formatCrore,
10.   formatDate,
11.   formatNumber,
12.   formatPrice,
13.   isRecommendedPlan,
14.   normalizeSymbol
15. } from "@/components/market-ui";
16. import type {
17.   AnalysisDriver,
18.   HorizonId,
19.   RecommendationPlan,
20.   RecommendationDataset,
21.   SearchAnalysisResult,
22.   Signal,
23.   StockAnalysis,
24.   StockPerformanceHistoryEntry
25. } from "@/lib/types";
26. 
27. type StockDetailProps = {
28.   data: RecommendationDataset;
29.   analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null;
30.   requestedSymbol: string;
31. };
32. 
33. type UiTone = "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral";
34. type TradeDecisionState = "buy" | "watch" | "avoid";
35. type AnalysisTabId = "technical" | "fundamental" | "sentiment" | "risk";
36. 
37. const HORIZON_THRESHOLDS: Record<HorizonId, number> = {
38.   single_day: 54,
39.   swing: 56,
40.   position: 58,
41.   long_term: 60
42. };
43. 
44. const TAB_INFO = {
45.   technical:
46.     "Technical analysis summarizes trend, breakout, momentum, and participation so you can judge whether buyers are actually in control.",
47.   fundamental:
48.     "Fundamental analysis summarizes business quality, growth, profitability, and ownership support behind the setup.",
49.   sentiment:
50.     "Sentiment analysis combines news tone, earnings cues, and analyst commentary that can accelerate or weaken the move.",
51.   risk:
52.     "Risk analysis highlights fragility, volatility, stop-loss pressure, and payoff quality so you can judge whether the setup is worth taking."
53. } as const;
54. 
55. function defaultHorizon(data: RecommendationDataset, analysis: SearchAnalysisResult | null) {
56.   const preferred = analysis?.recommendedHorizons?.[0];
57. 
58.   if (preferred && data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === preferred)) {
59.     return preferred;
60.   }
61. 
62.   return data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day";
63. }
64. 
65. function clampProgress(value: number) {
66.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, value));
67. }
68. 
69. function firstSentence(text: string) {
70.   const cleaned = text.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
71. 
72.   if (!cleaned) {
73.     return "";
74.   }
75. 
76.   const match = cleaned.match(/.+?[.!?](?:\s|$)/);
77.   return (match?.[0] ?? cleaned).trim();
78. }
79. 
80. function shorten(text: string, maxLength = 156) {
81.   const normalized = firstSentence(text);
82. 
83.   if (normalized.length <= maxLength) {
84.     return normalized;
85.   }
86. 
87.   return `${normalized.slice(0, maxLength - 1).trimEnd()}…`;
88. }
89. 
90. function parseNumericSignal(value: string) {
91.   const match = value.replace(/,/g, "").match(/-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?/);
92.   return match ? Number.parseFloat(match[0]) : null;
93. }
94. 
95. function reasoningDriversFor(plan: RecommendationPlan, area: AnalysisDriver["area"]): AnalysisDriver[] {
96.   const explicitDrivers = plan.analysisDrivers?.filter((driver) => driver.area === area);
97. 
98.   if (explicitDrivers?.length) {
99.     return explicitDrivers;
100.   }
101. 
102.   return [
103.     {

114. function scoreInsight(score: number | undefined, horizon: HorizonId) {
115.   const threshold = HORIZON_THRESHOLDS[horizon];
116.   const safeScore = score ?? 0;
117.

119.     return {
120.       label: "Awaiting score",
121.       tone: "neutral" as UiTone,
122.       progress: 0,
123.       threshold
124.     };
125.   }
126. 
127.   if (safeScore >= threshold + 12) {
128.     return {
129.       label: "High Probability",
130.       tone: "success" as UiTone,
131.       progress: clampProgress(safeScore),
132.       threshold
133.     };
134.   }
135. 
136.   if (safeScore >= threshold) {
137.     return {
138.       label: "Tradable",
139.       tone: "success" as UiTone,
140.       progress: clampProgress(safeScore),

128. function normalizeSearchSymbol(query: string) {
129.   return query.trim().toUpperCase().replace(/\.NS$/i, "");

132. function parseIndianNumber(value: string | null) {
133.   if (!value) {
134.     return null;
135.   }
136. 
137.   const normalized = value.replace(/,/g, "").trim();
138.   const parsed = Number.parseFloat(normalized);
139. 
140.   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
141. }
142. 
143. function parsePercent(value: string | null) {
144.   if (!value) {
145.     return null;
