153. function textContent(regex: RegExp, text: string) {
154.   const match = regex.exec(text);
155.   return match?.[1] ?? null;
156. }
157. 
158. function titleTone(title: string) {
159.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
160.   const positiveHits = POSITIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
161.   const negativeHits = NEGATIVE_WORDS.filter((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword)).length;
162. 
163.   if (positiveHits > negativeHits) {
164.     return "positive" as const;
165.   }
166. 
167.   if (negativeHits > positiveHits) {
168.     return "negative" as const;
169.   }
170. 
171.   return "neutral" as const;
172. }
173. 
174. function headlineCategory(title: string) {
175.   const lower = title.toLowerCase();
176. 
177.   if (ANALYST_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
178.     return "analyst" as const;
179.   }
180. 
181.   if (EARNINGS_KEYWORDS.some((keyword) => lower.includes(keyword))) {
182.     return "earnings" as const;
183.   }
184. 
185.   return "news" as const;
186. }
187. 
188. function sentimentScore(headlines: NewsHeadline[]) {
189.   if (!headlines.length) {
190.     return 50;
191.   }
192. 
193.   const toneTotal = headlines.reduce((total, headline) => {
194.     if (headline.tone === "positive") {
195.       return total + 1;
196.     }
197. 
198.     if (headline.tone === "negative") {
199.       return total - 1;
200.     }
201. 
202.     return total;
203.   }, 0);
204. 
205.   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 50 + (toneTotal / headlines.length) * 35));
206. }
207. 
208. function overallSentiment(score: number) {
209.   if (score >= 58) {
210.     return "Positive" as const;
211.   }
212. 
213.   if (score <= 42) {
214.     return "Negative" as const;
215.   }
216. 
217.   return "Neutral" as const;
218. }
219. 
220. async function fetchText(url: string) {
221.   const response = await fetch(url, {
222.     cache: "no-store",
223.     headers: {
224.       Accept: "text/html,application/xml,application/json",
225.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
226.     }
227.   });
228. 
229.   if (!response.ok) {
230.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
231.   }
232. 
233.   return response.text();
234. }
235. 
236. async function fetchJson<T>(url: string) {
237.   const response = await fetch(url, {
238.     cache: "no-store",
239.     headers: {
240.       Accept: "application/json",
241.       "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
242.     }
243.   });
244. 
245.   if (!response.ok) {
246.     throw new Error(`Failed request: ${response.status}`);
247.   }
248. 
249.   return (await response.json()) as T;
250. }
251. 
252. function isNseEquityQuote(quote: YahooSearchQuote): quote is YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string } {
253.   return quote.exchange === "NSI" && quote.quoteType === "EQUITY" && Boolean(quote.symbol);
254. }
255. 
256. function toCompanyLookup(quote: YahooSearchQuote & { symbol: string }): CompanyLookup {
257.   const normalizedSymbol = normalizeSearchSymbol(quote.symbol);
258. 
259.   return {
260.     symbol: normalizedSymbol,
261.     yahooSymbol: quote.symbol,
262.     companyName: quote.longname ?? quote.shortname ?? normalizedSymbol,
263.     sector: quote.sectorDisp ?? quote.sector ?? "Unknown",
264.     industry: quote.industryDisp ?? quote.industry ?? undefined
265.   };
266. }
267. 
268. function searchRank(lookup: CompanyLookup, normalizedQuery: string) {
269.   const symbol = lookup.symbol;
270.   const companyName = lookup.companyName.toUpperCase();
271. 
272.   if (symbol === normalizedQuery) {
273.     return 0;
274.   }
275. 
276.   if (symbol.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
277.     return 1;
278.   }
279. 
280.   if (companyName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {
281.     return 2;
282.   }
283. 
284.   if (companyName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {
285.     return 3;
286.   }
287. 
288.   return 4;
289. }
290. 
291. async function searchNseQuotes(query: string, limit: number) {
292.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
293. 
294.   if (!normalized) {
295.     return [];
296.   }
297. 
298.   const payload = await fetchJson<YahooSearchResponse>(
299.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(normalized)}&quotesCount=${Math.max(limit * 2, 8)}&newsCount=0&enableFuzzyQuery=true`
300.   );
301.   const matches = (payload.quotes ?? [])
302.     .filter(isNseEquityQuote)
303.     .map(toCompanyLookup)
304.     .sort((left, right) => {
305.       const leftRank = searchRank(left, normalized);
306.       const rightRank = searchRank(right, normalized);
307. 
308.       if (leftRank !== rightRank) {
309.         return leftRank - rightRank;
310.       }
311. 
312.       return left.symbol.localeCompare(right.symbol);
313.     });
314.   const deduped: CompanyLookup[] = [];
315.   const seen = new Set<string>();
316. 
317.   for (const match of matches) {
318.     if (seen.has(match.symbol)) {
319.       continue;
320.     }
321. 
322.     seen.add(match.symbol);
323.     deduped.push(match);
324. 
325.     if (deduped.length >= limit) {
326.       break;
327.     }
328.   }
329. 
330.   return deduped;
331. }
332. 
333. export async function lookupNseSuggestions(
334.   query: string,
335.   limit = 8
336. ): Promise<StockSearchSuggestion[]> {
337.   return (await searchNseQuotes(query, limit)).map(({ symbol, companyName, sector, industry }) => ({
338.     symbol,
339.     companyName,
340.     sector,
341.     industry
342.   }));
343. }
344. 
345. export async function lookupNseSymbol(query: string): Promise<CompanyLookup | null> {
346.   const normalized = normalizeSearchSymbol(query);
347. 
348.   if (!normalized) {
349.     return null;
350.   }
351. 
352.   const matches = await searchNseQuotes(normalized, 6);
353. 
354.   return matches.find((match) => match.symbol === normalized) ?? matches[0] ?? null;
355. }
356. 
357. async function fetchScreenerFundamentals(symbol: string) {
358.   try {
359.     const html = await fetchText(`https://www.screener.in/company/${encodeURIComponent(symbol)}/consolidated/`);
360.     const description = decodeHtmlEntities(
361.       textContent(/<meta name="description" content="([^"]+)"/i, html) ?? ""
362.     );
363. 
364.     if (!description) {
365.       return null;
366.     }
367. 
368.     const marketCapCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Mkt Cap:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Crore/i, description));
369.     const marketCapChange1YPct = parsePercent(
370.       textContent(/Mkt Cap:[^(]+\((?:up|down)\s*([\d.]+)% in 1 year\)/i, description)
371.     );
372.     const revenueCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Revenue:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
373.     const profitCrore = parseIndianNumber(textContent(/Profit:\s*([\d,]+)\s*Cr/i, description));
374.     const salesGrowthLabel = textContent(/company has delivered a ([a-z]+) sales growth/i, description);
375.     const salesGrowth5YPct = parsePercent(textContent(/sales growth of ([\d.]+)% over past five years/i, description));
376.     const returnOnEquityLabel = textContent(/company has a ([a-z]+) return on equity/i, description);
377.     const returnOnEquityPct = parsePercent(textContent(/return on equity of ([\d.]+)%/i, description));
378.     const promoterHoldingPct = parsePercent(textContent(/Promoter Holding:\s*([\d.]+)%/i, description));
379.     const netMarginPct =
380.       revenueCrore && profitCrore ? Number(((profitCrore / revenueCrore) * 100).toFixed(2)) : null;
381. 
382.     return {
383.       source: "Screener.in",
384.       summary: description,
385.       marketCapCrore,
386.       marketCapChange1YPct,
387.       revenueCrore,
388.       profitCrore,
389.       netMarginPct,
390.       salesGrowth5YPct,
391.       salesGrowthLabel,
392.       returnOnEquityPct,
393.       returnOnEquityLabel,
394.       promoterHoldingPct
395.     } satisfies FundamentalSnapshot;
396.   } catch {
397.     return null;
398.   }
399. }
400. 
401. async function fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(companyName: string, symbol: string) {
402.   try {
403.     const query = `"${companyName}" OR ${symbol} NSE stock when:30d`;
404.     const xml = await fetchText(
405.       `https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN:en`
406.     );
407.     const headlines: NewsHeadline[] = [];
408. 
409.     for (const match of xml.matchAll(/<item>([\s\S]*?)<\/item>/g)) {
410.       const item = match[1];
411.       const rawTitle = textContent(/<title>([\s\S]*?)<\/title>/i, item);
412. 
413.       if (!rawTitle) {
414.         continue;
415.       }
416. 
417.       const title = decodeHtmlEntities(rawTitle).replace(/\s+-\s+[^-]+$/, "").trim();
418.       const source = decodeHtmlEntities(textContent(/<source[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/source>/i, item) ?? "Google News");
419.       const publishedAt = textContent(/<pubDate>([\s\S]*?)<\/pubDate>/i, item) ?? "";
420.       const tone = titleTone(title);
421.       const category = headlineCategory(title);
422. 
423.       headlines.push({
424.         title,
425.         source,
426.         publishedAt,
427.         tone,
428.         category
429.       });
430. 
431.       if (headlines.length >= 6) {
432.         break;
433.       }
434.     }
435. 
436.     if (!headlines.length) {
437.       return null;
438.     }
439. 
440.     const score = Number(sentimentScore(headlines).toFixed(2));
441. 
442.     return {
443.       query,
444.       overall: overallSentiment(score),
445.       score,
446.       positiveCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "positive").length,
447.       neutralCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "neutral").length,
448.       negativeCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.tone === "negative").length,
449.       earningsMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings").length,
450.       analystMentionCount: headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "analyst").length,
451.       headlines
452.     } satisfies SentimentSnapshot;
453.   } catch {
454.     return null;
455.   }
456. }
457. 
458. export async function fetchCompanyResearch(lookup: CompanyLookup): Promise<CompanyResearch> {
459.   const [fundamentals, sentiment] = await Promise.all([
460.     fetchScreenerFundamentals(lookup.symbol),
461.     fetchGoogleNewsSentiment(lookup.companyName, lookup.symbol)
462.   ]);
463. 
464.   return {
465.     companyName: lookup.companyName,
466.     sector: lookup.sector,
467.     industry: lookup.industry,
468.     fundamentals,
469.     sentiment
470.   };
471. }
472. 
473. export function scoreFundamentals(fundamentals: FundamentalSnapshot | null) {
474.   if (!fundamentals) {
475.     return 50;
476.   }
477. 
478.   const growthScore =
479.     fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct === null
480.       ? 50
481.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 35 + fundamentals.salesGrowth5YPct * 3));
482.   const roeScore =
483.     fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct === null
484.       ? 50
485.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.returnOnEquityPct * 3));
486.   const marginScore =
487.     fundamentals.netMarginPct === null
488.       ? 50
489.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 30 + fundamentals.netMarginPct * 2.5));
490.   const promoterScore =
491.     fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct === null
492.       ? 50
493.       : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 25 + fundamentals.promoterHoldingPct));
494.   const labelBonus =
495.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "strong" || fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "high" ? 7 : 0) +
496.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "high" || fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "good" ? 6 : 0) -
497.     (fundamentals.salesGrowthLabel === "poor" ? 8 : 0) -
498.     (fundamentals.returnOnEquityLabel === "low" ? 8 : 0);
499. 
500.   return Number(
501.     (
502.       growthScore * 0.3 +
503.       roeScore * 0.35 +
504.       marginScore * 0.15 +
505.       promoterScore * 0.2 +
506.       labelBonus
507.     ).toFixed(2)
508.   );
509. }
510. 
511. export function scoreSentiment(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
512.   return sentiment?.score ?? 50;
513. }
514. 
515. export function scoreEarningsSignal(sentiment: SentimentSnapshot | null) {
516.   if (!sentiment) {
517.     return 50;
518.   }
519. 
520.   const earningsHeadlines = sentiment.headlines.filter((headline) => headline.category === "earnings");
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>88 lines</summary>

````
1. # Indian equities recommendation platform
2. 
3. This repository now includes a research-first stock recommendation system for **Indian equities**. The dashboard analyzes a live NSE watchlist using recent market history, produces ranked multi-horizon recommendations, and explains each idea with an entry, target, and stop-loss instead of acting like a black-box tip engine.
4. 
5. ## Chosen product scope
6. 
7. - **User:** analyst or serious retail investor
8. - **Market:** liquid NSE equities, now starting from a dedicated **Nifty 100 universe master**
9. - **Delivery:** web dashboard
10. - **Mode:** research and decision support, not broker execution
11. - **Batch cadence:** run **after market close** on trading days and publish a next-session trade plan
12. - **MVP horizons:** single-day, swing, position, and long-term
13. - **Later phase:** intraday once a separate high-frequency data stack exists
14. 
15. ## Chosen stack
16. 
17. | Area | Choice | Why |
18. | --- | --- | --- |
19. | Frontend | **Next.js + TypeScript** | Strong dashboard UX, routing, and server/client composition |
20. | UI style | **shadcn/ui-style patterns** | Clean analyst workflow without heavyweight theme lock-in |
21. | API | **FastAPI** | Good fit for Python-based data and model logic |
22. | Data and modeling | **Python + Pydantic** | Natural ecosystem for market data, features, and backtests |
23. | Operational database | **PostgreSQL** | Durable storage for batches, audit history, and app entities |
24. | Research store | **DuckDB / Parquet** | Efficient local analytics and reproducible snapshots |
25. 
26. ## Current repository layout
27. 
28. ```text
29. apps/
30.   web/          Next.js analyst dashboard
31. services/
32.   api/          FastAPI recommendation API
33. data/           Shared sample recommendation data used during scaffolding
34. ```
35. 
36. ## Live recommendation flow
37. 
38. - The web app fetches **live Yahoo Finance chart data** for the **Nifty 100 universe** plus the Nifty 50 benchmark.
39. - Recommendations are scored from recent momentum, relative strength, moving-average trend, volume, volatility, **chart-structure analysis** (breakouts, candle quality, range behavior, and moving-average slope), lightweight fundamentals, and headline-based sentiment / earnings / analyst signals.
40. - The live model now also applies **stop-loss learning**: repeated recent stop-loss failures reduce future model aggressiveness for similar setups.
41. - The app also backtests prior daily batches from the same historical price series so the dashboard can maintain horizon-specific success rates.
42. - On a successful live refresh, the generated dataset is cached to `data/generated-recommendations.json`.
43. - Each successful daily batch is also archived to `data/daily-batches/<batch-date>.json` for persistent run history.
44. - If persistent disk writes are not available on the host, the app falls back to **in-memory caching** for the running instance.
45. - If live requests fail later, the app falls back to the cached snapshot before falling back to the older sample data.
46. 
47. ## MVP user experience
48. 
49. 1. The dashboard shows the latest ranked recommendation batch with a broader live shortlist.
50. 2. Clicking a stock opens a detailed analysis view.
51. 3. The detail view explains:
52.     - key factors behind the recommendation
53.     - recommended entry price
54.     - target price
55.     - stop-loss
56.     - risk-reward profile
57.     - horizon-specific analysis for single-day, swing, position, and long-term modes
58. 4. The dashboard also reviews **previous daily recommendations**, explains why each call hit target or stop-loss, and maintains a **daily success-rate tracker** for every horizon.
59. 5. The search panel offers **autocomplete suggestions while you type**, then analyzes the selected NSE stock and says whether it should be **considered or avoided** for each supported horizon.
60. 6. Historical stop-loss outcomes now include a clearer explanation of **why the stop-loss was hit**, and the live model uses recent stop-loss history to reduce future aggressiveness for fragile setups.
61. 
62. ## Windows quick start
63. 
64. - Double-click `run-app.bat` in the repository root.
65. - On the first run it installs npm dependencies automatically.
66. - It starts the dashboard server in a new window, waits for `http://localhost:3000`, and opens your browser automatically.
67. - The run card in the app shows whether the current batch is coming from **LIVE**, **CACHED**, or **SAMPLE** data.
68. 
69. ## Analytics test command
70. 
71. - Run `npm run test:api` from `C:\copilot` to validate the daily recommendation scoring logic.
72. 
73. ## Deployment and free hosting
74. 
75. ### Production-readiness changes in this repo
76. 
77. - The Next.js app now exposes `GET /api/healthz` for simple uptime checks.
78. - The live dataset loader supports **in-memory runtime caching**, which is friendlier to serverless or ephemeral free hosts.
79. - `render.yaml` is included so you can deploy both the web app and the FastAPI service on Render with minimal setup.
80. 
81. ### Recommended free hosting options
82. 
83. 1. **Vercel** for the Next.js app only. This is the easiest choice for the dashboard because it handles Next.js SSR well. In Vercel, set the project root to `apps/web`.
84. 2. **Render** if you want both the Next.js app and the FastAPI API hosted together for free. The included `render.yaml` is aimed at this option. Render free web services can sleep when idle, so expect cold starts.
85. 3. **Cloudflare Pages** if you want a very low-cost global edge deployment for the web app and are comfortable with more setup than Vercel.
86. 
87. For this repository as it exists today, **Vercel** is the simplest free host for the dashboard, while **Render** is the simplest free option if you want to run both services from one repo.
88.
````

</details>

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\recommendation-data.ts\n@@\n function average(values: number[]) {\n   if (!values.length) {\n     return null;\n   }\n \n   return values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0) / values.length;\n }\n+\n+function median(values: number[]) {\n+  if (!values.length) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const ordered = [...values].sort((left, right) => left - right);\n+  const midpoint = Math.floor(ordered.length / 2);\n+\n+  return ordered.length % 2 === 0\n+    ? (ordered[midpoint - 1] + ordered[midpoint]) / 2\n+    : ordered[midpoint];\n+}\n+\n+function validNumbers(values: Array<number | null | undefined>) {\n+  return values.filter((value): value is number => value !== null && value !== undefined && Number.isFinite(value));\n+}\n+\n+function buildSectorFundamentalContextMap(stocks: SectorFundamentalSource[]) {\n+  const grouped = new Map<string, SectorFundamentalSource[]>();\n+\n+  for (const stock of stocks) {\n+    if (!stock.sector) {\n+      continue;\n+    }\n+\n+    const existing = grouped.get(stock.sector) ?? [];\n+    existing.push(stock);\n+    grouped.set(stock.sector, existing);\n+  }\n+\n+  const contexts = new Map<string, FundamentalSectorContext>();\n+\n+  for (const [sector, sectorStocks] of grouped) {\n+    const fundamentals = sectorStocks\n+      .map((stock) => stock.fundamentals)\n+      .filter((snapshot): snapshot is NonNullable<StockAnalysis[\"fundamentals\"]> => Boolean(snapshot));\n+\n+    contexts.set(sector, {\n+      sector,\n+      peerCount: fundamentals.length,\n+      medianPriceToEarnings: median(validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.priceToEarnings))),\n+      medianPriceToBook: median(validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.priceToBook))),\n+      medianReturnOnEquityPct: median(\n+        validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.returnOnEquityPct))\n+      ),\n+      medianReturnOnCapitalEmployedPct: median(\n+        validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.returnOnCapitalEmployedPct))\n+      ),\n+      medianDebtToEquity: median(validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.debtToEquity))),\n+      medianSalesGrowth5YPct: median(validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.salesGrowth5YPct))),\n+      medianEarningsGrowthPct: median(validNumbers(fundamentals.map((snapshot) => snapshot.earningsGrowthPct)))\n+    });\n+  }\n+\n+  return contexts;\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 465m 18s</sub>

C:\Users\rc18763\.copilot\session-state\f311fad0-ff21-42f7-aacc-14870f2768e9\plan.md (lines 6-32)

<details>
<summary>27 lines</summary>

```
6. ## Current state
7. - A Next.js dashboard and FastAPI API skeleton now exist in `C:\copilot`.
8. - The web app currently runs with a live recommendation loader that fetches Yahoo Finance chart data for a Nifty 100 universe master, ranks stocks per horizon, archives daily batch snapshots, and caches the latest successful dataset locally.
9. - Historical batch performance is now generated from price history so daily success rates and prior recommendation outcomes are tied to the same live market series.
10. - The dashboard now includes:
11.   - a stock search flow that analyzes a searched NSE symbol and returns a consider-or-not verdict,
12.   - autocomplete suggestions in the stock search box so typing a symbol or company name shows matching NSE ideas to select from,
13.   - a previous-recommendation menu for clicking older batches and seeing whether each call achieved its target,
14.   - live fundamentals from Screener meta data and headline-based sentiment, earnings, and analyst-tone signals from Google News RSS,
15.   - a single-day recommendation horizon alongside swing, position, and long-term,
16.   - chart-structure analysis inside the technical model, including breakout, candle-quality, range-behavior, and moving-average-slope signals,
17.   - a score-to-reasoning navigator in the stock workspace so technical, fundamental, sentiment, and risk scores jump to anchored explanation panels,
18.   - dedicated earnings and analyst reasoning panels, with clickable score tiles that explain why those scores moved and how they can impact the stock,
19.   - hover help on the analysis panels so each card explains what it is, why it is needed, and how it can impact the stock,
20.   - a threshold-separated home workspace where below-threshold names are shown only in a clearly labeled watchlist section instead of inside the active recommendation list,
21.   - richer stop-loss-hit explanations plus score penalties that reduce future aggressiveness after recent stop-loss failures,
22.   - a cleaner responsive dashboard layout and deployment-oriented runtime changes such as in-memory caching and health checks,
23.   - a Nifty 100 universe master with safer live-fetch scaling and daily batch snapshot persistence, plus a home shortlist that now surfaces ten names instead of only a few,
24.   - a card-based panel redesign with explicit category headers, color-coded analysis modules, a titled autocomplete results panel, and recommendation/history views that avoid horizontal scrolling,
25.   - a page split that keeps the home dashboard stable while moving historical review to `/history` and full stock analysis to `/stocks/[symbol]`,
26.   - a rebuilt stock detail page that now uses a compact decision-first layout with a compressed command bar, actionable trade strip, tabbed analysis workspace, horizon comparison table, and sticky summary rail.
27. - Remaining work is to harden the ingestion path, broaden the market universe and feature set, and reduce reliance on a sample fallback when remote requests fail.
28. 
29. ## Proposed approach
30. Start with a narrow MVP that produces explainable stock rankings for a defined Indian equity universe using historical prices, fundamentals, and a small set of macro or sentiment signals. The system should refresh on market working days, ingest the latest available data before each run, and then publish a daily recommendation set. Build the system in layers so the model can be evaluated and improved safely:
31. 
32. 1. Product definition
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>13 lines</summary>

```
46. 2. Data foundation
47.    - Ingest adjusted price history, company fundamentals, sectors, benchmark data such as Nifty 50 or Nifty 500, and optional news or macro inputs.
48.    - Normalize identifiers, align timestamps, and create reproducible training snapshots.
49.    - Add a market-calendar-aware scheduler so daily inference runs only execute on supported NSE and BSE trading days.
50.    - Current implementation note: the MVP now ingests live Yahoo Finance chart data plus a cached generated snapshot fallback.
51. 
52. 3. Research feature layer
53.    - Create features for momentum, valuation, quality, volatility, liquidity, analyst revisions, and market regime.
54.    - Version features so experiments and backtests are repeatable.

1393. async function fetchChartPayload(yahooSymbol: string) {
1394.   const encodedSymbol = encodeURIComponent(yahooSymbol);
1395.   const urls = [
1396.     `https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`,
1397.     `https://query2.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/${encodedSymbol}?interval=1d&range=${LIVE_LOOKBACK_RANGE}`
1398.   ];
1399. 
1400.   for (const url of urls) {
1401.     try {
1402.       const response = await fetch(url, {
1403.         cache: "no-store",
1404.         headers: {
1405.           Accept: "application/json",
1406.           "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"
1407.         }
1408.       });
1409. 
1410.       if (!response.ok) {
1411.         continue;
1412.       }
1413. 
1414.       return (await response.json()) as YahooChartResponse;
1415.     } catch {
1416.       continue;
1417.     }
1418.   }
1419. 
1420.   return null;
1421. }
1422. 
1423. function barsFromPayload(payload: YahooChartResponse) {
1424.   const result = payload.chart?.result?.[0];
1425.   const quote = result?.indicators?.quote?.[0];
1426.   const timestamps = result?.timestamp ?? [];
1427.   const opens = quote?.open ?? [];
1428.   const highs = quote?.high ?? [];
1429.   const lows = quote?.low ?? [];
1430.   const closes = quote?.close ?? [];
1431.   const volumes = quote?.volume ?? [];
1432.   const bars: PriceBar[] = [];
1433. 
1434.   for (let index = 0; index < timestamps.length; index += 1) {
1435.     const open = opens[index];
1436.     const high = highs[index];
1437.     const low = lows[index];
1438.     const close = closes[index];
1439.     const volume = volumes[index];
1440. 
1441.     if (
1442.       open === null ||
1443.       high === null ||
1444.       low === null ||
1445.       close === null ||
1446.       volume === null ||
1447.       open === undefined ||
1448.       high === undefined ||
1449.       low === undefined ||
1450.       close === undefined ||
1451.       volume === undefined
1452.     ) {
1453.       continue;
1454.     }
1455. 
1456.     bars.push({
1457.       date: tradingDate(timestamps[index]),
1458.       timestamp: timestamps[index],
1459.       open,
1460.       high,
1461.       low,
1462.       close,
1463.       volume
1464.     });
1465.   }
1466. 
1467.   return {
1468.     bars,
1469.     meta: result?.meta ?? {}
1470.   };
1471. }
1472. 
1473. async function fetchSeries(entry: UniverseEntry) {
1474.   const payload = await fetchChartPayload(entry.yahooSymbol);
1475. 
1476.   if (!payload) {
1477.     return null;
1478.   }
1479. 
1480.   const parsed = barsFromPayload(payload);
1481. 
1482.   if (!parsed.bars.length) {
1483.     return null;
1484.   }
1485. 
1486.   const research = entry.symbol.startsWith("^")
1487.     ? {
1488.         companyName: companyNameFromMeta(parsed.meta, entry.fallbackCompanyName),
1489.         sector: entry.sector,
1490.         industry: entry.industry,
1491.         fundamentals: null,
1492.         sentiment: null
1493.       }
1494.     : await fetchCompanyResearch({
1495.         symbol: entry.symbol,
1496.         yahooSymbol: entry.yahooSymbol,
1497.         companyName: companyNameFromMeta(parsed.meta, entry.fallbackCompanyName),
1498.         sector: entry.sector,
1499.         industry: entry.industry
1500.       });
1501.   const companyName = research.companyName || companyNameFromMeta(parsed.meta, entry.fallbackCompanyName);
1502. 
1503.   return {
1504.     ...entry,
1505.     companyName,
1506.     bars: parsed.bars,
1507.     indexByDate: new Map(parsed.bars.map((bar, index) => [bar.date, index])),
1508.     meta: parsed.meta,
1509.     sector: research.sector || entry.sector,
1510.     industry: research.industry ?? entry.industry,
1511.     marketCapBucket: marketCapBucketFromCrore(research.fundamentals?.marketCapCrore ?? null),
1512.     liquidityTier: liquidityTierFromVolume(parsed.meta.regularMarketVolume ?? parsed.bars.at(-1)?.volume ?? null),
1513.     fundamentals: research.fundamentals,
1514.     sentiment: research.sentiment
1515.   } satisfies MarketSeries;
1516. }
1517. 
1518. function benchmarkReturn(benchmark: MarketSeries, date: string, lookback: number) {
1519.   const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(date);
1520. 
1521.   if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex < lookback) {
1522.     return null;
1523.   }
1524. 
1525.   return pctChange(
1526.     benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,
1527.     benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - lookback].close
1528.   );
1529. }
1530. 
1531. function snapshotMetrics(series: MarketSeries, benchmark: MarketSeries, index: number): SnapshotMetrics | null {
1532.   if (index < 200) {
1533.     return null;
1534.   }
1535. 
1536.   const bars = series.bars.slice(0, index + 1);
1537.   const current = bars.at(-1);
1538. 
1539.   if (!current) {
1540.     return null;
1541.   }
1542. 
1543.   const previous = bars.at(-2) ?? null;
1544.   const closes = bars.map((bar) => bar.close);
1545.   const highs = bars.map((bar) => bar.high);
1546.   const lows = bars.map((bar) => bar.low);
1547.   const volumes = bars.map((bar) => bar.volume);
1548.   const sma20 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 20);
1549.   const sma50 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 50);
1550.   const sma200 = simpleMovingAverage(closes, 200);
1551.   const sma20Slope5Pct = movingAverageSlope(closes, 20, 5);
1552.   const sma50Slope10Pct = movingAverageSlope(closes, 50, 10);
1553.   const atr14 = averageTrueRange(bars, 14);
1554.   const avgVolume20 = average(volumes.slice(-20));
1555.   const fiftyTwoWeekHigh = Math.max(...highs.slice(-252));
1556.   const fiftyTwoWeekLow = Math.min(...lows.slice(-252));
1557.   const recentHigh20 = recentExtreme(highs, 20, "high");
1558.   const recentHigh55 = recentExtreme(highs, 55, "high");
1559.   const recentHigh120 = recentExtreme(highs, 120, "high");
1560.   const currentCandleCloseLocation = closeLocationPct(current);
1561.   const currentCandleBodyPct = candleBodyPct(current);
1562.   const currentUpperWickPct = upperWickPct(current);
1563.   const currentLowerWickPct = lowerWickPct(current);
1564. 
1565.   return {
1566.     date: current.date,
1567.     companyName: series.companyName,
1568.     currentPrice: current.close,
1569.     previousClose: previous?.close ?? null,
1570.     sessionChangePct: previous ? pctChange(current.close, previous.close) : null,
1571.     openingGapPct: previous ? pctChange(current.open, previous.close) : null,
1572.     closeLocationPct: currentCandleCloseLocation,
1573.     candleBodyPct: currentCandleBodyPct,
1574.     upperWickPct: currentUpperWickPct,
1575.     lowerWickPct: currentLowerWickPct,
1576.     sma20,
1577.     sma50,
1578.     sma200,
1579.     sma20Slope5Pct,
1580.     sma50Slope10Pct,
1581.     rsi14: relativeStrengthIndex(closes, 14),
1582.     atr14,
1583.     atrPct: atr14 ? pctChange(current.close + atr14, current.close) : null,
1584.     return20: closes.length > 20 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 21]) : null,
1585.     return60: closes.length > 60 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 61]) : null,
1586.     return120: closes.length > 120 ? pctChange(current.close, closes[closes.length - 121]) : null,
1587.     breakout20Pct: recentHigh20 !== null ? pctChange(current.close, recentHigh20) : null,
1588.     breakout55Pct: recentHigh55 !== null ? pctChange(current.close, recentHigh55) : null,
1589.     breakout120Pct: recentHigh120 !== null ? pctChange(current.close, recentHigh120) : null,
1590.     rangeCompression5v20: rangeCompressionRatio(bars, 5, 20),
1591.     benchmarkReturn20: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 20),
1592.     benchmarkReturn60: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 60),
1593.     benchmarkReturn120: benchmarkReturn(benchmark, current.date, 120),
1594.     relativeStrength20: null,
1595.     relativeStrength60: null,
1596.     relativeStrength120: null,
1597.     volatility20: annualizedVolatility(closes, 20),
1598.     avgVolume20,
1599.     volumeRatio: avgVolume20 ? current.volume / avgVolume20 : null,
1600.     rangePosition: rangePosition(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),
1601.     fiftyTwoWeekHigh,
1602.     fiftyTwoWeekLow,
1603.     distanceFromHighPct: pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekHigh),
1604.     distanceFromLowPct: fiftyTwoWeekLow ? pctChange(current.close, fiftyTwoWeekLow) : null,
1605.     benchmarkSessionChangePct: (() => {
1606.       const benchmarkIndex = benchmark.indexByDate.get(current.date);
1607. 
1608.       if (benchmarkIndex === undefined || benchmarkIndex === 0) {
1609.         return null;
1610.       }
1611. 
1612.       return pctChange(
1613.         benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex].close,
1614.         benchmark.bars[benchmarkIndex - 1].close
1615.       );
1616.     })(),
1617.     currentVolume: current.volume
1618.   };
1619. }
1620. 
1621. function enrichRelativeStrength(metrics: SnapshotMetrics): SnapshotMetrics {
1622.   return {
1623.     ...metrics,
1624.     relativeStrength20:
1625.       metrics.return20 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn20 !== null
1626.         ? metrics.return20 - metrics.benchmarkReturn20
1627.         : null,
1628.     relativeStrength60:
1629.       metrics.return60 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn60 !== null
1630.         ? metrics.return60 - metrics.benchmarkReturn60
1631.         : null,
1632.     relativeStrength120:
1633.       metrics.return120 !== null && metrics.benchmarkReturn120 !== null
1634.         ? metrics.return120 - metrics.benchmarkReturn120
1635.         : null
1636.   };
1637. }
1638. 
1639. function normalizeRsi(rsi: number | null) {
1640.   if (rsi === null) {
1641.     return 0.5;
1642.   }
1643. 
1644.   return 1 - clamp(Math.abs(rsi - 56) / 24, 0, 1);
1645. }
1646. 
1647. function liquidityScore(liquidityTier: string) {
1648.   return liquidityTier === "Tier 1" ? 1 : 0.7;
1649. }
1650. 
1651. function marketCapScore(marketCapBucket: string) {
1652.   return marketCapBucket === "Large Cap" ? 1 : 0.75;
1653. }
1654. 
1655. function scoreForHorizon(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: UniverseEntry, horizon: HorizonId) {
1656.   switch (horizon) {
1657.     case "single_day":
1658.       return weightedScore([
1659.         [0.24, scaleRange(metrics.sessionChangePct, -2.8, 4.8)],
1660.         [0.2, scaleRange(metrics.relativeStrength20, -5, 8)],
1661.         [0.16, scaleRange(metrics.volumeRatio, 0.8, 2.2)],
1662.         [0.14, metrics.sma20 && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.sma20 ? 1 : 0],
1663.         [0.1, metrics.previousClose && metrics.currentPrice > metrics.previousClose ? 1 : 0],
1664.         [0.08, normalizeRsi(metrics.rsi14)],
1665.         [0.08, inverseScale(metrics.atrPct, 1.2, 4.8)]
```

309. 
310. function horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
311.   return percentText(horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon));
312. }
313. 
314. function horizonRelativeStrengthSignalName(horizon: HorizonId) {
315.   switch (horizon) {
316.     case "single_day":
317.       return "Next-day relative strength";
318.     case "swing":
319.       return "20D relative strength";
320.     case "position":
321.       return "60D relative strength";
322.     case "long_term":
323.       return "120D relative strength";
324.   }
325. }
326. 
327. function horizonSummary(entry: MarketSeries, metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
328.   const momentumLabel = horizonMomentumLabel(metrics, horizon);
329.   const strengthLabel = horizonRelativeStrengthLabel(metrics, horizon);
330.   const newsTone = entry.sentiment?.overall?.toLowerCase() ?? "neutral";
331. 
332.   switch (horizon) {
333.     case "single_day":
334.       return `${metrics.companyName} is shaping up as a next-session tactical idea with ${momentumLabel} session strength, ${strengthLabel} short-term relative strength versus Nifty 50, and a ${newsTone} news tone.`;
335.     case "swing":
336.       return `${metrics.companyName} is holding a constructive short-term trend with ${momentumLabel} recent momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus Nifty 50, and a ${newsTone} news tone.`;
337.     case "position":
338.       return `${metrics.companyName} ranks well on the medium-term trend stack, combining ${momentumLabel} price follow-through, ${strengthLabel} benchmark outperformance, and improving fundamental support.`;
339.     case "long_term":
340.       return `${metrics.companyName} remains a longer-duration accumulation candidate with ${momentumLabel} multi-month momentum, ${strengthLabel} relative strength versus the index, and supportive fundamental quality markers.`;
341.   }
342. }
343. 
344. function horizonBreakoutValue(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId) {
345.   switch (horizon) {
346.     case "single_day":
347.     case "swing":
348.       return metrics.breakout20Pct;
349.     case "position":
350.       return metrics.breakout55Pct;
351.     case "long_term":
352.       return metrics.breakout120Pct;
353.   }
354. }
355. 
356. function horizonBreakoutWindowLabel(horizon: HorizonId) {
357.   switch (horizon) {
358.     case "single_day":
359.     case "swing":
360.       return "20-day";
361.     case "position":
362.       return "55-day";
363.     case "long_term":
364.       return "120-day";
365.   }
366. }
367. 
368. function buildTechnicalAnalysisDrivers(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId): AnalysisDriver[] {
369.   const breakout = horizonBreakoutValue(metrics, horizon);
370.   const breakoutWindow = horizonBreakoutWindowLabel(horizon);
371.   let breakoutImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
372.   let breakoutDetail =
373.     "The model could not confirm a clean breakout state, so it is leaning more on the broader trend stack than on a fresh trigger level.";
374. 
375.   if (breakout !== null) {
376.     if (breakout >= 0) {
377.       breakoutImpact = "positive";
378.       breakoutDetail = `Price is ${percentText(breakout)} above the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. That matters because a confirmed breakout usually means nearby supply has already been absorbed, which improves follow-through odds.`;
379.     } else if (breakout >= -1.5) {
380.       breakoutImpact = "neutral";
381.       breakoutDetail = `Price is ${percentText(breakout)} below the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. The setup is close to a trigger zone, but it still needs a clean push through resistance before the move becomes more reliable.`;
382.     } else {
383.       breakoutImpact = "negative";
384.       breakoutDetail = `Price is still ${percentText(breakout)} below the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. That keeps the stock under resistance, so upside continuation is less dependable until that ceiling is cleared.`;
385.     }
386.   }
387. 
388.   const closeLocation = metrics.closeLocationPct;
389.   const upperWick = metrics.upperWickPct;
390.   const candleBody = metrics.candleBodyPct;
391.   let candleImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
392.   let candleDetail =
393.     "The latest candle structure is mixed, so the model is not treating the most recent session as a decisive control candle.";
394. 
395.   if (closeLocation !== null && upperWick !== null && candleBody !== null) {
396.     if (closeLocation >= 65 && upperWick <= 25 && candleBody >= 45) {
397.       candleImpact = "positive";
398.       candleDetail = `The latest candle closed near the top of the range (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%) with only ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because strong closes show buyers kept control into the end of the session.`;
399.     } else if (closeLocation < 42 || upperWick > 45) {
400.       candleImpact = "negative";
401.       candleDetail = `The latest candle finished weakly (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% close location) with ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because sellers were able to push price away from intraday highs, which often leads to failed follow-through.`;
402.     } else {
403.       candleDetail = `The latest candle closed at ${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% of its range with a ${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}% real body. That gives the setup some balance, but not enough to treat the last bar as a strong confirmation candle on its own.`;
404.     }
405.   }
406. 
407.   const relativeStrength = horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon);
408.   const volumeRatio = metrics.volumeRatio;
409.   let participationImpact: AnalysisDriver["impact"] = "neutral";
410.   let participationDetail =
411.     "Participation signals are mixed, so the model is not assuming broad institutional sponsorship behind the current move yet.";
412. 
413.   if (relativeStrength !== null && volumeRatio !== null) {
414.     if (relativeStrength > 0 && volumeRatio >= 1.05) {
415.       participationImpact = "positive";
416.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} and volume is ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because breakouts work better when the stock is already outperforming the benchmark and participation is expanding.`;
417.     } else if (relativeStrength < 0 || volumeRatio < 0.9) {
418.       participationImpact = "negative";
419.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} while volume is only ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because weaker participation makes rallies easier to fade and harder to sustain.`;
420.     } else {
421.       participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;
422.     }
423.   }
424. 
425.   return [
426.     { area: "technical", impact: breakoutImpact, title: "Breakout position", detail: breakoutDetail },
427.     { area: "technical", impact: candleImpact, title: "Candle quality", detail: candleDetail },
428.     { area: "technical", impact: participationImpact, title: "Participation", detail: participationDetail }
429.   ];
430. }
```

</details>

1748. function technicalScoreBreakdown(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, entry: UniverseEntry, horizon: HorizonId) {
1749.   const factorScore = scoreForHorizon(metrics, entry, horizon);
1750.   const chartScore = chartAnalysisScore(metrics, horizon);
1751. 
1752.   switch (horizon) {
1753.     case "single_day":
1754.       return {
1755.         factorScore,
1756.         chartScore,
1757.         technicalScore: roundMetric(factorScore * 0.58 + chartScore * 0.42)
1758.       };
1759.     case "swing":
1760.       return {
1761.         factorScore,
1762.         chartScore,
1763.         technicalScore: roundMetric(factorScore * 0.64 + chartScore * 0.36)
1764.       };
1765.     case "position":
1766.       return {
1767.         factorScore,
1768.         chartScore,
1769.         technicalScore: roundMetric(factorScore * 0.7 + chartScore * 0.3)
1770.       };
1771.     case "long_term":
1772.       return {
1773.         factorScore,
1774.         chartScore,
1775.         technicalScore: roundMetric(factorScore * 0.76 + chartScore * 0.24)
1776.       };
1777.   }
1778. }

842. function minimumLiveSymbolsRequired() {
843.   return Math.max(25, Math.floor(MARKET_UNIVERSE.length * MINIMUM_LIVE_COVERAGE_RATIO));
844. }
845. 
846. async function mapWithConcurrency<T, TResult>(
847.   items: T[],
848.   concurrency: number,
849.   mapper: (item: T, index: number) => Promise<TResult>
850. ) {
851.   const results = new Array<TResult>(items.length);
852.   let nextIndex = 0;
853. 
854.   async function worker() {
855.     while (true) {
856.       const index = nextIndex;
857.       nextIndex += 1;
858. 
859.       if (index >= items.length) {
860.         return;
861.       }
862. 
863.       results[index] = await mapper(items[index], index);
864.     }
865.   }
866. 
867.   const workerCount = Math.max(1, Math.min(concurrency, items.length));
868.   await Promise.all(Array.from({ length: workerCount }, () => worker()));
869. 
870.   return results;
871. }
872. 
873. function pctChange(current: number, previous: number) {
874.   if (!Number.isFinite(current) || !Number.isFinite(previous) || previous === 0) {
875.     return null;
876.   }
877. 
878.   return ((current - previous) / previous) * 100;
879. }
880. 
881. function scaleRange(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
882.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
883.     return 0.5;
884.   }
885. 
886.   if (max === min) {
887.     return 0.5;
888.   }
889. 
890.   return clamp((value - min) / (max - min), 0, 1);
891. }
892. 
893. function inverseScale(value: number | null, min: number, max: number) {
894.   return 1 - scaleRange(value, min, max);
895. }
896. 
897. function percentText(value: number | null) {
898.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
899.     return "n/a";
900.   }
901. 
902.   const prefix = value > 0 ? "+" : "";
903.   return `${prefix}${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
904. }
905. 
906. function ratioText(value: number | null) {
907.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
908.     return "n/a";
909.   }
910. 
911.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(2)}x`;
912. }
913. 
914. function priceText(value: number | null) {
915.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
916.     return "n/a";
917.   }
918. 
919.   return `Rs ${roundPrice(value).toFixed(2)}`;
920. }
921. 
922. function compactNumber(value: number | null) {
923.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
924.     return "n/a";
925.   }
926. 
927.   return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
928.     notation: "compact",
929.     maximumFractionDigits: 1
930.   }).format(value);
931. }
932. 
933. function croreText(value: number | null) {
934.   if (value === null || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
935.     return "n/a";
936.   }
937. 
938.   return `${new Intl.NumberFormat("en-IN", {
939.     maximumFractionDigits: 0
940.   }).format(value)} Cr`;
941. }
942. 
943. function marketCapBucketFromCrore(value: number | null) {
944.   if (value === null) {
945.     return "Large Cap";
946.   }
947. 
948.   if (value >= 100000) {
949.     return "Large Cap";
950.   }
951. 
952.   if (value >= 30000) {
953.     return "Mid Cap";
954.   }
955. 
956.   return "Small Cap";
957. }
958. 
959. function liquidityTierFromVolume(volume: number | null) {
960.   if (volume === null) {
961.     return "Tier 2";
962.   }
963. 
964.   if (volume >= 5000000) {
965.     return "Tier 1";
966.   }
967. 
968.   if (volume >= 1000000) {
969.     return "Tier 2";
970.   }
971. 
972.   return "Tier 3";
973. }
974. 
975. function simpleMovingAverage(values: number[], period: number) {
976.   if (values.length < period) {
977.     return null;
978.   }
979. 
980.   return average(values.slice(-period));
981. }
982. 
983. function annualizedVolatility(closes: number[], period: number) {
984.   if (closes.length < period + 1) {
985.     return null;
986.   }
987. 
988.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
989.   const returns = window.slice(1).map((close, index) => (close - window[index]) / window[index]);
990.   const mean = average(returns);
991. 
992.   if (mean === null) {
993.     return null;
994.   }
995. 
996.   const variance =
997.     returns.reduce((total, value) => total + (value - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;
998. 
999.   return Math.sqrt(variance) * Math.sqrt(252) * 100;
1000. }
1001. 
1002. function relativeStrengthIndex(closes: number[], period: number) {
1003.   if (closes.length <= period) {
1004.     return null;
1005.   }
1006. 
1007.   let gains = 0;
1008.   let losses = 0;
1009.   const window = closes.slice(-(period + 1));
1010. 
1011.   for (let index = 1; index < window.length; index += 1) {
1012.     const change = window[index] - window[index - 1];
1013. 
1014.     if (change > 0) {
1015.       gains += change;
1016.     } else {
1017.       losses += Math.abs(change);
1018.     }
1019.   }
1020. 
1021.   if (losses === 0) {
1022.     return 100;
1023.   }
1024. 
1025.   const averageGain = gains / period;
1026.   const averageLoss = losses / period;
1027.   const relativeStrength = averageGain / averageLoss;
1028. 
1029.   return 100 - 100 / (1 + relativeStrength);
1030. }
1031. 
1032. function averageTrueRange(bars: PriceBar[], period: number) {
1033.   if (bars.length <= period) {
1034.     return null;
1035.   }

1062. function recentExtreme(values: number[], lookback: number, mode: "high" | "low") {
1063.   if (values.length <= lookback) {
1064.     return null;
1065.   }
1066. 
1067.   const window = values.slice(-(lookback + 1), -1);
1068. 
1069.   if (!window.length) {
1070.     return null;
1071.   }
1072. 
1073.   return mode === "high" ? Math.max(...window) : Math.min(...window);
1074. }
1075. 
1076. function movingAverageSlope(values: number[], period: number, lookback: number) {
1077.   if (values.length < period + lookback) {
1078.     return null;
1079.   }
1080. 
1081.   const currentAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values, period);
1082.   const priorAverage = simpleMovingAverage(values.slice(0, -lookback), period);
1083. 
1084.   if (currentAverage === null || priorAverage === null) {
1085.     return null;
1086.   }
1087. 
1088.   return pctChange(currentAverage, priorAverage);
1089. }
1090. 
1091. function averageBarRangePct(bars: PriceBar[]) {
1092.   const percentages = bars
1093.     .map((bar) => (bar.close !== 0 ? ((bar.high - bar.low) / bar.close) * 100 : null))
1094.     .filter((value): value is number => value !== null && Number.isFinite(value));
1095. 
1096.   return average(percentages);
1097. }
1098. 
1099. function rangeCompressionRatio(bars: PriceBar[], shortPeriod: number, longPeriod: number) {
1100.   if (bars.length < longPeriod) {
1101.     return null;
1102.   }
1103. 
1104.   const shortAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-shortPeriod));
1105.   const longAverage = averageBarRangePct(bars.slice(-longPeriod));
1106. 
1107.   if (shortAverage === null || longAverage === null || longAverage === 0) {
1108.     return null;
1109.   }
1110. 
1111.   return shortAverage / longAverage;
1112. }
1113. 
1114. function closeLocationPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1115.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1116. 
1117.   if (range <= 0) {
1118.     return null;
1119.   }
1120. 
1121.   return ((bar.close - bar.low) / range) * 100;
1122. }

1125.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1126.

1129.   }
1130. 
1131.   return (Math.abs(bar.close - bar.open) / range) * 100;

1133. 
1134. function upperWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1135.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1136. 
1137.   if (range <= 0) {
1138.     return null;
1139.   }
1140. 
1141.   return ((bar.high - Math.max(bar.open, bar.close)) / range) * 100;
1142. }
1143. 
1144. function lowerWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {
1145.   const range = bar.high - bar.low;
1146. 
1147.   if (range <= 0) {
1148.     return null;
1149.   }
1150. 
1151.   return ((Math.min(bar.open, bar.close) - bar.low) / range) * 100;
1152. }
1153. 
1154. function tradingDate(timestamp: number) {

1156. }
1157. 
1158. function publishedTimestamp(date: string) {
1159.   return `${date}T15:45:00+05:30`;

1162. function signalScoreText(value: number) {
1163.   return `${roundMetric(value).toFixed(1)}/100`;

null;\n+  const signal = signalSeries.at(-1) ?? null;\n+\n+  return {\n+    line,\n+    signal,\n+    histogram: line !== null && signal !== null ? line - signal : null\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function bollingerMetrics(closes: number[], period = 20, deviationMultiple = 2) {\n+  if (closes.length < period) {\n+    return {\n+      middle: null,\n+      upper: null,\n+      lower: null,\n+      bandwidthPct: null,\n+      positionPct: null\n+    };\n+  }\n+\n+  const window = closes.slice(-period);\n+  const middle = average(window);\n+  const standardDeviationValue = standardDeviation(window);\n+  const current = window.at(-1) ?? null;\n+\n+  if (middle === null || standardDeviationValue === null || current === null) {\n+    return {\n+      middle: null,\n+      upper: null,\n+      lower: null,\n+      bandwidthPct: null,\n+      positionPct: null\n+    };\n+  }\n+\n+  const upper = middle + standardDeviationValue * deviationMultiple;\n+  const lower = middle - standardDeviationValue * deviationMultiple;\n+  const bandwidthPct = middle !== 0 ? ((upper - lower) / middle) * 100 : null;\n+  const positionPct = upper > lower ? clamp(((current - lower) / (upper - lower)) * 100, 0, 100) : null;\n+\n+  return {\n+    middle,\n+    upper,\n+    lower,\n+    bandwidthPct,\n+    positionPct\n+  };\n+}\n+\n+function volumeTrendPct(volumes: number[], period = 20) {\n+  if (volumes.length < period * 2) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  const recentAverage = average(volumes.slice(-period));\n+  const priorAverage = average(volumes.slice(-(period * 2), -period));\n+\n+  if (recentAverage === null || priorAverage === null || priorAverage === 0) {\n+    return null;\n+  }\n+\n+  return ((recentAverage - priorAverage) / priorAverage) * 100;\n+}\n@@\n function lowerWickPct(bar: PriceBar) {\n   const range = bar.high - bar.low;\n@@\n \n   return ((Math.min(bar.open, bar.close) - bar.low) / range) * 100;\n }\n+\n+function detectCandlestickPattern(bar: PriceBar, previous: PriceBar | null) {\n+  const body = candleBodyPct(bar);\n+  const closeLocation = closeLocationPct(bar);\n+  const upperWick = upperWickPct(bar);\n+  const lowerWick = lowerWickPct(bar);\n+\n+  if (\n+    previous &&\n+    bar.close > bar.open &&\n+    previous.close < previous.open &&\n+    bar.open <= previous.close &&\n+    bar.close >= previous.open\n+  ) {\n+    return \"Bullish engulfing\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (\n+    previous &&\n+    bar.close < bar.open &&\n+    previous.close > previous.open &&\n+    bar.open >= previous.close &&\n+    bar.close <= previous.open\n+  ) {\n+    return \"Bearish engulfing\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (lowerWick !== null && body !== null && closeLocation !== null && lowerWick >= 55 && body <= 35 && closeLocation >= 60) {\n+    return \"Hammer\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (upperWick !== null && body !== null && closeLocation !== null && upperWick >= 55 && body <= 35 && closeLocation <= 40) {\n+    return \"Shooting star\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (\n+    body !== null &&\n+    upperWick !== null &&\n+    lowerWick !== null &&\n+    bar.close > bar.open &&\n+    body >= 70 &&\n+    upperWick <= 10 &&\n+    lowerWick <= 15\n+  ) {\n+    return \"Bullish marubozu\";\n+  }\n+\n+  if (\n+    body !== null &&\n+    upperWick !== null &&\n+    lowerWick !== null &&\n+    bar.close < bar.open &&\n+    body >= 70 &&\n+    upperWick <= 15 &&\n+    lowerWick <= 10\n+  ) {\n+    return \"Bearish marubozu\";\n+  }\n+\n+  return null;\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\copilot\\apps\\web\\lib\\recommendation-data.ts\n@@\n function buildTechnicalAnalysisDrivers(metrics: SnapshotMetrics, horizon: HorizonId): AnalysisDriver[] {\n   const breakout = horizonBreakoutValue(metrics, horizon);\n   const breakoutWindow = horizonBreakoutWindowLabel(horizon);\n   let breakoutImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n@@\n       breakoutImpact = \"negative\";\n       breakoutDetail = `Price is still ${percentText(breakout)} below the recent ${breakoutWindow} high. That keeps the stock under resistance, so upside continuation is less dependable until that ceiling is cleared.`;\n     }\n   }\n \n-  const closeLocation = metrics.closeLocationPct;\n-  const upperWick = metrics.upperWickPct;\n-  const candleBody = metrics.candleBodyPct;\n-  let candleImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n-  let candleDetail =\n-    \"The latest candle structure is mixed, so the model is not treating the most recent session as a decisive control candle.\";\n-\n-  if (closeLocation !== null && upperWick !== null && candleBody !== null) {\n-    if (closeLocation >= 65 && upperWick <= 25 && candleBody >= 45) {\n-      candleImpact = \"positive\";\n-      candleDetail = `The latest candle closed near the top of the range (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%) with only ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because strong closes show buyers kept control into the end of the session.`;\n-    } else if (closeLocation < 42 || upperWick > 45) {\n-      candleImpact = \"negative\";\n-      candleDetail = `The latest candle finished weakly (${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% close location) with ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That matters because sellers were able to push price away from intraday highs, which often leads to failed follow-through.`;\n-    } else {\n-      candleDetail = `The latest candle closed at ${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% of its range with a ${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}% real body. That gives the setup some balance, but not enough to treat the last bar as a strong confirmation candle on its own.`;\n-    }\n-  }\n+  let trendImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let trendDetail =\n+    \"The moving-average stack and momentum trend are balanced, so the broader tape is not giving a strong directional push yet.\";\n+\n+  if (metrics.trendClassification === \"Bullish\") {\n+    trendImpact = \"positive\";\n+    trendDetail = `Trend classification is bullish because price is holding above key moving averages, the 50DMA slope is ${percentText(metrics.sma50Slope10Pct)}, and MACD histogram is ${percentText(metrics.macdHistogramPct)}. That matters because aligned trend and momentum usually keep pullbacks shallower and make breakout follow-through more durable.`;\n+  } else if (metrics.trendClassification === \"Bearish\") {\n+    trendImpact = \"negative\";\n+    trendDetail = `Trend classification is bearish because price is lagging key moving averages, the 50DMA slope is ${percentText(metrics.sma50Slope10Pct)}, and MACD histogram is ${percentText(metrics.macdHistogramPct)}. That matters because weak trend alignment makes rallies easier to sell and harder to sustain.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  const rsiValue = metrics.rsi14;\n+  const macdHistogram = metrics.macdHistogramPct;\n+  const bollingerPosition = metrics.bollingerPositionPct;\n+  const bollingerBandwidth = metrics.bollingerBandwidthPct;\n+  let momentumImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let momentumDetail =\n+    \"RSI, MACD, and Bollinger-band position are balanced, so momentum is not stretched in either direction.\";\n+\n+  if (\n+    (rsiValue !== null && rsiValue >= 54 && rsiValue <= 68) &&\n+    (macdHistogram !== null && macdHistogram > 0) &&\n+    (bollingerPosition !== null && bollingerPosition >= 55 && bollingerPosition <= 88)\n+  ) {\n+    momentumImpact = \"positive\";\n+    momentumDetail = `RSI is ${rsiValue !== null ? roundMetric(rsiValue).toFixed(1) : \"n/a\"}, MACD histogram is ${percentText(macdHistogram)}, and price is sitting at ${bollingerPosition !== null ? `${roundMetric(bollingerPosition).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} of the Bollinger band range with bandwidth ${percentText(bollingerBandwidth)}. That matters because constructive momentum with room left inside the band often supports continuation without forcing the trade into an immediately overbought state.`;\n+  } else if (\n+    (rsiValue !== null && rsiValue < 45) ||\n+    (macdHistogram !== null && macdHistogram < 0) ||\n+    (bollingerPosition !== null && bollingerPosition < 45)\n+  ) {\n+    momentumImpact = \"negative\";\n+    momentumDetail = `RSI is ${rsiValue !== null ? roundMetric(rsiValue).toFixed(1) : \"n/a\"}, MACD histogram is ${percentText(macdHistogram)}, and price is sitting at ${bollingerPosition !== null ? `${roundMetric(bollingerPosition).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} of the Bollinger band range with bandwidth ${percentText(bollingerBandwidth)}. That matters because fading momentum usually shows up before breakouts fail or trends flatten.`;\n+  }\n+\n+  const pattern = metrics.candlestickPattern;\n+  const closeLocation = metrics.closeLocationPct;\n+  const upperWick = metrics.upperWickPct;\n+  const candleBody = metrics.candleBodyPct;\n+  let candleImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n+  let candleDetail =\n+    \"The latest candle structure is mixed, so the model is not treating the most recent bar as a decisive confirmation candle.\";\n+\n+  if (pattern === \"Bullish engulfing\" || pattern === \"Bullish marubozu\" || pattern === \"Hammer\") {\n+    candleImpact = \"positive\";\n+    candleDetail = `The latest candle registered a ${pattern.toLowerCase()} with ${closeLocation !== null ? `${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} close location, ${candleBody !== null ? `${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} body size, and ${upperWick !== null ? `${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} upper wick rejection. That matters because bullish candle patterns often show buyers regaining control near the end of the session.`;\n+  } else if (\n+    pattern === \"Bearish engulfing\" ||\n+    pattern === \"Bearish marubozu\" ||\n+    pattern === \"Shooting star\"\n+  ) {\n+    candleImpact = \"negative\";\n+    candleDetail = `The latest candle registered a ${pattern.toLowerCase()} with ${closeLocation !== null ? `${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} close location, ${candleBody !== null ? `${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} body size, and ${upperWick !== null ? `${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}%` : \"n/a\"} upper wick rejection. That matters because bearish candle patterns often signal supply stepping in near resistance.`;\n+  } else if (closeLocation !== null && upperWick !== null && candleBody !== null) {\n+    candleDetail = `No classic reversal pattern was detected. The latest candle closed at ${roundMetric(closeLocation).toFixed(1)}% of its range with a ${roundMetric(candleBody).toFixed(1)}% real body and ${roundMetric(upperWick).toFixed(1)}% upper wick rejection. That still matters because candle structure shows whether buyers kept control into the close.`;\n+  }\n \n   const relativeStrength = horizonRelativeStrengthValue(metrics, horizon);\n   const volumeRatio = metrics.volumeRatio;\n+  const volumeTrend = metrics.volumeTrend20Pct;\n   let participationImpact: AnalysisDriver[\"impact\"] = \"neutral\";\n   let participationDetail =\n-    \"Participation signals are mixed, so the model is not assuming broad institutional sponsorship behind the current move yet.\";\n+    \"Participation signals are mixed, so the model is not assuming broad institutional sponsorship behind the current move yet.\";\n \n-  if (relativeStrength !== null && volumeRatio !== null) {\n-    if (relativeStrength > 0 && volumeRatio >= 1.05) {\n+  if (relativeStrength !== null && volumeRatio !== null) {\n+    if (relativeStrength > 0 && volumeRatio >= 1.05 && (volumeTrend ?? 0) >= 0) {\n       participationImpact = \"positive\";\n-      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} and volume is ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because breakouts work better when the stock is already outperforming the benchmark and participation is expanding.`;\n-    } else if (relativeStrength < 0 || volumeRatio < 0.9) {\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)}, current volume is ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average, and the recent 20-day average volume trend is ${percentText(volumeTrend)}. That matters because breakouts work best when the stock is already outperforming the benchmark and participation is broadening rather than drying up.`;\n+    } else if (relativeStrength < 0 || volumeRatio < 0.9 || (volumeTrend ?? 0) < -5) {\n       participationImpact = \"negative\";\n-      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} while volume is only ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That matters because weaker participation makes rallies easier to fade and harder to sustain.`;\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)}, current volume is only ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average, and the recent 20-day average volume trend is ${percentText(volumeTrend)}. That matters because weaker participation makes rallies easier to fade and harder to sustain.`;\n     } else {\n-      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;\n+      participationDetail = `Relative strength versus Nifty 50 is ${percentText(relativeStrength)} with current volume at ${ratioText(volumeRatio)} of the 20-day average and a ${percentText(volumeTrend)} 20-day volume trend. That gives the move some support, but not enough to treat it as a high-energy expansion yet.`;\n     }\n   }\n \n   return [\n     { area: \"technical\", impact: breakoutImpact, title: \"Breakout position\", detail: breakoutDetail },\n+    { area: \"technical\", impact: trendImpact, title: \"Trend classification\", detail: trendDetail },\n+    { area: \"technical\", impact: momentumImpact, title: \"Momentum stack\", detail: momentumDetail },\n     { area: \"technical\", impact: candleImpact, title: \"Candle quality\", detail: candleDetail },\n     { area: \"technical\", impact: participationImpact, title: \"Participation\", detail: participationDetail }\n   ];\n }\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 476m 23s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Done:** I added the tests, finished step 2, finished step 3, and surfaced **sector** more consistently across the stock views.

- **Step 2:** richer fundamentals now feed scoring and reasoning: **P/E, P/B, ROE, ROCE, debt/equity, earnings growth, operating cash flow, free cash flow**, plus **sector-aware peer context**.
- **Step 3:** the technical engine now includes **MACD, Bollinger-band context, stronger volume-trend analysis, candlestick pattern labels, and clearer bullish / neutral / bearish trend classification**.
- **UI/API:** stock detail fundamentals were expanded, sector is shown on additional stock-history surfaces, the FastAPI stock schema now carries richer stock data, and the Python test suite now covers analytics, payload normalization, and direct API behavior.

**Blocker:** the repo’s existing test command (`npm run test:api`) could not be executed here because this environment is missing **PowerShell 6+ (`pwsh`)**.

<sub>⏱️ 479m 25s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**No code fix is needed.** The blocker is an **environment fix**: this machine is missing **PowerShell 7+ (`pwsh.exe`)**.

1. Install PowerShell 7:
   ```powershell
   winget install --id Microsoft.PowerShell --source winget
   ```
   Or install it manually from: https://aka.ms/powershell

2. Restart your terminal / VS Code.

3. Confirm it works:
   ```powershell
   pwsh --version
   ```

4. Then rerun:
   ```powershell
   cd C:\copilot
   npm run test:api
   ```

If you want, I can also give you the **manual test command** that works without `npm run test:api`.

}

<sub>⏱️ 481m 33s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Manual test command:** run this from `C:\copilot` in **Command Prompt**, PowerShell, or VS Code terminal:

```bat
py -3 -m unittest discover -s services\api\tests -t services\api
```

If `py` isn’t available, use:

```bat
python -m unittest discover -s services\api\tests -t services\api
```

**Remaining steps:** from the v1 work we’ve been doing, steps 1-3 are done. The practical remaining steps are:

1. **News / sentiment upgrade** — stronger stock-tagged and sector-aware news reasoning.
2. **Backtesting / evaluation** — compare recommendations vs outcomes more systematically.  
   **This is the next clean ready todo in the tracker.**
3. **Scheduled daily runs** — automate end-of-day refresh and batch generation.
4. **Monitoring / health / logs** — job success, failures, stale-data alerts.
5. **API hardening** — expand the existing FastAPI service further if you want more external/API-first usage.
6. **TradingView charts** — still marked **blocked**, so that should be treated as a separate follow-up after the core pipeline items.

So the best next implementation step is **backtesting and evaluation**.

1. import unittest
2. from unittest.mock import patch
3. 
4. from fastapi import HTTPException
5. 
6. from app import main as api_main
7. from app.analytics import build_daily_performance, build_stock_history
8. from app.main import load_dataset
9. 
10. 
11. class RecommendationAnalyticsTests(unittest.TestCase):
12.     @classmethod
13.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
14.         cls.dataset = load_dataset(prefer_generated=False)
15. 
16.     def test_swing_daily_performance_counts_successes_and_failures(self) -> None:
17.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "swing")
18.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
19. 
20.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 2)
21.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
22.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 3)
23.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 0)
24.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 66.67, places=2)
25. 
26.     def test_position_success_rate_ignores_open_trades(self) -> None:
27.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "position")
28.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
29. 
30.         self.assertEqual(latest.successful, 1)
31.         self.assertEqual(latest.failed, 1)
32.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 1)
33.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 2)
34.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest.successRate or 0, 50.0, places=2)
35. 
36.     def test_long_term_daily_performance_is_pending_when_nothing_is_closed(self) -> None:
37.         performance = build_daily_performance(self.dataset, "long_term")
38.         latest = next(row for row in performance if row.batchDate == "2026-04-14")
39. 
40.         self.assertEqual(latest.open, 3)
41.         self.assertEqual(latest.closed, 0)
42.         self.assertIsNone(latest.successRate)
43.         self.assertIsNone(latest.averageReturnPct)
44. 
45.     def test_stock_history_filters_by_symbol_in_latest_first_order(self) -> None:
46.         history = build_stock_history(self.dataset, "RELIANCE", "swing")
47. 
48.         self.assertEqual(
49.             [entry.batchDate for entry in history],
50.             ["2026-04-14", "2026-04-11", "2026-04-10"],
51.         )
52.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.symbol == "RELIANCE" for entry in history))
53. 
54.     def test_dataset_normalization_adds_single_day_profile(self) -> None:
55.         self.assertIn("single_day", self.dataset.currentBatch.recommendations[0].profiles)
56.         self.assertEqual(self.dataset.profiles[0].id, "single_day")
57. 
58.     def test_dataset_normalization_adds_single_day_history_profile(self) -> None:
59.         profile = self.dataset.history[0].recommendations[0].profiles["single_day"]
60. 
61.         self.assertTrue(
62.             profile.summary.startswith("Fallback single-day history derived from the saved swing result.")
63.         )
64. 
65. 
66. class RecommendationApiTests(unittest.TestCase):
67.     @classmethod
68.     def setUpClass(cls) -> None:
69.         cls.dataset = load_dataset(prefer_generated=False)
70. 
71.     def test_healthcheck_returns_ok_status(self) -> None:
72.         self.assertEqual(api_main.healthcheck(), {"status": "ok"})
73. 
74.     def test_recommendations_endpoint_returns_normalized_single_day_rows(self) -> None:
75.         with patch.object(api_main, "load_dataset", return_value=self.dataset):
76.             payload = api_main.list_recommendations("single_day")
77. 
78.         self.assertEqual(payload["selectedHorizon"], "single_day")
79.         self.assertGreater(len(payload["recommendations"]), 0)
80.         first = payload["recommendations"][0]
81.         self.assertEqual(first["horizon"], "single_day")
82.         self.assertIn("symbol", first)
83.         self.assertIn("sector", first)
84.         self.assertIn("summary", first)
85.         self.assertIn("riskReward", first)
86. 
87.     def test_daily_performance_endpoint_uses_requested_horizon(self) -> None:
88.         with patch.object(api_main, "load_dataset", return_value=self.dataset):
89.             payload = api_main.get_daily_performance("position")
90. 
91.         self.assertEqual(payload["selectedHorizon"], "position")
92.         latest = next(row for row in payload["performance"] if row["batchDate"] == "2026-04-14")
93.         self.assertEqual(latest["successful"], 1)
94.         self.assertEqual(latest["failed"], 1)
95.         self.assertEqual(latest["open"], 1)
96.         self.assertAlmostEqual(latest["successRate"] or 0, 50.0, places=2)
97. 
98.     def test_stock_detail_endpoint_returns_history_and_profiles(self) -> None:
99.         with patch.object(api_main, "load_dataset", return_value=self.dataset):
100.             response = api_main.get_recommendation("RELIANCE", "single_day")
101. 
102.         self.assertEqual(response.selectedHorizon, "single_day")
103.         self.assertEqual(response.stock.symbol, "RELIANCE")
104.         self.assertEqual(response.stock.sector, "Energy")
105.         self.assertIn("single_day", response.stock.profiles)
106.         self.assertGreater(len(response.history), 0)
107.         self.assertTrue(all(entry.horizon == "single_day" for entry in response.history))
108. 
109.     def test_stock_detail_endpoint_raises_404_for_unknown_symbol(self) -> None:
110.         with patch.object(api_main, "load_dataset", return_value=self.dataset):
111.             with self.assertRaises(HTTPException) as context:
112.                 api_main.get_recommendation("UNKNOWN", "swing")
113. 
114.         self.assertEqual(context.exception.status_code, 404)
115. 
116. 
117. if __name__ == "__main__":
118.     unittest.main()
119.
```
