```
262.   plan: RecommendationPlan;
263.   activeHorizon: HorizonId;
264.   profileLabel: string;
265.   isSelected: boolean;
266.   onSelect: () => void;
267. }) {
268.   const state = signalStateFor(plan, activeHorizon);
269.   const confidence = confidenceMeta(plan, activeHorizon);
270.   const riskReward = riskRewardMeta(plan.riskReward);
271.   const trend = trendMeta(stock.latestSessionChangePct);
272.   const stockHref = `/stocks/${stock.symbol}`;
273. 
274.   return (
275.     <tr
276.       aria-label={`Select ${stock.companyName}`}
277.       aria-pressed={isSelected}
278.       className={`dashboard-stock-row ${state}${isSelected ? " selected" : ""}`}
279.       onClick={onSelect}
280.       onKeyDown={(event) => {
281.         if (event.key === "Enter" || event.key === " ") {
282.           event.preventDefault();
283.           onSelect();
284.         }
285.       }}
286.       role="button"
287.       tabIndex={0}
288.     >
289.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell dashboard-stock-identity">
290.         <Link
291.           className="dashboard-stock-open"
292.           href={stockHref}
293.           onClick={(event) => event.stopPropagation()}
294.         >
295.           {stock.companyName}
296.         </Link>
297.         <div className="dashboard-stock-meta">
298.           <span>{stock.symbol}</span>
299.           <span>{stock.sector}</span>
300.         </div>
301.       </td>
302. 
303.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell">
304.         <span className={`dashboard-signal-badge ${state}`}>
305.           <span className="dashboard-signal-dot" />
306.           {signalLabel(state)}
307.         </span>
308.       </td>
309. 
310.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell">
311.         <div className="dashboard-table-score">
312.           <div className="dashboard-table-score-value">{(plan.score ?? 0).toFixed(1)} / 100</div>
313.           <div className={`dashboard-table-score-note ${confidence.tone}`}>{confidence.label}</div>
314.           <ScoreMeter tone={confidence.tone} progress={confidence.progress} threshold={confidence.threshold} />
315.         </div>
316.       </td>
317. 
318.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell">
319.         <div className="dashboard-price-stack">
320.           <strong>{formatPrice(stock.currentMarketPrice)}</strong>
321.           <span className={`dashboard-inline-trend ${trend.tone}`}>{trend.move}</span>
322.         </div>
323.       </td>
324. 
325.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell target">
326.         <strong>{formatPrice(plan.targetPrice)}</strong>
327.       </td>
328. 
329.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell stop">
330.         <strong>{formatPrice(plan.stopLoss)}</strong>
331.       </td>
332. 
333.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell">
334.         <div className={`dashboard-rr-badge ${riskReward.tone}`}>
335.           {plan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} - {riskReward.label}
336.         </div>
337.       </td>
338. 
339.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell">
340.         <span className="dashboard-return-value">{formatPercent(plan.expectedReturnPct)}</span>
341.       </td>
342. 
343.       <td className="dashboard-stock-cell dashboard-stock-action">
344.         <Link
345.           className={`dashboard-row-action ${state}`}
346.           href={stockHref}
347.           onClick={(event) => event.stopPropagation()}
348.         >
349.           {actionLabel(state, profileLabel)}
350.         </Link>
351.       </td>
352.     </tr>
353.   );
354. }
355. 
356. export function Dashboard({ data, searchedAnalysis }: DashboardProps) {
357.   const [activeHorizon, setActiveHorizon] = useState<HorizonId>(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
358.   const targetVisibleIdeas = 10;
359. 
360.   useEffect(() => {
361.     if (!data.profiles.some((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon)) {
362.       setActiveHorizon(data.profiles[0]?.id ?? "single_day");
363.     }
364.   }, [activeHorizon, data.profiles]);
365. 
366.   const activeProfile = data.profiles.find((profile) => profile.id === activeHorizon) ?? data.profiles[0];
367.   const profileLabel = activeProfile?.label ?? "Single-day";
368.   const sourceProvider = data.dataSource?.provider ?? "Live recommendation loader";
369.   const watchlistName = compactUniverseLabel(data.universe);
370.   const liveStatus = liveStatusMeta(data.dataSource?.mode);
371. 
372.   const localSearchSuggestions = useMemo(
373.     () =>
374.       data.currentBatch.recommendations.map((stock) => ({
375.         symbol: stock.symbol,
376.         companyName: stock.companyName,
377.         sector: stock.sector,
378.         industry: stock.industry
379.       })),
380.     [data.currentBatch.recommendations]
381.   );
382. 
383.   const recommendationBuckets = useMemo(() => {
384.     const sorted = [...data.currentBatch.recommendations].sort(
385.       (left, right) => planScore(right.profiles[activeHorizon]) - planScore(left.profiles[activeHorizon])
386.     );
387.     const actionable = sorted.filter(
388.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) !== "avoid"
389.     );
390.     const tradableCount = actionable.filter(
391.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) === "consider"
392.     ).length;
393.     const watchCount = actionable.filter(
394.       (stock) => signalStateFor(stock.profiles[activeHorizon], activeHorizon) === "watch"
395.     ).length;
396. 
397.     return {
398.       visibleRecommendations: actionable.slice(0, targetVisibleIdeas),
399.       tradableCount,
400.       watchCount
401.     };
402.   }, [activeHorizon, data.currentBatch.recommendations]);
403.   const visibleRecommendations = recommendationBuckets.visibleRecommendations;
404.   const [selectedSymbol, setSelectedSymbol] = useState<string | null>(visibleRecommendations[0]?.symbol ?? null);
405. 
406.   useEffect(() => {
407.     if (!visibleRecommendations.length) {
408.       setSelectedSymbol(null);
409.       return;
410.     }
411. 
412.     const searchedSymbol = searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol;
413.     const searchedVisible = searchedSymbol
414.       ? visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === searchedSymbol)?.symbol
415.       : null;
416. 
417.     if (!selectedSymbol || !visibleRecommendations.some((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol)) {
418.       setSelectedSymbol(searchedVisible ?? visibleRecommendations[0].symbol);
419.     }
420.   }, [searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol, selectedSymbol, visibleRecommendations]);
421. 
422.   const dailyPerformance = useMemo(
423.     () => buildDailyPerformance(data.history, activeHorizon),
424.     [activeHorizon, data.history]
425.   );
426.   const performanceSummary = useMemo(
427.     () => buildPerformanceSummary(dailyPerformance),
428.     [dailyPerformance]
429.   );
430. 
431.   const spotlightStock =
432.     visibleRecommendations.find((stock) => stock.symbol === selectedSymbol) ?? visibleRecommendations[0];
433.   const spotlightPlan = spotlightStock?.profiles[activeHorizon];
434.   const spotlightConfidence = spotlightPlan ? confidenceMeta(spotlightPlan, activeHorizon) : null;
435.   const spotlightTrend = trendMeta(spotlightStock?.latestSessionChangePct);
436.   const spotlightRisk = spotlightPlan ? riskRewardMeta(spotlightPlan.riskReward) : null;
437. 
438.   const recommendedCount = recommendationBuckets.tradableCount;
439.   const watchCount = recommendationBuckets.watchCount;
440. 
441.   const quickSearchSymbols = useMemo(() => {
442.     const items = [
443.       searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol,
444.       ...visibleRecommendations.slice(0, 4).map((stock) => stock.symbol)
445.     ].filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value));
446. 
447.     return [...new Set(items)].slice(0, 5);
448.   }, [searchedAnalysis?.stock?.symbol, visibleRecommendations]);
449. 
450.   const contextItems = [
451.     { label: "Exchange", value: data.exchange },
452.     { label: "Watchlist", value: watchlistName },
453.     { label: "Horizon", value: profileLabel },
454.     { label: "Data source", value: sourceProvider },
455.     { label: "Last updated", value: formatDateTime(data.currentBatch.generatedAt) },
456.     { label: "Status", value: liveStatus.label, tone: liveStatus.tone }
457.   ];
458. 
459.   const overviewCards = [
460.     {
461.       icon: "EX",
462.       label: "Exchange",
463.       tooltip: "The active market being screened in this dashboard.",
464.       value: data.exchange,
465.       footnote: watchlistName
466.     },
467.     {
468.       icon: "HZ",
469.       label: "Active horizon",
470.       tooltip: "The recommendation window controlling score thresholds and trade plans.",
471.       value: profileLabel,
472.       footnote: activeProfile?.window ?? "Model profile"
473.     },
474.     {
475.       icon: "WL",
476.       label: "Watchlist stocks",
477.       tooltip: "The shortlist shown in the live table for fast scanning.",
478.       value: formatNumber(visibleRecommendations.length),
479.       footnote: `${recommendedCount} tradable · ${watchCount} watch`
480.     },
481.     {
482.       icon: "AN",
483.       label: "Analyzed stocks",
484.       tooltip: "Names processed in the current recommendation batch.",
485.       value: formatNumber(data.currentBatch.recommendations.length),
486.       footnote: data.currentBatch.batchDate
487.     },
488.     {
489.       icon: "AR",
490.       label: "Avg return",
491.       tooltip: "Average return across completed recommendation batches.",
492.       value: formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct),
493.       footnote: `Success ${formatPercent(performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate)}`
494.     }
495.   ];
496. 
497.   const archiveSuccessRate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.successRate ?? performanceSummary.averageSuccessRate;
498.   const archiveReturn = performanceSummary.averageClosedReturnPct;
499.   const latestCompletedDate = performanceSummary.latestCompleted?.batchDate
500.     ? formatDate(performanceSummary.latestCompleted.batchDate)
501.     : "No closed batch yet";
502. 
503.   return (
504.     <main className="shell dashboard-redesign-shell">
505.       <section className="dashboard-context-bar" aria-label="Active dashboard context">
506.         {contextItems.map((item) => (
507.           <div className="dashboard-context-item" key={item.label}>
508.             <span className="dashboard-context-label">{item.label}</span>
509.             <strong className={item.tone ? `dashboard-context-value ${item.tone}` : "dashboard-context-value"}>
510.               {item.value}
511.             </strong>
512.           </div>
513.         ))}
514.       </section>
515. 
516.       <section className="dashboard-hero">
517.         <div className="dashboard-hero-copy">
518.           <span className="dashboard-mini-label">NSE equities workspace</span>
519.           <h1>Scan ideas, compare trade plans, and act fast.</h1>
520.           <p>
521.             Search a stock first, then use the table to compare score, price levels, and risk/reward in
522.             one pass.
523.           </p>
524.         </div>
525. 
526.         <div className="dashboard-search-zone">
527.           <StockSearchBox
528.             initialQuery={searchedAnalysis?.query ?? ""}
529.             localSuggestions={localSearchSuggestions}
530.           />
531. 
532.           <div className="dashboard-quick-searches" aria-label="Quick stock lookups">
533.             <span className="dashboard-quick-search-label">Quick lookups</span>
534.             <div className="dashboard-quick-search-list">
535.               {quickSearchSymbols.map((symbol) => (
536.                 <Link key={symbol} className="dashboard-quick-search-chip" href={`/?symbol=${symbol}`}>
537.                   {symbol}
538.                 </Link>
539.               ))}
540.             </div>
541.           </div>
542.         </div>
543. 
544.         {searchedAnalysis ? (
545.           <SearchResultBanner
546.             searchedAnalysis={searchedAnalysis}
547.             stockHref={searchedAnalysis.stock ? `/stocks/${searchedAnalysis.stock.symbol}` : null}
548.             activeHorizon={activeHorizon}
549.             profileLabel={profileLabel}
550.           />
551.         ) : null}
552.       </section>
553. 
554.       <section className="dashboard-kpi-strip" aria-label="Market KPI strip">
555.         {overviewCards.map((card) => (
556.           <article className="dashboard-kpi-card" key={card.label}>
557.             <div className="dashboard-kpi-top">
558.               <span className="dashboard-kpi-icon" aria-hidden="true">
559.                 {card.icon}
560.               </span>
561.               <span className="dashboard-kpi-label">
562.                 {card.label}
563.                 <InfoTooltip content={card.tooltip} />
564.               </span>
565.             </div>
566.             <strong className="dashboard-kpi-value">{card.value}</strong>
567.             <span className="dashboard-kpi-footnote">{card.footnote}</span>
568.           </article>
569.         ))}
570.       </section>
571. 
572.       <section className="dashboard-main-grid">
573.         <section className="dashboard-table-panel">
574.           <div className="dashboard-section-head">
575.             <div>
576.               <span className="dashboard-mini-label">Live shortlist</span>
577.               <h2>Today&apos;s ranked stocks</h2>
578.             </div>
579. 
580.             <div className="dashboard-horizon-pills" role="tablist" aria-label="Recommendation horizon">
581.               {data.profiles.map((profile) => (
582.                 <button
583.                   key={profile.id}
584.                   className={`dashboard-horizon-pill${profile.id === activeHorizon ? " active" : ""}`}
585.                   onClick={() => setActiveHorizon(profile.id)}
586.                   type="button"
587.                 >
588.                   {profile.label}
589.                 </button>
590.               ))}
591.             </div>
592.           </div>
593. 
594.           <div className="dashboard-table-card">
595.             {visibleRecommendations.length ? (
596.               <div className="dashboard-table-scroll">
597.                 <table className="dashboard-stock-table">
598.                   <thead>
599.                     <tr>
600.                       <th>Stock</th>
601.                       <th>Signal</th>
602.                       <th>Score</th>
603.                       <th>CMP</th>
604.                       <th>Target</th>
605.                       <th>Stop-loss</th>
606.                       <th>Risk / Reward</th>
607.                       <th>Return</th>
608.                       <th>Action</th>
609.                     </tr>
610.                   </thead>
611.                   <tbody>
612.                     {visibleRecommendations.map((stock) => (
613.                       <StockTableRow
614.                         key={`${activeHorizon}-${stock.symbol}`}
615.                         stock={stock}
616.                         plan={stock.profiles[activeHorizon]}
617.                         activeHorizon={activeHorizon}
618.                         profileLabel={profileLabel}
619.                         isSelected={spotlightStock?.symbol === stock.symbol}
620.                         onSelect={() => setSelectedSymbol(stock.symbol)}
621.                       />
622.                     ))}
623.                   </tbody>
624.                 </table>
625.               </div>
626.             ) : (
627.               <div className="empty-state">
628.                 No tradable or watch setups cleared the current horizon threshold in this batch.
629.               </div>
630.             )}
631.           </div>
632.         </section>
633. 
634.         <aside className="dashboard-sidebar">
635.           {spotlightStock && spotlightPlan && spotlightConfidence && spotlightRisk ? (
636.             <section className="dashboard-side-panel dashboard-side-panel-primary" key={`${activeHorizon}-${spotlightStock.symbol}`}>
637.               <div className="dashboard-side-head">
638.                 <span className="dashboard-mini-label">Selected stock</span>
639.                 <h3>{spotlightStock.companyName}</h3>
640.                 <p>
641.                   {spotlightStock.symbol} · {spotlightStock.sector} · {formatPrice(spotlightStock.currentMarketPrice)}{" "}
642.                   <span className={`dashboard-inline-trend ${spotlightTrend.tone}`}>{spotlightTrend.move}</span>
643.                 </p>
644.               </div>
645. 
646.               <div className="dashboard-side-score">
647.                 <div className="dashboard-side-score-top">
648.                   <strong>{(spotlightPlan.score ?? 0).toFixed(1)} / 100</strong>
649.                   <span className={`dashboard-confidence-chip ${spotlightConfidence.tone}`}>
650.                     {spotlightConfidence.label}
651.                   </span>
652.                 </div>
653.                 <ScoreMeter
654.                   tone={spotlightConfidence.tone}
655.                   progress={spotlightConfidence.progress}
656.                   threshold={spotlightConfidence.threshold}
657.                   label={`Threshold ${spotlightConfidence.threshold}`}
658.                 />
659.               </div>
660. 
661.               <div className="dashboard-price-ladder">
662.                 <div className="dashboard-price-ladder-row target">
663.                   <span>Target</span>
664.                   <strong>{formatPrice(spotlightPlan.targetPrice)}</strong>
665.                 </div>
666.                 <div className="dashboard-price-ladder-row entry">
667.                   <span>Entry</span>
668.                   <strong>{formatPrice(spotlightPlan.entryPrice)}</strong>
669.                 </div>
670.                 <div className="dashboard-price-ladder-row stop">
671.                   <span>Stop-loss</span>
672.                   <strong>{formatPrice(spotlightPlan.stopLoss)}</strong>
673.                 </div>
674.               </div>
675. 
676.               <div className="dashboard-side-grid">
677.                 <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
678.                   <span>Risk / Reward</span>
679.                   <strong className={spotlightRisk.tone}>
680.                     {spotlightPlan.riskReward.toFixed(2)} - {spotlightRisk.label}
681.                   </strong>
682.                 </div>
683.                 <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
684.                   <span>Signal</span>
685.                   <strong>{signalLabel(signalStateFor(spotlightPlan, activeHorizon))}</strong>
686.                 </div>
687.               </div>
688. 
689.               <div className="dashboard-side-actions">
690.                 <Link
691.                   className={`dashboard-row-action dashboard-row-action-primary ${signalStateFor(spotlightPlan, activeHorizon)}`}
692.                   href={`/stocks/${spotlightStock.symbol}`}
693.                 >
694.                   {actionLabel(signalStateFor(spotlightPlan, activeHorizon), profileLabel)}
695.                 </Link>
696.                 <Link className="dashboard-row-action secondary" href={`/stocks/${spotlightStock.symbol}`}>
697.                   Open stock
698.                 </Link>
699.               </div>
700.             </section>
701.           ) : null}
702. 
703.           <section className="dashboard-side-panel">
704.             <div className="dashboard-side-head">
705.               <span className="dashboard-mini-label">Archive summary</span>
706.               <h3>Recommendation outcomes</h3>
707.               <p>Use history to validate what the model has been doing, not just what it says now.</p>
708.             </div>
709. 
710.             <div className="dashboard-side-grid">
711.               <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
712.                 <span>Success rate</span>
713.                 <strong>{formatPercent(archiveSuccessRate)}</strong>
714.               </div>
715.               <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
716.                 <span>Avg return</span>
717.                 <strong>{formatPercent(archiveReturn)}</strong>
718.               </div>
719.               <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
720.                 <span>Batch count</span>
721.                 <strong>{formatNumber(data.history.length)}</strong>
722.               </div>
723.               <div className="dashboard-side-stat">
724.                 <span>Latest close</span>
725.                 <strong>{latestCompletedDate}</strong>
726.               </div>
727.             </div>
728. 
729.             <Link className="dashboard-row-action secondary" href="/history">
730.               Open archive
731.             </Link>
732.           </section>
733. 
734.           <section className="dashboard-side-panel">
735.             <div className="dashboard-side-head">
736.               <span className="dashboard-mini-label">Market context</span>
737.               <h3>Current batch details</h3>
738.               <p>Keep the active screening state visible while scanning the shortlist.</p>
739.             </div>
740. 
741.             <div className="dashboard-context-grid">
742.               <div className="dashboard-context-row">
743.                 <span>Exchange</span>
744.                 <strong>{data.exchange}</strong>
745.               </div>
746.               <div className="dashboard-context-row">
747.                 <span>Horizon</span>
748.                 <strong>{profileLabel}</strong>
749.               </div>
750.               <div className="dashboard-context-row">
751.                 <span>Snapshot size</span>
752.                 <strong>{formatNumber(data.currentBatch.recommendations.length)} stocks</strong>
753.               </div>
754.               <div className="dashboard-context-row">
755.                 <span>Data source</span>
756.                 <strong>{sourceProvider}</strong>
757.               </div>
```

```
1. "use client";
2. 
3. import Link from "next/link";
4. import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
5. import {
6.   EmptyState,
7.   formatDate,
8.   formatDateTime,
9.   formatNumber,
10.   formatPercent,
11.   formatPrice,
12.   isRecommendedPlan
13. } from "@/components/market-ui";
14. import {
15.   buildDailyPerformance,
16.   buildPerformanceSummary,
17.   outcomeClass,
18.   outcomeLabel
19. } from "@/lib/analytics";
20. import type {
21.   DailyPerformance,
22.   HistoricalRecommendationPlan,
23.   HistoricalStockRecommendation,
24.   HorizonId,
25.   RecommendationDataset
26. } from "@/lib/types";
27. 
28. type HistoryDashboardProps = {
29.   data: RecommendationDataset;
30. };
31. 
32. type UiTone = "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral";
33. type RangeFilter = "5" | "10" | "all";
34. type SignalFilter = "all" | "buy" | "watch" | "avoid";
35. type ScoreFilter = "all" | "60_plus" | "50_59" | "below_50";
36. type SortKey = "date" | "successRate" | "trades";
37. type SortDirection = "asc" | "desc";
38. 
39. type ArchiveEntry = {
40.   batchDate: string;
41.   publishedAt: string;
42.   stock: HistoricalStockRecommendation;
43.   plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan;
44.   riskReward: number;
45.   signal: {
46.     id: Exclude<SignalFilter, "all">;
47.     label: string;
48.     tone: UiTone;
49.   };
50. };
51. 
52. const HORIZON_THRESHOLDS: Record<HorizonId, number> = {
53.   single_day: 54,
54.   swing: 56,
55.   position: 58,
56.   long_term: 60
57. };
58. 
59. const RANGE_OPTIONS: Array<{ id: RangeFilter; label: string }> = [
60.   { id: "5", label: "Last 5" },
61.   { id: "10", label: "Last 10" },
62.   { id: "all", label: "All" }
63. ];
64. 
65. const SIGNAL_OPTIONS: Array<{ id: SignalFilter; label: string }> = [
66.   { id: "all", label: "All signals" },
67.   { id: "buy", label: "Buy" },
68.   { id: "watch", label: "Watch" },
69.   { id: "avoid", label: "Avoid" }
70. ];
71. 
72. const SCORE_OPTIONS: Array<{ id: ScoreFilter; label: string }> = [
73.   { id: "all", label: "All scores" },
74.   { id: "60_plus", label: "60+" },
75.   { id: "50_59", label: "50-59" },
76.   { id: "below_50", label: "Below 50" }
77. ];
78. 
79. function roundMetric(value: number) {
80.   return Math.round(value * 100) / 100;
81. }
82. 
83. function rangeLimit(range: RangeFilter) {
84.   switch (range) {
85.     case "5":
86.       return 5;
87.     case "10":
88.       return 10;
89.     default:
90.       return null;
91.   }
92. }
93. 
94. function rangeLabel(range: RangeFilter, visibleRows: number) {
95.   switch (range) {
96.     case "5":
97.       return `Last 5 sessions (${visibleRows})`;
98.     case "10":
99.       return `Last 10 sessions (${visibleRows})`;
100.     default:
101.       return `Full archive (${visibleRows})`;
102.   }
103. }
104. 
105. function firstSentence(text: string) {
106.   const normalized = text.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
107. 
108.   if (!normalized) {
109.     return "";
110.   }
111. 
112.   const match = normalized.match(/.+?[.!?](?:\s|$)/);
113.   return (match?.[0] ?? normalized).trim();
114. }
115. 
116. function shorten(text: string, maxLength = 132) {
117.   const normalized = firstSentence(text);
118. 
119.   if (normalized.length <= maxLength) {
120.     return normalized;
121.   }
122. 
123.   return `${normalized.slice(0, maxLength - 1).trimEnd()}…`;
124. }
125. 
126. function planScore(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan) {
127.   return plan.score ?? 0;
128. }
129. 
130. function planRiskReward(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan) {
131.   return roundMetric((plan.targetPrice - plan.entryPrice) / Math.max(plan.entryPrice - plan.stopLoss, 0.01));
132. }
133. 
134. function signalMeta(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan, horizon: HorizonId) {
135.   const score = planScore(plan);
136.   const threshold = HORIZON_THRESHOLDS[horizon];
137. 
138.   if (isRecommendedPlan(plan) || score >= threshold) {
139.     return {
140.       id: "buy" as const,
141.       label: "Buy",
142.       tone: "success" as UiTone
143.     };
144.   }
145. 
146.   if (score >= threshold - 8) {
147.     return {
148.       id: "watch" as const,
149.       label: "Watch",
150.       tone: "warning" as UiTone
151.     };
152.   }
153. 
154.   return {
155.     id: "avoid" as const,
156.     label: "Avoid",
157.     tone: "danger" as UiTone
158.   };
159. }
160. 
161. function outcomeMeta(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan) {
162.   const tone = outcomeClass(plan.outcome.result) as UiTone;
163. 
164.   return {
165.     tone,
166.     label: outcomeLabel(plan.outcome.result)
167.   };
168. }
169. 
170. function performanceMeta(successRate: number | null) {
171.   if (successRate === null) {
172.     return {
173.       tone: "neutral" as UiTone,
174.       shortLabel: "Pending",
175.       longLabel: "Pending"
176.     };
177.   }
178. 
179.   if (successRate >= 60) {
180.     return {
181.       tone: "success" as UiTone,
182.       shortLabel: `${successRate.toFixed(0)}% - Profitable`,
183.       longLabel: "Profitable"
184.     };
185.   }
186. 
187.   if (successRate < 40) {
188.     return {
189.       tone: "danger" as UiTone,
190.       shortLabel: `${successRate.toFixed(0)}% - Loss-making`,
191.       longLabel: "Loss-making"
192.     };
193.   }
194. 
195.   return {
196.     tone: "warning" as UiTone,
197.     shortLabel: `${successRate.toFixed(0)}% - Neutral`,
198.     longLabel: "Neutral"
199.   };
200. }
201. 
202. function scoreFilterMatches(score: number | undefined, filter: ScoreFilter) {
203.   const numericScore = score ?? 0;
204. 
205.   switch (filter) {
206.     case "60_plus":
207.       return numericScore >= 60;
208.     case "50_59":
209.       return numericScore >= 50 && numericScore < 60;
210.     case "below_50":
211.       return numericScore < 50;
212.     default:
213.       return true;
214.   }
215. }
216. 
217. function confidenceBucket(plan: HistoricalRecommendationPlan, horizon: HorizonId) {
218.   const threshold = HORIZON_THRESHOLDS[horizon];
219.   const score = planScore(plan);
220. 
221.   if (score >= threshold + 8) {
222.     return "High confidence";
223.   }
224. 
225.   if (score >= threshold) {
226.     return "Tradable";
227.   }
228. 
229.   if (score >= threshold - 8) {
230.     return "Watch setups";
231.   }
232. 
233.   return "Low score";
234. }
235. 
236. function maxDrawdown(entries: ArchiveEntry[]) {
237.   const chronologicalReturns = [...entries]
238.     .filter((entry) => entry.plan.outcome.result !== "open")
239.     .sort((left, right) => left.batchDate.localeCompare(right.batchDate));
240. 
241.   if (!chronologicalReturns.length) {
242.     return null;
243.   }
244. 
245.   let equity = 100;
246.   let peak = 100;
247.   let worstDrawdown = 0;
248. 
249.   for (const entry of chronologicalReturns) {
250.     equity *= 1 + entry.plan.outcome.returnPct / 100;
251.     peak = Math.max(peak, equity);
252.     worstDrawdown = Math.max(worstDrawdown, ((peak - equity) / peak) * 100);
253.   }
254. 
255.   return roundMetric(worstDrawdown);
256. }
257. 
258. function average(values: Array<number | null>) {
259.   const resolved = values.filter((value): value is number => value !== null);
260.
```

</details>

| **4. News layer** | **Partial** | Google News-based sentiment, earnings/analyst headline classification, reasoning | **NSE announcements**, optional **MoneyControl** ingestion, proper **stock tagging**, **sector grouping**, and **relevance ranking** |

| **7. Research-console dashboard** | **Partial / in progress** | Redesigned dashboard, search, archive, recommendation vs watchlist separation, stronger stock detail workspace | Full **stock table**, filters for **sector / sentiment / score / growth**, explicit **top gainers / losers** views, and finishing **sector everywhere** |
| **8. Production basics** | **Mostly missing** | Health check, cached fallback, basic source-mode visibility | **Structured logs**, source success/failure tracking, **freshness checks**, **retry handling**, and a simple **monitoring view** |

**Mapped to the current todo list:**
- **4** → `build-ingestion` (**in progress**), but it still needs the NSE/news-ranking expansion
- **5** → `build-backtesting` (**pending**)
- **6** → `schedule-daily-runs` (**pending**)
- **7** → `redesign-dashboard-ux` + `add-sector-columns-ui` (**in progress**)
- **8** → `add-monitoring` (**pending**)

So yes—these are still the key things left, and **5 + 6** are the biggest unfinished v1 capabilities.

**Show merged backlog counts**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Show merged backlog counts",
  "query": "SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count\nFROM todos\nWHERE status IN ('pending','in_progress','blocked')\nGROUP BY status\nORDER BY CASE status WHEN 'pending' THEN 1 WHEN 'in_progress' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END;"
}
```

````
# GitHub Copilot CLI Documentation

## README
# GitHub Copilot CLI

The power of GitHub Copilot, now in your terminal.

GitHub Copilot CLI brings AI-powered coding assistance directly to your command line, enabling you to build, debug, and understand code through natural language conversations. Powered by the same agentic harness as GitHub's Copilot coding agent, it provides intelligent assistance while staying deeply integrated with your GitHub workflow.

See [our official documentation](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli) for more information.

![Image of the splash screen for the Copilot CLI](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f40aa23d-09dd-499e-9457-1d57d3368887)


## 🚀 Introduction and Overview

We're bringing the power of GitHub Copilot coding agent directly to your terminal. With GitHub Copilot CLI, you can work locally and synchronously with an AI agent that understands your code and GitHub context.

- **Terminal-native development:** Work with Copilot coding agent directly in your command line — no context switching required.
- **GitHub integration out of the box:** Access your repositories, issues, and pull requests using natural language, all authenticated with your existing GitHub account.
- **Agentic capabilities:** Build, edit, debug, and refactor code with an AI collaborator that can plan and execute complex tasks.
- **MCP-powered extensibility:** Take advantage of the fact that the coding agent ships with GitHub's MCP server by default and supports custom MCP servers to extend capabilities.
- **Full control:** Preview every action before execution — nothing happens without your explicit approval.

We're still early in our journey, but with your feedback, we're rapidly iterating to make the GitHub Copilot CLI the best possible companion in your terminal.

## 📦 Getting Started

### Supported Platforms

- **Linux**
- **macOS**
- **Windows**

### Prerequisites

- (On Windows) **PowerShell** v6 or higher
- An **active Copilot subscription**. See [Copilot plans](https://github.com/features/copilot/plans?ref_cta=Copilot+plans+signup&ref_loc=install-copilot-cli&ref_page=docs).

If you have access to GitHub Copilot via your organization or enterprise, you cannot use GitHub Copilot CLI if your organization owner or enterprise administrator has disabled it in the organization or enterprise settings. See [Managing policies and features for GitHub Copilot in your organization](http://docs.github.com/copilot/managing-copilot/managing-github-copilot-in-your-organization/managing-github-copilot-features-in-your-organization/managing-policies-for-copilot-in-your-organization) for more information.

### Installation

Install with the install script (macOS and Linux):

```bash
curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash
```

Or

```bash
wget -qO- https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash
```

Use `| sudo bash` to run as root and install to `/usr/local/bin`.

Set `PREFIX` to install to `$PREFIX/bin/` directory. Defaults to `/usr/local`
when run as root or `$HOME/.local` when run as a non-root user.

Set `VERSION` to install a specific version. Defaults to the latest version.

For example, to install version `v0.0.369` to a custom directory:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | VERSION="v0.0.369" PREFIX="$HOME/custom" bash
```

Install with [Homebrew](https://formulae.brew.sh/cask/copilot-cli) (macOS and Linux):

```bash
brew install copilot-cli
```

```bash
brew install copilot-cli@prerelease
```


Install with [WinGet](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-cli) (Windows):

```bash
winget install GitHub.Copilot
```

```bash
winget install GitHub.Copilot.Prerelease
```


Install with [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot) (macOS, Linux, and Windows):

```bash
npm install -g @github/copilot
```

```bash
npm install -g @github/copilot@prerelease
```


### Launching the CLI

```bash
copilot
```

On first launch, you'll be greeted with our adorable animated banner! If you'd like to see this banner again, launch `copilot` with the `--banner` flag.

If you're not currently logged in to GitHub, you'll be prompted to use the `/login` slash command. Enter this command and follow the on-screen instructions to authenticate.

#### Authenticate with a Personal Access Token (PAT)

You can also authenticate using a fine-grained PAT with the "Copilot Requests" permission enabled.

1. Visit https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/new
2. Under "Permissions," click "add permissions" and select "Copilot Requests"
3. Generate your token
4. Add the token to your environment via the environment variable `GH_TOKEN` or `GITHUB_TOKEN` (in order of precedence)

### Using the CLI

Launch `copilot` in a folder that contains code you want to work with.

By default, `copilot` utilizes Claude Sonnet 4.5. Run the `/model` slash command to choose from other available models, including Claude Sonnet 4 and GPT-5.

### Experimental Mode

Experimental mode enables access to new features that are still in development. You can activate experimental mode by:

- Launching with the `--experimental` flag: `copilot --experimental`
- Using the `/experimental` slash command from within the CLI

Once activated, the setting is persisted in your config, so the `--experimental` flag is no longer needed on subsequent launches.

#### Experimental Features

- **Autopilot mode:** Autopilot is a new mode (press `Shift+Tab` to cycle through modes), which encourages the agent to continue working until a task is completed.

Each time you submit a prompt to GitHub Copilot CLI, your monthly quota of premium requests is reduced by one. For information about premium requests, see [About premium requests](https://docs.github.com/copilot/managing-copilot/monitoring-usage-and-entitlements/about-premium-requests).

For more information about how to use the GitHub Copilot CLI, see [our official documentation](https://docs.github.com/copilot/concepts/agents/about-copilot-cli).

## 🔧 Configuring LSP Servers

GitHub Copilot CLI supports Language Server Protocol (LSP) for enhanced code intelligence. This feature provides intelligent code features like go-to-definition, hover information, and diagnostics.

### Installing Language Servers

Copilot CLI does not bundle LSP servers. You need to install them separately. For example, to set up TypeScript support:

```bash
npm install -g typescript-language-server
```

For other languages, install the corresponding LSP server and configure it following the same pattern shown below.

### Configuring LSP Servers

LSP servers are configured through a dedicated LSP configuration file. You can configure LSP servers at the user level or repository level:

**User-level configuration** (applies to all projects):
Edit `~/.copilot/lsp-config.json`

**Repository-level configuration** (applies to specific project):
Create `.github/lsp.json` in your repository root

Example configuration:

```json
{
  "lspServers": {
    "typescript": {
      "command": "typescript-language-server",
      "args": ["--stdio"],
      "fileExtensions": {
        ".ts": "typescript",
        ".tsx": "typescript"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Viewing LSP Server Status

Check configured LSP servers using the `/lsp` command in an interactive session, or view your configuration files directly.

For more information, see the [changelog](./changelog.md).

## 📢 Feedback and Participation

We're excited to have you join us early in the Copilot CLI journey.

We're building quickly. Expect frequent updates--please keep your client up to date for the latest features and fixes!

Your insights are invaluable! Open an issue in this repo, join Discussions, and run `/feedback` from the CLI to submit a confidential feedback survey!


## Help Command Output
Global
  /help - show full help
  / - commands
  @ - mention files
  # - mention issues and pull requests
  ! - execute shell command
  shift+tab - switch modes
  ctrl+s - run command, preserve input
  ctrl+o/e - expand all timeline
  ctrl+c - cancel
  ctrl+c×2 - exit
  esc - cancel
  ctrl+d - shutdown
  ctrl+l - clear screen
  ctrl+x → o - open most recent link

Input
  ctrl+a - go to line start
  ctrl+e - go to line end
  ctrl+h - delete previous character
  ctrl+w - delete previous word
  ctrl+u - delete from cursor to beginning of line
  ctrl+k - delete from cursor to end of line
  meta+←/→ - move cursor by word
  ctrl+g - edit prompt in $EDITOR

Agent Environment
  /init - Initialize Copilot instructions for this repository.
  /agent - Browse and select from available agents (if any)
  /skills - Manage skills for enhanced capabilities
  /mcp - Manage MCP server configuration
  /plugin - Manage plugins and plugin marketplaces

Agents / Subagents
  /model - Select AI model to use
  /delegate - Send this session to GitHub and Copilot will create a PR
  /fleet - Enable fleet mode for parallel subagent execution
  /tasks - View and manage background tasks (subagents and shell sessions)

Code
  /ide - Connect to an IDE workspace
  /diff - Review the changes made in the current directory
  /pr - Operate on pull requests for the current branch
  /review - Run code review agent to analyze changes
  /lsp - Manage language server configuration
  /terminal-setup - Configure terminal for multiline input support (shift+enter)

Permissions
  /allow-all - Enable all permissions (tools, paths, and URLs)
  /add-dir - Add a directory to the allowed list for file access
  /list-dirs - Display all allowed directories for file access
  /cwd - Change working directory or show current directory
  /reset-allowed-tools - Reset the list of allowed tools

Session
  /resume - Switch to a different session (optionally specify session ID or task ID)
  /rename - Rename the current session, or auto-generate a name from conversation
  /context - Show context window token usage and visualization
  /usage - Display session usage metrics and statistics
  /session - View and manage sessions. Use subcommands for details.
  /compact - Summarize conversation history to reduce context window usage
  /share - Share session or research report to markdown file, HTML file, or GitHub gist
  /remote - Enable remote control of your session from GitHub web and mobile
  /copy - Copy the last response to the clipboard
  /rewind - Rewind the last turn and revert file changes

Help
  /help - Show help for interactive commands
  /changelog - Display changelog for CLI versions. Add 'summarize' to get an AI summary.
  /feedback - Provide feedback about the CLI
  /theme - View or set color mode
  /update - Update the CLI to the latest version
  /version - Display version information and check for updates
  /experimental - Show available experimental features, or enable/disable experimental mode
  /clear - Abandon this session and start fresh
  /instructions - View and toggle custom instruction files
  /streamer-mode - Toggle streamer mode (hides preview model names and quota details for streaming)

Other commands
  /ask - Ask a quick side question without adding to conversation history
  /env - Show loaded environment details (instructions, MCP servers, skills, agents, plugins, LSPs, extensions)
  /exit - Exit the CLI
  /login - Log in to Copilot
  /logout - Log out of an OAuth login session
  /new - Start a new conversation
  /plan - Create an implementation plan before coding
  /research - Run deep research investigation using GitHub search and web sources
  /restart - Restart the CLI, preserving the current session
  /undo - Rewind the last turn and revert file changes
  /user - Manage GitHub user list

Copilot respects instructions from these locations:
  CLAUDE.md
  GEMINI.md
  AGENTS.md (in git root & cwd)
  .github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md (in git root & cwd)
  .github/copilot-instructions.md
  $HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md
  COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS (additional directories via env var)

To learn about what I can do
  Ask me: "What can you do?"
  Or visit: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/use-copilot-cli

---

**Note**: If the above documentation doesn't answer the user's request, you can fetch additional documentation from the official GitHub Docs for more information.

Use the web_fetch tool to fetch https://docs.github.com/api/article/body?pathname=/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/use-copilot-cli

You can also follow hyperlinks to related docs pages using the same API pattern.
````

<sub>⏱️ 491m 19s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Best way:** use the same Copilot session later with **`/resume`**.

1. **Before closing**, run **`/rename`** and give this session a clear name, like `stock-v1-backlog`.
2. **Later**, reopen Copilot in the same repo and run **`/resume`** or **`/session`** to find and reopen that session.
3. If you want a stronger handoff, run **`/share`** and save a markdown summary before closing.
4. If the conversation is long, run **`/compact`** first so the important context is preserved more efficiently.

**Important:** if you start a brand-new session instead of resuming, the full conversation context will not automatically carry over. In that case, the safest handoff is:
- resume the old session, **or**
- give me the saved summary / shared markdown / current todo list and I can continue from there.

### ℹ️ Info

Share Command Usage:
/share [path] - Share session to a markdown file (defaults to current directory)
/share file [path] - Share session to a markdown file at the specified path
/share html [path] - Share session to an interactive HTML file
/share file html [path] - Share session to an interactive HTML file at the specified path
/share gist - Create a secret GitHub gist with the session content
/share html research [path] - Save research report to interactive HTML file
/share file html research [path] - Save research report to interactive HTML file at the specified path
/share file research [path] - Save research report to file
/share gist research - Share research report to gist
/share research [path] - Shorthand for /share file research

Examples:
/share - Share to copilot-session-\<id\>.md in current directory
/share html - Share to copilot-session-\<id\>.html in current directory
/share ~/sessions/my-session.md - Share to specific file path
/share gist - Create a secret gist (requires login)
/share research - Save research report to file
/share html research - Save research report as interactive HTML
